[{"id":146,"downloadCount":1633,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2025-11-03T10:17:35.123621Z","name":"AI Localization with Custom.MT","description":"<p><strong>AI Localization with Custom.MT plugin</strong> brings the latest neural machine translation (MT) and GenAI models (LLMs) to Trados Studio.</p>\r\n<h4><strong>Key Features</strong></h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?si=V8IDOgcpfcegTiL4&v=t-1TP6tbtwU&feature=youtu.be\"><strong>AI Workflows</strong></a> – build workflow templates that combine NMT and LLMs and generate automated translations based on style guide, formality, and units of measurement settings.  </li>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?si=0vEIqel1NDEcA42H&v=V-oiY5MgkN0&feature=youtu.be\"><strong>Automated Post-Editing (APE)</strong></a>: improve NMT output using LLMs with custom prompts.  </li>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://youtu.be/tsfTX6j2AjA?si=NgDI4wqpfuxTMkJP\"><strong>Custom Prompts</strong></a>: create, edit, and reuse your own GenAI prompts to stylize the text. You can define any instruction set to fit your workflow.  </li>\r\n<li><strong>Best Engine</strong>: evaluate MT or LLM models based on closeness to your translation memory (TM) using BLEU scores and Word-Error Rate metrics.  </li>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://youtu.be/Cg3gsqOuJtQ?si=loHiMIv55qI5QtGG\"><strong>AI Glossaries</strong></a>: add your terminology assets and let AI automatically inflect and adapt terms to the correct form, gender, and number in context.  </li>\r\n<li><strong>Centralized Access</strong>: no need to purchase API keys or create separate accounts – all models and engines are available through your Custom.MT Console.  </li>\r\n<li><strong>Data Security</strong>: all data is processed within the EU (AWS Frankfurt, ISO 27001 / SOC 2 compliant), with no data retained outside your project session. Custom.MT has opt-out and data protection agreements with OpenAI and Anthropic Claude.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h4><strong>Large Language Models (LLMs) supported</strong></h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o, GPT-5, mini and nano)  </li>\r\n<li>Anthropic Claude-4  </li>\r\n<li>Gemini 2.5 Pro  </li>\r\n<li>Widn Tower (Anthill, Sugarloaf, Vesuvius)  </li>\r\n<li>Lara</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h4><strong>Neural Machine Translation (MT) supported</strong></h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>DeepL  </li>\r\n<li>Google  </li>\r\n<li>Microsoft  </li>\r\n<li>Amazon  </li>\r\n<li>ModernMT  </li>\r\n<li>Baidu  </li>\r\n<li>Systran  </li>\r\n<li>Tencent TranSmart  </li>\r\n<li>XL8  </li>\r\n<li>Globalese  </li>\r\n<li>Yandex  </li>\r\n<li>LanguageWire  </li>\r\n<li>Sayqalchi  </li>\r\n<li>Tilmoch  </li>\r\n<li>Papago  </li>\r\n<li>Pangea  </li>\r\n<li>Tarjama  </li>\r\n<li>Alibaba  </li>\r\n<li>Supertext</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><a href=\"http://custom.mt\"><strong>Custom.MT</strong></a> supports custom and fine-tuned MT and GenAI models.  </p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h4><strong>Installation</strong></h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Download and install the Custom.MT Translation plugin from the RWS AppStore.  </li>\r\n<li>Sign up for the <a href=\"https://console.custom.mt/\"><strong>Custom.MT Console</strong></a>.  </li>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://youtu.be/yvG5p1ppLqg?si=M43yx3_TfmqLfKXW\"><strong>Create workflow templates</strong></a> with your preferred MT or LLM model, glossary, and prompts.  </li>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://youtu.be/nF7iDxPT-Q8?si=w6gEQ9NT6L-t52Wx\"><strong>Connect</strong></a> your Console API key to Trados.  </li>\r\n<li>When creating a project, make sure the language pair in Trados matches the one in your template. The plugin will automatically display all templates available for that language pair.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p>See pricing and plans here: <a href=\"https://custom.mt/custom-machine-translation-platform/\">https://custom.mt/custom-machine-translation-platform/</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h4>Introduction</h4>\r\n<p>Full setup and usage guide: <a href=\"https://custom-mt.atlassian.net/wiki/x/EgAH\"><strong>Custom.MT Documentation</strong></a></p>\r\n<p>Have questions about Custom.MT Console? <a href=\"https://custom.mt/book-a-call/?utm_source=rws_appstore\"><strong>Contact us here</strong></a></p>\r\n<h4>Key Features</h4>\r\n<p>The plugin connects Custom.MT Console with Trados Studio, enabling translators to access AI-powered translation workflows directly inside their CAT environment. Professional","description":"<p>The<strong> AI Professional</strong> plugin for Trados Studio 2022 leverages from both <strong>Azure </strong>and <strong>OpenAI</strong>'s language models to assist users in translation projects. Key features include a <strong>Translation Provider</strong>, an <strong>AI companion</strong> that is available from the Editor and <strong>Terminology-aware</strong> Translation Suggestions.</p><p><br></p><p>The plugin supports <strong>Azure OpenAI</strong> models, alongside <strong>OpenAI</strong> (<em>same models used by </em><strong><em>ChatGPT</em></strong>), each with different capabilities and performance levels. Users can create custom prompts to guide the AI, and the plugin offers a few default prompts to get you started.</p><p><br></p><p>The<strong> AI Professional</strong> plugin can be installed via the RWS AppStore or through the integrated AppStore in Trados Studio. To use the plugin, users must sign up for an OpenAI account, obtain an API key, and specify the desired model.</p><p>More details about AI Professional can be found in this <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/trados-studio/b/weblog/posts/unleash-the-power-of-translation-with-ai-professional-plugin-for-trados-studio\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">blog article</a>.</p>","documentation":"<h3>Translation Provider</h3><p>The Translation Provider integration aims to improve efficiency in facilitating batch tasks like analysis or pre-translation for enhanced productivity. Given this automation nature of batch tasks, it's now more important than ever to be aware of the <strong>rate limits</strong> that OpenAI imposes on the usage of it services via API.</p><p><br></p><h3>AI Companion</h3><p>The Companion is a view available from the Editor.&nbsp;It offers an array of advanced functionalities designed to enhance the translation process. One of its key features is the ability to search for alternative Translation Suggestions. Users can customize these suggestions by specifying specific prompts and settings, including <strong>Terminology-aware</strong>, according to their preferences. The Companion also highlights translated terms &amp; provides a visual representation of comparison changes, making it easier for users to identify and track modifications made during the translation.</p><p><br></p><h3>Terminology-aware Translation Suggestions</h3><p>This option enhances translation suggestions by incorporating terminology context with the prompt when requesting translations from OpenAI technology. By enabling this feature, OpenAI can refine the generated translation suggestions using translated terms, leading to more accurate and contextually relevant translations. This smart integration of terminology context ensures precision and consistency in all your translated content.</p><p><br></p><h3>Support for tags</h3><p>Full roundtrip support for tags in the xml source/target content that is included with the prompts. This enhancement greatly reduces time required in post-editing work to reintroduce tags and formatting after applying translation suggestions from the OpenAI technology.</p><p><br></p><h3>Revised prompt content structure</h3><p>We revised the content structure that is included with the prompts when requesting translations.&nbsp;This was necessary to simplify how we write the prompt referring to elements in the content structure and ensure what we communicate is fully understood by the OpenAI technology.</p><p><strong>Content structure</strong></p><ul><li><strong>TransUnit</strong>: The translation unit; must contain 1 <strong>&lt;Source&gt;</strong> element and can contain 1 <strong>&lt;Translation&gt;</strong> element and/or 1 <strong>&lt;Terms&gt;</strong> element.&nbsp;</li><li><strong>Source</strong>: The source segment content.</li><li><strong>Translation</strong>: The target segment content.&nbsp;<em>Note:</em><strong><em> </em></strong><em>Optionally enbled by the user if they are explicitly making reference to the existing translation in the prompt and therefore should be included in the translation request. For example, if the prompt is using the existing translation as context to provide a new translation.</em></li><li><strong>Terms</strong>: A list of terms that are matched against the source segment from the default Terminology Provider that is attached to the project.&nbsp;<em>Note:</em><strong><em>&nbsp;</em></strong><em>Optionally enabled by the user.&nbsp;If enabled and terms exist, then there is an additional system message added to the prompt to instruct OpenAI to always use the provided translations for terms when generating the translated output, except for those labeled as 'Forbidden'.\uFEFF</em></li></ul><pre class=\"ql-syntax\" spellcheck=\"false\">&lt;TransUnit&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;Source Language=\"en-US\"&gt;source text&lt;/Source&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;Translation Language=\"it-IT\"&gt;translated text&lt;/Translation&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;Terms&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;Term&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;Source&gt;source term&lt;/Source&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;Translation&gt;translated term&lt;/Translation&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;Status&gt;Preferred&lt;/Status&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;/Term&gt;\r\n&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;/Terms&gt;\r\n&lt;/TransUnit&gt;\r\n</pre><p><br></p><p><strong>Can you give me an example of a simple prompt to translate the source content?</strong></p><p>If we want to write a prompt to translate the source segment, then we could simply write “Translate this content” or “Translate the source content”.&nbsp;It is clear to the OpenAI technology that you want to translate the content from the <strong>&lt;Source&gt;</strong> element of the <strong>&lt;TransUnit&gt;</strong></p><p><br></p><p><strong>What if I’m a reviewer and already have the translations, but would like to update them given the translated terms?</strong>&nbsp;</p><p>No problem, the prompt might look like this “Update this translation using the translated terms.”&nbsp;It’s clear to the OpenAI technology that it should match the terms in the content and use the provided translations of those terms in the translation result.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>What if I also wanted to instruct the AI technology to use gender neutral nouns and pronouns as well as applying the translated terms?</strong></p><p>Easy, the prompt might look like this “Update this translation using the translated terms. Use gender neutral nouns and pronouns in the translation.”</p><p><br></p><h2>OpenAI API Rate Limits</h2><p>Rate limits are restrictions that OpenAI imposes on the number of times a user or client can access services within a specified period of time.</p><p><strong>How do these rate limits work?</strong></p><p>Rate limits are measured in five ways:&nbsp;<strong>RPM</strong> (requests per minute), <strong>RPD</strong> (requests per day), <strong>TPM</strong> (tokens per minute), <strong>TPD</strong> (tokens per day), and <strong>IPM</strong> (images per minute). Rate limits can be hit across any of the options depending on what occurs first.&nbsp;For example, you might send 20 requests with only 100 tokens to the ChatCompletions endpoint and that would fill your limit (if your RPM was 20), even if you did not send 150k tokens (if your TPM limit was 150k) within those 20 requests.</p><p>Other important things worth noting:</p><ul><li>Rate limits are imposed at the <a href=\"https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/production-best-practices\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">organization level</a>, not user level.</li><li>Rate limits vary by the <a href=\"https://platform.openai.com/docs/models/models\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">model</a> being used.</li><li>Limits are also placed on the total amount an organization can spend on the API each month. These are also known as \"usage limits\".</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Tier 1 rate limits per model</strong></p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/62127e69-0d36-4830-a7b6-ccd1b0a934f2/Resources/1bf17ed2-c0ba-4ff0-ab99-58c26e5c711d.png?dl=1\"></p><p><br></p><h3>Usage tiers</h3><p>You can view the rate and usage limits for your organization under the <a href=\"https://platform.openai.com/account/rate-limits\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">limits</a> section of your account settings. As your usage of the OpenAI API and your spend on OpenAI API goes up, you will be automatically graduated to the next usage tier. This usually results in an increase in rate limits across most models.</p>","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/200/versions/1286","changelog":{"Trados Studio 2022 (SR2)":"<p><strong>1.0.10.0</strong></p><ul>\r\n<li>Resolved bug where the plugin was crashing frequently in with error System.FormatException: Unexpected token &#39;m58,9966666666667,0 l0, 2&#39; It is an advanced format that offers a range of features for precise subtitle placement and styling.</p><h5>Getting Started</h5><p><strong>1.Minimum Requirements</strong></p><ul><li>Trados Studio 2019 SR2</li><li><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/5\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Studio Subtitling</a></li></ul><p><strong>2.Installation</strong></p><p>You can install ASS File Type either directly within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore or alternatively&nbsp;you can download and install from the RWS AppStore&nbsp;</p><p><strong>3.Access</strong></p><p>Once installed, the ASS File Type can be accessed in various ways:</p><ul><li>Trados Studio Tell Me Feature</li><li>Default Path: Go to File -&gt; Options -&gt; File Types</li><li>New Project: Create New Project -&gt; File Type Identifier Settings</li></ul><p class=\"ql-indent-1\"><em>Tip:</em> Update your selected project template with your new file type preference, after completing the project preparation tasks. Alternatively you can update Project Templates by going to File -&gt; Setup -&gt; Project Templates, select your project template to edit and then manage your list of supported file types.</p><ul><li>Existing Project Selected: Project Settings -&gt; File Types</li></ul><p class=\"ql-indent-1\"><em>Tip: </em>You can add new files to an existing project, as long as the project is recognised as a Studio project and not a Single file project. However file type need to be manged prior to adding new files (conversion from native to *sdlxliff)</p><p><u>Note:</u> If you don’t see ASS File Type as a listed file type, you may need to click on \"Additional installed File Types Exist\" to enable it as a supported file type.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/1e52fc13-1850-428b-860d-f36ffd5e7256/Resources/ac5105d0-0f32-4fb7-a1cd-5530ea1762d7.png?dl=1\"></p><h5>Working In Trados Studio</h5><p><strong>1.Recommendation</strong></p><p>We recommend working with subtitling files through the Creating projects workflow as apose to C<span style=\"background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color); color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground); font-family: var(--pa-admin-input-fontfamily); font-size: var(--pa-admin-input-fontsize);\">reating projects using Translate Single Document workflow.&nbsp;This is so you can take full advantage of all the benefits that comes with managing Studio projects where as Single file projects are restricted.&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></p><p><strong>2.File Type Identifier</strong></p><p>When adding your ASS file to a project, make sure the File Type Identifier lists ASS V 5.0.0.0. Optionally, the user can upload a zip package containing images and fonts referenced by the InDesign file.</p>\r\n<h1>Key Features</h1>\r\n<p>In-Context Preview of Adobe - InDesign File (idml)</p>\r\n<h1>Installation</h1>\r\n<h2>New Installation</h2>\r\n<p>This is a paid app, Trados tenants require the entitlement to be added before installation. Contact RWS support for details. Following are the steps to install app (first time):</p>\r\n<p><strong><u>Option-1:</u></strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Visit URL <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/\">https://appstore.rws.com/</a></li>\r\n<li>Search for “Adobe – InDesign Preview”.</li>\r\n<li>When you &quot;download&quot; the app you will be redirected and asked to sign in to your Trados account.</li>\r\n<li>Click on the “Install” button.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong><u>Option-2:</u></strong></p>\r\n<p>Login to Trados.</p>\r\n<p>Click on Account Settings icon on the top right corner</p>\r\n<p>Choose “RWS AppStore”</p>\r\n<p>Under the Add–Ons tab, you will find the app named “Adobe – InDesign Preview”.</p>\r\n<p>Click on the “Install” button.</p>\r\n<h2>Updates</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>If an update is available, you should get a notification by mail and or in the general Trados notification window.</li>\r\n<li>To check if any update to app is available, follow the steps below:</li>\r\n<li>Login to Trados.</li>\r\n<li>Click on Account Settings icon on the top right corner</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/e861b8ae-1c88-4a22-ad71-0dca6caabbdd.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Choose “RWS AppStore”</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/9da89f70-0abe-4df3-8e48-503859c4c1f8.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Under the tab “Installed Add-Ons”, you will see either “Up to date” OR “Update available”.</li>\r\n<li>If update is available, click on addon name. It will display like following, where you can click on Update button.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/662e9d98-286d-45ae-aa1e-97b1f48f9e40.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<h1>Technical Requirements</h1>\r\n<h2>File Type Configuration</h2>\r\n<p>After app is installed successfully, an InDesign Preview Extension is added automatically in the file type configuration.</p>\r\n<p>To verify:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Go to Resources</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/84a9fa4a-a9e7-44e2-8a7d-6a1617a09903.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Choose tab “File Type Configurations”.</li>\r\n<li>Open the file type configuration which will be in use for InDesign preview projects.</li>\r\n<li>Open the Adobe InDesign CS4-CC IDML file type.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/925a7f4f-81c2-4483-8f29-7e06ac6f3874.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>At the bottom expand “Preview Settings”.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/757fc4b4-1e29-49fb-9080-51875f9f2c1a.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Verify that InDesign Preview Extension is available and checked.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/a1efdc06-0acb-425d-bd0c-4e9f77b48c68.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>If InDesign Preview Extension is not available by default, click on &quot;Add Preview Type&quot; then check &quot;Show unsupported preview types&quot;. It will display InDesign Preview Extension, just select it and click on &quot;Add&quot; button.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/IDP-AddPreviewExtV3.png\" alt=\"\"></h3>\r\n<h2>Important information when using the app</h2>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>The limit for IDML file size is 50MB and reference package (zip) size recommendation is 1 GB.&nbsp;</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Reference Package (Zip) file naming: the reference package name must be in a particular format so that it can associate the reference package file to IDML file.&nbsp;Format: Preview_&lt;IDML file name without extension&gt;.zip</p>\r\n<p>Example:</p>\r\n<p>If IDML file name is abc.idml</p>\r\n<p>Then preview package name should be Preview_abc.zip&nbsp;</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Reference Package (Zip) layout</p>\r\n<p>Zip file must contain contain 2 directories:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>Document fonts: includes referenced font files which are used in IDML.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Links: includes referenced images which are used in IDML.&nbsp;</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Note: It is recommended to include images with small file size. Even actual images are high resolution and larger in size, lower size images should be used to include in the package, as these will be used just to display the preview. As small as the reference package size is, preview loading will be faster.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/07682ab3-f908-4678-b790-75e03adfa5c7.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Preview uses a PDF format to display the IDML file content so you cannot edit the text in the preview.&nbsp;</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>The preview window provides a few features like: Zoom In/Out, Print, Rotate, Search Text, Scrolling &amp; Page level navigation etc.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Selecting translation segments will navigate to the segment in the preview window</p>\r\n<p>If the preview does not navigate, it will display a pop-up message:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/dca15762-5c6e-4b29-af53-4deb796c72a6.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>If the file name is included in the layout by using a variable, Preview might display it differently like a temporary file name. E.g.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/309afe97-5c78-4515-85d8-65f414f6e91f.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\">&nbsp;</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Any text placed in embedded graphics will show the text in the source language in the Preview.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>There are some limitations in the online preview for Asian and bi-directional target languages currently. Enhancements will come in future versions of the app to support these languages.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h1>Using the App</h1>\r\n<h2>Create Project</h2>\r\n<p>Login to Trados - <a href=\"https://languagecloud.sdl.com/lc\">https://languagecloud.sdl.com/lc</a></p>\r\n<p>Click on Projects.</p>\r\n<p>Click on New Project.</p>\r\n<p>It will open the screen to input all needed information to create a project.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Name: Enter project name.</li>\r\n<li>Description (optional): Enter project description.</li>\r\n<li>Location: Select a location from list.</li>\r\n<li>Project Template: Select project template from list.</li>\r\n<li>Source Language:</li>\r\n<li>Target Languages:</li>\r\n<li>Due Date:</li>\r\n<li>File Type Configuration:</li>\r\n<li>Click on Add more files or drag &amp; drop IDML and Zip files:</li>\r\n<li>Sample File Name: <em><strong>SampleInDesignFile.idml</strong></em></li>\r\n<li>Reference Package Name: <em><strong>Preview_SampleInDesignFile.zip</strong></em></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>When dropping the zip file, it asks you to select&nbsp;options</p>\r\n<p>Choose “Keep ZIP files” and confirm.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/0986bbd3-197c-4bb1-8b12-9efab5fb838e.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Click Create. It will create the project.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Preview</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>When the project is in a translation stage, with access to Online Editor, preview can be used.</li>\r\n<li>Open project in online editor. It will open a new window of Trados Online Editor for selected project.</li>\r\n<li>Click on “View”. Under “Preview” group, “Preview Off” is selected by default. Change it to one of the options for how the preview window is displayed.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/da748882-091a-4078-aee9-f6348b7df696.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>It will load preview section, on the same window/page.</li>\r\n<li>Click any segment in the bilingual editor, the app will highlight the corresponding text in the preview.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/737080bc-3574-4264-abd2-0cf8f4186bb9/Resources/ca15d645-03e0-4c63-895d-7084b8afb877.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>If you modify any text in the bilingual editor, the icon above the preview will refresh the preview.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong>AWS CLI</strong></a>&nbsp;(Command Line Interface).&nbsp;You can find more information on how to do this here in the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/awscli-install-windows.html\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong>AWS Documentation</strong></a>.</p><p>Once you have installed the AWS CLI you can run it by&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.lifewire.com/how-to-open-command-prompt-2618089\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong>opening a windows command prompt</strong></a>&nbsp;and type in<strong><em>&nbsp;aws configure</em></strong>:</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7853.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7853.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>The first time you do this you will be prompted for four pieces of information (<em style=\"color: rgb(255, 0, 0);\">this is an example and not anything you should copy and type in!</em>):</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2656.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2656.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><br></p><p>All of this information can be found in your own AWS account settings.&nbsp;It's recommended that you use the documentation provided by Amazon to get this information as they have explained in detail how to set this up&nbsp;<a href=\"https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/userguide/cli-chap-getting-started.html\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong>here</strong></a>.&nbsp;They could also change the process so it's better to go back to AWS if you have any problems.</p><p><br></p><p>When you have completed all of this you will see something like the following if you run the&nbsp;<strong><em>aws configure</em></strong>&nbsp;command again:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6014.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6014.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h1><br></h1><h1>Setting up the Studio plugin</h1><p>Once you have set up your Amazon account correctly you can activate the plugin for your project.&nbsp;The steps are as follows:</p><p><br></p><h1>Adding the provider to your project or default settings</h1><p><br></p><p>In your&nbsp;<strong><em>Translation Memory and Automated Translation</em></strong>&nbsp;settings (<strong><em>Project Settings</em></strong>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<strong><em>File -&gt; Options</em></strong>) add the provider by selecting it from here:</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1263.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1263.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><br></p><h1>Adding your Amazon credentials</h1><h3><br></h3><h3>Authentication</h3><h3><br></h3><p>Choose AWS auth type and select the authentication method you intend to use.</p><h3><br></h3><h3>Access key / Secret access key.</h3><p><br></p><p>All of these details need to be added or the plugin will fail to work:</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0804.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0804.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><br></p><p>Click on&nbsp;<strong><em>OK</em></strong>&nbsp;and the plugin will be added to your settings.&nbsp;Now you should be able to receive machine translation results from the Amazon Translate service:</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8424.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8424.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h4><br></h4><h3>Locally configured AWS profile</h3><p><br></p><p>This method provides a way to easily maintain different profiles based on a configuration saved locally on your computer.&nbsp;To use this method you simply choose the&nbsp;<strong><em>Local AWS profile</em></strong>&nbsp;option and add the name of your profile in the&nbsp;<strong><em>Locally configured AWS profile</em></strong>&nbsp;field.</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5228.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5228.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><br></p><p>In this example we have used a profile called&nbsp;<strong><em>myprofile</em></strong>.&nbsp;The name of the profile can be whatever you wish and you can create as many as you like.&nbsp;But to use this you need to set this up in two files that are stored here: <strong><em>c:\\Users\\[USERNAME]\\.aws\\</em></strong></p><p><br></p><p>These files will be created when you install the Command Line Interface and you can add the necessary information manually or by running this command in a command prompt (you would enter the name of your profile, here we used&nbsp;<strong><em>myprofile</em></strong>):</p><p><strong><em>aws configure --profile myprofile</em></strong></p><p><br></p><p>Add&nbsp;the appropriate information when prompted in exactly the same interface as earlier and the configuration files will be updated accordingly.&nbsp;If you wish to do this manually then the content of the&nbsp;<strong><em>config</em></strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong><em>credentials</em></strong>&nbsp;files would look something like this:</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0121.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong><em><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0121.png\" alt=\" \"></em></strong></a></p><h2><br></h2><h2>Other options</h2><p><br></p><p>The plugin supports a few other options in a simlar way to those provided by the MT Enhanced plugin that is also available on the SDL AppStore:</p><ul><li>Re-send draft and translated segments</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Using this option causes&nbsp;all segments&nbsp;to be sent for translation regardless of the status. If you leave it unchecked, only untranslated segments will be sent.</li><li>Send plain text only (no tags)</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">If you use this option any tag information in the segment will be stripped from the source segment before it's sent to the machine translation service.&nbsp;If you don't use this and a segment in Trados Studio contains tags then these tags will also be sent to the translation service and converted back to tags in the target segment&nbsp;of Trados Studio.</li><li>Do pre-lookup find/replace</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">You can select a file containing specially formatted xml markup that indicates a list of find/replace pairs to be applied to the source text before it is sent to the MT service.</li><li>Do post-lookup find/replace</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">This is similar to the pre-lookup option, except that the find/replace pairs are applied to the target text after it is returned from the&nbsp;machine translation service.&nbsp;</li></ul><h3><br></h3><h3>Preparing the XML file for the find/replace options</h3><p><br></p><p><em>Taken from the articles prepared by Patrick Porter in the original MT Enhanced plugin</em></p><p>How to create the necessary xml structure in a text file for batch find/replace lists?</p><p>You can use a text editor (e.g. Notepad) to create files containing the necessary xml structure for use as batch find/replace lists.</p><p>The plugin will apply these lists to the source text before sending to the machine translation service and/or to the returned translated text, depending on the options configured in the plugin settings form.</p><p>The files can be saved with any file extension. They must have the following structure:</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4370.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4370.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><br></p><p>The first node is &lt;EditCollection&gt;. It contains a list of &lt;Items&gt;.</p><p>The items list contains EditItem elements, each of which has two attributes Enabled and EditItemType.</p><p><br></p><p>The acceptable values for Enabled are true or false. If Enabled is “false” the plugin will ignore the pair. This parameter allows you to temporarily disable a find/replace pair without having to delete it from the file.</p><p><br></p><p>The acceptable values for EditItemType are plain_text and regular_expression. You can use regular expression matching/replacement by setting the EditItemType to regular_expression. For simple plain text replacements, it is best to set it to plain_text to avoid unexpected behavior or the need to insert any special regular expression escape characters. The plugin uses .NET regular expressions.</p><p><br></p><p>Each EditItem has two elements: &lt;FindText&gt; and &lt;ReplaceText&gt;. They represent the text to find and the replacement text, respectively.</p><p><br></p><h4>Important: note that there are several special XML characters that will cause problems if you type them directly into the XML file as the values for FindText and ReplaceText.</h4><p>If you need to search and replace things like HTML tags, or any other text containing the &lt; and/or &gt; bracket character, the brackets must be ‘escaped’ with &amp;lt; for &lt;, and &amp;gt; for &gt;. Also, the ampersand character (&amp;) must be escaped with &amp; to avoid problems. Escaping single quotes (') and double quotes (\") in the text values is not absolutely mandatory, but it is a good idea in order to avoid any unexpected errors. Some XML editor programs will automatically escape these characters for you when saving the file.</p><p>After the list of EditItems nodes, the other nodes are closed with the corresponding closing tags: and .</p><p><br></p><h4>Example</h4><h4><br></h4><p>The following is a complete example of the content for a find/replace list file:</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3010.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3010.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><br></p><p>Here, there is one plain text pair specifying to find the string “Hello World” and replace it with “Hello world!”. There is also a regular expression pair specifying to find the regular expression “\\bhello\\b” (a whole-word match for “hello”) and replace it with “Hello!”.</p><p>You can copy and paste the example xml into a text file and modify it as needed. Add EditItem blocks for additional pairs and set the attributes and elements accordingly.</p><p>Keep in mind:</p><p><br></p><ul><li>The find/replace text specified is case sensitive, i.e., specifying “hello” will not find “Hello”. To find both, two separate EditItem elements are necessary.</li><li>The replacement is a direct string replacement and not a whole-word search. For example, specifying “no” / “not” as a find/replace pair will cause the word “none” to become “notne”. To use whole-word matching, regular expressions are necessary.</li><li>The replacements are performed incrementally in the order they appear in the file. Once a replacement is made, the next find/replace will be performed on the updated text. For that reason, with similar strings, it is better to place longer ones first. For example, if you have entries to replace “dogs” with “pets” and “dog” with “a pet”, place the entry for “dogs” first. Otherwise you may get unexpected behavior.</li></ul><p><br></p><h4>Alternative method for creating these XML files</h4><h4><br></h4><p>It's worth noting that&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/members/spirosdoikas\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: rgb(245, 250, 250); color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Spiros Doikas</a>&nbsp;has created a blog post on how to create the find/replace files using Excel here:&nbsp;<a href=\"https://metafrastis.wordpress.com/2016/05/06/customise-machine-translation-in-sdl-studio/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://metafrastis.wordpress.com/2016/05/06/customise-machine-translation-in-sdl-studio/</a></p><p><br></p><p>Tell Me feature</p><p><br></p><p>Starting with Studio 2019 version, users are allowed to access the following actions using TellMe feature from Studio:</p><ul><li>Community Support</li><li>Wikipedia documentation</li><li>Download the plugin from the AppStore</li></ul><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1100x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4807.pastedimage1595490786417v1.png\" (SR2)":"<p><strong>4.2.2.0</strong></p><ul>\r\n<li>Improved tag handling.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n Introduction</strong></h4><p><br></p><p>The Antidote plugin for Trados Studio integrates the powerful Antidote writing assistance tools directly into the Trados Studio environment. This allows you to enhance your translation quality by using Antidote’s spelling, grammar, and style correction tools within your translation workflow.</p><p><br></p><h4><strong>2. System Requirements</strong></h4><p><br></p><p>Before installing the Antidote plugin, ensure your system meets the following requirements:</p><ul><li><strong>Trados Studio</strong>: Version 2019 or later.</li><li><strong>Antidote</strong>: Ensure you have a licensed copy of Antidote installed on your machine.</li></ul><p><br></p><p>To install Antidote app, please use the user guide from Antidote website:</p><p><a href=\"https://www.antidote.info/en/druide-account/client-portal-user-guide/login\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://www.antidote.info/en/druide-account/client-portal-user-guide/login</a></p><h4><br></h4><h4><strong>3. How do I install the plugin?</strong></h4><p><br></p><p>Follow these steps to install the Antidote plugin in Trados Studio:</p><ul><li>Visit the Trados AppStore and search for the Antidote plugin.</li><li>Download Antidote plugin from the integrated Appstore views inside Trados Studio.</li></ul><p>Once installed, you will find in in Review tab&gt;Antidote Verifier Group.</p><p><br></p><h4><strong>4. Using the Antidote Plugin</strong></h4><p>With the plugin installed and configured, you can now use Antidote within Trados Studio to enhance your translation quality.</p><ul><li><strong>Open a Document</strong>:Open a translation project or document in Trados Studio.</li><li><strong>Launch Antidote</strong>:Highlight the segment you want to check.Right-click and select <code>Corrector</code> from the context menu, or use the configured shortcut key. You can also run the Verifier of the whole document by clicking on Corrector in Review tab.</li><li><strong>Correct Errors</strong>:Antidote will launch and display any errors or suggestions for the selected text.Make the necessary corrections in Antidote.Once done, save the changes and return to Trados Studio.</li><li><strong>Review Changes</strong>:Review the corrected text in Trados Studio and displays all the scripts which are available on master script.</p><p>After first installing the plugin, the grid is empty. As you add 'scripts' the grid will be populated.</p><p>If you hover over a row in the grid, a tool tip with the description will appear.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2364.pastedimage1618838644047v2.png\"></p><p>The options available are:</p><p>1.&nbsp;<strong>Add</strong>&nbsp;new script. After this action a new page will appear from where new scripts can be added.</p><p>2. With the SYSTRAN plugin you can get customized translations in Trados Studio, either from your SYSTRAN Pure Neural® Server or SYSTRAN translate Server on-premise environments, or from SYSTRAN Translate Cloud online service. Once installed, you will now find the option to choose SYSTRAN for Trados Studio as an MT provider to use in your translation projects.</p>\r\n<h1>Key Features</h1>\r\n<h4>PROJECT SETUP USING SYSTRAN</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Once installed the SYSTYRAN plugin appears in the list of Automated Translation provider</li>\r\n<li>You can then connect to your SYSTRAN Server or use SYSTRAN API to use it on your project\u2028\r\nSYSTRAN PLUGIN SETTINGS</li>\r\n<li>The CMLess Trados Studio plugin includes some advanced settings that are global to all projects on the host system.</li>\r\n<li>To access the advanced settings, on the SYSTRAN settings dialog, double-click on the gear icon in the SYSTRAN plugin setup window.</li>\r\n<li>You can adapt the configuration in settings (including NFA (Neural Fuzzy Adaptation) activation)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h4>SYSTRAN TRANSLATION PROFILE SELECTION</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>You can select one or several translation profiles and the expected language direction</li>\r\n<li>Translations may be done for projects with one or more target languages. In all cases, only a single SYSTRAN provider is needed.</li>\r\n<li>Once defined, your connection details can be easily reused next time</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h4>MATCH CUSTOMIZATION</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Adapt the match conditions to be used in the translation project</li>\r\n<li>Pre-translate match value will consider TMs that are fuzzy matches down to the specified threshold.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h4>SYSTRAN AUTOMATED TRANSLATION RESULTS</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Once you have configured a service and performed the translation, you can view the translated results using the Editor view of your SDL Trados Studio project</li>\r\n<li>You will automatically get Automated Translations (AT) whenever you click on a translation unit that hasn&#39;t been translated by 100% match against translation memory nor by fuzzy matching or other means.</li>\r\n<li>It is possible to redo a translation within an existing project, which means you may add a reference to the SYSTRAN plugin after the project has been created and initially processed. In addition, you can protect some segments from the updated translation by locking those segments</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>Technical Requirements</h1>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Trados Studio 2024,
