source/target, lock segments and delete tags with custom batch tasks.</p><p><br></p><p> You can find an excellent explanation of how to use this tool on <a href=\"http://jessegood.github.io/translation/cleanuptool.html\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>the developers website</strong></a>.</p>","documentation":"<p><strong>Important Note:</strong></p><p>Please note that there is a possible scenario when using this application where you may find you are seeing results and information in the user interface (UI) that don't seem to match the project you are working on.&nbsp;It is not possible to change this behavior using the APIs that are currently available in Studio without completely rewriting the application to work in another way.&nbsp;So for now it's important that you note the following.</p><p>If you are working in the \"Files View\" and you have managed to activate a project in the \"Projects View\" which is different to the project belonging to the files you are working on, the&nbsp;<strong>Cleanup Tasks</strong>&nbsp;batch task will not work as expected.&nbsp;This is because the batch task will be applied to the files you are working on, but the information used to perform the task within the UI will be drawn from the active project.&nbsp;For example, this is what I'm expecting to see for the project I am working on:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0537.pastedimage1578759715179v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>But as I have managed to activate a different project altogether in the \"Projects View\" these are the settings that are being used:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8524.pastedimage1578759946257v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>It is more likely that this could occur when you open a single file for translation.&nbsp;When&nbsp;you select \"Translate Single Document\"&nbsp;your document will be opened in the editor but the active and highlighted project in the \"Projects View\"&nbsp;may still remain highlighted and influence the batch task&nbsp;as described above.</p><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">So before starting the CleanUp Tasks while in the \"Files View\" or the \"Editor View\" make sure&nbsp;that the CURRENT project&nbsp;is also active, HIGHLIGHTED.</strong></p><p><strong style=\"background-color: rgb(255, 255, 153);\">The content below was mostly provided by Jesse Good in his original article on this plugin.</strong></p><p>So what does this tool do?</p><ul><li>You can lock segments based on structure or content</li><li>You can remove unwanted tags in the source</li><li>You can modify the source or target text as you like and create “settings” files for easy reuse</li><li>You can create tags for embedded xml or html content</li><li>You can create placeholders for fixed words or phrases</li></ul><p>Some of the above is possible already with other tools, but the best part is this is a Batch Task, so you can run it directly in Trados. If you think any of the above may be of interest, please read on.</p><p><strong>New Batch Task Menu Items:</strong></p><p>The tool adds 2 new items to your batch task menu:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8688.cleanup_2D00_batchtask_2D00_menu.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/567x498/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8688.cleanup_2D00_batchtask_2D00_menu.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Cleanup Source</strong></p><p>When you click on&nbsp;Cleanup Source&nbsp;and then hit “Next”, you will be greeted with the following screen:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7220.cleanup_2D00_settings_2D00_menu.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/786x593/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7220.cleanup_2D00_settings_2D00_menu.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Locking segments</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2577.segment_2D00_locker.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/500x220/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2577.segment_2D00_locker.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>You can lock segments based on search expressions using the left-hand box (the&nbsp;Content Locker). In order to lock based on the document structure, use the right-hand box (the&nbsp;Structure Locker).</p><p>Content Locker Example</p><p>I mainly translate from Japanese to English and often times you get segments that contain no Japanese characters. It can be useful to lock these sometimes, the following regular expression would check for that:&nbsp;^[^亜-熙ぁ-んァ-ヶ]+$</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5736.content_2D00_locker.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/326x125/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5736.content_2D00_locker.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><em>Make sure you turn on&nbsp;</em><code><em>Regex</em></code><em>&nbsp;for the above to work</em></p><p>The headers in the above screenshot are abbreviated for space reasons, so they might be a little difficult to understand:</p><ul><li>Regex: Regular expression matching</li><li>Case: Case-sensitive searching</li><li>Whole: Whole word matching</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><p>Structure Locker Example</p><p>This should be straightforward, the structure info is read from the sdlxliff files of the project. The example file I used happens to be an Excel file, which is why you see items like&nbsp;sdl:worksheet&nbsp;and&nbsp;sdl:textbox. In the following screenshot I selected&nbsp;sdl:textbox&nbsp;to lock any text that appears in text boxes.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4722.structure_2D00_locker.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/152x125/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4722.structure_2D00_locker.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Removing tags</p><p>The plug-in divides tags into two categories,&nbsp;Formatting Tags&nbsp;and&nbsp;Placeholder Tags:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3718.tag_2D00_remover.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/500x176/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3718.tag_2D00_remover.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><ul><li>Formatting Tags: These always start with&nbsp;<code>&lt;cf&gt;</code>.</li></ul><p><code>&lt;cf&gt;</code>&nbsp;tags can contain a range of information such as font name, font size, italic, bold, etc. In Example 1 below, each tag contains the font name and size only, while Example 2 contains an&nbsp;<code>italic=\"True\"</code>attribute.</p><p><em>Example 1 (Font Name and Size):&nbsp;</em></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7167.cf_2D00_tag_2D00_example1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/282x57/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7167.cf_2D00_tag_2D00_example1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><em>Example 2 ( italic =\"True\")</em></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1325.cf_2D00_tag_2D00_example2.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><em><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/282x42/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1325.cf_2D00_tag_2D00_example2.png\" alt=\" \"></em></a></p><p>In order to remove the tags in Example 1, you need to select&nbsp;Font Name&nbsp;and&nbsp;Font Size&nbsp;(see screenshot below), since the tag specifies both of these:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5684.tag_2D00_remover_2D00_select_2D00_fontname_2D00_and_2D00_size.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/239x102/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5684.tag_2D00_remover_2D00_select_2D00_fontname_2D00_and_2D00_size.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>However, the tag in Example 2&nbsp;will not&nbsp;be removed as it contains&nbsp;italic=\"True\". To remove this tag, you also need to select&nbsp;Italic:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0842.tag_2D00_remover_2D00_select_2D00_italic.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/239x102/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0842.tag_2D00_remover_2D00_select_2D00_italic.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><ul><li>Placeholder Tags:</li></ul><p>In short, these are the&nbsp;<code>&lt;ph&gt;</code>&nbsp;(Placeholder) tags in the sdlxliff file. Sometimes they contain inline formatting which may not be needed.</p><p>I would exercise caution when removing these tags though as often times they are necessary!</p><p>In the following screenshot, the&nbsp;<code>&lt;br&gt;</code>&nbsp;tags are used for aligning text in text boxes in the original Excel file, they are probably required, but there might be times when you want to remove this type of formatting.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3201.tag_2D00_remover_2D00_placeholder.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/239x102/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3201.tag_2D00_remover_2D00_placeholder.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Currently, I do not permit removing other types of tags other than the above. Let me know though if you have a use case for removing other types of tags.</p><p>Modifying text</p><p>Now to the main part of the plug-in. When you first start out, you will have an empty screen like below:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6825.conversion_2D00_blank.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/496x165/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6825.conversion_2D00_blank.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>First, click on the&nbsp;<code>New</code>&nbsp;button to create a new “Conversion File”.</p><p>The following window should pop up and it will appear blank at first:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1273.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_window.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/791x323/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1273.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_window.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Click the “+” mark in the top right corner as shown and a new row will be added to the grid like so:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5432.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_view_2D00_row_2D00_added.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/791x323/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5432.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_view_2D00_row_2D00_added.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Now, I would like to demonstrate a few use cases to show how to use the tool.</p><h4>Use Case: Converting wide characters to their narrow equivalent</h4><p>In Japanese text, wide and narrow forms of characters are used:</p><p>WideNarrowＡＢＣＤABCD１２３４1234カタカナｶﾀｶﾅ</p><p>One issue is that, depending on the client, they may use different forms in their documents. You may even find a mix of these forms in the same document. These mixed forms can also cause problems with your matching results, and your translation memories will be cluttered with them.</p><p>One solution is to unify these forms before translation:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8780.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_wide_2D00_to_2D00_narrow_2D00_example.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/823x323/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8780.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_wide_2D00_to_2D00_narrow_2D00_example.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>In the above screenshot I have created 3 rules:</p><ul><li>Wide to narrow: Alphabetic</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Ensure all alphabetic characters are narrow</li><li>Wide to narrow: Numbers</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Ensure all numbers are narrow</li><li>Narrow to wide: Katakana</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Ensure all Katakana characters are wide</li></ul><p>To create a rule, you enter your information in the input area shown below:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3058.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_view_2D00_input_2D00_window.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/324x316/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3058.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_view_2D00_input_2D00_window.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><ol><li><em>Title</em>: This field can be left blank, it just gives a description of the search item, and allows you to find an item easier in the grid view.<em>Search</em>: The text you want to search for. In the example I use a regular expression to search for a single wide alphabetic character, it probably would be more efficient to use&nbsp;<code>[Ａ-Ｚ]+</code>&nbsp;to search for groups of characters though.<em>Search Settings</em>: The search settings explained from left to right are:</li></ol><ul><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Case Sensitive: Case sensitive searching</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Regex: Use regular expression matching</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Whole Word: Match whole words</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Tag Pair</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Embedded Tags</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">StrConv</li></ul><p>Embeded Tags</p><p>A common issue with translations, is handling embedded tags.</p><p>For example:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2045.embedded_2D00_tags.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/772x61/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2045.embedded_2D00_tags.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>The cleanup tasks tool provides a way to convert these into “real” tags.</p><p>You could use the following setting:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1565.embedded_2D00_tags_2D00_setting.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/324x316/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1565.embedded_2D00_tags_2D00_setting.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>The above setting will detect the&nbsp;<code>&lt;b&gt;</code>&nbsp;tag in the example.</p><p>When you run the task on the example, it will be converted as shown below:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6012.embedded_2D00_tags_2D00_after.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/769x64/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6012.embedded_2D00_tags_2D00_after.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><em>Important Note</em>: Note that in my example, I did not show a setting converting the&nbsp;<code>&lt;span&gt;</code>&nbsp;tag. This is important, as even though I only created a rule to detect the&nbsp;<code>&lt;b&gt;</code>&nbsp;tag, the plug-in will convert&nbsp;<em>all</em>&nbsp;tags it finds within the segment.</p><p>Now, when you generate the target translations, any converted tags will be restored to their former form:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6712.embedded_2D00_tags_2D00_generate_2D00_target.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/887x76/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6712.embedded_2D00_tags_2D00_generate_2D00_target.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>StrConv</p><p><code>StrConv</code>&nbsp;happens to be a handy&nbsp;<a href=\"https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.visualbasic.strings.strconv?redirectedfrom=MSDN&amp;view=netframework-4.7.2#Microsoft_VisualBasic_Strings_StrConv_System_String_Microsoft_VisualBasic_VbStrConv_System_Int32_\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">method&nbsp;</a>from Visual Basic. You can find it in a lot of Microsoft Products, such as&nbsp;<a href=\"https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/office/gg264628.aspx\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">Office VBA</a>.</p><p>The handy part is shown in the following screenshot (courtesy MSDN).</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6076.strconv_2D00_chart_2D00_microsoft.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/875x512/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6076.strconv_2D00_chart_2D00_microsoft.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>All the options above are available under their same names in the tool: By selecting&nbsp;<code>Narrow</code>&nbsp;in the tool, I can convert any wide character to its narrow equivalent.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0245.strconv_2D00_options.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/225x275/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0245.strconv_2D00_options.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>When you turn on the&nbsp;<code>StrConv</code>&nbsp;option, the&nbsp;<code>Replace</code>&nbsp;window becomes greyed out.</p><p>Storing conversion files for reuse</p><p>One problem I have found with current solutions, is there is little ability for reuse. For example,&nbsp;<a href=\"http://appstore.sdl.com/app/sdlxliff-toolkit/296/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1700054084250.1700063699942.46&amp;__hssc=110027290.5.1700063699942&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">SDLXLIFF Toolkit</a>&nbsp;is a great tool, but you have to retype each item you need to search for. With this tool, click&nbsp;Save As&nbsp;in the bottom right corner to save your settings file for later use:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0410.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_saveas.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/338x291/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0410.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_saveas.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Once you have saved your file, it will appear in the following list.</p><p><em>Important Note</em>: Order matters! Each file will be used for processing starting from top to bottom.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8306.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_list.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/496x165/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8306.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_list.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>I would recommend creating separate conversion files based on project, or divide them into categories.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>Tag Pair</p><p>I actually don’t know how useful this feature will be, but you can detect tag pairs in the source text and modify them.</p><p>For example, in the following screenshot, I look for a&nbsp;<code>&lt;cf highlight=\"yellow\"&gt;</code>&nbsp;tag and replace the contents with some random text:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7026.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_tag_2D00_pair_2D00_example1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/324x291/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7026.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_tag_2D00_pair_2D00_example1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Another example is taking a tag pair and replacing it with a placeholder instead:</p><p>Say you had the following made up&nbsp;<code>&lt;inline&gt;</code>&nbsp;tag pair in your XML file:</p><p>&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6013.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_placeholder_2D00_example1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/305x43/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6013.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_placeholder_2D00_example1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>With the following rule (make sure&nbsp;<code>placeholder</code>&nbsp;is turned ON!):</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2450.rule.jpg\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/328x293/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2450.rule.jpg\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>You can turn it into a placeholder:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2158.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_placeholder_2D00_example2.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/342x43/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2158.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_placeholder_2D00_example2.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>However, placeholders have a much more useful application, which will be discussed next.</p><p>Placeholders</p><p>The main use case for placeholders I see is marking proper nouns, such as product or company names that never change in a translation.</p><p>For example, imagine a company named&nbsp;<code>Contoso</code>&nbsp;as below:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1780.contoso_2D00_example.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/344x186/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1780.contoso_2D00_example.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>You can mark these as placeholders with the following rule:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0310.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_contoso_2D00_placeholder.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/324x291/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0310.conversion_2D00_file_2D00_contoso_2D00_placeholder.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><em>Note</em>: You can create placeholders that do not use attributes, for example in the above, I could of made the rule&nbsp;<code>&lt;Contoso /&gt;</code>&nbsp;instead. However, when using elements only, you have to make sure it is a valid XML name.</p><p>This will take each instance of&nbsp;<code>Contoso</code>&nbsp;and replace it with a placeholder:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0841.contoso_2D00_example_2D00_after.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/314x221/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0841.contoso_2D00_example_2D00_after.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>However, when you do this, a few issues come up:</p><ol><li>You will notice it replaced&nbsp;<code>Contoso</code>&nbsp;in the third example, but left the&nbsp;<code>, Ltd</code>. In other words, you have to be careful what you replace. To fix this problem, you could create a rule that replace&nbsp;<code>Contoso, Ltd</code>&nbsp;<em>before</em>&nbsp;<code>Contoso</code>&nbsp;or use regular expression to match both versions.</li><li>Having placeholders is great for translation purposes, but you want to return these to their original forms when generating the translation. This functionality&nbsp;is part of the next section,&nbsp;<code>Cleanup Target and Generate Files</code>.</li></ol><p>Cleanup Target and Generate Files</p><p>This is the second batch task, which allows you to run modify text in the target and generate the target translations. The settings screen looks like this:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4377.cleanup_2D00_target_2D00_and_2D00_generate_2D00_files.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/496x449/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4377.cleanup_2D00_target_2D00_and_2D00_generate_2D00_files.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Save Folder</p><p>If you want to generate the target translations when this batch task is ran, click&nbsp;Generate Target. You can also specify where the files will be saved. The default is the&nbsp;Desktop.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3364.cleanup_2D00_target_2D00_save_2D00_folder.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/416x80/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3364.cleanup_2D00_target_2D00_save_2D00_folder.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h2>Backup Folder</h2><p>Before generating target translations, you can save the sdlxliff files to a backup folder. Click&nbsp;<code>Make Backups</code>&nbsp;to make this happen. You can specify the folder to save them, and if not, by default a folder called&nbsp;<code>Cleanup Backups</code>&nbsp;is created in the project.</p><p>Also, if you click&nbsp;<code>Preserve Placeholder Tags</code>, the backups you make will&nbsp;<em>overwrite</em>&nbsp;the original sdlxliff files in the project.</p><h3>Preserve Placeholder Tags</h3><p>If you have been following along, under&nbsp;<a href=\"http://jessegood.github.io/translation/cleanuptool.html#placeholders\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">Placeholder</a>&nbsp;we converted some proper nouns into placeholders. The problem is that when you generate the target translation, if these placeholders appear in the target segment, they will remain as a tag, or even worse be ignored completely when generating the translation. In order to solve this problem, any placeholders created during&nbsp;<code>Cleanup Source</code>&nbsp;will be stored in the project file so that they can be returned to text when generating the translation.</p><p>It is an automatic process, but when you run&nbsp;<code>Cleanup Target and Generate Files</code>, if a stored placeholder is found in the target segment, it will be converted back to plain text.</p><p>This is all done by convention, so if the placeholder was&nbsp;<code>&lt;Contoso /&gt;</code>, it will be replaced with simply&nbsp;<code>Contoso</code>. When using attributes, the placeholder will be replaced with the contents of the attribute, so&nbsp;<code>&lt;Locked Name=\"Contoso\" /&gt;</code>&nbsp;will be replaced with&nbsp;<code>Contoso</code>.</p><p>Before</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4617.placeholder_2D00_before.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/388x258/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4617.placeholder_2D00_before.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>After</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8154.contoso_2D00_after.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/418x218/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8154.contoso_2D00_after.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h3>Automating the Process</h3><p>They key to any successful translation workflow is&nbsp;<code>Automation</code>, the more we can automate, the more we can focus on the important stuff. Now for this Batch Task Plug-in, you can create a custom sequence so that this is ran automatically during project creation. However, there is an important point that you need to be aware of when doing this:</p><p>Segments are not generated until the&nbsp;<code>Pre-Translate Files</code>&nbsp;task is ran, so you need to make sure&nbsp;<code>Cleanup Source</code>&nbsp;appears after that.</p><p>This is somewhat limiting though, as&nbsp;<code>Cleanup Source</code>&nbsp;may affect matching. I will look into other options when I get a chance.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3312.custom_2D00_task_2D00_sequence.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/786x593/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3312.custom_2D00_task_2D00_sequence.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h3>Reports</h3><p>Also, note that the tool generates reports and log files of what it does!</p><p>The report itself (shown below) does not give very much information, only a count of each item and a path to the log file. The log file itself though will contain a lot more detailed information about what happened, so if you need to find out why something changed, please look at the log file. (I considered adding all the info about what happened to the report here, but I found the size of the report gets too large quickly.)</p><p>Example:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6661.report_2D00_example.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x119/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6661.report_2D00_example.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><em>Note</em>: I wanted to create links so you can just click on it to open the log file, but I haven’t figured it out. I asked a question on&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/products-solutions/solutions/customer_experience_cloud/language/language-developers/f/57/t/7246\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">sdl community</a>&nbsp;about it.</p><h1>Updating Formatting Attributes (Experimental Support)</h1><p>There is current partial (read as “very limited”) support for updating formatting attributes.</p><p>For example, with the following text:</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6201.highlight_2D00_change_2D00_before.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/452x53/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6201.highlight_2D00_change_2D00_before.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>If you create the following rule (note how I changed&nbsp;<code>yellow</code>&nbsp;to&nbsp;<code>blue</code>):</p><p>&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0550.tagpair_2D00_attribute_2D00_update_2D00_settings.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/324x291/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0550.tagpair_2D00_attribute_2D00_update_2D00_settings.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>You can update the color of the highlight:</p><p>&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0181.highlight_2D00_change_2D00_after.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/444x58/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0181.highlight_2D00_change_2D00_after.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>&nbsp;However, there are a lot of issues with this feature that need to be worked on, so I advise&nbsp;<em>against</em>&nbsp;using it except for experimentation. The biggest problem stems from the fact that you need to understand the underlying file type to update the formatting in the generated target file.</p><p>For anyone interested, all source code is&nbsp;<a href=\"https://github.com/sdl/Sdl-Community/tree/master/CleanUpTasks\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: Problem</u></em></p><p>One of the benefits of working with Studio is that it is unique amongst CAT tools in providing Document Structure Information that can be used to help provide additional context when translating. The downside of this feature is that you have to actively click the DSI column to see this information for each segment and you can't work on the translation while it is being displayed. The same thing goes for comments which can only be seen by scrolling through the comments view.</p><p><br></p><p><em><u>The Solution</u></em></p><p>The <strong>DSI Viewer</strong> solves this issue by providing you with a small viewer that you can position somewhere convenient on your screen and this displays the DSI information and comments for every segment you enter without you having to click anything.</p><p>Very handy when working with DITA files, custom XML files or even SDLXLIFF files that have been exported from SDL Passolo (these contain string IDs that are very useful when translating).</p><p>It's also particularly useful when working with Bilingual Excel files as these support the ability to add information from the Excel file to the Document Structure Information and/or comments.</p><p><br></p><p>To learn how to use this application, please visit <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/5563/dsi-viewer\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>DSI Viewer </strong></a> in the RWS Community wiki</p>","documentation":"<h3>1. Prerequisites</h3><p>To use the DSI Viewer, make sure the following prerequisites are met:</p><ul><li><strong>Trados Studio Compatibility</strong>: DSI Viewer is compatible with Trados Studio 2019 and later.</li><li><strong>Installation Requirements</strong>: Administrator privileges may be required for installation on some systems.</li><li><strong>Project Files</strong>: Ensure that you have a project with SDLXLIFF files ready to use with DSI Viewer.</li></ul><h3>2. Access &amp; Logins</h3><p>After installation, accessing the DSI Viewer in Trados Studio is easy:</p><ol><li><strong>Launch Trados Studio</strong> and open a project.<strong>Navigate to the View Tab</strong> in the main menu.<strong>Select DSI Viewer</strong>: You’ll see the DSI Viewer option available, ready for use.</li></ol><blockquote><strong>No additional login</strong> is required as it integrates directly within Trados Studio.</blockquote><h3>3. Core Features</h3><p>The DSI Viewer includes several valuable features designed to enhance productivity and accuracy in Trados Studio:</p><h4><strong>Quality Estimation Model</strong></h4><ul><li><strong>Description</strong>: The DSI Viewer now includes a <strong>Quality Estimation Model</strong> that provides insights into translation quality at a glance.</li><li><strong>Functionality</strong>: This feature is accessible within the DSI Viewer and helps quickly assess the quality of content without needing in-depth analysis.</li></ul><h4><strong>Tell Me Functionality</strong></h4><ul><li><strong>Description</strong>: Use the <strong>Tell Me</strong> feature in Trados Studio to locate commands, settings, and features quickly.</li><li><strong>How to Use</strong>: Simply type in a keyword or phrase in the Tell Me search bar, and Trados Studio will suggest relevant commands, options, and settings.</li><li><strong>Benefit</strong>: Saves time and offers intuitive access to DSI Viewer settings and functions, streamlining navigation.</li></ul><h4><strong>Optimized Field Width for Displayed Information</strong></h4><ul><li><strong>Description</strong>: DSI Viewer now fully displays the <strong>ID content</strong> due to an optimized, expanded field width.</li><li><strong>Context</strong>: Previously, if SDLXLIFF files with Source, Target, and ID columns were exported from Passolo, space constraints in the DSI Viewer window limited the ID column's display.</li><li><strong>Improvement</strong>: The expanded field width now allows for full-length display of ID content, making it easier to view details without needing to click into the column.</li></ul><h3>4.Supported Workflows</h3><p>The DSI Viewer supports workflows that enhance file navigation, quality assessment, and content display:</p><ul><li><strong>Project Navigation</strong>: Access the DSI Viewer directly from the View Tab in any open project, allowing easy switching between documents.</li><li><strong>Quality Assessment Workflow</strong>: Use the Quality Estimation Model to identify areas for review or re-translation, ensuring that all content meets project standards.</li><li><strong>Enhanced ID Viewing Workflow</strong>: Export SDLXLIFF files from Passolo with three columns (Source, Target, and ID), and use the DSI Viewer’s expanded field to review full ID content at a glance.</li></ul><p><br></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/1310/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/25/versions/1447","changelog":{"Trados Cloud","description":"<p>This add-on enables users working in the cloud (through Trados Studio, Trados Team or Trados Enterprise) to receive machine translation results from the DeepL Translator - <a href=\"https://www.deepl.com/translator\">https://www.deepl.com/translator</a>.</p>\r\n<p>You can directly install the DeepL Machine Translation Provider from within your cloud account. Simply select your <strong>account icon</strong> in the top right hand corner of the screen&gt; <strong>RWS AppStore</strong>.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Please note:</strong> You will need to purchase a subscription to DeepL Pro and you&#39;ll need an API key to use the add-on which you can obtain through <a href=\"https://www.deepl.com/api-contact.html\">DeepL sales</a>. Pricing for the DeepL service is available through this <a href=\"https://www.deepl.com/pro.html\">DeepL website</a>.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h1>Trados Cloud</h1>\r\n<h1>Introduction</h1>\r\n<p>Trados Cloud add-on is available by &quot;downloading&quot; the add-on from here. When you &quot;download&quot; the application you will be redirected and asked to sign in to your Trados Cloud account. If you don&#39;t have one, you won&#39;t be able to access the application at all.</p>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>access to Trados Cloud account</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>There is no installation on your computer required as the installation takes place in your Trados Cloud account where you will see something like this:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/Picture1.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"></p>\r\n<p>Clicking on the link will take you to the installation page where you simply click Install:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/Picture2.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"></p>\r\n<p>You&#39;ll then be asked to update needed configuration:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/DeepL%20-%20Config%20New.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"></p>\r\n<h1>API Version</h1>\r\n<p>There are 2 choices:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>V1 - DeepL Advanced or Ultimate subscription</li>\r\n<li>V2 - DeepL Pro API subscription</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>API Key</h1>\r\n<p>DeepL API Key for selected API Version value.\r\nExample: \r\nxxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx</p>\r\n<h1>Preserve Formatting</h1>\r\n<p>The Preserve Formatting setting controls whether the translation engine should retain the original formatting of the text, even when it would typically apply corrections.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/Picture4.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Possible Values</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p><strong>Checked</strong> ✅ – The original formatting is preserved as closely as possible.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p><strong>Unchecked ⬜ (Default)</strong> – The translation engine may adjust formatting for correctness.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Formatting Aspects Affected</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p><strong>Punctuation</strong> at the beginning and end of a sentence (e.g., extra spaces, missing periods).</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p><strong>Upper/lower case</strong> at the beginning of a sentence (e.g., maintaining capitalization exactly as in the source text).</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>Tag Handling</h1>\r\n<p>The Tag Handling setting specifies how the translation engine processes XML or HTML tags within the text. When enabled, the translation engine correctly processes and preserves structured content.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/Picture5.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Available Options</strong><br>\r\n•\tXML – Enables XML tag handling.<br>\r\n•\tHTML – Enables HTML tag handling.<br>\r\n•\tNONE – No specific tag handling applied.<br></p>\r\n<h1>Tag Handling Version</h1>\r\n<p>The Tag Handling Version field enables users to choose between two versions for its tag handling algorithm:<br>•\tv1 (default): The traditional algorithm.<br>•\tv2: This is an improved algorithm for translating XML and HTML content. It balances both translation quality and formatting.    </p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/DeepL%20-%20TH%20Version.png\" alt=\"Installation step\">   </p>\r\n<p><strong>Validation Rules</strong><br></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Tag Handling Version v2 can not be used with Model Type &quot;Latency Optimized&quot;.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>Tag Handling JSON</h1>\r\n<p>The Tag Handling JSON field enables users to define tag handling settings for specific languages, overriding the global tag handling value.<br> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/Picture6.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"><br>\r\n<strong>Configuration Format</strong><br>\r\n[<br>\r\n  {&quot;TargetLanguage&quot;: &quot;ko-KR,de-DE&quot;, &quot;TagHandling&quot;: &quot;xml&quot;},<br>\r\n  {&quot;TargetLanguage&quot;: &quot;ja-JP&quot;, &quot;TagHandling&quot;: &quot;html&quot;},<br>\r\n  {&quot;TargetLanguage&quot;: &quot;fr-FR&quot;, &quot;TagHandling&quot;: &quot;none&quot;}<br>\r\n]</p>\r\n<p><strong>Explanation</strong><br></p>\r\n<p>•\t<strong>TargetLanguage</strong>: Specifies one or multiple target languages using language codes.<br>\r\n•\t<strong>TagHandling</strong>: Defines how tags should be processed for the specified languages.<br></p>\r\n<p>Supported values:<br></p>\r\n<p>o\t&quot;XML&quot; – Handles tags as XML.<br>\r\no\t&quot;HTML&quot; – Handles tags as HTML.<br>\r\no\t&quot;NONE&quot; – No specific tag handling applied.<br>\r\nThis feature enhances flexibility in managing language-specific tag processing.<br></p>\r\n<p><strong>Validation Rules</strong><br></p>\r\n<p>•\tThe TagHandlingJson <strong>must be a JSON array</strong>.<br>\r\n•\tEach entry must contain:<br></p>\r\n<p>o\tA valid <strong>language code</strong> in the format xx-XX (e.g., &quot;en-US&quot;, &quot;fr-FR&quot;).<br>\r\no\tA <strong>TagHandling</strong> value that must be either &quot;XML&quot;, &quot;HTML&quot;, or &quot;NONE&quot;.<br></p>\r\n<p>•\t<strong>Invalid Configurations</strong>:<br>\r\no\tEntries missing TargetLanguage or TagHandling.<br>\r\no\tInvalid language formats (e.g., &quot;English&quot; instead of &quot;en-US&quot;).<br>\r\no\tDuplicate language entries.<br>\r\no\tIncorrect TagHandling values (e.g., &quot;text&quot; instead of &quot;HTML&quot;).<br></p>\r\n<h1>Ignore The Following Tags<br></h1>\r\n<p>This setting allows users to specify a comma-separated list of XML tags whose content should not be translated. This ensures that elements such as trademarks, product names, or technical terms remain unchanged.<br></p>\r\n<p><strong>Example Usage</strong><br></p>\r\n<p>If <strong>Ignore The Following Tags</strong> is set to keep, the text inside  tags will not be translated.<br></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/Picture7.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"><br></p>\r\n<p><strong>Example Request</strong>:<br></p>\r\n<p>Please open the page Settings to configure your system.<br></p>\r\n<p><strong>Example Response</strong>:<br></p>\r\n<p>Bitte öffnen Sie die Seite Settings, um Ihr System zu konfigurieren.<br>\r\nHere, &quot;Settings&quot; remains in English because it is enclosed in the  tag.<br></p>\r\n<h1>Translation Formality<br></h1>\r\n<p>Choose if the translated text should lean towards formal or informal language.<br>\r\nPossible options are: Default, More, Less, Prefer Less, Prefer More<br></p>\r\n<h1>Glossary Settings<br></h1>\r\n<p>This is a Language Pair to Glossary mapping: Enter your language pair to glossary associations in JSON format.<br>\r\nThere are 2 ways in which you can map LPs to glossaries:<br>\r\n•\tLanguage Code:<br>\r\n{ &quot;en&gt;de&quot;:&quot;glossary1&quot;, &quot;de&gt;fr&quot;:&quot;glossary2&quot;}<br>\r\n•\tNot detailed:<br>\r\n[&quot;glossary1&quot;,&quot;glossary2&quot;,&quot;glossary3&quot;]<br></p>\r\n<h1>Style Rules<br></h1>\r\n<p>The Style Rules JSON field allows users to define a Style ID for specific target languages.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/DeepL%20-%20Style%20Rule.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"> </p>\r\n<p><strong>Configuration Format</strong><br>\r\n{&quot;LanguageCode1&quot;:&quot;StyleID1&quot;, &quot;LanguageCode2&quot;:&quot;StyleID2&quot;}<br><br>\r\n<strong>Example</strong>:<br>\r\n{<br>\r\n   &quot;de&quot;: &quot;7ff9bfd6-cd85-4190-8503-d6215a321519&quot;,<br>\r\n   &quot;fr&quot;: &quot;4ff9bff4-gd23-4190-8503-e6215a321435&quot;<br>\r\n}   </p>\r\n<p><strong>Validation Rules</strong><br></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Style Rules must be a valid JSON object.</li>\r\n<li>If Style Rules is set, Model Type must be either Quality Optimized or Prefer Quality Optimized. Combining Style ID with Latency Optimized is not supported.</li>\r\n<li>The target language must match the language defined in the Style Rule list on DeepL.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>Split Sentences<br></h1>\r\n<p>The <strong>Split Sentences</strong> option controls how the translation engine divides the input text into sentences before translating. This setting is essential to ensure accurate sentence segmentation, especially when working with structured or complex content like HTML or XML.<br>\r\n<img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/Picture8.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"><br></p>\r\n<p><strong>Available Options</strong><br></p>\r\n<table>\r\n<thead>\r\n<tr>\r\n<th>Option</th>\r\n<th>Description</th>\r\n</tr>\r\n</thead>\r\n<tbody><tr>\r\n<td>0</td>\r\n<td>No splitting: The entire input is treated as a single sentence. Recommended if your input text is already segmented.</td>\r\n</tr>\r\n<tr>\r\n<td>1</td>\r\n<td>Split on punctuation and newlines (default when Tag Handling is not set to html): Splits on punctuation and line breaks.</td>\r\n</tr>\r\n<tr>\r\n<td>nonewlines</td>\r\n<td>Split on punctuation only, ignoring newlines (default when Tag Handling is set  to html): Ideal for HTML content where line breaks should be ignored.</td>\r\n</tr>\r\n</tbody></table>\r\n<p><br><strong>Default Behavior (If No Option Is Selected)</strong><br></p>\r\n<p>If you leave the <strong>Split Sentences</strong> field empty (unselected), the translation engine applies its default behavior, based on the Tag Handling configuration:<br></p>\r\n<p>•\t<strong>If Tag Handling is not set to html</strong>: <br>\r\no\tThe default is 1. <br>\r\no\tSentences are split on punctuation and newlines.<br></p>\r\n<p>•\t<strong>If Tag Handling is set to html</strong>: <br>\r\no\tThe default is nonewlines.<br>\r\no\tSentences are split on punctuation only, and newlines are ignored.<br></p>\r\n<p><strong>Recommendations</strong><br></p>\r\n<p>•\tUse 0 when your content is pre-segmented into sentences.<br>\r\n•\tUse nonewlines for structured documents like HTML, where you want to preserve line breaks without affecting sentence splitting.<br>\r\n•\tLeave the field blank for automatic default behavior, based on your Tag Handling settings.<br></p>\r\n<h1>Model Type<br></h1>\r\n<p>This setting allows you to choose which DeepL translation model to use, based on your priorities for speed and translation quality.<br></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2abc7421-37d0-4220-a881-5c04307f5f8e/Resources/00882ef2-582b-4742-bb20-eb047fa1236d.png\" alt=\"Installation step\"></p>\r\n<p><b>Available Options:</b><br></p>\r\n<p>• <b>Latency Optimized (Default)</b><br>\r\nFastest option. Uses classic DeepL models and supports all language pairs.</p>\r\n<p>• <b>Quality Optimized</b><br>\r\nUses next-generation models for improved translation quality.<br>\r\n⚠️ Only available for a limited set of language pairs. Translation will fail if an unsupported pair is used.</p>\r\n<p>• <b>Prefer Quality Optimized</b><br>\r\nAttempts to use higher quality models. If the selected language pair is unsupported, it will automatically fall back to the classic model.<br></p>\r\n<p>Future Changes Notice: DeepL may begin using quality-optimized models by default, even when model_type is set to latency_optimized, if performance parity is achieved in the future.</p>\r\n<h1>Troubleshooting<br></h1>\r\n<p>Sometimes errors can be reported when trying to get a translation result from DeepL. This may be related to firewall restrictions which can be resolved by whitelisting the following domains:<br>\r\nwww2.deepl.com<br>\r\napi.deepl.com<br>\r\nFor more details on this you may find this link helpful:<br>\r\n<a href=\"https://gateway.sdl.com/apex/communityknowledge?articleName=000013232\">https://gateway.sdl.com/apex/communityknowledge?articleName=000013232</a> </p>\r\n<style>\r\n }\r\n</style>","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/140/versions/2254","versionNumber":"1.6.41.0","updatedAt":"2026-04-23T13:03:36.417916Z","createdAt":"2026-04-23T13:02:08.704536Z","publishedAt":"2026-04-23T13:03:36.632472Z","supportedProducts":[{"isLegacy":false,"id":12,"parentProductID":2,"productName":"Trados translation"}]},{"id":24,"downloadCount":32421,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2018-04-27T10:03:02.367Z","updatedAt":"2026-04-14T12:18:44.169274Z","name":"DeepL Translation Provider","description":"<p>This plugin provides machine translation results from the DeepL Translator found here - <a href=\"https://www.deepl.com/translator\">https://www.deepl.com/translator</a>.</p>\r\n<p>You will need to purchase a subscription to DeepL Pro and you&#39;ll need an API key to use the plugin which you can obtain through <a href=\"https://www.deepl.com/api-contact.html\">DeepL sales.</a></p>\r\n<p>Pricing for the DeepL service is available <a href=\"https://www.deepl.com/pro.html\">through this DeepL website.</a> .</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h3>Introduction</h3>\r\n<p><strong>DeepL MT Provider</strong> connects <strong>Trados Studio</strong> and <strong>Trados Cloud</strong> to the <strong>DeepL API</strong> for machine translation. To use this provider, you need a <strong>DeepL API Pro</strong> subscription and a valid <strong>DeepL API authentication key</strong> from your DeepL account. The integration supports the <strong>DeepL Pro API endpoint only</strong> (<code>api.deepl.com</code>) and does <strong>not</strong> support <strong>DeepL API Free</strong> (<code>api-free.deepl.com</code>). DeepL documents the endpoint distinction explicitly and also documents that API authentication is provided by sending the key in the <code>Authorization</code> header. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/auth\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<p>You can find and manage your API key in the <strong>API Keys</strong> section of your DeepL account. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/auth\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>DeepL API Pro</strong> subscription</li>\r\n<li>Valid <strong>DeepL API authentication key</strong></li>\r\n<li><strong>Trados Studio 2024</strong> for the current glossary workflow used with Trados Cloud</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Key Features</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Machine translation using the <strong>DeepL API Pro</strong> service</li>\r\n<li>Support for <strong>DeepL Glossaries</strong> in supported workflows</li>\r\n<li>Support for advanced DeepL request settings, including:<ul>\r\n<li><code>tag_handling</code></li>\r\n<li><code>ignore_tags</code></li>\r\n<li><code>split_sentences</code></li>\r\n<li><code>model_type</code> (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n<li>Optimized batch processing: the integration is moving to sending <strong>multiple segments in a single request</strong> where possible, instead of sending each segment independently. DeepL supports sending <strong>up to 50 text items in one translation request</strong>, which reduces roundtrips and improves throughput during batch processing. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Subscription Support</h2>\r\n<p>This integration supports <strong>DeepL API Pro</strong> only.</p>\r\n<p><strong>DeepL API Free is not supported</strong> by this integration because the plugin connects to the <strong>Pro API endpoint</strong> only. DeepL distinguishes <strong>API Free</strong> and <strong>API Pro</strong> by endpoint:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>API Pro</strong>: <code>https://api.deepl.com</code></li>\r\n<li><strong>API Free</strong>: <code>https://api-free.deepl.com</code> (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/auth\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>To avoid confusion, do <strong>not</strong> refer to <strong>v1</strong>, <strong>v2</strong>, or <strong>v3</strong> as subscription types. In DeepL documentation, these terms refer to <strong>API versions and endpoints</strong>, not commercial plans. DeepL states that the primary API version is <strong>v2</strong>, while some endpoints are also available under <strong>v3</strong>. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/auth\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<h2>DeepL Glossaries</h2>\r\n<p>To use <strong>DeepL Glossaries</strong> in Trados Cloud (<strong>Trados Enterprise</strong>, <strong>Trados Accelerate</strong>, and <strong>Trados Team</strong>), you need <strong>DeepL API Pro</strong> and <strong>Trados Studio 2024</strong>.</p>\r\n<p>For the <strong>current release</strong>, create the glossary in <strong>Trados Studio 2024</strong>, then add it in <strong>Trados Cloud</strong> through the <strong>DeepL plugin configuration</strong>.</p>\r\n<p>DeepL’s current documentation recommends using the <strong>v3 glossary endpoints</strong> for ongoing glossary development. The <strong>v3</strong> glossary API includes all v2 functionality and adds support for <strong>editing glossaries</strong> and working with <strong>multilingual glossaries</strong>. DeepL also states that <strong>v2 is kept for backward compatibility</strong>, but recommends <strong>v3</strong> going forward. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/api-reference/glossaries/v2-vs-v3-endpoints\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<p>DeepL’s Help Center also states that glossaries can be managed through the <strong>DeepL website</strong> and that glossaries created via the website and DeepL desktop apps can be used in <strong>CAT tools</strong>, and vice versa. (<a href=\"https://support.deepl.com/hc/en-us/articles/360021634540-About-the-glossary\">support.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<h3>Planned Changes</h3>\r\n<p>In upcoming releases, the ability to <strong>create a glossary directly in the plugin UI</strong> will be removed.</p>\r\n<p>Going forward, users will create and manage glossaries in the <strong>DeepL UI</strong>, then connect to them from the plugin. This change reflects DeepL’s current glossary direction toward <strong>v3</strong>, removes reliance on older <strong>v2-based</strong> deep integration in the plugin UI, and aligns the <strong>Trados Studio plugin</strong> with the <strong>DeepL add-on for Trados Cloud</strong> so that both integrations follow the same glossary-management approach.</p>\r\n<p>DeepL recommends <strong>v3</strong> for glossary work, notes that future glossary functionality will continue to be added to <strong>v3</strong>, and strongly discourages mixing <strong>v2</strong> and <strong>v3</strong> glossary handling in the same implementation. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/api-reference/glossaries/v2-vs-v3-endpoints\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<h2>Translation Settings</h2>\r\n<h3>Tag Handling</h3>\r\n<p>The plugin supports DeepL tag-handling options for structured content:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><code>tag_handling=xml</code> enables XML tag handling</li>\r\n<li><code>tag_handling=html</code> enables HTML tag handling</li>\r\n<li><code>ignore_tags</code> lets you specify comma-separated XML tags whose content should not be translated (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Newline and Sentence Splitting</h3>\r\n<p>The <code>split_sentences</code> setting controls how DeepL handles punctuation and line breaks during translation.</p>\r\n<p>Available options:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p><strong>Default</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>When <code>tag_handling</code> is not set to <code>html</code>, the default is <code>1</code>, which means DeepL splits on punctuation and newlines.</li>\r\n<li>When <code>tag_handling=html</code>, the default is <code>nonewlines</code>, which means DeepL splits on punctuation only and ignores newlines. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p><strong>No splitting</strong> (<code>0</code>)</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>No sentence splitting is performed; the whole input is treated as one sentence. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p><strong>No newlines</strong> (<code>nonewlines</code>)</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>DeepL splits on punctuation only and ignores newlines. DeepL also recommends this option when unwanted line breaks may affect translation quality. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For applications that send <strong>one sentence per text parameter</strong>, DeepL recommends setting <code>split_sentences=0</code> to avoid unintended splitting. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<h3>Model Type</h3>\r\n<p>The plugin supports DeepL’s <code>model_type</code> parameter for translation requests.</p>\r\n<p>DeepL documents the following values:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><code>latency_optimized</code> – uses the lowest-latency models available</li>\r\n<li><code>quality_optimized</code> – uses the highest-quality models available</li>\r\n<li><code>prefer_quality_optimized</code> – prefers the highest-quality models and falls back if needed (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>DeepL also notes that <strong>tag handling v2</strong> is compatible only with next-generation models and implicitly sets <code>model_type</code> to <code>quality_optimized</code>. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<h2>How the Plugin Works</h2>\r\n<p>After installing the plugin in <strong>Trados Studio</strong>, open:</p>\r\n<p><strong>Project Settings &gt; All Language Pairs &gt; Translation Memory and Automated Translation</strong></p>\r\n<p>Then select the DeepL provider and enter your <strong>DeepL API authentication key</strong>.</p>\r\n<p>The key must be valid for the <strong>DeepL Pro API endpoint</strong> supported by this integration. DeepL documents that API keys are managed in the <strong>API Keys</strong> section of the account and that authentication should be sent using the <code>Authorization</code> header. (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/auth\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</p>\r\n<blockquote>\r\n<p><strong>Note:</strong> If the settings window does not immediately enable the <strong>OK</strong> button after entering the key, re-entering the key may help refresh the UI validation.  </p>\r\n<p><em>This is a product behavior note rather than a DeepL requirement.</em></p>\r\n</blockquote>\r\n<h2>Troubleshooting</h2>\r\n<p>If DeepL translation requests fail, check the following:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Your network allows outbound access to the <strong>DeepL Pro API endpoint</strong> used by the integration: <code>api.deepl.com</code></li>\r\n<li>Your API key is valid and belongs to a <strong>DeepL API Pro</strong> subscription</li>\r\n<li>Authentication is being sent using the <strong><code>Authorization</code> header</strong>, as documented by DeepL (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/auth\">developers.deepl.com</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>If your environment uses strict firewall or proxy rules, make sure the required DeepL domains are allowlisted according to your organization’s network policy.</p>\r\n<h2>DeepL Reference Documentation</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/auth\">DeepL Documentation - Access and authentication</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/translate\">DeepL Documentation - Translate Text</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/glossaries/v2-vs-v3-endpoints\">DeepL Documentation - v2 vs v3 glossary endpoints</a></li>\r\n<li><a supported-languages, that is based on DeepL public documentation (<a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/supported-languages\">https://developers.deepl.com/docs/getting-started/supported-languages</a> ). DeepL have confirmed that this is the recommended approach until their languages API endpoint is updated for wider customer release.</p>\r\n<p>Support for selecting Style rules <a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/style-rules\">https://developers.deepl.com/api-reference/style-rules</a></p>\r\n<p>Support for tag_handling_version=v2 <a href=\"https://developers.deepl.com/docs/xml-and-html-handling/tag-handling-v2#html\">https://developers.deepl.com/docs/xml-and-html-handling/tag-handling-v2#html</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n download raw data that can be used to create your own reports.\u00A0The data is downloaded in a very raw form to try and ensure that you can maximise the range of reports it&#39;s possible to create.\u00A0You are only limited by your imagination and your ability to work with Excel.\u00A0A couple of important notes on this:</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>you can connect to GroupShare 2017 or 2020</li>\r\n<li>the data available to you will be restricted by your permissions on the GroupShare server</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Installation</h2>\r\n<p>When you visit the\u00A0<a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/excel4groupshare/904/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1701362240216.1701365453901.103&amp;__hssc=110027290.8.1701365453901&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\">SDL AppStore</a>\u00A0to download the application it will be downloaded as a zip file.\u00A0You should unzip the file to somewhere on your computer before running the installer as running it within the zip can cause problems for some users.</p>\r\n<p><em>NOTE:</em>\u00A0If you have a previous version of the application installed you should uninstall it\u00A0through\u00A0<em><strong>Add/Remove Programs</strong></em>\u00A0in Windows before attempting to install a new version.</p>\r\n<p>Once you have unzipped the file you will find a folder with three entries:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7571.pastedimage1588578709537v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>To install you simply run the &quot;setup.exe&quot; file which will deliver a dialogue like this:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2474.pastedimage1588578810225v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Click on Install and the application installation process will be completed in a few seconds:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6366.pastedimage1588578853618v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Click on close and now you should be able to find the GroupShare toolbar in Excel like this:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5807.pastedimage1588578966643v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<h2>Getting Started...</h2>\r\n<p>The first thing to do is click on Login so you have access to your GroupShare instance:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0020.pastedimage1588579225820v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Once you are successfully logged in you&#39;ll have access to the other menu items.\u00A0If you click on the other items before logging in you&#39;ll simply be taken to the same login screen first.</p>\r\n<h2>What can you download?</h2>\r\n<p>Once you&#39;re logged in you have three options in the Excel ribbon:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4456.pastedimage1588585048683v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<h3>Get Project Data</h3>\r\n<p>This feature will pull data held on your GroupShare projects and offers similar filtering to that available in GroupShare to narrow down the data being pulled.\u00A0This is important because the volume of data available is significantly higher than that available through the conventional reporting in GroupShare itself and te more you attempt to download the longer it will take.\u00A0So the more options you select, the more data you will get, the more cups of coffee you&#39;re going to need!</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3187.pastedimage1588585246841v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>However, once you do have the data downloaded you&#39;ll see the amount of data available to you is significant.\u00A0To initiate the download select your options and click on\u00A0<em><strong>Get Data</strong></em>.\u00A0You&#39;l see something like this:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7345.pastedimage1588585432888v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>You can cancel the download at any time and the operation will stop, leaving you with only the data that was downloaded by the time you clicked cancel.\u00A0With all the options checked you can expect to see data in columns relating to the following:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Organisation</li>\r\n<li>Project Name</li>\r\n<li>Created at (date)</li>\r\n<li>Delivery Date</li>\r\n<li>Client</li>\r\n<li>Project Language Pairs</li>\r\n<li>Project Status</li>\r\n<li>Project Description</li>\r\n<li>File Status</li>\r\n<li>File Name</li>\r\n<li>File Type</li>\r\n<li>File Size (bytes)</li>\r\n<li>File Role (source/translatable/reference)</li>\r\n<li>File Language Code</li>\r\n<li>File Check In By\u00A0(user name)</li>\r\n<li>File Check Out To\u00A0(user name)</li>\r\n<li>File Last Check In Date</li>\r\n<li>File Last Check Out Date</li>\r\n<li>File Last Modified Date</li>\r\n<li>File Approved SignOff Words</li>\r\n<li>File Approved Translation Words</li>\r\n<li>File Draft Words</li>\r\n<li>File Rejected SignOff Words</li>\r\n<li>File Rejected Translation Words</li>\r\n<li>File Total Words</li>\r\n<li>File Translated Words</li>\r\n<li>File Unspecified Words</li>\r\n<li>File Type Percent Complete</li>\r\n<li>Assignees Names</li>\r\n<li>Assignees Roles</li>\r\n<li>Assignees Emails</li>\r\n<li>Current Phase (phase name)</li>\r\n<li>Preparation (user
