Number</li>\r\n<li>Role</li>\r\n<li>Project (role related)</li>\r\n<li>Organization</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>Excel Tips for Reporting</h1>\r\n<p>The raw data pulled from GroupShare has been designed to make it possible to create complex reports in as many ways as possible.\u00A0This means there is a trade-off between how much granularity we wanted and how much skill you need in using Excel to get what you need.\u00A0It also means a huge trade-off in terms of the time it takes to download data since the application is doing some very complex lookups between tables to pull the data in a way that makes it accessible for reporting.</p>\r\n<h2>Simple Reporting</h2>\r\n<p>For very simple reports you can of course use the filtering capabilities in Excel under the\u00A0<em><strong>Home ribbon</strong></em>:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7725.pastedimage1588587738382v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>You can also add columns for your own calculations, and these can often be helpful to improve the more complex reporting by manipulating the data tables in Excel before creating your reports.\u00A0It can take some thought to report accurately on what you need and to ensure you are not duplicating or omitting details.\u00A0A good check is always to look in GroupShare and make sure you have the simple stuff right, such as number of organisations, or projects, as a cross check.</p>\r\n<h2>Detail Reporting</h2>\r\n<p>For detailed reporting your best bet is to use a Pivot Table.\u00A0If you have not used these before here&#39;s a couple of useful tips.</p>\r\n<h3>Add to your Quick Access bar in Excel</h3>\r\n<p>This make it far easier and faster to access the menu item needed.\u00A0First of all access the\u00A0<em><strong>More Commands menu...</strong></em>:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7080.pastedimage1588589794395v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Select\u00A0<em><strong>All Commands</strong></em>\u00A0from the drop down and then\u00A0<em><strong>Add&gt;&gt;</strong></em>\u00A0using the add button the\u00A0<em><strong>PivotChart &amp; PivotTable</strong></em>\u00A0to your list of commands that will be displayed in the Quick Access Toolbar:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6076.pastedimage1588589905911v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Now you can select all the data from your downloaded worksheet and start the Pivot creation process in one-click:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6864.pastedimage1588590106547v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>It&#39;s also worth noting that you probably need to check this option which is not selected by detail:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6038.pastedimage1588590146676v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>This is because it adds an additional value called\u00A0<em><strong>Distinct Count</strong></em>\u00A0to your choice when setting the Values field in your pivot.\u00A0Without this you don&#39;t have this option and the results of yor reports may contain duplicate counts depending on how you compiled your report:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5025.pastedimage1588590224611v5.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Pivot tables work by allowing you to drag and drop the fields you want from the field list at the top (essentially the column headings from your download) into the four different areas at the bottom:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4011.pastedimage1588591764876v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>You can manipulate this as much as you like and you&#39;ll soon get the idea of how useful this can be as you see the tables and charts being drawn automagically in front of you.\u00A0Some good resources would be:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://www.excel-easy.com/data-analysis/pivot-tables.html\">https://www.excel-easy.com/data-analysis/pivot-tables.html</a></p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://support.office.com/en-us/article/create-a-pivottable-to-analyze-worksheet-data-a9a84538-bfe9-40a9-a8e9-f99134456576\">https://support.office.com/en-us/article/create-a-pivottable-to-analyze-worksheet-data-a9a84538-bfe9-40a9-a8e9-f99134456576</a></p>\r\n<h3>Technical before starting a translation.&nbsp;</p><p>Support for Trados Studio 2021 and Trados Studio 2019 versions is available through the RWS Community.</p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">How to use Fail Safe Task?</span></p><ul><li>Right-click on the selected file(s) on which you&nbsp;wish to run the verification on.</li><li>Click on Failsafe task.</li><li>Select one of the settings option.</li><li>Press Next/Finish.</li></ul><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2604.pastedimage1549452735235v1.png\" the .sdlxliff files actions which are performed are only temporary. Basically, all the .sdlxliff files are copied in advance before the task starts and then restored again after the task is complete.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>Waiting screen</p><p>Note: When having&nbsp;a large amount of files,&nbsp;user might&nbsp;have to wait a little bit on finishing the performing of the task (which happens on the Waiting screen). Since Trados Studio is the preferred choice for many users, the Wordfast TXML File Type integration allows us to support TXML files while retaining their stored translation data.</p><h5>Getting Started</h5><p><strong>1.Minimum Requirements</strong></p><p>Trados Studio 2017</p><p><strong>2.Installing</strong></p><p>You can install Wordfast TXML as a supported file type, either directly within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore or alternatively you can download and install from the RWS AppStore&nbsp;</p><p><strong>3.Access</strong></p><p>Once installed, TMXL can be accessed in various ways:</p><ul><li>Trados Studio Tell Me Feature</li><li>Default Path: Go to File -&gt; Options -&gt; File Types</li><li>New Project: Create New Project -&gt; File Type Identifier Settings</li></ul><p class=\"ql-indent-1\">Tip: Update your selected project template with your new file type preference, after completing the project preparation tasks. Alternatively you can update Project Templates by going to File -&gt; Setup -&gt; Project Templates, select your project template to edit and then manage your list of supported file types.</p><ul><li>Existing Project Selected: Project Settings -&gt; File Types</li></ul><p class=\"ql-indent-1\">Tip: You can add new files to an existing project, as long as the project is recognised as a Studio project and not a Single file project. translation"},{"id":6,"name":"Reference"}]},{"id":174,"downloadCount":12602,"paidFor":false,"updatedAt":"2025-01-13T06:59:36.383985Z","name":"Google Cloud Translation Provider","description":"<p>Google Cloud Translation Provider is a dedicated plugin that integrates directly into Trados Studio while making use of all the capabilities supported by Google Cloud Translation, including:</p><p><br></p><ul><li>Basic and Advanced authentication</li><li>Custom trained engines - AutoML</li><li>Glossary Reference</li></ul><p>Other standared features suported by this plugin include:</p><p><br></p><ul><li>Custom name reference</li><li>Resend draft and translated segments</li><li>Do not include tags in the transalted segments</li><li>Do pre lookup find/repalce</li><li>Do post lookup find/replace</li></ul><p>To learn how to use this app and for more information, please check the <strong>Documentation </strong>tab.</p><p><br></p><p><br></p>","documentation":"<p>MTEnhanced was a plugin that supported both the Microsoft and Google translation providers in one.&nbsp;Since the relese of the Google V3 API it became apparent that it was getting more and more difficult to maintain the code in this combined form.&nbsp;It was also apparent that the UI needed improving to support users who were struggling with the setup of the V3 API.&nbsp;So a decision was made to split MTEnhanced into each of the respective MT providers, now catering for a dedicated Google Cloud Translation Provider&nbsp;as explained in this wiki.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>Minimum Requirements</strong></h1><p><br></p><ul><li>Trados Studio 2022</li><li>Google Cloud Account including a Google Cloud console project.</li><li>For more information about&nbsp;<a href=\"https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/projects\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Google Cloud console projects, click here</a></li></ul><p>Google has restrictions over the number of characters that can be used,&nbsp;<a href=\"https://cloud.google.com/translate/quotas\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">which can be seen here</a></p><h1><strong>How to find the plugin?</strong></h1><p><br></p><p>You can install the Google Cloud Translator Provider either from:</p><ul><li><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/174/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Trados AppStore</a></li><li>From within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore</li></ul><p>Provided you have successfully installed the Google Cloud Translator Provider plugin, you can access it within Trados Studio via:</p><ul><li>Tell Me</li><li>Default settings under File – Options – Language Pairs – Translation Memory and Automation</li><li>Or as part of your project settings.</li></ul><h1><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675294200146v1.png\" alt=\" \"></h1><h1><br></h1><h1><strong>Authentication</strong></h1><p><br></p><p>Google supports 2 editions, Basic and Advanced. These options reflect the range of capabilities offered by Google Cloud Translate when using their services and based on your requirements, how you authenticate when accessing these services through the pulgin.</p><ul><li>V2 Basic Translation – API Key</li><li>V3 Advanced Translation - JSON</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675294222425v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h2><br></h2><h2>Further information</h2><p><br></p><p>For more information about the differences between the Basic and Advanced options please&nbsp;<a href=\"https://cloud.google.com/translate/docs/editions\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">see here</a>&nbsp;on the Google website.&nbsp;</p><p>Authentication credentials for both V2 Basic Translation and V3 Advanced, can be obtained within your Google Cloud account under APIs and Services&nbsp;<a href=\"https://console.cloud.google.com/apis\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">here</a>.&nbsp;</p><h1><strong>Settings</strong></h1><p><br></p><p>There are 2 newly added settings</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675294375914v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><br></p><p><strong>1. Use Custom name for this provider</strong></p><p><br></p><p>This allows for you to associate a friendly name, which may help in the event you have access to multiple API keys or wish to work across several custom models.</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675294395451v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><br></p><p><strong>2. JSON location</strong></p><p><br></p><p>V3 – Advanced Translation requires direct access to a valid JSON file which can now be supported by referencing a URL. It does however require to be downloaded and the path can be user defined by browsing</p><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675326412587v1.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><h1><strong>Reference Files:</strong></h1><p><br></p><p>Log files can be located by browsing to&nbsp;C:\\Users\\[username]\\AppData\\Roaming\\Trados AppStore\\Google Cloud TP</p><p><br></p><p><br></p><h1>2 Basic Translation</h1><h1>Creating API Key</h1><p>Your API keys are tied to a specific Google Cloud Project.&nbsp;<a href=\"https://console.cloud.google.com/cloud-resource-manager\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Click here to see your list of projects</a>.</p><p>If you don’t already have an API Key, you will need to create one by following these steps:</p><ol><li><a href=\"https://console.cloud.google.com/apis\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Locate API and Services&nbsp;</a>Ensure you have the correct project – Example API ProjectClick on Create CredentialsSpecify API Key</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1540x582/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675326805940v1.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"291\" width=\"770\"></p><p>Initially the name used for reference will be numerical. The actual key will contain a sequence of numbers and letters. Once they key is generated you may notice an orange warning as seen below.&nbsp;By default API keys are unrestricted as marked by an orange warning as seen below. The green circle tick implies restrictions have been imposed.&nbsp;To prevent unauthorised use, Google recommends that you restrict where and for which APIs it can be used.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1346x398/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675326836735v2.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"199\" width=\"672\"></p><p>On the left you can:</p><ul><li>Show your key</li><li>Edit your API key</li><li>Delete your API Key</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1368x310/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675326850514v3.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"155\" width=\"683\"></p><p>If you edit your key you can do any of the following</p><ul><li>Give it a friendly name</li><li>Place restrictions namely: Application and API</li><li>View Creation Date</li><li>Access API Key</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1256x736/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675326866751v4.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"368\" width=\"627\"></p><h1>Authentication within Trados Studio</h1><p>When using V2 Basic Translation, all that is required is your API key.&nbsp;</p><p>For future Trados Studio projects you can enable the feature “Remember key” to eliminate ongoing reliance in knowing your API key.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1220x454/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675326911184v5.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"227\" width=\"610\"></p><p><br></p><p><br></p><h1>3 Advanced Translation</h1><p>To use V3 Advanced Translation in Trados Studio, a user must have direct access to a valid JSON file.</p><p>&nbsp;JSON files are dependent on:</p><ul><li>A specific Google Cloud Project</li><li>A Service Account</li></ul><p>Service Account used needs to be linked to a bucket. Buckets  manage your custom models and glossaries. You can access your bucket&nbsp;<a href=\"https://console.cloud.google.com/storage/browser\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">by accessing your Cloud Storage</a></p><p>If you want to access your custom models and glossaries in Trados Studio, you need to authenticate using JSON files</p><h1><strong>Generating JSON files</strong></h1><p>To download a JSON file, follow these steps:</p><ol><li>Locate&nbsp;<a href=\"https://console.cloud.google.com/apis\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">API and Services</a>Ensure you have the correct project – Example Google Translate V3See list of Service AccountsClick on your chosen service account email<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/2124x730/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327025974v1.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"365\" width=\"1061\"></li><li>Access Keys tab<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/756x820/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327075548v2.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"609\" width=\"377\"></li><li>Select Add Key then JSON<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/302x206/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327184052v3.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"128\" width=\"150\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/698x450/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327192165v4.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"284\" width=\"348\"></li></ol><p>Once you see this popup, check your browser’s download location for the JSON file</p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/916x236/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327249267v5.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"118\" width=\"457\"></p><h1>Creating a Service Account</h1><p>If you don’t have a service account listed you can create one by clicking on Create Credentials and selecting Service Accounts</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327290915v6.png_2D00_1800x1200.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h1>Accessing your Data Sets</h1><p>In order to access&nbsp;the datasets for your custom translation models within Google Translate,&nbsp;<a href=\"https://console.cloud.google.com/translation/datasets\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">click here</a></p><h1><strong>Authenticating in Trados Studio</strong></h1><p>You can reference your JSON file either by:</p><ol><li>Drag/Drop Or Browse (pre-existing download)URL</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1144x470/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327323843v7.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"235\" width=\"571\"></p><p><strong>1. Drag/Drop Or Browse (pre-existing download)</strong></p><p>To upload your JSON file, use any of these supported methods:</p><ul><li>Drag &amp; Drop</li><li>Browse</li><li>UNC path</li></ul><p>Once your JSON file has validated, all the features supported by that JSON such as location, AutoML and glossaries are made available.</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/968x1130/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327344028v8.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"565\" width=\"483\"></p><p>If you selected the wrong JSON file, simply reattempt to make reference to another JSON file.</p><p>&nbsp;<strong>2.&nbsp;URL</strong></p><p>This is ideal when sharing a JSON file among a team of people.</p><p>By enabling URL you can pate your sharing link to read the JSON file.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1100x452/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327445870v9.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"226\" width=\"550\"></p><p>By validating the URL, you are able to select&nbsp;the range of supported capabilities that fall part of the JSON parameters</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/764x890/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327462733v10.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"445\" width=\"381\"></p><p>If the wrong URL was reference, simply&nbsp;paste in a new link. Upon confirming your JSON file, it then gets downloaded locally to the user.</p><p>Download location is based on user preference and managed via the settings.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/986x540/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327558831v11.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"270\" width=\"492\"></p><p>NOTE: Dropbox as a storage and sharing platform is supported and tested, out the box.</p><h1><strong>Updating Settings Mid Project</strong></h1><p>Once you have validated and confirmed your JSON file it will be added to your Trados Studio Project.</p><p>However you can access the settings at any point</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327580696v12.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Upon clicking on settings you get a summary of the project details that cant be amended.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/712x822/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327659187v13.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"411\" width=\"355\"></p><p>By clicking on provider, you are taken back to the features that V3 Advanced Translations supports such as your glossaries and custom models that can be amended mid project and applied to your Trados Studio Project</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/954x810/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675327690125v14.png\" alt=\" \" height=\"405\" width=\"476\"></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/174/versions/1988","changelog":{"Trados Studio - The root cause was that tags were being encoded as HTML-style elements (e.g. &lt;0tg5&gt;, ) and sent with MimeType = text/html, causing Google&#39;s NMT engine to interpret them as real HTML markup and reorder or absorb them into the translated output. Tags are now encoded as plain-text bracket tokens (e.g. [[s5]], [[e5]], [[x5]]) and sent as text/plain, which Google&#39;s engine treats as opaque placeholders and preserves reliably. This ensures translated segments are correctly reconstructed with their original Trados tags in the right positions.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, to install and use the Google Machine Translation App:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>Users should have an active Trados account.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Users should have necessary permissions on Trados to (JSON):</strong></em>  </p>\r\n<p> <em><strong>Step 1</strong></em>: Access Your Google Cloud Platform Account<br> Log in to your Google Cloud Platform account. If you don’t have one, create it at <a href=\"https://cloud.google.com/\">https://cloud.google.com/</a>.   </p>\r\n<p> <em><strong>Step 2</strong></em>: Get the Google Translate API Key<br> a. Create a key for the service account:<br> •    In the list of service accounts, locate the one you just created. Click the three vertical dots on the right and select “Manage keys”.   </p>\r\n<p>  <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/a5fb5539-d925-40a0-8e89-739f58aac1fc/Resources/A%202.4%20SA%20Key.png\" alt=\"SA Key\"></p>\r\n<p> •    Click “ADD KEY” and then “Create new key”.   </p>\r\n<p>  <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/a5fb5539-d925-40a0-8e89-739f58aac1fc/Resources/A%202.5%20Create%20Key.png\" alt=\"Create Key\"></p>\r\n<p> •    Select the key type “JSON” and click “CREATE”. You may need to configure billing information.   </p>\r\n<p>  <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/a5fb5539-d925-40a0-8e89-739f58aac1fc/Resources/A%203.2%20Select%20API.png\" alt=\"Select API\"></p>\r\n<p> e. GroupShare server with valid credentials for interaction.</li><li><strong>Installation:</strong></li><li>Download the toolkit files.</li><li>Unblock the downloaded files as needed.</li><li>Create necessary directories in your PowerShell user folder, such as Documents\\PowerShell\\Modules.</li><li>Copy the relevant script and module files to the designated directories.</li></ul><p><strong>Configuration:</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Server Configuration:</strong></li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Set the $server parameter in each module's .psm1 file or specify the server details when importing the modules using the -ArgumentList parameter.</li><li><strong>Authentication:</strong></li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Configure the Sample_Roundtrip.ps1 script or other scripts with your GroupShare server URL and credentials (username and password) for authentication.</li></ul><p><strong>Running Scripts:</strong></p><ul><li>Open PowerShell 7.4 or higher.</li><li>Set the execution policy to Unrestricted if required (Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy Unrestricted -Scope CurrentUser).</li><li>Navigate to the folder where the script (e.g., Sample_Roundtrip.ps1) is stored.</li><li>Run the script using .\\Sample_Roundtrip.ps1.</li></ul><p><strong>Module Usage:</strong></p><ul><li>Import individual modules or load them all using:</li></ul><pre class=\"ql-syntax\" spellcheck=\"false\">powershell\r\nCopy code\r\nImport-Module -Name AuthenticationHelper -ArgumentList \"https://your.groupshare.server/uri\"\r\n</pre><ul><li>Once imported, functions within each module can be executed for automating various GroupShare operations.</li></ul><p><strong>Help &amp; Troubleshooting:</strong></p><ul><li>Use the Get-Help cmdlet to access detailed documentation for each function after the module is imported.</li><li>Ensure all file paths are correctly configured, and the required modules are available in the Documents\\PowerShell\\Modules directory.</li></ul><p>For full documentation and detailed instructions, please refer to the <a Create dictionary</p><p>If an existing dictionary already exists for that language, an informative message will be displayed with possibility to override the dictionary, otherwise the dictionary will be created and an informative message will be displayed.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0005.pastedimage1540971483535v3.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/800x600/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0005.pastedimage1540971483535v3.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5611.pastedimage1540971414171v2.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/800x600/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5611.pastedimage1540971414171v2.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>4. Delete dictionary</p><p>User has possibility to select an existing dictionary from the list which displays all available Studio dictionaries and delete it.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6064.pastedimage1540971656948v1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/800x600/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6064.pastedimage1540971656948v1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3223.pastedimage1540971576137v5.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/800x600/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3223.pastedimage1540971576137v5.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>5. Restore the deleted dictionary</p><p>User is able to restore the deleted dictionary, by selecting a dictionary from the deleted list and press on \"Restore\" button.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4111.pastedimage1540971204370v3.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" process is done, an informative message is displayed.</p><p><a Terminology","description":"<p><strong>IATE (Interactive Terminology for Europe)</strong> is the EU&#39;s terminology database maintained by the Translation Centre for the Bodies of the European Union /Centre de traduction des organes de l&#39;Union européenne.</p>\r\n<p>At the end of 2018, IATE published their API to make it possible for developers to access the terminology using their own tools.</p>\r\n<p>After running a Beta version for several months, the RWS AppStore Team are pleased to make available an integration to IATE through Trados Studio using the terminology provider API.</p>\r\n<p><em><strong><u>Features</u></strong></em></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>term recognition as you are translating</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>autosuggest functionality</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Terminology previews</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>right-click search</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h3>Introduction</h3>\r\n<p>The plugin represents an integration with IATE European Union terminology and is available for Studio 2017* Studio 2019 and later. Admin rights are required to install this plugin.</p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<p><strong>Trados Studio 2019 and Trados Studio 2021</strong>\u00A0users, who have\u00A0installed the****<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/translationproductivity/w/customer-experience/5523/changelog-iate-real-time-terminology\"><strong>latest version of the plugin</strong></a>\u00A0(25th May 2021 or later) do\u00A0<strong>not</strong>\u00A0need to install SQL Server Express LocalDB and can install the plugin directly.</p>\r\n<p>If you are using Trados Studio 2017*** or earlier releases of the IATE plugin, please follow the instructions and install\u00A0SQL Server Express LocalDB, before installing the plugin!</p>\r\n<p>***Only the latest versions for Trados Studio  are fully supported.***</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=42299\">https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=42299</a></p>\r\n<p>A\u00A0<em>new</em>\u00A0DB is created for\u00A0<em>each project,</em>\u00A0where the IATE Terminology provider is added.</p>\r\n<p>Each DB contains two files- .mdf and .ldf</p>\r\n<p>You will find these here - (Trados Studio 2017 users only)</p>\r\n<p>C:\\Users\\yourname\\AppData\\Roaming\\SDL Community\\IATEProviderCache</p>\r\n<p>Once IATE Terminology provider has been added to a project, we will first search the local DB to see if there is cached result.</p>\r\n<p>If there are none, only then, will we make a search to the IATE service and the search response will be added to the database.\u00A0</p>\r\n<p>Next time the user navigates to that segment, no further calls are made to IATE, but instead the results from the local DB will be shown.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Trados Studio 2019 or Trados Studio 2021</strong>\u00A0users will find the temporary storage and cache folders here\u00A0</p>\r\n<p>C:\\Users\\username\\AppData\\Roaming\\RWS AppStore\\IATETerminologyProvider</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3051.pastedimage1621943371181v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<h3>Key Features</h3>\r\n<p>When a user adds the IATE Terminology Provider to the project, the following settings can be configured for the search process:</p>\r\n<p>\u00A01.\u00A0<strong>Domain:</strong>\u00A0one or multiple domain(s) can be selected. The search will return terms from IATE, based on the selected domain(s).</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>S<em>electing\u00A0multiple domains will slow down the search</em>- so it&#39;s advised to select only relevant domains.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>\u00A02.<strong>Term Type:</strong>\u00A0if no option is selected, the IATE search will return results for all kind of term types.\u00A0</p>\r\n<p>\u00A03.\u00A0<strong>Clear local cache</strong>- is\u00A0additional functionality, that now allows you to clear the database from the project.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3582.pastedimage1621945129935v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Termbase Search</strong></p>\r\n<p>The results are returned from the IATE terminology database, based on the entered term, project languages and settings.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Termbase Viewer</strong></p>\r\n<p>The following information can be seen directly in the Termbase Viewer by clicking right on the term-&gt; View term details. The first group of information it is representative for the source language, and the second group, for target language:\u00A0- language flag + language name.\u00A0</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>searched term</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>term type</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>term domain</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>term subdomain (if exists for the specified term)</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>term definition\u00A0(if exists)</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Besides the details displayed through &quot;View term details&quot; option, the Termbase Viewer shows a list of all returned searched results (user can navigate through the list and based of the term selection, the source and target languages details will be displayed near)</p>\r\n<p>User has 2 options to navigate on the IATE official website and check for more term details following the next steps:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>select term(s) in the source/target segment</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>right-click on term -&gt; select &quot;Search IATE(all) or &quot;Search IATE (source and target only)</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>*For &quot;Search IATE (all), the website will return term details for the Studio active document source language and all available IATE target languages.</p>\r\n<p>*For &quot;Search IATE (source and target only), the website will return term details for Studio active document source and target language.</p>\r\n<p>The navigation options works in two-way mode: users can select term(s) either from the source segment side, either from the target segment side.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1193x559/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4405.pastedimage1545049415376v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>The IATE web site, after selecting &quot;Search IATE(all)&quot; option, will get opened in the &quot;Search Results Viewer&quot; (it\u00A0can also be started from the ribbon in the &quot;View&quot; tab).</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1920x1040/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0121.pastedimage1563435581010v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Studio TellMe</strong></p>\r\n<p>TellMe feature is integrated with IATE provider and makes available 3 actions:</p>\r\n<p>\u00A01. Community Support action: type words iate/iate community/iate support</p>\r\n<p>\u00A02. Store action: type words iate/iate store/iate download</p>\r\n<p>\u00A03. Contact action: type words iate/iate contact/iate official/iate website/iate web search</p>\r\n<p><strong>Logging</strong></p>\r\n<p>The application logs information about the flow, which is useful to identify\u00A0issues that might occur.</p>\r\n<p>The\u00A0<strong>IATEProviderLogs.txt</strong>\u00A0file can be found at the following location:\u00A0</p>\r\n<p><strong>C:\\Users{userName}\\AppData\\Roaming\\SDL\\IATEProviderLogs\\IATEProviderLogs.txt.</strong></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, Provider","description":"<p><strong>InterpretBank </strong>is a specialised tool crafted for professional interpreters, offering robust features for managing and accessing terminology efficiently. Tailored to meet interpreters' needs, it facilitates seamless preparation and enhances performance during assignments. Now, as a supported term provider in Trados Studio, InterpretBank can be fully integrated with your localisation workflows.</p><p>With the InterpretBank plugin for Trados Studio, you gain direct access to your InterpretBank database as a terminology provider, enabling intuitive term recognition and management directly within your translation environment. This integration allows you to leverage InterpretBank’s powerful terminology functionalities while translating in Trados Studio, replicating many features available in the InterpretBank application itself. For details on setting up and using this plugin, please refer to the Documentation tab.</p><p>For more information about the InterpretBank application visit the InterpretBank website (<a href=\"https://interpretbank.com/site/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://interpretbank.com/site/</a>)</p>","documentation":"<h4>Minimum Requirements</h4><ul><li>Trados Studio 2024</li><li>An InterpretBank database *.db</li></ul><h4>Getting Started</h4><p>You can install the plugin either via the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">RWS AppStore </a> or the Integrated AppStore within Trados Studio by going to: Add-Ins &gt; RWS AppStore</p><p>Once installed, InterpretBank can easily be accessed in various ways:</p><p>1.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trados Studio Tell Me Feature</p><p>2.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Add-Ins tab</p><p>3.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Short Cuts: File &gt; Options &gt; Keyboard Shortcuts</p><h4>Configure Database</h4><p>Configuring your InterpretBank database allows you to continuously manage various aspects:</p><h5>1. For customers with Language Weaver Auto-Adaptive or GenLP models.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: Contents</h2>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><a href=\"#intro_section_id\">Introduction</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#features_section_id\">Key Features</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#install_section_id\">Installation</a><br>  3.1. <a href=\"#prerequisites\">Technical Requirements</a><br>  3.2. <a href=\"#new-installation\">Step-by-Step Installation</a>  </li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#updates_section_id\">Updates</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#setup_section_id\">Configuration and Setup</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#usingapp_section_id\">Using the App</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#entitlement_section_id\">Entitlement</a></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"intro_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>The LW Auto-Adaptive Model Updater App is an automated custom workflow task that will send updated segments from Trados project to Language Weaver for model adaptation (This applies to customers with Language Weaver Auto-Adaptive or GenLP models).</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"features_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>Following are the core features of the app:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Installs on the <strong>Trados</strong> platform</li>\r\n<li>Integrates seamlessly with <strong>Language Weaver (LW)</strong>  </li>\r\n<li>Automates project-level bilingual data transfer to LW once configured</li>\r\n<li>Allows the user to configure bilingual data sync with all LW language pairs in the Trados project</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"install_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Installation Guide</h2>\r\n<p><a id=\"prerequisites\"></a></p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<p>The following are the prerequisites to install and use the LW Auto-Adaptive Model Updater App:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Users should have an active Trados account.</li>\r\n<li>Users should have an active LW account.</li>\r\n<li>Users should have necessary permissions on Trados to install apps as well as create/configure workflow templates.</li>\r\n<li>Users should have the needed permissions on LW to create Auto-Adaptive or GenLP models.</li>\r\n<li>Users should have available API credentials from LW or permissions to create such credentials within LW.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><a id=\"new-installation\"></a></p>\r\n<h3>Step-by-Step Installation</h3>\r\n<h4>Option 1: Install via RWS AppStore</h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Visit <strong><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/\">RWS AppStore</a></strong></li>\r\n<li>Search for <strong>&quot;LW Auto-Adaptive Model Updater&quot;</strong></li>\r\n<li>Sign in to your <strong>Trados account</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>Install</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h4>Option 2: Install via Trados Account</h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Login</strong> to <strong>Trados</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click on <strong>Account Settings</strong></li>\r\n<li>Navigate to <strong>RWS AppStore</strong></li>\r\n<li>Locate <strong>LW Auto-Adaptive Model Updater</strong> under <strong>Apps</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>Install</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Install_1.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\">   </p>\r\n<p>Once installed, enter the <strong>LW Client ID and Secret Key</strong> and click  <strong>Complete</strong>.   </p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Config_1.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\">  </p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"updates_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Updates</h2>\r\n<p>If an update is available, a <strong>notification</strong> will be sent via email or displayed in <strong>Trados notifications</strong>.\r\nTo check if any update to app is available, follow the steps below:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>Login to Trados  </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Click the Account Settings icon in the top-right corner  </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/update_settings.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Choose “RWS AppStore”.  </p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/update_options.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Under <strong>Installed Apps</strong>, it will show either <strong>Up to Date</strong> or <strong>Update Available</strong>.  </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>If an update is available, click on the app and select <strong>Update</strong>.  </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/update_final_1.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"setup_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Configuration and Setup</h2>\r\n<p>Following are the steps to configure the application:</p>\r\n<h3>Workflow Template Setup</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>After application is installed, go to <strong>Resources</strong> -&gt; <strong>Workflows</strong> -&gt; Click on <strong>Workflow Templates</strong>.  </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Select any existing Workflow Template and click on Copy to duplicate it.<br> &gt; Alternatively, you can click on <strong>New Workflow Template</strong> button.    </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_WT.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Enter appropriate Name, Description and select Location. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_Details.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It is recommended to place the app after <strong>Translation Memory Update</strong> in FINALIZE phase. Click on three dots on the <strong>done</strong> outcome of  <strong>Translation Memory Update</strong>.</p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_Dots.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Click on <strong>Change Transition</strong>.</p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_Transition.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It will open a pop-up. Under <strong>Automated Tasks</strong>, search for the app name &quot;LW Auto-Adaptive Model Updater&quot;. Click on the App.</p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_Search_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It will place the &quot;LW Auto-Adaptive Model Updater&quot; app after <strong>Translation Memory Update</strong>. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_Place_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Click on plus icon, it will open the same pop-up.  Under <strong>Automated Tasks</strong>, search for the app name <strong>Target File Generation</strong>. Click on the App with <strong>Existing</strong> text after its name. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_NextApp.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It will complete the Workflow Template changes. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_Complete_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Now, Click on <strong>Configure</strong> option of <strong>LW Auto-Adaptive Model Updater</strong> app. It will open the configuration settings on the right.<br>Enter <strong>Language Pair Mapping JSON</strong>: It is the mapping of source and target languages (TE language codes) with the Language Weaver model name in JSON format. Refer <a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/en-US/language-weaver-791593/language-pair-adaptation-900589\">LW Documentation Center</a><br>Example:</p>\r\n<pre><code>[\r\n  {\r\n     &quot;SourceLanguage&quot;: &quot;en-US&quot;,\r\n     &quot;TargetLanguage&quot;: &quot;de-DE&quot;,\r\n     &quot;ModelName&quot;: &quot;engger_autoadaptive-107&quot;\r\n  },\r\n  {\r\n     &quot;SourceLanguage&quot;: &quot;en-US&quot;,\r\n     &quot;TargetLanguage&quot;: &quot;fr-FR&quot;,\r\n     &quot;ModelName&quot;: &quot;engfra_autoadaptive-x-108&quot;\r\n  }\r\n]\r\n</code></pre>\r\n<p>Enter <strong>Fuzzy match exclusion threshold</strong>: It will be a numeric value between 0 and 100. Segments at or above the provided threshold value will not be included in the feed.<br>Select <strong>Excludable from the Workflow?</strong>: Make sure that option &quot;Enabled by default, but excludable&quot; is selected.  </p>\r\n<p>Save the configuration.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_Configure_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Click on <strong>Create &amp; Activate</strong>. </p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Setup_Activate_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h3>Workflow Setup</h3>\r\n<p>Go to <strong>Resources</strong> -&gt; <strong>Workflows</strong>, and ensure that the translation Workflow uses the previously set up Workflow Template.  </p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"usingapp_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Usage Instructions</h2>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>When any <strong>Trados</strong> project reaches the <strong>FINALIZE</strong> stage,  <strong>LW Auto-Adaptive Model Updater</strong> app will run (as per workflow order) and it will create the TMX (Translation Memory eXchange format) file with Segments that meet the configured criteria &amp; threshold. </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>TMX file will be sent to the Language Weaver auto-adaptive language pair as defined in the configuration step, which can be verified in the <strong>Language Weaver</strong> portal at Settings&gt;Language Pairs&gt;{your defined auto-adaptive or generative language pair}. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/67de8bf3-bddc-4692-8a15-89fd5b2673a3/Resources/Usage_TMXList.png\" alt=\"UsingApp\"></p>\r\n<p> &gt; TMX (Translation Memory eXchange) is a standard format for storing bilingual translation units.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>TMX file name will follow the pattern:<br> TM_[Source Language Code]_[Target Language Code]_[Trados Task ID].tmx<br> <strong>Example</strong>: TM_en-GB_de-DE_68ad7740c7425706f0beaa56.tmx</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>In TMX file, header would include Trados Tenant ID, Project ID, Name and Task ID.<br> Body would include Segment Number,  Source &amp; Target Language Codes and Segment Text.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"entitlement_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Entitlement</h2>\r\n<p>This app does not require additional entitlement for use.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n not longer being maintained. Please use the </strong><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/240\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>Language Weaver Provider</strong></a><strong> app if you want to rely on the latest supported features and enhancements </strong></p><p><br></p><p>Language Weaver Cloud is the cloud deployment of the award-winning RWS Machine Translation software that is made for enterprises. It combines RWS's Neural Machine Translation (NMT) capabilities with Linguistic AI™ to remove language barriers in content-intensive business functions.</p><p><br></p><p>Language Weaver Cloud addresses the needs that businesses have for a reliable, cloud-based, enterprise-grade machine translation solution with the assurance of content privacy.</p><p><br></p><p>Language Weaver Cloud is powered by RWS's state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms and supports a large number of languages.</p><p><br></p><p><strong><em>Notes on authentication:</em></strong>The Client ID and Secret can be retrieved by an admin user from the Language Weaver Cloud user interface. The Username and Password are the same as the RWS ID you use for the Language Weaver Cloud user interface.</p>","documentation":"<h2>Overview</h2><p>Language Weaver is RWS's state-of-the-art secure machine translation cloud product for enterprises with various paid subscription models.</p><h2>Setup</h2><h3>Language Weaver&nbsp;Plugin</h3><ul><li>You can download the Language Weaver plugin using the&nbsp;RWS App Store Integration from within Studio 2021 (or later), or from the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/sdl-machine-translation-cloud/941/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1700034807414.1700037722877.41&amp;__hssc=110027290.2.1700037722877&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">RWS AppStore</a>&nbsp;if you have an earlier version of Studio</li></ul><p>If you have downloaded from the RWS AppStore, double-click the application, to start the installation and follow the instructions. You should have ADMIN rights on your computer to install.</p><p><em>(You may need to move the plugin from your downloads' folder to your desktop, as some browsers prevent installation directly from the download's folder)</em></p><p><br></p><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p><p>To add the provider to your project</p><ol><li>Select&nbsp;<strong>Project Settings</strong>&nbsp;-&gt;&nbsp;<strong>Language Pairs</strong>-&gt;&nbsp;<strong>Translation Memory and Automated Translation</strong>Click on the button \"<strong>Use...</strong>\" and select \"<strong>Language Weaver provider</strong>\" from the drop-down list</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7128.pastedimage1649927645654v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h3>Authentication</h3><p><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Language Weaver&nbsp;provides various methods for authenticating your credentials.&nbsp;Select the appropriate authentication type and provide the data that is required.</span></p><h4><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Authentication Type</span></h4><ul><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Single Sign-On - RWS Language Cloud</span></li><li><em style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</em><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Inherits the&nbsp;SSO credential and 'Sign In' workflow from Studio</span></li><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">User credentials -&nbsp;Language Weaver&nbsp;</span></li><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The&nbsp;same Email and Password that you use to log into Language Weaver&nbsp;</span></li><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Client credentials -&nbsp;Language Weaver ClientID and secret</span></li><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;The Client ID and Client Secret can be retrieved by an admin user from&nbsp;Language Weaver</span></li></ul><p><span style=\"background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251); color: rgb(71, 85, 97);\">Note:&nbsp;For the user and client credentials type of authentication, you need to specify the \"Account region\" you need to connect to: Europe or US.</span></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/842x644/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1679986598586v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Note: The authentication type&nbsp;</span><strong style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">\"</strong><strong>Single Sign-On\"</strong><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">&nbsp;automatically inherits the SSO (i.e. Single Sign-On) credential and 'Sign In' workflow from Studio, so if you are already signed in, then that credential can be reused to authenticate you against Language Weaver.</span></p><p>&nbsp;<strong>Settings</strong></p><p>To access the provider settings, select the \"<strong>Language Weaver&nbsp;provider</strong>\" from the project settings and click on the button \"<strong>Settings</strong>\"</p><p>The entries presented in the settings reflect the project language pairs, and are automatically mapped to the&nbsp;Language Weaver languages via the \"<strong>Language Mapping</strong>\" interface.&nbsp;</p><p>When loading the provider for the first time, the generic model will be selected for each of the language pairs.&nbsp;To choose a different model and/or dictionary, simply select them from the available options in drop-down menus appropriately.</p><p>The models and dictionaries for each language pairs will reflect what is available from your subscription.</p><p><strong>Note:</strong>&nbsp;by default the&nbsp;<strong>Re-send draft and translated segments</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>Enable \"Rate Translations\"</strong>&nbsp;are checked.</p><p>To learn more about about the above configurations, click on the link and review the \"Rate translations\" wiki.&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Additional features:</strong></p><p>(available for Trados Studio 2019 and later)</p><ul><li><strong>Add Term to&nbsp;Language Weaver Dictionary</strong></li></ul><p>Now it is possible with a simple&nbsp;<em>right click</em>&nbsp;(in Editor view) to add a term to your&nbsp;Language Weaver Dictionary.</p><p>To do this, go to the Project Settings and select the required dictionary depending on the language pair selected.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0385.pastedimage1615391878138v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Return to the Project, select the source term and equivalent target term then right click on the source term and 'Add Term to&nbsp;Language Weaver&nbsp;Dictionary'</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1634x560/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1679988522259v6.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Tell Me</strong></p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">The&nbsp;SDL Machine Translation&nbsp;(soon to become Language Weaver) version compatible with Studio 2019 allows users to access the following actions using TellMe feature from Studio:</span></p><ul><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Community Support</span></li><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Machine Translation Cloud contact</span></li><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Wikipedia documentation</span></li><li><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Download the plugin from AppStore</span></li></ul><h1>Language Mapping</h1><h2>Overview</h2><p>The Language Mapping window displays all of the available languages from SDL MT Cloud, mapped to the Studio languages.&nbsp;The MT Code and/or MT Code (locale) can be modified to align with the users subscription settings, if required.</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/818x422/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2772.2020_5F00_04_5F00_08_5F00_16_5F00_35_5F00_03_5F00_SDL_5F00_Trados_5F00_Studio_5F00_SDL_5F00_MT_5F00_Cloud_5F00_Dev_5F00_Testing.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h3>How to load the Language Mappings window</h3><p>You can access the Language Mapping window from either the Add-Ins ribbon or directly from the provider settings</p><h4>Studio Add-Ins ribbon</h4><ul><li>Select the Studio Add-In Ribbon</li><li>Select the button Language Mapping from the SDL MT Cloud group</li><li><br></li><li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/352x181/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7357.2020_5F00_04_5F00_08_5F00_16_5F00_17_5F00_46_5F00_SDL_5F00_Trados_5F00_Studio_5F00_SDL_5F00_MT_5F00_Cloud_5F00_Dev_5F00_Testing.png\" alt=\" \"></li></ul><h4>Provider Settings</h4><ul><li>From the&nbsp;<strong>SDL Machine Translation Cloud provider</strong>&nbsp;settings, select the button \"<strong>View Language Mapping</strong>\"</li></ul><h1>Rating Translations</h1><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/70x70/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6012.rating.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><br></p><p><strong>Rate-It&nbsp;Language Weaver</strong></p><p>A new feature of&nbsp;<strong>Language Weaver</strong>&nbsp;is the&nbsp;<strong>Rate Translation</strong>&nbsp;(RateIT) option, only available with Trados Studio 2021 (SR1) and above.</p><p>Our aim with this additional functionality, it to continually improve our Language Weaver experience.&nbsp;So this feature&nbsp;provides similar functionality for providing feedback on the quality of the translations to the tools that&nbsp;are&nbsp;available when working via the browser at&nbsp;<a href=\"https://portal.languageweaver.com/translate\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://portal.languageweaver.com/translate</a>&nbsp;.</p><p><strong>What has changed?</strong></p><p>Previously, when you have used machine translation, once you have sent the source for translation and received the target translation RWS will automatically discard the source translation request and this will not be used at all.&nbsp;&nbsp;With the updated plugin, if you choose to use the added functionality, your feedback will be used by for the sole purpose of improving the baseline Language Weaver services for all users.</p><p>This functionality has always been available when using&nbsp;<a href=\"https://translate.sdl.com/translate?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1700037722877.1700040862935.42&amp;__hssc=110027290.7.1700040862935&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Language Weaver&nbsp;</a>,&nbsp;we have simply added this&nbsp;<em>optional functionality</em>&nbsp;for Trados Studio users.</p><p><strong>Installation</strong></p><p>After updating Studio 2021 to SR1 and installing the updated Language Weaver plugin (version 4.2.1.2) via the integrated AppStore feature, you will see the new feature:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1184.pastedimage1607083159091v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Enable ‘Rate Translations’ is enabled by default.</p><p><em>If you do not wish to use the functionality- simply untick the box at any time.</em></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8156.pastedimage1607083230766v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>If you can't see the feature when you open a file in the Studio Editor you need to go to the&nbsp;<strong><em>View -&gt; Information</em></strong>&nbsp;menu in the ribbon and click on the icon for&nbsp;<strong><em>Language Weaver Rate Translations</em></strong>:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8765.pastedimage1607101849883v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This will add the new view to the bottom of your \"page\" where you can then position it wherever you prefer:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4846.pastedimage1607101935707v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The view has been designed to be as thin as possible so it doesn't take up too much screen estate.&nbsp;So you may prefer it here for example as it's nearer to where the TM results are coming from:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4555.pastedimage1607102095757v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Options</strong></p><p><strong>AutoSend Feedback</strong></p><p>If this option is enabled, feedback is only sent when the segment is confirmed and as long as a change was made to the machine translation.&nbsp;There is a also a useful feature built in to show you exactly what information is being sent, and you can verify this using&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/developers-more/developers/language-developers/w/wiki/5070/05-what-is-fiddler-and-how-to-use-it\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Fiddler</a>.&nbsp;For example, if there are no changes to the machine translation and if you don't rate the translation in any way, then you'll see this when you hover over the \"Send Feedback\" button:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4721.pastedimage1607111237728v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>If you do make a change then you'll see something like this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8623.pastedimage1607111430542v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>It's also worth noting that your userID and accountID is also sent back which means we retain the history of all the feedback you provide and it's possible for you to see this in your&nbsp;<a href=\"https://translate.sdl.com/translate?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1700037722877.1700040862935.42&amp;__hssc=110027290.7.1700040862935&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Language Weaver account:</a></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0066.pastedimage1607111776811v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Once in here you can also edit or delete any specific piece of feedback you have provided.&nbsp;So there is complete transparency for you when you choose to provide feedback to help us improve the machine translation engines:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0131.pastedimage1607111968869v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Feedback Options</strong></p><p>You can also choose to only send feedback manually when you feel the need by disabling the&nbsp;<strong>Auto Send Feedback</strong>&nbsp;checkbox shown above.&nbsp;But irrespective of whether this is enabled or not you can always add additional information by simply rating the translation (up to 5 stars) or also selecting one or more of the available categories, and adding a comment.&nbsp;All of this information will then be sent to your account when you click on the&nbsp;<strong>Send feedback</strong>&nbsp;button and then confirm the segment:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5381.pastedimage1607112560911v5.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The Edge","description":"<p><strong>This app is not longer being maintained. Please use the </strong><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/240\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>Language Weaver Provider</strong></a><strong> app if you want to rely on the latest supported features and enhancements </strong></p><p><br></p><p>This plugin allows you to retrieve translations from your corporate approved secured Language Weaver Edge.</p><p>Increasing concerns over data privacy laws, regulatory compliance, and exposure of corporate intellectual property require machine translation inside the corporate network.</p><p>Because it can be deployed on-premises or private cloud, Language Weaver Edge offers a secure, client- managed MT service for enterprise users, mitigating the risks that users may inadvertently be exposing sensitive data to non-approved MT services.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>NOTE</strong>:</p><p>After entering your Credentials (Authentication), please click <strong>'enter'</strong> on your keyboard to enable OK to proceed.</p><p><br></p><p>Language Weaver cloud provider and Language Weaver Edge form the deployment-agnostic Language Weaver solution for enterprise users with an Edge-Cloud architecture to enable seamless transition between cloud and on-premises environments.</p><p><br></p><p><br></p>","documentation":"<p>\"Language Weaver Edge (former ETS) is a machine translation solution for enterprise businesses that aim at protecting their intellectual property or sensitive, proprietary information.&nbsp;</p><p>Language Weaver Edge&nbsp;can be deployed behind an organization's secure firewall and then accessed via a web browser from any approved computer with a valid user account.</p><p>Language Weaver&nbsp;Edge&nbsp;enables organizations to securely translate multilingual content into their languages of interest at high speed and in a cost-effective way. Language Weaver&nbsp;Edge&nbsp;scalable architecture and multi-server deployment option, allows for quick and efficient translation of large volumes of content, while its optimized footprint and single-server deployment option is well-suited for a desktop environment with a low translation volume requirement. It&nbsp;can be accessed either via an intuitive&nbsp;&nbsp;Web&nbsp;GUI&nbsp;for an interactive experience or via a rich REST API for easy integration with other applications and workflows. LW&nbsp;Edge&nbsp;uses state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms and supports a large number of languages.\" (text taken from the official<a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/833475/886777/language-weaver-edge-documentation/welcome-to-the-------------language-weaver-edge-------------8-5-documentation\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" 2019, and Studio 2021 versions.</p><p>What&nbsp;Language Weaver Edge provider can do</p><p>Language Weaver Edge&nbsp;can:</p><ul><li>Facilitate security and privacy control by implementing multi-user roles and preventing exposure of corporate assets to public MT (Machine Translation).</li><li>Ensure cost-effectiveness at scale.&nbsp;ETS&nbsp;translates large volumes of content quickly, when needed, without maintenance overhead.</li><li>Be used intuitively via its browser-based GUI.&nbsp;ETS&nbsp;also benefits from workflow integration and automation via REST API.</li><li>Accelerate 0);\">!</strong><strong>&nbsp;Important:</strong>&nbsp;When trying to connect using User authentication: \"User name\" and \"Password\", after writing both fields values, the user should press the&nbsp;<strong>Enter</strong>&nbsp;key in order to be able to connect to LW Edge server and to see the \"Language Pair Mappings\" tab (the flow was decided to work this way in order to avoid too many requests which were done to the LWEdge server when entering the credentials).</p><ul><li>After the plugin was installed, it can be accessed in Studio&nbsp;from<strong>&nbsp;Project Settings -&gt; Language Pairs-&gt; Translation Memory and Automated Translation -&gt; Click on Use button to expand the options:</strong></li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1667.pastedimage1574325924926v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><ul><li>A new window will appear where the user needs to insert the Host, Port, and SDL Credentials/API Key information.&nbsp;</li><li>Window can be closed using the&nbsp;<strong>\"Cancel\"</strong>&nbsp;button or&nbsp;<strong>\"X\"</strong>&nbsp;from the window.</li><li>The second tab&nbsp;<strong>\"Language Pair Mappings\"</strong>&nbsp;is available when the user connects to a Machine Translation Edge server of version 8.1 or higher.</li><li>Options to&nbsp;<strong>\"Set as Default Host and Port\"</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>\"Save credentials\"</strong>&nbsp;are available.</li><li>To save the settings, the user needs to press the&nbsp;<strong>\"OK\"</strong>&nbsp;button.</li></ul><p>* If the&nbsp;<strong>\"Set as Default Host and Port\"</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>\"Save credentials\"&nbsp;</strong>are checked, the Host, Port and Credentials/Api Key information is saved&nbsp;when user clicks on \"<strong>Ok\"</strong>&nbsp;button<strong>.&nbsp;</strong>The configuration information is automatically<strong>&nbsp;</strong>populated in the following cases:</p><ul><li>When the user uses&nbsp;<strong>\"Settings\"</strong>&nbsp;button to open provider's window.</li><li>When the user removes and adds back the provider.</li><li>When the user creates a new project and adds the LW Edge provider to it.</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x1300/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5635.pastedimage1574326017425v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>*The&nbsp;<strong>Language Pair Mappings</strong>&nbsp;tab will be shown only after the user enters the&nbsp;information from the&nbsp;<strong>General</strong>&nbsp;Tab (the information is
