Language Weaver provides AI-powered machine translation that is secure by design, infinitely adaptive and fully scalable.&nbsp;</p><p>The app seamlessly supports both Language Weaver Cloud and Language Weaver Edge, providing flexible solutions tailored to user access and preference.</p><p>For more about RWS Language Weaver, please visit<a href=\"https://www.rws.com/language-weaver/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"> here </a></p><h2>Getting Support</h2><p>Customers with an active Trados support contract are entitled to submit a support case for any issues related to this app through the support portal (Gateway). Customers without a support contract can seek assistance by raising their queries in the AppStore forums within the RWS Community, accessible via the support tab.</p>","documentation":"<p>Language Weaver Provider supports the following functionality for both Language Weaver Cloud and Language Weaver Edge, depending on your subscription.</p><p><br></p><h3>Core Features</h3><p><br></p><h4>1. Authentication preferences</h4><p><br></p><ul><li>Singe Sign On</li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>User Credentials:&nbsp;<em>Authenticate with Username and Password if you have a desktop application where translation requests are triggered by a specific user request.</em></li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>API Key:&nbsp;<em>Authenticate with Client ID and Secret if you have machine-to-machine workflows where translations are requested as part of an automated workflow that is not triggered by a specific use request.</em></li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>Federated SSO*:&nbsp;<em>Which is used to to authenticate with your own Active Directory service</em></li></ul><p><br></p><h4>2. Machine Translation Model engines</h4><p><br></p><p>Which supports:</p><ul><li>Generic</li><li>Custom models</li><li>Quality Estimate (QE)*</li></ul><p><br></p><p>*Applicable for Language Weaver only</p><h4><br></h4><h4>3. Dictionaries</h4><p>Dictionaries are very useful when users want to enforce consistency of terms or phrases throughout all the translations for a language pair.</p><p><br></p><h4>4. Linguistic Options</h4><p>Based on what is supported in Language Weaver, linguistic options change the default translation according to available alternatives. These are defined separately for each language pair.</p><p><br></p><h4>5. Language Mapping</h4><p>Used to align unrecognised or new language codes between Language Weaver and the language codes recognised in Trados Studio.</p><p><br></p><h4>6. Settings</h4><h4><br></h4><ul><li>Friendly provider name:&nbsp;<em>A way of identifying/labelling your instance of Language Weaver within a given project, based on custom configuration</em></li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>Option to include tags in translations</li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>Retranslation of unconfirmed segments:&nbsp;<em>Control when to reapply NMT based on segment status which helps to manage your allowance based on subscription agree</em></li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>Feedback for Language Weaver translations:&nbsp;<em>Interactively provide feedback at segment level if the NMT output does not meet your requirements</em></li></ul><p><br></p><ul><li>Pre Lookup&nbsp;Find/Replace &amp; Post&nbsp;Lookup&nbsp;Find/Replace: <em>In the absence of a dictionary managed within Language Weaver, manage term substitution as part of NMT output which supports ongoing post edit&nbsp;</em></li></ul><p><br></p><h3>New features include:</h3><h4><br></h4><h4>1. Creating Language Weaver dictionary entries&nbsp;</h4><p>To create a new Language Weaver term entries within the Editor View, right click to access the menu pane and select: Creating a new dictionary term</p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/001afcbc-7d82-4af9-9ce8-312e65cb16eb/Resources/11a7ee2a-4831-4cc0-b24e-d7aa2c138da3.png?dl=1\"></p><p><br></p><h4>2. Pre &amp; Post Lookup Find/Replace</h4><p>Adapt your NMT output by uploading a special formatted XML</p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/001afcbc-7d82-4af9-9ce8-312e65cb16eb/Resources/81d91f61-c5f4-402c-a745-675d36c946a7.png?dl=1\"></p><p><br></p><h3>Instructions:</h3><h4>Enabling an active instance</h4><h3><br></h3><p>Once installed, you can enable your Language Weaver as part of a particular project via Project Settings. Or as a default resource within Trados Studio. This is done via:</p><ol><li>File -&gt; Options Language Pairs – ensure you have required pairs listed All Language Pairs -&gt; Translation Memory and Automated Translations - &gt; Use</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/001afcbc-7d82-4af9-9ce8-312e65cb16eb/Resources/835766fe-11df-4389-ad5d-8b0bf3d64090.png?dl=1\"></p><p><br></p><h4>Authentication</h4><p>When Language Weaver is launched, you select the service solution you want to enable, namely Language Weaver Cloud or Language Weaver Edge. This would be based on your subscription and service solution accessibility.&nbsp;Thereafter your select your preferred log in method.</p><p><br></p><p>In the event you are unable to log in:</p><p>Depending on your preferred authentication method, you may be prompted to contact your Language Weaver administrators who could provide you the necessary information.&nbsp;</p><p><br></p><p>TIP: If you selected Language Cloud, ensure you select the correct region. This would be based on where your Language Weaver Account has been provisioned. Either being US or EU.</p><p><br></p><h4>Customising NMT engine&nbsp;</h4><p>Once logged in you are taken to a table that is language pair specific, whereby you can configure your preferences in order to customise and tailor the output of NMT based on your requirements.</p><p><br></p><p>This would be based on what was selected under: Model, Dictionary and Linguistic Options. Available features of which, is based on what your subscription supports and how your Language Weaver Portal has been configured.</p><p><br></p><h4>Applying NMT translation</h4><p>You can apply NMT either interactively on a per segment basis or via Batch Task – PreTranslate, as a means of automating the process of populating target translations.</p><p><br></p><p>CAUTION: If you are using Batch Task – PreTranslate, ensure you have enabled: Apply automated translation</p><p><br></p><h4>Working with Quality Estimations &amp; Feedback</h4><p>These features require metadata that is obtained from Language Weaver. They are automatically provided when working interactively between segments.</p><p><br></p><p>CAUTION: If you are relying on automation via&nbsp;Batch Task – PreTranslate, you will need to run a second Batch Task: Apply Language Weaver Metadata</p><p><br></p><p>TIP: You can create a custom batch task to combine these two tasks. This can be saved as part of a project template for ongoing ease.</p><p><br></p><h4>Working with Feedback</h4><p>Feedback is by default enabled and as a result segments that have been translated using Language Weaver, will automatically be submitted for feedback. You can disable this by going to your active instance of Language Weaver Settings and disabling it.</p><p><br></p><p>Under the View toolbar you will find Language Weaver Feedback, which will open the Feedback Window.</p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/001afcbc-7d82-4af9-9ce8-312e65cb16eb/Resources/4d342eb3-8cc4-4f93-adc8-b8290e927ffc.png?dl=1\"></p><p><br></p><p>If the required metadata is missing and you have yet to run the Apply Language Weaver Metadata&nbsp;Batch Task, its expected you will see the following message:</p><p><em>To provide feedback the segment requires metadata, that originates from Language Weaver. To obtain this Run the Apply Language Weaver Metadata Batch task. It will provide the necessary metadata against any existing segments, that already has NMT originating from from Language Weaver</em></p><p><br></p><p>If you submit duplicate feedback entries, expect to see the following error: Code xxx: feedback could not be created</p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/001afcbc-7d82-4af9-9ce8-312e65cb16eb/Resources/18b09561-9c27-4078-aad4-3018fc16b512.png?dl=1\"></p><p>In this event, you can go to your Language Weaver portal to review your submitted feedback and remove unwanted entries. Feedback submitted can be evaluated by those who have either Admin or Linguistic roles, within the Language Weaver Portal.</p><p><br></p><h4>Working with Quality Estimates (QE)</h4><p>This is only applicable for those who use Language Weaver Cloud and based on your active model selected.</p><p><br></p><p>If applicable, ensure your model is set to QE and under linguistic options its enabled. If not, you should expect to see this popup: <em>In order to use Quality Estimation, it needs to be enabled. Do you wish to proceed?</em>&nbsp;</p><p><br></p><p>When working with the segments interactively, QE will automatically be applied. This can be seen from both the Language Weaver – Feedback UI as well as the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/25\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">DSI Viewer</a></p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/001afcbc-7d82-4af9-9ce8-312e65cb16eb/Resources/1084741c-f8c3-4abf-a2f0-4136018a63a3.png?dl=1\"></p><p><br></p><p>If the required QE metadata is not available, then you will need to run Apply Language Weaver Metadata&nbsp;Batch Task.</p><p><br></p><h4>QE Reporting</h4><p>To further support the Quality Estimations, there is a Create QE Report. This can be found within the list of Batch Tasks.</p><p><br></p><p>The report has the following features:</p><ul><li>Ability to exclude locked segments</li><li>QE status per project file, per target language</li></ul><p><br></p><h3>Key Paths</h3><h3><br></h3><ul><li>Language Pair Mapping Database: C:\\Users\\[USERNAME]\\AppData\\Roaming\\Trados AppStore\\Language Here are displayed available tasks, if the row is&nbsp;grey&nbsp;this means the task is&nbsp;</span><span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">disabled&nbsp;</span>because it was checked out for a user and you are not able to create a Studio Project with it.</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1326x336/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8321.gr2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Click on expand filter button to see more options to filter tasks. All the dates displayed in the grid are shown based on user time zone.&nbsp;<strong>Please make sure in Lingotek web application you select the same time zone in order to see the same dates.</strong></p><h3>Navigation area</h3><ul><li>You can select how many tasks you want to see on page.</li></ul><p><em>For performance&nbsp;reasons we set the size to be 10 by default.&nbsp;&nbsp;</em></p><ul><li>To see next / previous page click on arrow buttons.&nbsp;</li></ul><p><em>For performance reasons we cache the tasks for each page you navigate. This means if you already navigated on 4 pages when you click on \"Previews\" button the grid will be populated instantly.&nbsp;</em></p><ul><li>If you want to&nbsp;<span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">Jump</span>&nbsp;to a page&nbsp;change the number from text box and hit&nbsp;<span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">\"Enter\"</span>.</li></ul><h2><span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">How to create a Studio Project</span></h2><p><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Multiple tasks can be selected from the grid by</span><strong style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">&nbsp;checking the box and Right click on grid.</strong><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">&nbsp;they will be added to the same Studio project.&nbsp;</span><strong style=\"color: rgb(0, 0, 0);\">Only projects with the same Language Pair and Stage can be added in the same project.&nbsp;</strong></p><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">We use Project Automation&nbsp;API to create the projects in Studio. <strong>Localyzer file type settings</strong> for handling <code>.pxml</code> files in Trados Studio and Trados Cloud. While the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/359\">Localyzer Connect</a> for Trados plugin enhances preview functionality in Trados Studio, this guide is focused entirely on the required file type settings, not the plugin itself. References to the plugin are only for context.</p>\r\n<p>These settings are essential when working with <code>.pxml</code> files generated by Localyzer, ensuring both Trados Cloud and Studio can correctly parse and preview them.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>Unified filetype support for .pxml in both Trados Cloud and Studio</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>XSL-based preview for Trados Cloud, rendering content based on the active segment</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Seamless integration with the Localyzer Connect plugin in Trados Studio</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Support for contextual metadata, including LRM_INCONTEXT URLs and Command Center attributes</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Elimination of format-specific issues by standardising translation input as .pxml</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>Trados Studio 2024 or later (for using the .sdlftsettings file and plugin preview)</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Trados Cloud (Enterprise/Accelerate/Team) for Online Editor preview functionality</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Localyzer <code>.pxml</code> files structured according to Lingoport&#39;s PXML format</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>A valid <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/359\">Localyzer Connect</a> plugin installation (for desktop preview)</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Understanding of InContext translation workflows</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>.PXML</h2>\r\n<h3>Why Use Custom XML File Type Settings for PXML in Trados Studio?</h3>\r\n<p>When localising software, source strings often come from various standard resource formats such as <code>.json</code>, <code>.resx</code>, or <code>.properties</code> for example. While these formats are ideal for development, they are not always optimal for translation environments like Trados Studio. To streamline translation workflows and provide in-context accuracy Lingoport have adopted a standard <code>.xml</code> based format called <code>.pxml</code> that can be used specifically for localization workflows. More information on this file type can be found in the <a href=\"https://wiki.lingoport.com/PXML\">Lingoport wiki</a>.</p>\r\n<p>Using a <strong>custom <code>.xml</code> file type configuration in Trados</strong> offers significant advantages:</p>\r\n<h4>Unified Parsing Logic</h4>\r\n<p>Instead of configuring multiple file types for each resource format, a single parser handles all <code>.pxml</code> files. This ensures consistent behaviour and reduces the risk of parsing errors or misaligned segmentation rules.</p>\r\n<h4>Seamless Context Integration</h4>\r\n<p><code>.pxml</code> files contain attributes like <code>LRM_INCONTEXT</code> which store URLs pointing to visual context hosted on Lingoport’s InContext server. Custom settings in Trados make it easy to extract and utilise these URLs, enabling better translator understanding and higher-quality output.</p>\r\n<h4>Workaround for Format Limitations</h4>\r\n<p>Formats like <code>.json</code> do not natively support metadata such as comments or external context references. By converting to <code>.pxml</code>, these limitations are bypassed, allowing you to embed essential attributes such as segment IDs, length constraints, reviewer notes etc. directly in the file.</p>\r\n<h4>Compatibility with Command Center Metadata</h4>\r\n<p>Additional data from Lingoport&#39;s Command Center (e.g. context type, usage constraints, and localisation metadata) can be preserved and mapped using the custom file type settings, providing richer context to translators.</p>\r\n<h2>The Settings Files</h2>\r\n<p>The settings files are provided in a zip, containing three files:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><code>readme.pdf</code></li>\r\n<li><code>LRM_INCONTEXT_TE_[version].sdlftsettings</code></li>\r\n<li><code>LRM_INCONTEXT_TE_[version].xsl</code></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>The <code>readme.pdf</code> is this guide so it&#39;s also available to you locally.</p>\r\n<p>The <code>LRM_INCONTEXT_TE_[version].sdlftsettings</code> file can be used to create the file type in Trados Studio and/or Trados Cloud (Trados Enterprise/Accelerate/Team) so that you can successfully parse a <code>.pxml</code> file for translation. The same settings file works in both products.</p>\r\n<p>The <code>LRM_INCONTEXT_TE_[version].xsl</code> file, a stylesheet, is only <strong>used for previewing content in Trados Cloud</strong>. It is <strong>not used by the Trados Studio plugin</strong>, which handles preview rendering separately. The stylesheet controls the preview so that it reflects the content relevant to the active segment in the Online Editor and doesn&#39;t render the content for the entire file.</p>\r\n<h3>Using the Settings Files</h3>\r\n<h4>Trados Cloud</h4>\r\n<p>To add the settings file you need to create a &quot;New File Type Configuration&quot; under the &quot;Resources&quot; menu:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Trados Cloud interface showing the path to create a new file type configuration: Resources → File Type Configurations → + New File Type Configuration.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/01.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>You then enter three pieces of information:\r\n1.\tName - this is mandatory, recommendation would be to use the same name as the settings file for clarity\r\n2.\tLocation - this is mandatory and should be based on the <a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/en-US/trados-enterprise-%26-accelerate-791595/account-structure%2C-folders%2C-inheritance-796862\">inheritance principles</a> in Trados Cloud\r\n3.\tDescription - this is optional, but useful if you have a lot of custom fileytpes</p>\r\n<p>Like this for example:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"New File Type Configuration form showing Name as LRM_INCONTEXT_v1.0.0, Location as Customers, and a description referencing Localyzer and InContext Preview.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/02.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>Then click on &quot;Import&quot; and select the sdlftsettings file which will create the new file type within your new resource:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Importing the LRM_INCONTEXT_v1.0.0.sdlftsettings file into Trados Cloud by selecting it from the file browser after clicking the Import button in the File Types section.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/03.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>You can move the file closer to the top by dragging it so it&#39;s more convenient to access:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"File Types list in Trados Cloud showing LRM_INCONTEXT_v1.0.0 enabled for .pxml files below SDL XLIFF at the top of the list.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/04.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>The final step is to click on your new file type and navigate down to your &quot;Preview Settings&quot;, click on &quot;Add Preview Type&quot;, select the &quot;XML preview based on XSLT&quot; and then &quot;Add&quot;:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Preview Settings section in Trados Cloud showing the process of adding a new preview type by selecting 'XML preview based on XSLT'.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/05.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>This will add another group below the default &quot;Xml Node Tree&quot; where you browse and select the <code>LRM_INCONTEXT_TE_[version].xsl</code> stylesheet provided in the zip.  It&#39;s recommended that you provide a name and description as these make it easier to identify which preview type you are working with in the Online Editor:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Preview Settings in Trados Cloud showing two preview types: Xml Node Tree and Localyzer InContext Preview with an associated XSLT file (LRM_INCONTEXT_TE_v1.0.0.xsl).\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/06.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>Once you enter the name the new group will automatically inherit the same name making it easy to identify in the future.</p>\r\n<h4>Previewing the .PXML</h4>\r\n<p>Opening a PXML file in the Online Editor can now be previewed by enabling the preview:\r\n1.\tIn the &quot;View menu&quot; click on &quot;Preview Off&quot; and select the location you wish the preview to be shown.\r\n2.\tIn the &quot;Preview Type&quot; select the &quot;Localyzer InContext Preview&quot; (or whatever name you used when you added the stylesheet)</p>\r\n<p>All being well, you&#39;ll see something like this:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Trados Online Editor showing a .pxml file with the 'Localyzer InContext Preview' selected in the Preview Type menu and a corresponding visual preview displayed on the right.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/07.png\">\r\n\r\n<hr>\r\n<h4>Trados Studio</h4>\r\n<p>The settings file is only required if you wish to handle <code>.pxml</code> files natively in Trados Studio.  For example, you are provided with the <code>.pxml</code> files as opposed to receiving a link to the files in Trados Cloud.  If you are working with Localyzer through Trados Cloud then all you need is the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/359\">Localyzer Connect</a> for Trados plugin and this will take care of everything for you.</p>\r\n<p>If you do receive <code>.pxml</code> to translate in Trados Studio then you will need the plugin, and also the sdlftsettings file in the zip.  It&#39;s the same settings file as the one you use for Trados Cloud, but you will not require the stylesheet because the plugin will take care of this for you.</p>\r\n<p>To add the settings file you need to import the settings file by going to &quot;File -&gt; Options -&gt; File Types&quot; and then click on &quot;Import Settings&quot;:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Trados Studio Options window showing the File Types list and the Import Settings button used to add a new filetype configuration.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/08.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>From there you select the <code>LRM_INCONTEXT_v1.0.0.sdlftsettings</code> file and Trados Studio will ask you to confirm your choice:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Trados Studio confirmation dialog asking whether to create a new file type based on imported settings.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/09.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>Once you click &quot;Yes&quot; the new file type settings will be available in your options:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Trados Studio Options window showing confirmation message 'Settings successfully imported' and the newly added file type LRM_INCONTEXT_v1.0.0 associated with .pxml files.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/63610ff5-8fdf-4acc-a734-441c64906d8c/Resources/10.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>You are now in a position to translate <code>.pxml</code> directly, or when downloaded from Trados Cloud.  In both cases the preview will be provided by the  <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/359\">Localyzer Connect</a> for Trados plugin which you&#39;ll find in the &quot;Add-Ins&quot; menu when your file is open in the translation editor:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Trados Studio with the Localyzer Connect plugin active, showing a .sdlxliff file in the Editor view and an in-context visual preview on the right corresponding to the selected translation segment.\" Trados","description":"<h1>Localyzer Connect for Trados</h1>\r\n<p><strong>Localyzer Connect for Trados</strong> is a preview-only plugin for Trados Studio that improves translation workflows for software development by displaying contextual metadata and in-context previews from <code>.pxml</code> and <code>.sdlxliff</code> files generated by <a href=\"https://lingoport.com/\">Localyzer</a>, a product by Lingoport. (<a href=\"https://lingoport.com/get-a-demo/\">Contact Lingoport</a> for a demo if you are interested in Localyzer)</p>\r\n<p>It provides a real-time, segment-level preview within a dedicated View Part in Trados Studio, helping translators and reviewers work more accurately and confidently. The plugin supports files from both cloud projects downloaded into Trados Studio and <code>.pxml</code> files used to create projects directly in the desktop environment.</p>\r\n<h3>What is Localyzer?</h3>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://lingoport.com/software-localization-tools/localyzer-l10n/\">Localyzer</a> is an automated localization platform by Lingoport. It extracts, manages, and returns localizable content from development repositories, supporting agile software localization across web and mobile projects.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h1>Localyzer Connect for Trados</h1>\r\n<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>Localyzer Connect for Trados is a Trados Studio preview-only plugin that enhances the user experience when working with software localisation content from Localyzer. It allows translators and reviewers to visualise contextual metadata and in-context URLs embedded within .pxml files and corresponding .sdlxliff files exported from Trados Cloud projects.\r\nThis plugin is designed for localisation engineers, linguists, and QA reviewers working in a Localyzer-to-Trados workflow, helping ensure accurate and context-aware translations.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>Preview of .PXML Files (Localyzer Preview-XML): Renders  elements and displays contextual information for the active segment based on the LRM_INCONTEXT URLs within a new View Part in Trados Studio.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Cloud or Desktop: The preview is available for Localyzer projects irrespective of whether the SDLXLIFF was downloaded from Trados Cloud (Enterprise/Accelerate/Team) or from a project created directly in Trados Studio.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>File-type Detection Override: The preview is only useful for Localyzer projects which are determined by the expected  (e.g. LRM_INCONTEXT_v6.0). Creating a file-type to support .PXML that is not based on the approved settings file for Trados may not work correctly.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Installation Guide</h2>\r\n<p>Detailed instructions for installing the app, tailored to different user needs or system environments if necessary.</p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Trados Studio Versions</strong>: Trados Studio 2024</li>\r\n<li><strong>Supported Filetypes</strong>: SDLXLIFF files created in Trados Cloud or Trados Studio from a .PXML file</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Step-by-Step Installation</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p><strong>Step 1</strong>: Start Trados Studio and navigate to the integrated AppStore</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p><strong>Step 2</strong>: Search for &quot;Localyzer Connect for Trados&quot;</p>\r\n<img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Screenshot of the RWS AppStore search results showing the plugin 'Localyzer Connect for Trados' by multifarious, listed as free.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/982777ff-51ed-43a6-aa13-60c425b0ff4b/Resources/step2.png\">\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p><strong>Step 3</strong>: Click on the install icon and restart Trados Studio when prompted</p>\r\n<img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Download complete. Restart Trados Studio to finish installing the plug-ins. The plugin ‘Localyzer Connect for Trados’ by multifarious is listed with a green checkmark indicating successful download.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/982777ff-51ed-43a6-aa13-60c425b0ff4b/Resources/step3.png\"></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h2>Configuration and Setup</h2>\r\n<p>Setting up is a simple process requiring nothing more than deciding where you wish the preview to be displayed. The functionality will be available for a docked or an undocked View Part.</p>\r\n<p>To activate the View Part open your file for translation and then go to the Add-Ins tab where you can click on the Localyzer Connect icon to activate the View Part:</p>\r\n   <img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Screenshot of the Trados Studio Add-Ins ribbon tab, highlighting the ‘Localyzer Connect Lingoport’ button with a red arrow pointing to it.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/982777ff-51ed-43a6-aa13-60c425b0ff4b/Resources/step4.png\">\r\n\r\n<p>Once the View Part is active in the Editor you can position it to your preferred location, something like this perhaps:\r\n<img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Screenshot of Trados Studio showing a supported file open in the Editor. The 'Localyzer Connect' preview panel on the right displays a web interface titled 'Rebel Outfitters', showing a table of past orders with order status and delivery dates.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/982777ff-51ed-43a6-aa13-60c425b0ff4b/Resources/step5.png\"></p>\r\n<p>If you open an unsupported filetype you will be shown a preview like this:\r\n<img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Screenshot of Trados Studio showing a non-Localyzer supported file with various segment in the Editor. The 'Localyzer Connect' preview panel on the right displays a textual introduction to the plugin, describing its purpose and integration with Lingoport's platform to ensure users are aware of the reason for not seeing an InContext preview of their translation.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/982777ff-51ed-43a6-aa13-60c425b0ff4b/Resources/step6.png\"></p>\r\n<h2>Instructions for Use</h2>\r\n<p>Open a supported file from a Localyzer project in Trados Studio, and translate the segments.  Each segment will return a preview relevant to the string being translated as long as the Context information has been captured and exported in the .PXML from Localyzer.</p>\r\n<h3>Troubleshooting</h3>\r\n<p>There are most likely four reasons why you may not see the proview you are expecting:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>The project you are working with is not a Localyzer project</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>The context has not been captured yet and is missing from the .PXML file.  You will need to contact your client.  In this case you will preview the following:</p>\r\n<img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Screenshot of the 'Localyzer Connect' panel displaying an InContext Translation &amp; QA message stating that context has not yet been captured for the current key. The panel includes branding, a placeholder image of red tulips, and a footer showing InContext Server Version 1.6.1 by Lingoport.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/982777ff-51ed-43a6-aa13-60c425b0ff4b/Resources/step7.png\">\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>The token allowing you have access to the InContext material has expired.  You will need to contact your client.  In this case you will preview the following:</p>\r\n<img style=\"display: block; margin: 12px 0; max-width: 750px;\" alt=\"Screenshot of the 'Localyzer Connect' panel displaying an error message in red: “Please provide a context token.” The panel includes InContext Translation &amp; QA branding and a footer indicating InContext Server Version 1.6.1 by Lingoport.\" src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/982777ff-51ed-43a6-aa13-60c425b0ff4b/Resources/step8.png\">\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>You are working with a .pxml file directly in Trados Studio and created a single-file project. If you have not saved the project yet the necessary information is not available in the project settings and you will most likely see a nessage there is &quot;<strong>No Context</strong>&quot;.  If you save the project and re-open it the preview should be available.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n Problem</strong></p><p> Today the use of Machine Translation is on the up and for many project managers, translators and localization engineers the problem,</p><p> is knowing, which provider to use for a particular project, so you can be sure the amount of post-editing effort will be reduced to a minimum.</p><p><br></p><p> <strong>The Solution</strong></p><p> Before the MT Comparison plugin, the solution would be to pre-translate the projects several times and review the results,all of which takes a lot of time.</p><p> But now, this plugin provides you with the ability to pre-translate the project with up to five different Machine Translation providers at a time, and </p><p>generate a single report in Excel with the results of each engine side by side. </p><p> This makes the review of the translations much easier to compare.</p><p> </p><p> To learn how to use this application, please visit <a href=\" https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/3317/mt-comparison\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>MT Comparison</strong></a> in the RWS Community wiki</p><p><br></p>","documentation":"<p>The&nbsp;MT Comparison Tool&nbsp;allows you to&nbsp;generate translation reports based on&nbsp;Language Cloud Translation engines and/or any other MT provider in your project settings.</p><h4>Good to know:&nbsp;</h4><ul><li><span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">Application runs analysis for only one project at the time</span>. If you select multiple projects -&gt; \"MT Comparison Report\" a message window will appear.&nbsp;</li><li>Some of the Translation Provider plugins can store Supported Languages in the settings of the project&nbsp;<strong>IF</strong>&nbsp;they are up to date -&gt; use the latest updates (of the TPs) and you'll be able to see their supported language pairs and their flags in the MT Comparison Tool.</li></ul><h3>Where can I find it?</h3><p><strong>Right click on&nbsp;the project</strong>&nbsp;you want to run the application and select<strong>&nbsp;\"MT Comparison Tool\" action.</strong></p><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/270x370/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3323.pastedimage1566808186966v3.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><p>After the connection to LC was established MT Comparison Tool window will appear:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1400x700/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4744.pastedimage1575364737617v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This window is divided in 3 areas:</p><ol><li><span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">Translation providers&nbsp;grid</span>. The grid will be populated will all translation engines available in LC&nbsp;which matches the language pairs from selected project.<span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">Files grid.</span>&nbsp;This grid will be populated with the files available in selected project.<span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">Report settings</span>. We'll add in the report only the segments which match the criteria selected in the interface</li></ol><h2>Report settings details</h2><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">By default the application will add only untranslated,&nbsp;unlocked segments in the report</strong><span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">.</span>&nbsp;If the status of the segment isn't \"Not Translated\" that segment will not be added to report</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8103.NotTranslated.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1400x300/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8103.NotTranslated.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h4>Options available</h4><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 102, 255);\">Inclusions</strong></p><ul><li>\"<strong>Include drafts and translated segments</strong>\"&nbsp;</li><li>\"<strong>Include locked segments</strong>\"&nbsp;</li><li>\"I<strong>nclude draft segments below</strong>\"</li><li>\"<strong>Include AT segments</strong>\" Include&nbsp;segments that were translated and confirmed by&nbsp;automated translation.</li></ul><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 102, 255);\">Filters</strong></p><ul><li>\"<strong>Only translate ... segments</strong>\". Add there the number of segments you want to add in your report for each file. Because the application allows selection of multiple engines and&nbsp;multiple files we added 5 segments by default.</li><li>\"<strong>Only translate engine from segment ... to segment ...</strong>&nbsp;\". This option allows you to specify the&nbsp;range of segments you want to see in the report</li><li>\"<strong>Only translate segments that are ... characters or over</strong>\". Allows you to specify the length of the segment.</li></ul><h2>How to generate the report?</h2><p>After you selected the engines, files and settings for your report click on \"Generate report\". A new window will appear from where you need to select the destination path and the name of your report.</p><p>After the report was generated successfully a confirmation message will appear and the generated report will be opened automatically.</p><h3>Structure of the report</h3><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7180.report.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1500x800/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2671.report.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>For each language pair a new style sheet is created. For each file a new table with translation results is follows Japanese)</p>\r\n<p>MTrans Diff Viewは、Trados Studio用のリアルタイム翻訳比較プラグインです。法人向けの製品です。無料トライアルをご利用頂けます。こちらのページからヒューマンサイエンスまでお問い合わせください： <a href=\"https://www.science.co.jp/localization/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&utm_medium=appstore.rws\">https://www.science.co.jp/localization/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&amp;utm_medium=appstore.rws</a></p>\r\n<h2>リアルタイムに差異を表示</h2>\r\n<p>レビュー/チェック作業中に編集中の訳文と、翻訳メモリ（TM）/機械翻訳（MT）の訳文の差異を即座に比較し、差分を分かりやすく表示します。</p>\r\n<h3>比較パターン</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>編集中の訳文 vs. 翻訳メモリ（TM）の訳文</li>\r\n<li>編集中の訳文 vs. 機械翻訳（MT）の訳文</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>効率的なレビュー</h2>\r\n<p>訳文を修正する際に、TMまたはMTの出力と比較して、テキストのどこがどのように変更されたかを即座に確認できます。これにより、レビュー/チェックの時間を短縮できます。</p>\r\n<blockquote>\r\n<p><strong>注：</strong> MTrans Diff ViewはMTrans for Trados <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/209\">https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/209</a> と連携動作するプラグインです。MTrans Diff Viewを使用するには、MTrans for Tradosの有効なサブスクリプションが別途必要です。MTrans for Tradosは、複数のMTエンジン、MT用の用語集、自動ポストエディット、翻訳メモリのあいまい一致の自動修正などの機能を提供します。</p>\r\n</blockquote>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p>MTrans Diff View is a real-time translation comparison plugin for Trados Studio. This product is intended for <strong>corporate use</strong>. A <strong>free trial</strong> is available. Please contact Human Science at: <a href=\"https://www.science.co.jp/en/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&utm_medium=appstore.rws\">https://www.science.co.jp/en/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&amp;utm_medium=appstore.rws</a></p>\r\n<h2>Real-time Difference Visualization</h2>\r\n<p>The plugin instantly compares the differences between the target text being edited and the TM/MT results during the review process, displaying them with <strong>clear highlights</strong>.</p>\r\n<h3>Comparison Patterns:</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>The translation being edited vs. <strong>Translation Memory (TM)</strong> translation</li>\r\n<li>The translation being edited vs. <strong>Machine Translation (MT)</strong> translation</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Efficient Review</h2>\r\n<p>When modifying the translation in the editor, users can <strong>immediately verify</strong> where and how the text has changed compared to the TM or MT output. This cuts down on review and checking time, contributing to <strong>quality consistency</strong>.</p>\r\n<blockquote>\r\n<p><strong>Note:</strong> MTrans Diff View works with <strong>MTrans for Trados</strong> <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/209\">https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/209</a> . A separate, active subscription to MTrans for Trados is <strong>required</strong> to use MTrans Diff View. MTrans for Trados offers features such as multiple MT engines, glossary for MT, automatic post-editting, and automatic correction of TM fuzzy-matches, enhancing overall translation efficiency.</p>\r\n</blockquote>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n You can use it to leverage TMs from different projects with different style rules and terminology. <strong>MTrans TM Find and Replace</strong> is useful in the following cases.</p><p><br></p><ul><li>Different products or services use different style rules and terminology.</li><li><span style=\"background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color); color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground);\">Web and printed materials use different style rules.</span></li><li>The style rules and terminology have changed since the previous version of the document.</li></ul><p><span style=\"background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color); color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground);\">It supports normal string replacement and regular expression replacement as replacement conditions. The translation in the TM will not be changed (unless you overwrite it). The TM can be either a file TM or a server TM stored in GroupShare.</span></p><p><br></p><p>A free trial is available. Please&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.science.co.jp/en/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&amp;utm_medium=appstore.rws\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">contact&nbsp;</a>Human Science for details.</p><p><br></p><p>翻訳メモリ(TM)の訳文に対して文字列置換を自動的に実行します。スタイルルールや用語が異なる別のプロジェクトのTMを活用するためにご利用ください。<strong>MTrans TM Find and Replace</strong>は以下のような場合に便利です。</p><p><br></p><ul><li>製品やサービスごとに異なるスタイルルールや用語を使用している</li><li>Webや印刷物によって異なるスタイルルールを使用している</li><li>以前の版からスタイルルールや用語が変更になった</li></ul><p><br></p><p>置換条件として通常の文字列置換と正規表現置換に対応します。TM内の訳文は変更されません(上書き登録しない場合)。「プロジェクトの設定」にTMとして追加し、置換対象のTMと置換条件を設定して使用します。TMはファイル形式のTMとGroupShareに保存されたサーバーTMの両方に対応します。</p><p><br></p><p>無料トライアルをご利用頂けます。詳細はヒューマンサイエンスまで<a href=\"https://www.science.co.jp/localization/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&amp;utm_medium=appstore.rws\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">お問い合わせください。</a></p>","documentation":"<p><br></p>","supportUrl":"https://www.science.co.jp/en/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&utm_medium=appstore.rws","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/201/versions/418","supportEmail":"hsweb_inquiry@science.co.jp","changelog":{"Trados Studio 2022":"<p><strong>22.0.0.0</strong></p><p>No related information.</p><hr/>"},"icon":{"mediaUrl":"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/d1857016-fbec-444d-85a2-53fc5b5a5fa0/Resources/MTrans TM Find and Replace.png?dl=1"},"developer":{"developerName":"Human Science Co. GPT-5.2 / GPT-5.1 / GPT-5 / GPT-5 mini / GPT-5 nano / GPT-4.1 / GPT-4.1 mini / GPT-4.1 nano / GPT-4o / GPT-4o mini / GPT-4 / GPT-4 Turbo（プロンプトのカスタマイズが可能）</li>\r\n<li>Amazon</li>\r\n<li>NAVER Papago</li>\r\n<li>NICT（みんなの自動翻訳）</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>用語の自動適用</h3>\r\n<p>原文と訳文で使用される用語をあらかじめ登録することで、機械翻訳の訳文で使用される訳語を統一できます。また、機械翻訳エンジンを問わず用語集を一元管理できます。</p>\r\n<h3>スタイルの自動置換</h3>\r\n<p>文字列置換、正規表現置換を使って機械翻訳の訳文のスタイルや表記を自動修正できます。</p>\r\n<h3>OpenAI API連携による自動書き換え</h3>\r\n<p>機械翻訳の訳文をマーケティング向けに書き換えたり、要約して文字数を削減したりすることができます。訳文の書き換え方法を言葉を使って指示できます。</p>\r\n<h3>翻訳メモリのあいまい一致の自動修正</h3>\r\n<p>文書が改版され原文の一部が変更されたときに、翻訳メモリの中にある過去の原文と比較して、変更された箇所を機械翻訳して旧訳文に反映します。</p>\r\n<h3>書式、タグ</h3>\r\n<p>HTMLファイルやXMLファイルを翻訳する場合に、MTrans for Tradosは原文に含まれるタグを訳文に挿入します。訳文が途中で分割されたり、タグが削除されたりすることはありません。</p>\r\n<h3>リアルタイム翻訳比較プラグインと連携可能</h3>\r\n<p>MTrans Diff View <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/403\">https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/403</a> と組み合わせて使用することで、編集中の訳文と、翻訳メモリ（TM）/機械翻訳（MT）の訳文の差分を即座に比較できます。</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p>MTrans for Trados provides multiple machine translation engines, automatic terminology application, automatic post-editing (style replacement, rewriting via OpenAI API integration), and automatic correction of fuzzy matches in translation memory, greatly streamlining translation work. This product is intended for corporate use.&nbsp;A free trial is available. Please contact Human Science at:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://www.science.co.jp/en/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&amp;utm_medium=appstore.rws\"><u>https://www.science.co.jp/en/contact/index.html?utm_source=rws&amp;utm_medium=appstore.rws</u></a></p>\r\n<h3>Simultaneous translation by multiple machine translation engines</h3>\r\n<p>The following machine translation engines are available. You can compare translations from multiple machine translation engines on the editor screen.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>DeepL</li>\r\n<li>Google</li>\r\n<li>Google AutoML Translation</li>\r\n<li>Microsoft</li>\r\n<li>Microsoft Custom Translator</li>\r\n<li>OpenAI ChatGPT API - GPT-5.2 / GPT-5.1 / GPT-5 / GPT-5 mini / GPT-5 nano / GPT-4.1 / GPT-4.1 mini / GPT-4.1 nano / GPT-4o / GPT-4o mini / GPT-4 / GPT-4 Turbo (the prompt is customizable)</li>\r\n<li>Amazon</li>\r\n<li>NAVER Papago</li>\r\n<li>NICT (Minna no Jido Hon&#39;yaku)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Automatic application of terms</h3>\r\n<p>By registering the terms used in the source and target texts in advance, you can unify terminology used in the machine translation. In addition, terminology can be centrally managed regardless of the machine translation engines.</p>\r\n<h3>Automatic Style Replacement</h3>\r\n<p>String replacement and regular expression replacement can be used to automatically correct the style and character notation of machine-translated translations.</p>\r\n<h3>Automatic rewriting by OpenAI API</h3>\r\n<p>Machine-translated sentences can be rewritten for marketing purposes or summarized to reduce the number of characters. You can instruct how to rewrite the translation by using the natural language.</p>\r\n<h3>Automatic correction of fuzzy matches in translation memory</h3>\r\n<p>When a document is revised and a part of the source sentence is changed, it is compared with the previous source sentence in the translation memory and the changed parts are machine-translated and reflected in the legacy translation.</p>\r\n<h3>Formatting and tags</h3>\r\n<p>When translating HTML or XML files, MTrans for Trados inserts the tags contained in the source sentence into the translation. MTrans for Trados does not split the translation in the middle or delete tags.</p>\r\n<h3>Seamless Integration with MTrans Diff View for real-time translation comparison</h3>\r\n<p>When used alongside MTrans Diff View  <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/403\">https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/403</a>, you can quickly and easily compare your ongoing translation with results from translation adds filetype support to Studio for the Memsource flavour of XLIFF which is identified as MXLIFF.</p><p> For more details on how to use this app, please visit the <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/4036/mxliff-file-type-support\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">RWS Community Wiki</a>.</p><p> This app is maintained from 2019 onwards by the RWS Community team.</p>","documentation":"<p>Introduction</p><p><strong>Prerequisite</strong>: Memsource account (It can be used a free account&nbsp;which allows 2 projects creation. Access Memsource web app:&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.memsource.com/pricing/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">https://www.memsource.com/pricing/</a>&nbsp;to create the account)</p><p>The application represents file type support for MXLIFF for Trados Studio.&nbsp;The plugin is available for Studio 2017 and Studio 2019.</p><p>The MXLIFF file type can be found at: File-&gt; Options-&gt; File Types-&gt; Memsource MXLIFF</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8484.pastedimage1549369274161v1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1399x848/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8484.pastedimage1549369274161v1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>&nbsp;</p><h1>So what does this file type do?</h1><p>If you google “MXLIFF Trados”, you can find a lot of freelance translators who have clients that work with Memsource, but would like to do their translations in Trados Studio or memoQ.</p><p>Up until now, the common workaround for Trados Studio was to changed the file extension from&nbsp;<code>.mxliff</code>&nbsp;to&nbsp;<code>.xliff</code>. However, MXLIFF files use many attributes that other tools do not understand like&nbsp;<code>m:score</code>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<code>m:locked</code>&nbsp;(all Memsource specific attributes start with an&nbsp;<code>m:</code>).</p><ul><li>&nbsp;Usefull documentation for Memsource REST Api:&nbsp;<a href=\"https://cloud.memsource.com/web/docs/api\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">https://cloud.memsource.com/web/docs/api</a></li></ul><p>How to use the plugin:</p><ol><li>After loggin to Memosurce, go to&nbsp;<strong>Projects&nbsp;→ Create project</strong>&nbsp;using a source and target languageAdd new&nbsp;<strong>Job&nbsp;</strong>→ select a document file (it could be .docx/.txt etc)Download the file as bilingual MXLIFF format</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1100/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3465.pastedimage1594213332284v6.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><br></p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;6. Open the file in Studio as single file</p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;7. Translate the file (if using Studio 2019, the project needs to be saved, because of a bug from Studio)</p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;8. Confirm the segments (so we can check inside the Memsource if the file is successfully uploaded)</p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;9. Press on&nbsp;<strong>File&nbsp;→ Save Target As</strong>&nbsp;(save the new .mxliff locally)</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7723.pastedimage1594213351898v7.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><br></p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;10. Go back to Memsource web and within the project, select&nbsp;<strong>Tools&nbsp;→ Upload</strong>&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/800x900/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2626.pastedimage1594213362334v8.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;11. Select the new .mxliff which contains the translations</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1512.pastedimage1594213382974v9.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><br></p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;12. Upload the bilingual file</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1100/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6683.pastedimage1594213393432v10.png\" alt=\" \"></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/29/versions/1549","changelog":{"Trados Studio - 2026 Release":"<p><strong>6.0.1.0</strong></p><p>Manifest file changes:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Changed the max version to 19.0.9 to prevent loading into the next SR version.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: Cloud","description":"<p>This add-on enables users working in Trados Cloud to receive machine translation results from the Microsoft Translator - <a href=\"https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/translator/\">https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/translator/</a>.</p>\r\n<p>You can directly install the Microsoft Translator Machine Translation Provider from within your cloud account. Simply select your <strong>account icon</strong> in the top right hand corner of the screen&gt; <strong>RWS AppStore</strong>.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Please note:</strong> You will need to purchase a subscription to the Microsoft Translator and you&#39;ll need an API key to use the add-on which you can obtain through <a href=\"https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/contact-sales/\">Microsoft sales</a>. Pricing for the Microsoft Translator service is available through this <a href=\"https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/cognitive-services/translator/\">Microsoft website</a>.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h1>App Name</h1>\r\n<p>Microsoft Machine Translation Provider for Trados Cloud</p>\r\n<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>This add-on enables users working in the cloud (through Trados Studio, Trados Team or Trados Enterprise) to receive machine translation results from the Microsoft Translator - <a href=\"https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/translator/\">https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/translator/</a>.</p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<p>1.\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0An Azure Subscription with an active Resource Translator Service Plan.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/translator/translator-overview\">See Microsoft Documentation for further information</a></p>\r\n<p>2.\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0For\u00A0custom models, then you require a number of prerequisites.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/translator/custom-translator/quickstart#prerequisites\">See Microsoft Documentation for further information</a></p>\r\n<p><strong>Installing Microsoft Machine Translation Provider AddOn</strong></p>\r\n<p>It can be\u00A0installed in 2 ways:</p>\r\n<p>1.\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0Within your Language Cloud tenant.</p>\r\n<p>Access your account settings and from the drop down menu, select RWS AppStore. From the AddOn’s listed select Microsoft Machine Translation Provider to install.</p>\r\n<p>2.\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0Alternatively you can install it via the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/143\">RWS AppStore</a></p>\r\n<h3>Key Features</h3>\r\n<p>Configuring Microsoft Machine Translation Provider</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/0ba0385a-d23b-4592-8a53-75a0a938b7af/Resources/52f0b4cb-5976-41f5-9cff-0034eff28477.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>1.Subscription Key</p>\r\n<p>This is a mandatory field. It requires your Named Translator Resource API key. This can be found by looking at all your <a href=\"https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_ProjectOxford/CognitiveServicesHub/~/TextTranslation\">Named Translator Resources</a> &gt;- Manage Keys</p>\r\n<p>2.EndPoint</p>\r\n<p>This is an optional field. EndPoint would be the URL that translation requests are sent to. Default value is preset.</p>\r\n<p>3.Subscription Region</p>\r\n<p>This is the location of the Translator Resource. Default value is set to Global. Can be confirmed via:\u00A0Resource (Type Translator) &gt; Resource Management &gt; Keys &amp; Endpoint within your Azure Portal</p>\r\n<p>4.Category</p>\r\n<p>This is an optional field. This parameter is used to support customised translations. Default value is set to general. The Category field can be reconfirmed by accessing your <a href=\"https://portal.customtranslator.azure.ai/workspaces/\">Azure Workspace</a></p>\r\n<p>5.Allow Fallback</p>\r\n<p>This is an optional field. Specified that the serice is allowed to fall back to a general system when a custom system does not exist. Possible values are: true or false. Default set to true</p>\r\n<p>6.Overriding Settings</p>\r\n<p>The only required field is the Subscription Key field.\u00A0Endpoint, Subscription Region, category, and Allow Fallback are all optional values, and determine details of how Microsoft Machine Translation Provider will perform your translation. The Overriding Settings value allows you to specify different values for all the above settings, depending on the language pair being translated. This is managed by entering a JSON Strings. See See **Override JASON examples below.**\u00A0</p>\r\n<p>7.Custom Model Settings</p>\r\n<p>This is required when using custom models. You need to specify the resources&#39; names associated with the custom translators.  This can be found by looking at all your <a href=\"https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_ProjectOxford/CognitiveServicesHub/~/TextTranslation\">Named Translator Resources</a>. This is implemented by entering a JSON Strings as seen below.</p>\r\n<pre><code>[\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0{\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;ResourceName&quot;:&quot;NAME&quot;,\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;SubscriptionKey&quot;:&quot;SUBSCRIPTION_KEY&quot;\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0}\r\n]\r\n</code></pre>\r\n<p>If the subscription key you want to use is not different from the default one, you don&#39;t need to specify it. As a result, the JSON String would be:</p>\r\n<pre><code>[\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0{\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;ResourceName&quot;:&quot;NAME&quot;\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0}\r\n]\r\n</code></pre>\r\n<p>After successfully authentication and configuring your Microsoft Translator AddOn, the list of custom models will be displayed when adding a machine translation model against the language pairs supported.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Separate Target Languages</strong></p>\r\n<p>Entries in the Machine Translation list will be separated by target language. This increases flexibility in scenarios in which the engine can translate more than one language, but you don’t want to use the same one for all of them.</p>\r\n<p>So, instead of the following:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/0ba0385a-d23b-4592-8a53-75a0a938b7af/Resources/19d5e8a1-72ae-4742-a58c-63b899740a61.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>separated entries will show up:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/0ba0385a-d23b-4592-8a53-75a0a938b7af/Resources/656b81e5-a1aa-466b-851a-9b726b810100.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Override JSON examples below:</strong></p>\r\n<p>This is controlled by entering a json string in the following format. In this example only the language pairs (e.g.\u00A0<strong>en&gt;zh-CN</strong>) and the\u00A0<strong>EXAMPLE</strong>fields would need to be updated according to your needs.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Source language flavour not supported. Use only language code.</strong></p>\r\n<p><strong>Target language flavour is supported and must be specified.</strong></p>\r\n<pre><code>{\r\n  &quot;OverridingSettings&quot;: [\r\n    {\r\n      &quot;ValueProvider&quot;: &quot;LanguagePairValueProvider&quot;,\r\n      &quot;ValueProviderVariable&quot;: &quot;Unused&quot;,\r\n      &quot;Matches&quot;: [\r\n        {\r\n          &quot;MatchValue&quot;: &quot;en&gt;zh-CN&quot;,\r\n          &quot;Settings&quot;: [\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;SUBSCRIPTION_KEY&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            },\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;ENDPOINT&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            },\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;SUBSCRIPTION_REGION&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            },\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;CATEGORY&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            },\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;ALLOW_FALLBACK&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            }\r\n          ]\r\n        },\r\n        {\r\n          &quot;MatchValue&quot;: &quot;en&gt;ro&quot;,\r\n          &quot;Settings&quot;: [\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;SUBSCRIPTION_KEY&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            },\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;ENDPOINT&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            },\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;SUBSCRIPTION_REGION&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            },\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;CATEGORY&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            },\r\n            {\r\n              &quot;SettingName&quot;: &quot;ALLOW_FALLBACK&quot;,\r\n              &quot;SettingValue&quot;: &quot;EXAMPLE&quot;\r\n            }\r\n          ]\r\n        }\r\n      ]\r\n    }\r\n</code></pre>\r\n<p>The number of languages specified and the configuration fields they override can be changed, so that only relevant fields are specified, for example:</p>\r\n<pre><code>{\r\n\u00A0\u00A0&quot;OverridingSettings&quot;: [\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0{\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;ValueProvider&quot;:\u00A0&quot;LanguagePairValueProvider&quot;,\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;ValueProviderVariable&quot;:\u00A0&quot;Unused&quot;,\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;Matches&quot;: [\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0{\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;MatchValue&quot;:\u00A0&quot;en&gt;zh-CN&quot;,\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;Settings&quot;: [\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0{\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;SettingName&quot;:\u00A0&quot;SUBSCRIPTION_REGION&quot;,\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0&quot;SettingValue&quot;:\u00A0&quot;Asia&quot;\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0}\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0]\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0}\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0]\r\n\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0\u00A0}\r\n\u00A0\u00A0]\r\n}\r\n</code></pre>\r\n<style>\r\n Provider</strong></h2><p>MT Enhanced was a plugin that supported several providers. As of February 2023, a decision was made to split MTEnhanced into each of the respective MT providers, now catering for a dedicated&nbsp;Microsoft Translator Provider.</p><p><br></p><h2>Minimum Requirements</h2><ul><li>Trados Studio 2022</li><li>A paid for Microsoft Azure Account and&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/translator/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">subscription&nbsp;to the&nbsp;Microsoft Translator&nbsp;service</a></li><li><span style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">\uFEFF</span></li></ul><h2>Getting the Plugin</h2><p>You can install the Microsoft Translator Provider either from:</p><ul><li><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/179/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">Trados AppStore</a></li><li>From within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore</li></ul><p>Provided you have successfully installed the Microsoft Translator Provider plugin, you can select it with Trados Studio.&nbsp;Either as part of your default settings under File – Options – Language Pairs – Translation Memory and Automation or as part of your project settings.</p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675292144143v1.png\"></p><p><br></p><h2>Authentication within Trados Studio</h2><h1><strong>\uFEFF</strong></h1><p>All that is required is your API key&nbsp;which&nbsp;is generated by Azure after you have subscribed to&nbsp;Microsoft Translator.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1675292430179v2.png\"></p><p>If you have created your own custom engine,you can use this in Trados Studio by specifying the&nbsp;Category ID&nbsp;in the plugin.&nbsp;</p><p><br></p><h2><strong>Generating Keys</strong></h2><h1><br></h1><p>If you don't have a key, go to your Azure account&nbsp;go to&nbsp;<strong>Keys</strong>&nbsp;under&nbsp;the&nbsp;<strong>Resource Management</strong>&nbsp;tab you should&nbsp;see in the right hand side two fields with keys</p><p>&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7838.keys_5F00_azure.jpg\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7838.keys_5F00_azure.jpg\"></a></p><p><br></p><h2><strong>Security</strong></h2><h1><br></h1><p><strong>Storage Security</strong></p><p>The API key, which acts as a unique identifier for accessing the MS Translator services, is encrypted for secure storage within Trados.&nbsp;It is also not sent for use by others if you send a project package based on a project you created with this provider.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Data Transmission</strong></p><p>The data that is transmitted between Studio and&nbsp;the&nbsp;Microsoft Translator Provider for translation purposes is not encrypted by the plugin itself.&nbsp;&nbsp;HTTPS protocol is used to encrypt the data during transfer between Trados Studio and&nbsp;the&nbsp;Microsoft Translator Provider. This ensures that any information sent or received is secured from potential eavesdropping or tampering during transmission.&nbsp;&nbsp;This includes not only the translated content but also any metadata or other associated information that might be Moreover, you can organize your memories as you prefer: you can have all the multilingual translation memories that you want, and for every project, you can choose your preferred combination.</p><p>ModernMT will <strong>react in real-time </strong>to your decisions and will provide a machine translation that is adapted following your settings. In other words: there are no pre-built customized models or long-running training processes.</p><p>You can create new empty memories, by clicking the “New Memory” button, or just import your existing ones from “.tmx” files&nbsp;with the “Import TMX” button.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/ada56836-b958-4588-aab3-567b2ad727d0/Resources/b65d2bb3-518f-407a-ae1c-a19d3513c865.png?dl=1\">You can select the memories you src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/ada56836-b958-4588-aab3-567b2ad727d0/Resources/6869b994-1f09-491a-b2aa-6edd51fb1278.png?dl=1\"></p><p>ModernMT is able to learn
