Trados Studio 2019 (SR2)","minimumStudioVersion":"15.2.0"}]}],"categories":[{"id":3,"name":"Terminology"}]},{"id":17,"downloadCount":3670,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2022-05-20T12:38:42.143Z","updatedAt":"2025-01-10T22:04:08.738099Z","name":"Multilingual Excel FileType","description":"<p><strong>IMPORTANT:</strong> If you are running Trados Studio 2022 please note that the available download is only for CU1 and above. If you still need the previous version you can find it in the <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/6444/archived-versions-multilingual-excel-filetype\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>Archive wiki</strong></a>.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>The Problem</strong></p><p>Dealing with multilingual Excel files is something many users encounter on a regular basis, especially when handling gaming or software localization projects. The problems of these files range from not being able to handle the content itself in a single project to having to deal with embedded content and sometimes pixel length checks. Then you have to put the different projects back together into a single file when you're done!</p><p><br></p><p><strong>The Solution</strong></p><p>The Multilingual Excel FileType solves all of these problems and more. Some common usecases for it would be:</p><ol><li>create bilingual or multilingual projects from an Excel file containing at least two languages handle large blocks of embedded content in the files using embedded content processors handle html AND placeable content in one go handle html embedded content without doing anything at all! handle bilingual or multilingual Excel files without the need to use the bilingual Excel filetype in multiple projects support pixel length verification</li></ol><p><br></p><p>You can learn more about the app itself in the <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/6290/multilingual-excel-filetype\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>Multilingual Excel FileType wiki</strong></a>.</p>","documentation":"<h1>Overview</h1><p>The Multilingual Excel filetype is designed to provide support for translating Excel files that support more than one language.&nbsp;The Excel file does have to be structured so that the content is within columns, but this is the typical layout for most files used in this context.&nbsp;Examples would be Excel files used for Gaming and Software localization, and files used for translating terminology.&nbsp;For example:</p><p><em>Image:</em>&nbsp;Gaming Excel with a column for character length check, software key and comments from the developer:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652108450141v1.png\"></p><p><br></p><p><em style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">Image:</em><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">&nbsp;Gaming Excel with a column for character length check, software key and comments from the developer:</span></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652108563671v2.png\"></p><p><br></p><p>At a high level the filetype is designed to do&nbsp;several useful things:</p><ol><li>create a single multilingual filetype project from one source file for any number of target languagessupport embedded contentsupport character length checks (can be language specific)support pixel length checks (can be language specific)generate a single multilingual excel file from the completed project</li></ol><p>At a more detailed level the app supports many features the user will find helpful, and in many cases provides support for files that are not easily handled out of the box by the existing Excel filetypes.</p><h2>Using the filetype (Video)</h2><p>The following video provides an overview of how the filetype works in practice:</p><iframe class=\"ql-video\" frameborder=\"0\" allowfullscreen=\"true\" src=\"https://www.youtube.com/embed/qH8sS4ncz1g?showinfo=0\"></iframe><p><br></p><h2>File Type Information</h2><p>Standard filetype interface providing minimal info about the filetype name, identifier&nbsp;and the type of files it supports.</p><h2>Worksheet Mapping</h2><p>This page summarises the settings used to map the content from the excel file so Trados Studio knows how to handle it.&nbsp;It does this by mapping the column references in excel to the data being held in Trados Studio.&nbsp;This page, and all others have good&nbsp;mouse and keyboard support.</p><h3>General</h3><p>These type of excel files often contain instructions in the first few rows of the file.&nbsp;You specify here where the relevant data for the project starts, and if you have multiple worksheets in the file you have the option to read the data from all the worksheets as long as they follow the same layout for the content.&nbsp;</p><h3>Languages</h3><p>Using the tabs across the top you can&nbsp;<strong>Add</strong>&nbsp;as many language as you like,&nbsp;<strong>Edit</strong>&nbsp;them,&nbsp;<strong>Delete</strong>&nbsp;them, and&nbsp;<strong>Set Default Language</strong>.&nbsp;The&nbsp;<strong>Set Default Language</strong>&nbsp;feature is useful because it allows you to populate one language with all the settings so that all additional languages will use these settings when you add them.&nbsp;You can of course change the settings but this can significantly reduce the data entry time for files with many languages.</p><p><strong>Language:</strong>&nbsp;one language can be entered in here either by selecting from&nbsp;a&nbsp;dropdown list, typing the language name in full (english (united kingdom)) or by using shortcodes (en-gb).</p><p><strong>Content:</strong>&nbsp;the column name associated with the language content in the excel spreadsheet.&nbsp;In this example the english source content is in column D.&nbsp;Letters are not case sensitive as the application will correct them for you.</p><p><strong><em>Context Information / Comments</em></strong></p><p><strong>Context Information:</strong>&nbsp;the column name associated with the&nbsp;context information in the excel spreadsheet, separated by a semicolon.&nbsp;You can add as many columns as you like in here and the information will be represented as Document Structure Information (DSI) in Trados Studio.&nbsp;&nbsp;For example, in this case four columns were added, B, C, E &amp; F and they are all represented under the&nbsp;<strong>Location</strong>&nbsp;\"Context\".&nbsp;All segments will have the&nbsp;<strong>Location</strong>&nbsp;\"Paragraph and \"Multilingual Paragraph\" by default:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652114338834v2.png\"></p><p><strong>Comment/Notes:</strong>&nbsp;the column name associated with the&nbsp;comments&nbsp;in the excel spreadsheet, separated by a semicolon.&nbsp;&nbsp;You can add as many columns as you like in here and the information will be represented as&nbsp;Comments in Trados Studio.&nbsp;For example, in this case two columns were added,&nbsp;G &amp;&nbsp;H and they are all represented under the&nbsp;<strong>Comments</strong>:&nbsp;</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652114848446v3.png\"></p><p>An important point to note here is illustrated by segment #2.&nbsp;There are two comments, one source and one target.&nbsp;The source comment was taken from an entry in the excel spreadsheet in column G.&nbsp;The target comment was taken from column M which was specified in the language settings for German.&nbsp;You can filter the comments by source or/and target using the Trados Studio settings here:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652115149403v4.png\"></p><p>As these have been added as Trados Studio comments they are also marked up in the Editor so you can see where the comments are:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652115221508v5.png\">&nbsp;</p><p>This could be a little distracting, especially if you wish to add your own comments as they will not be easily picked out unless you make sure to use a different \"severity\" level for the colour:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652115352939v6.png\"></p><p>Or use the DSI Viewer from the appstore.&nbsp;This is probably a better option for viewing the context anyway as it will display all comments (three Studio comments have been manually added here) and DSI context in one view dynamically as you work through your file:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652242958280v2.png\"></p><p>If you want a cleaner view in the editor then it may make sense to add all the comments as DSI instead.&nbsp;The DSI Viewer from the appstore has been updated to make sure it wraps text correctly when long sentences are used as Document Structure, so the view may be better like this where the target language comments have been moved into DSI instead:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652243143798v3.png\"></p><p>Now the editor looks cleaner with only source language comments in this view:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652243262673v4.png\"></p><p>Working in this way means you will not clutter the editing environment with comment markup, or mix and match by adding the source comments as real comments, and the target comments as DSI.&nbsp;The choice is down to personal preference and well supported with through Trados Studio and the apps possible through the integration framework.</p><p>Another important point to note is that the row headings in your Excel file are used as&nbsp;<strong>Location</strong>&nbsp;information in the DSI column below, and the DSI Viewer as you can see above.&nbsp;This feature is almost equivalent to a preview for this type of file since layout isn't important, but understanding the context is:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652243399079v5.png\"></p><p>If there are no headers in your file, or the cell content for a column is empty then the&nbsp;<strong>Location</strong>&nbsp;will be&nbsp;<strong>Excel Context</strong>&nbsp;by default:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652244123093v6.png\"></p><p><strong><em>Verification Checks</em></strong></p><p>Note that these settings link to the&nbsp;<strong>Multilingual Excel Verifier</strong>&nbsp;which is covered&nbsp;in the next section.&nbsp;The reason they are here in the filetype is to avoid the need to have language specific verification settings as well as the language mapping in the filetype.&nbsp;This way all the mapping to content from the Excel spreadsheet is on the same page which significantly simplifies the creation and maintenance of the filetype settings.</p><p><strong>Characters - Max Length:</strong>&nbsp;the column name associated with the&nbsp;maximum character length in the excel spreadsheet.&nbsp;This feature is a simple length check by counting the number of characters in the source and/or target segment and comparing the result with the number specified in the relevant Excel column.&nbsp;This can also be language specific, so you can have a different length for each language, and for each paragraph (Excel cell) being translated.</p><p><strong>Pixels - Max Length:</strong>&nbsp;the column name associated with the&nbsp;maximum pixel length&nbsp;in the excel spreadsheet.&nbsp;&nbsp;This feature is also a length check, but this time the length is determined by the length of pixels occupied by the paragraph text in the source and/or target segment and comparing the result with the number specified in the relevant Excel column.&nbsp;This can also be language specific, so you can have a different length for each language, and for each paragraph (Excel cell) being translated.&nbsp;&nbsp;To calculate the pixel length you need to know the font size and also the font family since pixel length will be variable based on these values.</p><p><strong>Pixels - Font Family:</strong>&nbsp;the column name associated with the pixel&nbsp;font family&nbsp;in the excel spreadsheet.&nbsp;In the example settings used in this wiki column I contains the value for the&nbsp;<strong>Font Family</strong>.&nbsp;However, you must always have a default font specified in case Trados is unable to recognise the type of font entered into the Excel spreadsheet.&nbsp;In this example the default is Berlin Sans FB.&nbsp;In the Excel file column I may contain the value Calibri for example.&nbsp;So the font used for the pixel verification will be Calibri.&nbsp;However, if Calibri is missing from a row, or if it's spelt incorrectly, then the default will be used.&nbsp;So it's important to make sure the values in the spreadsheet can be found by Trados Studio.&nbsp;This also means you must have the font installed on your computer because the application uses this to be able to calculate the pixel length.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652191537849v1.png\"></p><p><strong>Pixels - Font Size:</strong>&nbsp;the column name associated with the&nbsp;pixel font size&nbsp;in the excel spreadsheet.&nbsp;&nbsp;In the example settings used in this wiki column&nbsp;J contains the value for the&nbsp;<strong>Font Size</strong>.&nbsp;However, you must always have a default font specified in case Trados is unable to recognise the&nbsp;size of font entered into the Excel spreadsheet.&nbsp;In this&nbsp;example&nbsp;the default is 15.&nbsp;In the Excel file column&nbsp;J may contain the value&nbsp;12 for example.&nbsp;So the font used for the pixel verification will be 12.&nbsp;However, if a value is missing from a row, then the default 15 will be used.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652191936297v2.png\"></p><h2>Multilingual Excel Verifier</h2><p>This new filetype also incorporates a verification part which is accessed under the&nbsp;<strong>Verification</strong>&nbsp;node in your settings.&nbsp;It contains four settings which use the information from your mapped Excel file to determine what is reported:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652245411547v7.png\"></p><p><strong>Check maximum character length:</strong>&nbsp;the value used for the character length is taken from the value in your spreadsheet as explained under&nbsp;<strong><em>Verification Checks</em></strong>&nbsp;above.&nbsp;You have the option to decide whether you need to report on this or not, and to specify the severity of the message.</p><p><strong>Check maximum pixel length:</strong>&nbsp;the value used for the character length is taken from the value in your spreadsheet as explained under&nbsp;<strong><em>Verification Checks</em></strong>&nbsp;above.&nbsp;You have the option to decide whether you need to report on this or not, and to specify the severity of the message.&nbsp;The pixel length check also clearly identifies if the default values were used because they were either missing, or incorrectly spelt.&nbsp;In this example the&nbsp;<strong>Font Family</strong>&nbsp;for segment #22 was Calibri, but in the spreadsheet there was a typo and it was entered as \"Calbr\",&nbsp;As a result the report identifies that the&nbsp;<strong>Default</strong>&nbsp;was used for that segment allowing you to correct the typo and re-run the file:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652246781812v1.png\"></p><p><strong>Check maximum lines in a paragraph:</strong>&nbsp;this option allows you to specify how many lines are allowed in a paragraph (paragraph equates to an Excel cell).&nbsp;So if you use a soft return to add an additional line when the value is set to report when there is more than one line (the default) then the verification will report an error. For example, if you did this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652246094671v8.png\"></p><p>Then a&nbsp;warning (default) is flagged because you exceeded one line:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652246204712v9.png\"></p><p>You have the option to decide whether you need to report on this or not, and to specify the severity of the message.</p><p><strong>Scope of verification checks:</strong>&nbsp;the verifier can check source and/or target for the reportable errors mentioned above.</p><h2>Embedded Content</h2><p>The embedded content processing in this page supports the use of the Embedded Content Processors available in Trados Studio:</p><p>This provides good functionality for html support, or plain text through the use of regular expressions.&nbsp;For more information on this please see the regular&nbsp;<a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/813470/554599/trados-studio-2021-sr2/configuring-embedded-content-processors\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong>Trados Studio help</strong></a>.</p><p>However, this filetype has some built in html support which means that handling Excel files that do contain html embedded content may not require the use of an embedded content processor at all.&nbsp;You may only need to use the Embedded Content Processor for content that is particularly complex.&nbsp;For example, in the sample gaming file at the start of this wiki you can see content like this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652282880372v1.png\"></p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">If you were using the out of the box Bilingual Excel filetype you would have to define your own rules to handle this content.&nbsp;In the Multilingual Excel Filetype you can use the HTML embedded content processor.</span></p><h2>Placeholders</h2><p>One of the downsides of using the embedded content processors in Trados Studio is that you have a choice, html markup&nbsp;OR&nbsp;other types of placeholders, but not both.&nbsp;</p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">The Multilingual Excel filetype provides the ability to support both types of content at the same time.&nbsp;In this case you could allow the out of the box html markup capability to deal with the html, and then use the&nbsp;</span><strong style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">Embedded Content Plain Text processor</strong><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">&nbsp;to create rules using regular expressions.&nbsp;Or you could use this&nbsp;</span><strong style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">Placeholders</strong><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">&nbsp;page to define simple rules to deal with the content that is not being tagged as you require.</span></p><h2>Entities</h2><p>The&nbsp;entity support in this page uses the same support available for other filetypes in&nbsp;Trados Studio.</p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">For more information on this please see the documentation on entity support in the Trados Studio filetypes,&nbsp;</span><a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/813470/552039/trados-studio-2021-sr2/configuring-html-5-file-types\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong>for example HTML</strong></a><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">.</span></p><h2>QuickInsert</h2><p>The QuickInsert support in this page uses the same support available for other filetypes in Trados Studio.</p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">For more information on this please see the regular&nbsp;</span><a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/813470/334599/trados-studio-2021-sr2/quickinsert-page\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong>Trados Studio help</strong></a><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">.</span></p><h1>Batch Tasks</h1><p>There are two batch tasks associated specifically with this filetype:</p><ol><li>Import Multilingual Translations (Excel)Generate Multiligual Translations (Excel)</li></ol><h2>Import Multilingual Translations (Excel)</h2><p>This task imports the content of the target language information in your Excel files.&nbsp;It isn't mandatory to import this all the time, for example if none of the target languages have been translated yet, but it may be required if you have language specific character or pixel length checks, comments or other contextual information.&nbsp;So this means that for all but the most simple Excel files you will probably need to run this batch task to be able to work correctly with target language verification and contextual data.</p><p>If you are using the Freelance version of Trados Studio this will have to be a separate task after you have created your project because you won't have the ability to create custom batch tasks.&nbsp;But if you have the Professional version of Trados Studio you can of course create the tasks you wish to be run while creating your project.&nbsp;For example, here we have used three tasks only to create the project which can significantly speed up the time it takes to create a project:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652286862719v11.png\"></p><p><br></p><p><span style=\"background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251); color: rgb(71, 85, 97);\">In this example we added a pre-translate task to the end and use four tasks:</span></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652286960978v12.png\"></p><p>Running the task after the project is created is not a serious problem, but you must remember to do this to ensure the availability of all the features available in this filetype if you have more than just columns for the translatable text.</p><p>It's also worth noting that if you hide rows in Excel, or filter them out, then the import will ignore these hidden rows.&nbsp;Additionally&nbsp;there are options on the import to Overwrite existing translations, set the Origin System (this might be helpful for TM updates or filtering in Trados Studio with the Advanced Display Filter), or set the status of the segments after import (Draft, Translated, Translation Rejected, Translation Approved, SignOff Rejected, SignOff Approved):</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652435941983v1.png\"></p><h2>Generate Multilingual Translations (Excel)</h2><p>This task generates the target Multilingual Excel file.&nbsp;It's different to the out of the box&nbsp;<strong>Generate Target Translations</strong>&nbsp;because this task will create a separate Excel file for each language pair in your Multilingual project.&nbsp;The&nbsp;<strong>Generate Multilingual Translations (Excel)</strong>&nbsp;batch task will also merge all your target languages into one file.&nbsp;So if you had forty different target languages in your Excel file this is going to save you a considerable amount of work putting the file together.&nbsp;The larger the project, number and size of files, amount of content, number of languages, the longer this task will take.&nbsp;So waiting times of a few minutes will not be unusual.</p><p>The target files will be created in a folder in your project folder called&nbsp;<strong>Multilingual</strong>:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1652287541370v13.png\"></p><p>It's also worth noting that running this task will finalise your project.&nbsp;This is a rather irritating feature of Trados Studio that we were unable to workaround using the API.&nbsp;So if you run this mid-project to provide an update to your customer be prepared to have to&nbsp;<strong>Revert to SDLXLIFF</strong>&nbsp;for each file in each language in your project.</p><p>Note to Trados AppStore Team: create an app to&nbsp;<strong>Revert to SDLXLIFF</strong>&nbsp;for an entire project in one click!</p><h2>Generate Target Translations</h2><p>We also support regenerating the file using the standard batch task:&nbsp;<strong>Generate Target Translations</strong>.&nbsp;This is always run&nbsp;and will generate the standard bilingual version of the target native file in each target language folder.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><h2>Future Planned Enhancements</h2><p>There are already enhancements planned for this filetype that we will address after the initial release:</p><ul><li>Preview</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">this may be useful, although using the DSI Viewer from the appstore in conjunction with this filetype somewhat negates the priority on this for now.</li><li>Support for multiple worksheets with different layouts</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">the intention is to provide a mechanism for Excel worksheet specific settings so there won't be a need to create separate projects for each worksheet and manually merge afterwards</li></ul><p>If there are features for this filetype you think we should be supporting please use the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/ideas/trados-portfolio-ideas/i/rws-appstore-ideas\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong>ideas platform</strong></a>&nbsp;to share them.</p><p><br></p><p><br></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/17/versions/1768","changelog":{"Trados If you can recognise these sort of problems then this filetype will bring some welcome relief!</p><p><br></p><p><strong>The Solution</strong></p><p>The Multilingual XML FileType solves all of these problems and more. Some common usecases for it would be:</p><ol><li>create bilingual or multilingual projects from an XML file containing at least two languages handle large CDATA dumps in XLIFF files correctly handling well formed, but invalid XLIFF files that Trados Studio will not handle out of the box handle bilingual or multilingual XML files without the need to copy the source into the target element and translate the target element only. handle partially translated bilingual or multilingual XML files</li></ol><p><br></p><p>You can learn more about the app itself in the <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/6039/multilingual-xml-filetype\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>Multilingual XML FileType wiki</strong></a>.</p>","documentation":"<h2>Introduction</h2><p>The Multilingual XML Filetype is a new filetype available for Trados Studio SR2 and later.&nbsp;The basic usecases for this filetype are:</p><ul><li>create bilingual or multilingual projects from an XML file containing at least two languages</li><li>handle large CDATA dumps in XLIFF files correctly</li><li>handling well formed, but invalid XLIFF files that Trados Studio will not handle out of the box</li><li>handle bilingual or multilingual XML files without the need to copy the source into the target element and translate the target element only.</li><li>handle partially translated bilingual or multilingual XML files</li></ul><h2>Installation</h2><p>The filetype can be installed through the AppStore integration in Trados Studio (image below), or by downloading the sdlplugin file from&nbsp;<a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/multilingual-xml-filetype/1261/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1699951943647.1699960728538.37&amp;__hssc=110027290.1.1699960728538&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong>the website</strong></a>&nbsp;and double clicking it to invoke the plugin manager.&nbsp;</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8816.pastedimage1636752006723v2.png\"></p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">Once installed you should find the new filetype installed</span> under File Types.</p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">If you don't see the filetype in your options make sure you have enabled it in the options \"Additional filetypes exist\"</span></p><p>You will also find two new batch tasks:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3010.pastedimage1636736445672v2.png\"></p><p>These will be explained below.</p><h2>Settings</h2><h3>Multilingual XML</h3><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1348.pastedimage1636736620290v3.png\"></p><ol><li>the only option available here is to specify the file dialog wildcard expression if needed.&nbsp;In this example you can see&nbsp;<strong>*.tmx</strong>&nbsp;has been added.&nbsp;This would allow you to use this filetype to translate a TMX file. The only requirement is that the type of file you are opening is a well-formed XML file.</li></ol><h3>Language Mapping</h3><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7534.pastedimage1636736807057v4.png\"></p><ol><li>here you specify the location of the languages root element.&nbsp;This is done by using an XPath expression.&nbsp;In this example the TMX file looks like this:</li></ol><p><br></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1423.pastedimage1636736927237v5.png\"></p><ol><li>The XPath expression to the languages root element would be:&nbsp;</li><li><strong>/tmx/body/tu</strong></li><li>This is because the languages are defined within the&nbsp;<strong>tuv</strong>&nbsp;element for each&nbsp;<strong>tu</strong>&nbsp;element.</li><li>each language in your file can be added to this section by clicking on the Add button, defining the Language and specifying the XPath query that locates the actual translatable content:</li><li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2275.pastedimage1636737129586v6.png\"></li><li>In this example the translatable content is actually in the&nbsp;<strong>seg</strong>&nbsp;element, but the language defined in each case is controlled by an attribute in the&nbsp;<strong>tuv</strong>&nbsp;element.&nbsp;So the XPath expression needs to specifically point to where each language will be found:</li><li><strong>tuv[@xml:lang='EN']/seg</strong></li><li>The screenshot above shows multiple languages because the file is a multilingual TMX and this filetype can support the creation of a multilingual project from this single TMX file.</li><li>A simpler example could be something like a basic bilingual XML file:</li><li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8244.pastedimage1636737434413v7.png\"></li><li>Here the settings would be like this:</li><li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0675.pastedimage1636737483666v8.png\"></li><li>The Language Root XPath would be:</li><li><strong>/sitecore/item</strong></li><li>The location would be the language element used in the file:</li><li><strong>en</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>de&nbsp;</strong>respectively.</li></ol><p>It's very important to make sure that the paths are correct.&nbsp;The main reason for the filetype not being associated with the file opened for translation is failing to accurately specify the XPath locations.&nbsp;If you have any questions on how to do this for your filetype the recommendation is to ask for help in the appropriate forum</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/translationproductivity/f/regex_and_xpath\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Regex and XPath</a></p><p>XML files come in all shapes and sizes so some knowledge of how to use XPath is essential.&nbsp;If you are completely new to XPath you may also find these resources useful:</p><ul><li>XPath tutorial in W3Schools -&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.w3schools.com/xml/xpath_intro.asp\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://www.w3schools.com/xml/xpath_intro.asp</a></li><li>XML in a nutshell, chapter 9 XPath -&nbsp;<a href=\"https://docstore.mik.ua/orelly/xml/xmlnut/ch09_01.htm\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://docstore.mik.ua/orelly/xml/xmlnut/ch09_01.htm</a></li><li>More regex? No, it's time for something completely different -&nbsp;<a href=\"https://multifarious.filkin.com/2013/07/30/xpath/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://multifarious.filkin.com/2013/07/30/xpath/</a></li><li>X Files - ATA 56 -&nbsp;<a href=\"https://multifarious.filkin.com/2015/11/07/x-files-ata56/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://multifarious.filkin.com/2015/11/07/x-files-ata56/</a></li></ul><p>The last two at least relate to using Trados Studio.</p><h4>Adding additional languages</h4><p>A last point on this section.&nbsp;If you wish to add a new language into the file that wasn't present in the source then this is also possible.&nbsp;Just add the new language to your language settings for the project and then create the appropriate rule in the language mappings.&nbsp;You can now translate this additional language and when you save your Multilingual Translations via the batch task you will find the new language has been added to the file based on the XPath expression you used to map this additional language.</p><h3>Embedded Content</h3><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5381.pastedimage1636738282015v9.png\"></p><p>This section is the same as for any embedded content processing in Trados Studio.&nbsp;An important point is that you do not have this ability to use another filetype (html for example) as an embedded content processor in the XLIFF filetypes supported by Trados Studio.&nbsp;So this is a big advantage, especially when handling XLIFF files that make heavy use of large CDATA sections in single translation units.</p><h3>Placeholders</h3><p>This section supports the ability to \"tag\" text as a placeholder in Trados Studio irrespective of whether you are also using an embedded content processor or not.&nbsp;This is an extremely useful feature because all other filetypes in Trados Studio that offer embedded content processing are either one or the other, so regex rules for everything OR use another fietype like html for the embedded content processing.</p><p>The filetype comes with four predefined rules that you can use if appropriate, or remove and just create your own rules.&nbsp;The process is simple, just click on Add:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6518.pastedimage1636738816010v11.png\"></p><p>Then write your regular expression, define the segmentation hint, and if you wish you can add a description.&nbsp;It's worth adding a description because you can also i<strong>mport/export</strong>&nbsp;the rules you have created to make it easier to share with others, or even maintain a library for yourself that you can simply import and then remove the ones you don't need as a starting point when setting up your filetype for a specific project.&nbsp;The different options available to you are defined across the toolbar.</p><h3>Entities</h3><p><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">This section provides entity support that works the same way as it does for any filetype in Trados Studio that supports them.&nbsp;See the&nbsp;</span><a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/LiveContent/content/en-US/Trados%20Studio%20Help-v6/GUID-BBB0BA35-984B-4BDD-9412-F33C8D7C3EB5\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong>producthelp</strong></a><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">&nbsp;for more details.</span></p><h3>QuickInsert</h3><p>This section provides support for QuickInserts that works the same way as it does for any filetype in Trados Studio that supports them.&nbsp;&nbsp;See the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/813470/515254/trados-studio-2021-sr2/quickinsert-page\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong>producthelp</strong></a>&nbsp;for more details.</p><h2>Using the Multilingual Filetype</h2><h3>Creating a project</h3><ol><li>the first thing to note is that your project template/settings must contain all the languages you have specified in your filetype settingsthe second thing to note is that if your filetype settings are incorrect in any way then the&nbsp;<strong>Multilingual XML Filetype</strong>&nbsp;will not be recognised as it has in the screenshot above</li></ol><p>When you add a multilingual XML file, properly set up, your multilingual project will be created for all the language pairs specified allowing you to manage the project in the usual way.</p><h3>Batch Tasks</h3><p>In the&nbsp;Installation section above it was mentioned that two new batch tasks are added to Trados Studio:</p><ul><li>&nbsp;Import Multilingual Translations</li><li>Generate Multilingual Translations</li></ul><p><em>Import Multilingual Translations</em></p><p>This batch task will import all translations that may be present in the source file.&nbsp;If you have the Professional version of Trados Studio you can create custom tasks that include this task as part of the project preparation.&nbsp;In the screenshot below you can see the task is a custom task that only does three things - converts to translatable format, copies to target languages and imports the multilingual translations:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1256.pastedimage1636739965657v3.png\"></p><p>If you are using the Freelance version of Trados Studio then you will need to run this task after the project has been created.</p><p>The options allow you to do four things:</p><ol><li><strong>overwrite existing translations</strong>. So if you are importing a file translated elsewhere and you need to use that as the approved translations you can import it and replace anything in your project with the target translations of your approved file.<strong>define the origin system</strong>.&nbsp;This will add whatever information to write in here into the SDLXLIFF file.&nbsp;Maybe useful for filtering on the file at some point or for identifying where the imported translations came from for example.<strong>set the segment status</strong>. This just allows you to define what status the imported translations should have when opened in the Trados Studio editor.<strong>exclude segments</strong>. This will prevent segments with the selected criteria in Trados Studio from being updated with the contents of the imported file.<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6683.pastedimage1636740413383v4.png\"></li></ol><p><em>Generate Multilingual Translations</em></p><p>This batch task will generate the target multilingual file when you have completed the project (or earlier if you wish).&nbsp;It is different to the out of the box generate target translations, which will also work, because it will take the target translations from each of the files you have translated in a multilingual project and merge them back into single files again.&nbsp;This will be very beneficial because the out of the box task will create separate files for each language pair being translated and you would then have to merge them all into single files After all the options are done, click the&nbsp;<strong>\"OK\"</strong>&nbsp;button to save the settings.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/c045267a-a75b-4ac4-8385-4c3bf369c86c.png?dl=1\"></p><p>2) Click the&nbsp;<strong>\"Sign out\"</strong>&nbsp;button to delete the user credentials. After sign out is done, click on the&nbsp;<strong>\"Settings...\"</strong>&nbsp;to sign in again.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/1aedab24-9dd8-44d1-8848-9631e83bdf9c.png?dl=1\"></p><h1>2.How to use the dictionary and translation memory of NiuTrans Cloud Platform</h1><h2>2.1 Create Dictionary and add terms</h2><p>1) Open NiuTrans cloud platform homepage, and sign in with your account credentials. After you sign in, click&nbsp;<strong>\"Console(控制台)\"</strong>.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/2b2ce3bf-32b6-4484-a79a-4722f6f60049.png?dl=1\"></p><p>2) At the console page you can create dictionary by going to&nbsp;<strong>Resource Management(资源管理) -&gt; Dictionary(术语词典) -&gt; Create Dictionary(创建词库)</strong>&nbsp;.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/a1906daa-f94e-4a2a-abfb-31fa1641a390.png?dl=1\"></p><p>3) At the window of \"Create Dictionary(创建词库)\", create a dictionary name(词库名称) and click&nbsp;<strong>\"OK(确定)\"</strong>. Note that you may take it as the default dictionary(设为默认词库).</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/c1212588-111b-491a-856e-ca848c6e7e6d.png?dl=1\"></p><p>4) After the dictionary is created(创建词库), click&nbsp;<strong>\"Add Terms(添加术语)\"</strong>&nbsp;and then enter the term management page.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/9fb32a3f-95ba-4328-9658-fc37835b78fb.png?dl=1\"></p><p>5) At the term management page you may click&nbsp;<strong>\"Add a Term(添加术语)\"</strong>&nbsp;to add your own terms to the NiuTrans engine.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/52c99f8d-5997-4b59-bf8b-8ef2d1284d56.png?dl=1\"></p><p>6) At the window of \"Add a Term(添加术语)\", select the language direction(语言方向) for the term, enter its original text(源语) and target translation(目标语).</p><p>Note that the language direction(语言方向) is Chinese-centered, that is, you may define the language direction between Chinese and another one foreign language, but not between two foreign languages.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/4b576bf2-0924-4cb3-8885-6c082a909d52.png?dl=1\"></p><p>7) At the term management page you may also click&nbsp;<strong>\"Add Terms in Batch(批量添加)\"</strong>.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/fb71a386-a490-4eb8-8190-e80834e62316.png?dl=1\"></p><p>8) At the window of “Add Terms in Batch(批量添加术语)” you may download the “Sample File(示例文件)” by clicking on it, write your terms into the downloaded sample file, select the language direction(请选择语言方向) of the terms, and then upload the file to the NiuTrans engine.</p><p>Note that the file should be txt, UTF-8 without BOM, no more than 2M.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/fb8c01e9-cbcb-402c-abf0-67d2b98957f7.png?dl=1\"></p><h2>2.2 Create Translation Memory(创建句库)</strong>&nbsp;, and its operations are similar to the dictionary.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/a1906daa-f94e-4a2a-abfb-31fa1641a390.png?dl=1\"></p><p>2) At the window of \"Create memory is created(创建句库), click&nbsp;<strong>\"Add Sentence Pairs(添加记忆)\"</strong>&nbsp;and then enter the translation memory management page.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2f1b406d-feca-44d5-b6e4-2e88004a8f15/Resources/e6a6d1ae-5946-43c2-9c40-8303a6f952aa.png?dl=1\"></p><p>4) At the translation memory and Trados Studio. Enhance your productivity through seamless integration with the OpenAI provider for Trados. This powerful combination automates complex tasks, boosts efficiency, and delivers precise, contextually aware translations.</p>\r\n<h3>Supported providers</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>OpenAI</li>\r\n<li>Azure OpenAI</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Supported ways of working with the OpenAI Provider for Trados</h3>\r\n<p>For initial translation and revisions, you can use OpenAI in these supported workflows:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Interactively</strong>: On a per-segment basis</li>\r\n<li><strong>Batch Automation:</strong> Automate translation tasks for increased efficiency</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>Technical Requirements</h1>\r\n<h3>1. Minimum Requirements</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Trados Studio 2024</li>\r\n<li>Subscription with OpenAI</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>2. Installation</h3>\r\n<p>Install the OpenAI provider for Trados directly within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore or from the RWS AppStore.</p>\r\n<h3>3. Access</h3>\r\n<p>Once installed, OpenAI can be accessed in various ways:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Trados Studio &gt; Tell Me</li>\r\n<li>Default: <code>File</code> &gt; <code>Options</code> &gt; <code>Language Pairs</code> &gt; <code>All Language Pairs</code> &gt; <code>Translation Memories and Automated Translation</code> &gt; &quot;Use&quot; dropdown list</li>\r\n<li>Selected Project: <code>Project Settings</code> &gt; <code>Language Pairs</code> &gt; <code>All Language Pairs</code> &gt; <code>Translation Memories and Automated Translation</code> &gt; &quot;Use&quot; dropdown list</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>Key Features</h1>\r\n<p>There are four areas to set up as part of the configuration:</p>\r\n<h5><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/dd1862b1-dd61-4f61-a29f-f45e8ad19fd8/Resources/d55da80e-2cc5-4256-aa09-d1e3f5a1d2ce.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></h5>\r\n<br>  \r\n\r\n<h3>1. Connections</h3>\r\n<p>Authenticate your connection based on your subscription. Configuration fields include:</p>\r\n<p><strong>Name:</strong> User-defined for easy reference.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Provider:</strong> Choose from OpenAI or Azure.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Endpoint:</strong> </p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>OpenAI default: <code>https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions</code></li>\r\n<li>Azure: Uses a private IP address for a secure connection, based on your custom configuration.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>API Key:</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>OpenAI: Generate or obtain it from your OpenAI profile.</li>\r\n<li>Azure: Get support from your Azure Administrator.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Model:</strong> Availability depends on your subscription.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Model Type:</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Chat Completions: Generates responses in a conversational context.</li>\r\n<li>Completions: Generates text based on a given prompt.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Reasoning Effort:</strong> Limits the amount of reasoning performed by reasoning models. Lower values can produce faster responses and reduce the number of tokens used for reasoning.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Verbosity:</strong> Adjusts response verbosity. Lower values produce shorter answers.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Authentication Validation:</strong> Test your configuration using the <strong>Test Connection</strong> feature.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Managing Connections:</strong> Each successful connection is listed for future reference and can be edited or deleted.</p>\r\n<h3>2. Prompts</h3>\r\n<p>Out-of-the-box prompts include:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Default translation</li>\r\n<li>Formal translation</li>\r\n<li>Multiple translations</li>\r\n<li>Reduced length</li>\r\n<li>No 3rd person singular pronouns</li>\r\n<li>Refine translations</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>You can edit or delete existing prompts. Any updates will be listed within Trados Copilot - AI Assistant.</p>\r\n<p>To add a new prompt, select <strong>Add</strong>, and then configure the following:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p><strong>Prompt Name:</strong> A short, descriptive name for your prompt. Used to identify it in the list.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p><strong>System Instructions:</strong> If necessary, adjust the default instructions to match your preferred tone, terminology, or persona.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Important!</strong> To ensure compatibility with Trados Studio, do not remove or modify the required JSON output schema. The integration relies on this structured format to map translations and comments to the correct segments.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Prompt Text:</strong> An instruction given to generate a specific response.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>3. Search Options</h3>\r\n<p>You can enable various features that impact behavior and output:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Ignore translated segments:</strong> Only generate output for segments that are Not Translated or Draft.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Ignore locked segments:</strong> Skip segments with a locked status.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Enable Terminology-aware Translation Suggestions:</strong> Incorporate terminology context for accurate translations.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Include target segment with the translation request:</strong> Use existing translations as context.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Include tags in the Translation Suggestions:</strong> Preserve tag markup from source and target content.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Use In-Processing Caching:</strong> Select this option to reuse translations already obtained from the AI provider. The in-process cache will be reset when you start Trados Studio.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>4. Search Results</h3>\r\n<p>This applies only to the <strong>Batch Pre-translate</strong> task. You can use OpenAI&#39;s translations either during the initial translation stage or later in the revision phase. During revision, you may want to automate a batch replace to update some of your existing translations, using refined prompts for improved quality and accuracy.</p>\r\n<p>In summary, you can either:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Supplement the TM: OpenAI can complete translations that were not translated using your translation memory (TM), working alongside your TM for a fully translated file.</li>\r\n<li>Prioritize OpenAI: OpenAI can replace existing translations, including those from your TM, with its own suggestions for improved accuracy, even if those earlier translations were previously validated.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Batch pre-translation is managed by:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>The status setting for <strong>Ignore translated segments.</strong></li>\r\n<li>The values set for <strong>Assign Match value</strong> compared against the <strong>Minimum match value</strong> set for the <strong>Batch Pre-translate</strong> task.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Examples:</h3>\r\n<p><em>Example 1:</em> <strong>Batch Pre-translate</strong> task with <strong>Minimum match value</strong> set to 75%, OpenAI <strong>Assign Match value</strong> to 100%, and <strong>Ignore translated segments</strong> enabled.<img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/dd1862b1-dd61-4f61-a29f-f45e8ad19fd8/Resources/ac635a1c-e719-449f-981c-af97d7e3f5f4.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Result: Confirmed segments that were pre-translated from the TM remain unchanged. All other segments in a draft or unconfirmed state are overwritten with OpenAI translations.</p>\r\n<p><em>Example 2:</em> <strong>Batch Pre-translate</strong> task with <strong>Minimum match value</strong> set to 75%, OpenAI <strong>Assigned Match value</strong> to 100%, and <strong>Ignore translated segments</strong> disabled.<img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/dd1862b1-dd61-4f61-a29f-f45e8ad19fd8/Resources/43517cb9-950b-4130-a685-e4b6196d4155.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Result: All segments are overwritten with OpenAI translations because <strong>Ignore translated segments</strong> was disabled.</p>\r\n<p><em>Example 3</em>: <strong>Batch Pre-translate</strong> task with <strong>Minimum match value</strong> set to 75%, OpenAI <strong>Assigned Match value</strong> to 99%, and <strong>Ignore translated segments</strong> enabled. In the SDLXLIFF file, there are also 100% fuzzy match value segments that are not confirmed.<img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/dd1862b1-dd61-4f61-a29f-f45e8ad19fd8/Resources/70e4f333-4117-4863-850f-7fb622bcdef5.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Result: Confirmed segments that were pre-translated from the TM remain unchanged. Segments in a draft or unconfirmed state, as well as those with a fuzzy match of 99% or lower, are overwritten with OpenAI translations. Segments with an unconfirmed 100% TM match are not overwritten because the 100% TM match takes precedence over the OpenAI <strong>Assigned Match value</strong> of 99%.</p>\r\n<h1>Working with OpenAI in Trados Studio</h1>\r\n<p>You can engage with OpenAI in Trados Studio in the following ways:</p>\r\n<h3>1. Translation Provider</h3>\r\n<p>Use OpenAI to retrieve and apply translations through the Editor&#39;s <strong>Translation Results</strong> window. Automate tasks such as <strong>Analysis</strong> and <strong>Pre-translation</strong> to boost productivity.</p>\r\n<p>Batch Pre-translate workflow support:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Incremental</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>First, review translations obtained from initial <strong>Batch Pre-Translate</strong> tasks.</p>\r\n<p>Then, run a second batch task to apply OpenAI for refined translations.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>New batch task: <strong>Pre-translate Files with OpenAI provider for Trados</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>To use certain OpenAI features effectively, translations must be applied to SDLXLIFF files. To support a fully automated approach that supports OpenAI being leveraged despite even when other providers are enabled, we recommend creating a custom batch task:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>Initially use <strong>Pre-Translate Files</strong> to leverage translations from other listed providers.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>In the same process, add <strong>Pre-translate Files with OpenAI provider for Trados</strong> to refine these translations based on your preferred configuration.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/dd1862b1-dd61-4f61-a29f-f45e8ad19fd8/Resources/c3a2ee0a-cc9a-48ca-b94b-48377f36678f.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<h3>2. Trados Copilot - AI Assistant</h3>\r\n<p>AI Assistant is a great companion when it comes to working with your LLM interactively.</p>\r\n<p>It offers extended features such as:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Alternative custom suggestions</li>\r\n<li>Specific prompts and settings</li>\r\n<li>Highlighting translated terms</li>\r\n<li>Visual tracking of changes</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>How much will this cost me?</h1>\r\n<p>To estimate token consumption with OpenAI&#39;s API integration in Trados Studio, you could approach the task by breaking down your testing and estimation process as follows:</p>\r\n<p><strong>1. Understanding token consumption for full document vs. segment-based prompts</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Full document:</strong> Test token consumption by translating a few representative full documents you normally process in Trados Studio 2024 using OpenAI. Keep track of each document’s word count and token usage. This provides a base estimate of average tokens per word or per document.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Segment-based prompts:</strong> Run translations on a representative sample of segments (e.g., 20-30) and calculate the average token usage per segment. OpenAI&#39;s token consumption varies with segment length, including segments of different lengths will help you build a more accurate estimate.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>2. Calculating average token consumption per word/prompt</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>For OpenAI’s GPT models, one token corresponds to <strong>roughly 4 characters in English</strong>, which is approximately <strong>0.75 words</strong>. Keep in mind that languages with longer or more complex word structures (such as German) may consume more tokens per segment.</li>\r\n<li>Based on OpenAI’s average, you could use the following formula: <strong>Approximate tokens per word</strong> = Total tokens / Total word count of the translated document</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>3. Practical testing in the Trados Studio 2024 AI prompting framework</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Leverage Trados Studio’s new flexible AI prompting options for both document and segment-based translations.</li>\r\n<li>Track token usage across a sample set of documents and segments, calculating the average for typical work cases.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>4. Estimating impact on existing token consumption</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Once you have an average tokens-per-word figure, apply it to your typical daily/weekly/monthly word count to estimate expected consumption. This helps you project your annual token needs and understand the potential cost impact of using OpenAI.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>5. General reference values for planning</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>As a rough benchmark, you can treat <strong>1,000 tokens as equivalent to about 750 words in English</strong>. For more accurate planning, tailor your estimates to your specific content and translation volume.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>By carrying out some initial trials with typical documents and segments, you’ll be able to fine-tune estimates and better understand the potential cost implications of incorporating OpenAI into your workflows.</p>\r\n<h1>Frequent Questions and Answers</h1>\r\n<p><strong>Q: What can I do if I am having issues connecting?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Check if you have a paid OpenAI API account; you should be able to confirm this from <a href=\"https://platform.openai.com/account/billing/overview\">here</a>. You can also check your API key that you created from <a href=\"https://platform.openai.com/api-keys\">here</a>, and added this to the model connection settings. For further assistance, review the log files. You can find them at: <code>C:\\Users\\username\\AppData\\Roaming\\Trados AppStore\\OpenAI provider for Trados\\Logs</code>.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: How can I tell if a segment was translated using OpenAI?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: When you have an SDLXLIFF file open in the <strong>Editor</strong> view, users will see a blue NMT in the middle <strong>Context</strong> view with details confirming the provider name. You can identify it via the <strong>Provider Name</strong> being set to <strong>OpenAI provider for Trados.</strong></p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: What is the default status of my segments if I use OpenAI?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Segments will be in draft state and will need confirmation.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: How can I set segments to confirmed?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: While there is no built-in feature for this, you can use the Advanced Display Filter against all segments that have the origin set as <strong>Neural Machine Translation.</strong> Once filtered, you can either batch select the segments to change status or use the Segment Status Switcher available via the Integrated RWS AppStore.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Which terminology providers are supported for terminology-aware translation?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: All terminology providers are supported, provided they offer recognized terms when in the <strong>Editor</strong> view.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: I have multiple terminology providers and recognized terms listed. Which one will be used for terminology awareness?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: When multiple terms are recognized, the AI determines which one to apply based on its internal prioritization logic.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: What do I need to do for my terms to be used for OpenAI translation?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Regardless of your selected prompt, <strong>Terminology-aware Translation Suggestions</strong> needs to be enabled. However, we recommend that you keep your prompt scope updated for future reference. Therefore, you may want to reference any dependencies on terms for clarity.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Can I have more than one instance of OpenAI listed as a provider?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Yes, you can. For output comparison, it’s best to list your primary OpenAI instance under <strong>Project Settings</strong> and review the initial OpenAI translation via the <strong>Translation Results</strong> window. For alternative translations and comparisons, add your second instance within Trados Copilot - AI Assistant.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Does OpenAI support track changes?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Yes, this feature is supported within Trados Copilot - AI Assistant.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: What if I want to update translations as part of a review?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: You can either apply the revised changes as part of a <strong>Batch Pre-translate</strong> task or interactively within Trados Copilot - AI Assistant. Once you define the type of change, use an updated prompt and ensure <strong>Include target segments with the translation request</strong> is enabled.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Why is the output the same even after changing settings or prompts?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: The output might be cached. Disable <strong>Use In-Processing Caching</strong> in the search options to see the changes.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Are logs available?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Yes, logs are located at: <code>C:\\Users\\[username]\\AppData\\Roaming\\Trados AppStore\\OpenAI provider for Trados\\Logs</code>.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Where are my connection details stored?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Connection details are stored at: <code>C:\\Users\\[username]\\AppData\\Roaming\\Trados AppStore\\OpenAI provider for Trados\\Settings\\Connections.xml</code>.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Where are my prompts stored and can I share them?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Prompts can be shared by sharing the relevant file located at: <code>C:\\Users\\[username]\\AppData\\Roaming\\Trados AppStore\\OpenAI provider for Trados\\Settings\\ProviderSettings.xml</code>.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n Enterprise"}]}],"categories":[{"id":7,"name":"Connectors"}]},{"id":197,"downloadCount":4022,"paidFor":false,"updatedAt":"2025-01-10T22:06:20.542815Z","name":"PDF Assistant for Trados","description":"<p>The application is designed to support the conversion of PDF files into a DOCX so that you can improve the quality of the DOCX prior to translating it in Trados Studio.&nbsp;The reason we have taken this approach of convert prior to translation is because PDF to DOCX conversion without professional editing software can&nbsp;sometimes cause formatting issues, resulting in a document that looks different from the original PDF.&nbsp;If you convert first you can correct these issues before translating which leads to a better user experience overall.</p><p><span style=\"color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground); background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color);\">It's important to note that the quality of the conversion largely depends on the quality of the original PDF and the conversion software used.&nbsp;Some conversion tools may produce better results than others.&nbsp;This \"</span><strong style=\"color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground); background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color);\">Add-In</strong><span style=\"color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground); background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color);\">\" initially makes use of the Microsoft Word desktop API providing simple text conversion and also some OCR capabilities.&nbsp;Whilst you could simply use Word and avoid the \"</span><strong style=\"color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground); background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color);\">Add-In</strong><span style=\"color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground); background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color);\">\" altogether it's worth noting that the plugin does provide more support than Microsoft makes available through Microsoft Word, in particular around OCR capability.</span></p><p><br></p><p>To learn how to use this application, please visit&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/6598/pdf-assistant-for-trados\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">PDF Assistant for Trados</a>&nbsp;in the RWS Community wiki</p>","documentation":"<p>The&nbsp;<strong><em>PDF Assistant for Trados</em></strong>&nbsp;is an&nbsp;<strong><em>Add-In</em></strong>&nbsp;for Trados Studio that supports the conversion of a PDF to a DOCX so it can be successfully translated and delivered as a DOCX target file.</p><h2>Installation</h2><p>The application is an sdlplugin and can be installed either by visiting the RWS AppStore, downloading, and then manually installing by double clicking the sdlplugin file in the usual way.&nbsp;Alternatively the plugin can be installed through the Integrated AppStore in Trados Studio.&nbsp;For this to work you must have Microsoft Office installed.&nbsp;The testing was carried out on computers using Office 365 and not on older versions.</p><p>It's important to note that whilst this tool can do a decent job of converting most PDF files to DOCX it is not a process that is guaranteed to work 100% of the time.&nbsp;Working with PDF files can be a tricky business and it's recommended you review the section on \"Working with PDFs\" to understand a little where limitations can occur.&nbsp;If you cannot handle your PDF with this plugin you may require a more sophisticated tool such as&nbsp;<a href=\"https://pdf.abbyy.com/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">Abbyy FineReader</a>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.adobe.com/uk/acrobat.html\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">Adobe Acrobat Pro</a>&nbsp;which are designed specifically for working with this format.</p><h2>Where is it installed?</h2><p>The plugin is installed into the ribbon in the \"<strong><em>Add-Ins</em></strong>\" tab and into the \"<strong><em>Toolbox</em></strong>\" group:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1678138141855v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h2>Working with PDFs</h2><p>The application is designed to support the conversion of PDF files into a DOCX so that you can improve the quality of the DOCX prior to translating it in Trados Studio.&nbsp;The reason we have taken this approach is because PDF to DOCX conversion without professional editing software can&nbsp;sometimes cause formatting issues, resulting in a document that looks different from the original PDF.</p><p>The more common problems that can occur during PDF to DOCX conversion would be things like:</p><ol><li><strong><em>Text and image placement:</em></strong>&nbsp;Sometimes, the text and image placement can become distorted during conversion, causing the final document to look different from the original PDF.</li><li><strong><em>Formatting issues:</em></strong>&nbsp;PDFs often have complex formatting, such as columns, tables, and graphs. These elements can be difficult to convert to DOCX, leading to formatting issues in the final document.</li><li><strong><em>Fonts:</em></strong>&nbsp;If the PDF contains fonts that are not installed on the computer doing the conversion, the text can appear differently in the final document.</li><li><strong><em>Large files:</em></strong>&nbsp;PDF files can be very large, and converting them to DOCX can result in large files that take up a lot of storage space.</li><li><strong><em>Security features:</em></strong>&nbsp;Some PDFs have security features that prevent copying and pasting, which can make it difficult to convert the document to DOCX.</li><li><strong><em>OCR issues:</em></strong>&nbsp;If the PDF contains scanned images or text that was not originally digital, OCR (optical character recognition) software is needed to convert the text.&nbsp;However, OCR can sometimes produce errors or miss characters, leading to mistakes in the final document.</li><li><strong><em>Unnecessary Tags:</em></strong>&nbsp;any of the above problems can lead to many unnecessary control tags being inserted into the DOCX that will become visible when working with a translation tool.</li><li><strong><em>Poor Segmentation:</em></strong>&nbsp;similarly any of the above issues can lead to unnecessary hard returns being added into the DOCX and these will also make translation more difficult than is necessary.</li><li><strong><em>Incorrect character display:</em></strong>&nbsp;If the character encoding is incorrect, it can cause characters to be displayed incorrectly in the final document. For example, some characters may appear as question marks or boxes especially with Asian character sets.</li><li><strong><em>Missing characters:</em></strong>&nbsp;In some cases, incorrect encoding can cause certain characters to be missing from the final document.&nbsp;This can result in text that is difficult to read or understand.</li><li><strong><em>Encoding conflicts:</em></strong>&nbsp;If different parts of the document are encoded in different ways, it can cause conflicts and errors during conversion.&nbsp;For example, some characters may be encoded in UTF-8 while others are encoded in ASCII, leading to errors when the document is converted to a PDF or other format.</li></ol><p>It's important to note that the quality of the conversion largely depends on the quality of the original PDF and the conversion software used.&nbsp;Some conversion tools may produce better results than others.&nbsp;This \"<strong><em>Add-In</em></strong>\" initially makes use of the Microsoft Word desktop API providing simple text conversion and also some OCR capabilities.&nbsp;Whilst you could simply use Word and avoid the \"<strong><em>Add-In</em></strong>\" altogether it's worth noting that the plugin does provide more support than Microsoft makes available through Microsoft Word, in particular around OCR capability.</p><h2>Using the \"<em>Add-in</em>\"</h2><h3>Adding your files</h3><p>The&nbsp;<strong><em>PDF Assistant for Trados</em></strong>&nbsp;is started by clicking on the icon in the ribbon.&nbsp;This opens up a small wizard where you can add your files:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1678656126017v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>You can add as many files as you like, in as many languages as you like, but keep in mind the process could take a considerable amount of time and may even run out of memory if you ask for too much.&nbsp;How many files you can use really depends on the number of pages, number of images in the file, amount of OCR work required etc.&nbsp;Think about the work you are about to carry out and don't expect miracles!</p><p>The files or folders can be added via drag and drop, or by using the small icons in the wizard.&nbsp;In this example two PDF files have been added.&nbsp;An English language text containing two images, one that needs to be OCR'd and one that does not; and a Korean document that is non-readable, so the entire content is one big image in the PDF.</p><h3>Selecting your Provider and OCR options</h3><p>This screen allows you to do several things:</p><ol><li>select the PDF Assistant you wish to use.&nbsp;For now there is only Microsoft Word to select from.check the option to specify whether or not you wish to extract text from the images and if so (in the next screens) which ones you would like to be processed (OCR'd)</li><li><em>keep in mid that when you OCR the images you will lose any background image that was there and will only have the text that the software was able to extract</em></li></ol><p>You can cancel the process at any time if the file is too complex for the application to manage.</p><h3>Image Selection</h3><p>This part of the wizard will extract the images the software was able to identify and allow you to specify which of the images contain translatable text.</p><h3>Summary Stage</h3><p>This screen in this stage of the wizard displays a summary of the options you have chosen for the conversion.</p><h3>Preparation</h3><p>The final stage provides an indication of the progress until the conversion has completed:</p><h2>DTP the&nbsp;converted files before Translation</h2><p>Now you can open your converted PDF files as a DOCX in Microsoft Word and improve the quality of the file before you translate it.&nbsp;This way the target file will probably be ready to go, or at least require minimal editing to accommodate changes required as a result of text expansion/contraction in the target language.</p><p>A good tool for tidying up files resulting from a messy PDF conversion is TransTools available here -&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.translatortools.net/products/transtools\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">https://www.translatortools.net/products/transtools</a></p><p>In the example files, the English file contained two images, one that was OCR'd and the other treated as an image.&nbsp;The result isn't bad (PDF on the left, converted DOCX on the right) and if you were to open this PDF file in Microsoft Word both images would be handled as images, so the \"<strong><em>Add-In</em></strong>\" does provide considerable value here.&nbsp;The table needs tidying up but it is editable and could save time when more extensive text is involved:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1678655808574v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>On the Korean non-readable PDF.&nbsp;Some formatting would be required, but it's not too bad.&nbsp;The image is floating and can be positioned wherever I like, and all the text is available to me for translation.&nbsp;So some small amount of DTP work and I'll have a file that is easily translatable and the target file should be good with minimum work required:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1678656000093v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/197/versions/1438","changelog":{"Trados
