translation"}]},{"id":15,"downloadCount":8789,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2017-02-08T17:09:12.33Z","updatedAt":"2025-05-15T22:46:20.97003Z","name":"Post-Edit Compare","description":"<p>Post-Edit Compare is a tool designed to report translation modifications during the post-edit phases of a translation workflow. The tool works by comparing two versions of the same SDLXLIFF file (before and after changes were applied); the files with changes are highlighted and all modifications are included in a comparison report.</p>\r\n<p> The comparison report is formatted in a way that simplifies the understanding of what changes were applied from one version of the file to the other, along with a full break down of the modifications and related cost analysis.</p>\r\n<p> Improved handling for TERp analysis, much improved and simplified reporting process from the main Studio Projects View, ability to save reports in Excel</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>Post-Edit Compare is a tool designed to report translation modifications during the post-edit phases of a translation workflow.</p>\r\n<p>The tool works by comparing two versions of the same SDLXLIFF file (before and after changes were applied); the files with changes are highlighted and all modifications are included in a detailed comparison report.</p>\r\n<p>The comparison report is formatted in a way that simplifies the understanding of what changes were applied from one version of the file to the other, along with a full break down of the modifications and related cost analysis.</p>\r\n<p>Post-Edit Versions is fully integrated with SDL Trados Studio 2014, represented as a &#39;view&#39; from the application interface. It includes functionality that allows users to create and maintain versions of the SDL project that can be later passed to Post-Edit Compare to generate the comparison reports.</p>\r\n<p>To compare the project files, simply select two project versions and choose <strong>Compare with Post-Edit Compare</strong> to generate a comparison report. <a href=\"\">Refer to this video</a></p>\r\n<h2>Key features</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Identify quickly and keep track of the modified files by comparing the (before &amp; after) folder directories</li>\r\n<li>Create comparison reports that identify clearly the changes applied to the translations (and segment properties) of the files in a translation workflow.</li>\r\n<li>Report an overview and full break down of the cost analysis related to the translation modifications.</li>\r\n<li>Customize how the differences are highlighted in the comparison reports</li>\r\n<li>Provides full support for SDLXLIFF files through the integration of the SDL Trados Studio APIs</li>\r\n<li>Maintain comparison reports as a permanent record of the changes applied during each stage of the translation workflow.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Trados Studio 2024 has some additional features:</p>\r\n<p>Regulated industries require strict compliance with the approved source content. When comparing previously translated material with new files, differences in the source text can now be clearly identified.\r\nProject Managers norw able to better compare and track source changes directly against Translation Memories (TMs). This allows them to address potential issues with linguists before client delivery, and to provide clients with detailed reports that highlight the consistency and quality of the managed source content</p>\r\n<h2>Newly added features</h2>\r\n<p>Post-Edit Versions</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Create Comparison Report now lets you choose specific translation origins to generate targeted reports.</li>\r\n<li>Default Settings now includes custom report backup location</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p> Post-Edit Report View</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Project Navigation tree to help report navigation</li>\r\n<li>Generated Report has additional coloums: TM and TU (with track changes)</li>\r\n<li>Embedded navigation from Report View to Editor View at segment level</li>\r\n<li>Report Segment Filter to assist with report navigation based on segment status </li>\r\n<li>Actions to perform to support batch changes to segment status </li>\r\n<li>Segment level comments</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p> Home Tab:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Synchronization:<ul>\r\n<li>Enabled: Supports segment status chages made at report level, directly updating sdlxliff </li>\r\n<li>Disabled: Updates made to segment status , comments remain at report level</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n<li>Undock Report Viewer to support multiple screen preference</li>\r\n<li>Export Full table report into Excel format</li>\r\n<li>Report location nagivational choice</li>\r\n<li>Open Report Folder</li>\r\n<li>Open Report Backup Folder</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<p>Trados Studio minimun </p>\r\n<h2>Support</h2>\r\n<p>Post-Edit Compare was originally developed by Patrick Hartnett but today it&#39;s maintained by the Trados AppStore Team.&nbsp;All your&nbsp;support questions&nbsp;or your&nbsp;feedback/suggestions to better understand how we can improve the next releases should be posted in the&nbsp;<a Forum</a>.</p>\r\n<h2>Related Articles</h2>\r\n<p>A very interesting article (<a href=\"https://multifarious.filkin.com/2013/12/28/solving-the-post-edit-puzzle/\">Solving the Post Edit puzzle</a>) written by Paul Filkin explains in some detail the considerations to take into account when analyzing Post-Edit modifications and the benefits of using Post-Edit Compare to solve/automate this.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n Cloud","description":"<p>Drawing inspiration from the Trados Studio concept, this AddOn enables users to simulate translations.</p>\r\n<p>The main goal is to identify potential issues in the user interface (UI) or user experience (UX) before the actual translation process begins. It allows Engineers and Project Managers to spot problems such as source content processing, truncation and layout issues or encoding errors early in the development process, thereby saving time and resources. See <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/257?tab=documentation\">documentation</a> for more details.</p>\r\n<p>How it works:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Text expansion/contraction: The pseudo-translator alters the text to simulate how translations might expand or contract</li>\r\n<li>Special character support: This helps identify issues with encoding, font rendering, or text input/output</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>Drawing inspiration from the Trados Studio concept, this AddOn enables users to simulate translations using expansion factors which can be specified on a per target language basis. Dummy translation either originates from the pseudo-translation dictionary, or special characters like $.\u00A0The AddOn supports two key workflows: running pseudo-translation only or sequentially whereby after the pseudo-translation task has been completed, the project files are reprocessed for actual translation.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p><strong>1.Early Detection of Layout Issues</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Text Expansion: Simulating how text length might change when translated into different languages helps identify whether UI elements can accommodate longer or shorter text without truncation or overlap.</li>\r\n<li>Right-to-Left Language Support: It ensures that the UI correctly handles languages with right-to-left text direction, avoiding alignment and display issues.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>2.Improved Character Set and Encoding Handling</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Special Characters and Non-Latin Scripts: Pseudo-translation introduces characters from various languages, helping to detect encoding problems, font rendering issues, or missing character support early on.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>3.Cost and Time Efficiency</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Preemptive Problem Solving: By catching potential issues before the actual translation process, it reduces the need for expensive and time-consuming post-translation bug fixes.</li>\r\n<li>Streamlined Process: Engineers and Project Managers can address localisation issues earlier in the development cycle, reducing the likelihood of delays later on.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>4.Better User Experience (UX) Design</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Consistency Across Languages: Ensures that the application provides a consistent user experience across all supported languages, with proper spacing, layout, and text readability.</li>\r\n<li>Accessibility: Helps in designing an interface that remains accessible and user-friendly, even when translated into languages with different reading directions or text lengths</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>5.Enhanced Localisation Testing</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Automated Testing: Pseudo-translation can be integrated into automated testing frameworks, allowing for continuous verification of localisation readiness as the software evolves.</li>\r\n<li>Comprehensive Coverage: It helps in covering edge cases that might not be immediately obvious, such as how the application handles very short or very long strings in different languages</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>6.Risk Mitigation</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Avoiding Last-Minute Changes: Identifying localisation issues early reduces the risk of having to make last-minute changes that could introduce new bugs or disrupt the release schedule.</li>\r\n<li>Quality Assurance: Improves overall software quality by ensuring that all potential localisation challenges are addressed before the product goes to market.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<p>Once AddOn has been installed and configured, you need to\u00A0create a Translation Engine that is dedicated to the Pseudo-translation task. This would be done under Resources &gt; Linguistic Resources &gt; Translation Engines &gt; New Translation Engine.</p>\r\n<p>Here you are able to add Pseudo-translation as a Machine Translation provider</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<h5><strong>Supported Workflows</strong></h5>\r\n<p><strong>1.Pseudo-translation only</strong></p>\r\n<p>A recommended workflow for any project manger who is on boarding a new client or getting familiar with a new range of source files as the focus will be on content processing.</p>\r\n<p>A project is dedicated purely for the purpose of applying Pseudo-translation, with a dependency on the Pseudo-translation Translation Engine. Project files are left at the translation stage so they can be viewed in the Editor view.</p>\r\n<p>Benefit of this workflow is that users can assess how source content has been processed based on the file type configuration. Choosing to finalise the files will then give insight, as to how the the native target document has been generated and if additional provisions would be require, example custom file type management, extensive DTP.</p>\r\n<p><strong>2.Pseudo-translate followed by official translation\u00A0(sequential)</strong></p>\r\n<p>Requirement is for the project name to start with &quot;ext&quot;, The project is created,initially with the Pseudo-translation Translation Engine. Project files get to the translation stage so that pseudo-translation can be applied. Thereafter all other workflow stages are skipped. The pseudo-translated files are then left in a cancelled sate so that the target files can be downloaded.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"data:image/png;base64,\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Once the pseudo-translation has been completed, the project source files are automatically readded to the project for the purpose of official translation and complete workflow compliance, using the translation engine specified within the Pseduo-translation configuration.</p>\r\n<p>Benefit of this workflow is that within the same project, users can estimate post translation efforts and requirements in parallel with the start of official translation workflow, without Note with this workflow, users cant address any file type related issues that may occur.</p>\r\n<h5><strong>Installing Pseudo-translation Provider AddOn</strong></h5>\r\n<p>It can be\u00A0installed in 2 ways:</p>\r\n<p>1.Within your Language Cloud tenant.</p>\r\n<p>Access your account settings and from the drop down menu, select RWS AppStore. From the AddOn’s listed select Pseudo-translation Provider to install</p>\r\n<p>2.Alternatively you can install it via the\u00A0<a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/143\">RWS AppStore</a></p>\r\n<h5><strong>Configuring Pseudo-translation Provider</strong></h5>\r\n<p>Once installed you can configure your preferences which will be used for all future projects that require Pseudo-translation.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/f7ff5b3d-567f-4d21-8b6f-151784cac6eb/Resources/config.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Replacement translation engine name:</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>In the event you wish to apply Pseudo-translate followed by official translation, this field is used to confirm which Translation Engine will be used for the the official translation once the project files have been reprocessed.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Apply pseudo-translation from the dictionary:</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>If the option is enabled, it will apply pseudo-translation from the dictionary.\r\nIf not enabled, it will apply the $ character</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Expansion factor:</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>When translating text between languages, the &quot;expansion factor&quot; refers to the typical change in the length of the text due to differences in language structure, vocabulary, and grammar.\r\nExpansion factor starts with 1. If you wish to see what impact the various target language expansions will have within the final document layout, you can specify custom expansions factors under Language-specific settings.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Language-specific settings:</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>An example of a custom expansion on a per target language basis {&quot;de-DE&quot;: {&quot;expansionFactor&quot;: &quot;2.0&quot;}, &quot;jp-Jp&quot;: {&quot;expansionFactor&quot;: &quot;.05&quot;}}</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Pseudo Translate Type:</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Specifies the method used for pseudo-translation:\r\n    - Random: Applies random characters or strings.\r\n    - Deterministic: Applies consistent transformations, resulting in predictable pseudo-translations.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Append Start String:</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Specify a string to be automatically appended to the beginning of each translation segment.\r\nExample: We configured the value as _ (underscore) then “Hello World” will be translated as “_Hello world”.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Append End String:</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Specify a string to be automatically appended to the end of each translation segment.\r\nExample: We configured the value as _ (underscore) then “Hello World” will be translated as “Hello world_”.\u00A0</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code 2019","minimumStudioVersion":"15.0.0"}]}],"categories":[{"id":3,"name":"Terminology"}]},{"id":16,"downloadCount":4616,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2016-11-24T16:51:44.53Z","updatedAt":"2025-01-12T18:28:51.750448Z","name":"Qualitivity","description":"<p>The name <strong>Qualitivity</strong> suggests that it is a cross between productivity and quality, simply because that is the type of functionality it is equipped to provide.</p><p>Qualitivity is a plugin for Trados Studio, that fully integrates through the API, geared to manage the data related to productivity &amp; quality as the users are working on documents from the Studio Editor.</p><p>In short, it provides functionality to track the time spent on translating, reviewing &amp; post-editing the segments from documents, but additionally includes functionality to track every single change made to the segments at a granular level and a means to generate reports based on that data in structured and readable format.</p><p><br></p><p>It also supports four Quality Metric standards out of the box (TAUS DQF, MQM Core, SAE J2450, LISA QA Model) and allows for infinite customisation to suit any rules you like.</p><p><br></p><p>It works in the background without any interaction from the linguist while they are working, gathering the data that they require for their activity.</p><p><br></p><p>To learn how to use this app and for more information,</p><p>please visit the <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/2251/qualitivity\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Qualitivity</a> Wiki on our RWS Community</p>","documentation":"<p>This wiki is based on the original links from the application to the developers home pages.&nbsp;It's not a comprehensive guide to the use of Qualitivity but we can enhance it over time and will be happy to add any guides that may have been written by people using this application.</p><p>=====================================</p><p><strong>Qualitivity</strong>&nbsp;is a plugin for SDL Studio that fully integrates with the API, geared to manage the data related to productivity &amp; quality as the users are working on documents from the Studio Editor.&nbsp;&nbsp;In short, it provides functionality to track the time spent on translating, reviewing &amp; post-editing the segments from documents, but additionally includes functionality to track every single change made to the segments at a granular level and a means to generate reports based on that data in structured and readable format.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3513.Document.Overview.Report.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3513.Document.Overview.Report.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>The name&nbsp;<strong>Qualitivity</strong>&nbsp;suggests that it is a cross between productivity and quality, simply because that is the type of functionality that it is equipped to provide.&nbsp;It works in the background without any interaction from the linguist while they are working, gathering the data required for their activity.</p><p>One of the main advantages of working with a tool like this is that it keeps a complete comparable copy of every single action applied that qualifies as an addition, deletion or adaption of any of the properties in the segments of the document that the linguist is working on.</p><p>By comparable, we mean that a record is created that encapsulates not only the latest version of the segment (<em>after the changes have been applied</em>), but also a cloned copy of the segment prior to those changes;&nbsp;so that it provides a complete cascading type of report representing each adaption to the segments.&nbsp;This has enormous potential in the type of useful information that can be generated from the data that could benefit all parties in the process, that being the linguist, LSP and/or the client.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7041.Document.Records.Report.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7041.Document.Records.Report.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>All of these are important characteristic as they provide the basis for calculating the production costs understanding not only what changed but also the time taken to make those changes. Because the data that is maintained with the plugin is so robust, some very interesting type of reports can be generated, which might make it seem like the reports are always a work in progress…</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>The built in document overview is a very comprehensive overview of all the work entertained on the document from the linguist for single or multiple edit sessions. It compiles all interactions applied by the linguist while working on the document and represents those changes in a structured/ readable format.</p><p>The cost of production is also integrated with this report taking into consideration the association between the weight of the changes applied to the segments by the linguist and the language rates associated with the activity for that document or documents.</p><p>A very important aspect in production is the time taken to do the work, including how and what changes are applied; for this purpose the tool manages the production time and keystroke data.&nbsp;&nbsp;<em>The decision to track the keystroke data is ultimately the decision of the user and ONLY tracks keystrokes from the Studio Editor window.</em></p><p>The type of production time data that is managed with the plugin:</p><ul><li>Opened and closed a document</li><li>Document active on the screen and being worked on by the linguist; useful when the linguist is working on multiple documents contemporarily or in the case that they are working on a merged file and/or a virtually merged file.&nbsp;In this case the time spent on each document depends on what segment the user is working on.</li><li>Entered and exited a segment</li><li>The date/time spent typing or adapting the content in the segment; in this case the date/time is associated with the individual keystrokes</li><li>The movement across the file</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><p>Integrated with the keystroke data, is the feature to understand what TM or MT engine was used for providing the translation, if the linguist selected more than one or adapted the translation before finalizing the translation.&nbsp;It also identifies correctly when the translation was copied from source etc…</p><p>The plugin also includes a feature that can be activated from within the Studio Editor that manages the&nbsp;<strong>Quality Metrics</strong>; this allows the user to import some of the more known standards such as&nbsp;<strong>TAUS DQF</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>SAE J2450</strong>&nbsp;&amp;<strong>&nbsp;MQM Core</strong>... for measuring quality.&nbsp;This has enormous benefit to how the quality of the translations from a document are measured in a revision cycle.&nbsp;&nbsp;The Quality Assessment report generated from this data is useful in providing an overview on the commonality of the linguistic errors, where they occurred,&nbsp;in what context and who (or what TM, MT etc…) applied them; keeping in mind that the type of reports generated from these assessments are relative to what the user is evaluating; meaning, it might also be applicable in assessing the translations from an MT engine in a certain domain.</p><p>Besides the reports built into the plugin that are generated to provide a visual representation on the data gathered, you can also export that data to Excel and/or XML format.&nbsp;This allows for interoperability at a basic level, in that it provides all parties with a means to integrate the data gathered from the plugin into their own platforms or systems… from there, they can then generate their own statistical reports, charts etc…</p><p>There is also a feature built into the plugin that permits the user to create a hand-off package of all the reports for a specific project, activity in the case that these reports from the plugin are used in the workflow between the linguist and client.</p><p>Something that I have noticed quite a lot recently as we are constantly evolving in more competitive environments, is when things go drastically wrong in a project.&nbsp;It happens to the most prepared professionals (PM, Linguists alike).</p><p>Let’s examine a case where a linguist is required to use a specific MT engine, or TM or Terminology term base as part of a contract with the LSP and because of this, the linguist spends twice as much time working on translating the content due to specific issues (i.e. incorrect TM, Termbase, bad MT et.c..) that push the initial deadline out and also has a direct impact on the quality (simply because the linguist was forced to work in a certain way…)&nbsp;The unforeseen aspects that turn your projects into a living nightmare for everyone involved is something we try to avoid, but when deadlines are pushed and the quality of your work degrades to a certain degree, the involvement with your client is inevitable and understanding what went wrong in the process becomes paramount.&nbsp;&nbsp;Because this plugin provides you with unique insight in to all interactions during the translation/revision life cycle, it can improve drastically your approach in explaining these scenarios.</p><ul><li>From the&nbsp;<strong>Linguist</strong>&nbsp;point of view: they have proof of the work that they entertained and the time it took to achieve it; this might also help in renegotiating their contract in some cases where the issues are part of the initial planning from the LSP.</li><li>From the&nbsp;<strong>LSP</strong>&nbsp;(or Language Provider) point of view: they can explain clearly the issues along with detailed reports as to reasons why deadlines are not met; these reports should also form the basis for a structured review of what could improve in the next cycle/planning</li><li>From the&nbsp;<strong>Client</strong>&nbsp;point of view: they have a clear overview of the issues with view to the details gathered at the translation level; this also builds on the trust aspect with the LSP as they are working on a much more transparent level</li></ul><p><strong>Technical Requirements</strong></p><p>The following is a list of the minimum technical requirements</p><ul><li>SDL Trados Studio 2014 CU10/2015</li><li>Windows 7, 8</li><li><a href=\"https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=42643\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Microsoft .NET Framework Version 4.5.2+</a></li><li><a href=\"https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=5124\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Open XML SDK 2.0</a></li></ul><h3>&nbsp;</h3><h3><strong>Related Articles</strong></h3><ul><li>A very comprehensive article from&nbsp;<strong>Paul Filkin</strong>&nbsp;(<em>Client Services Director for SDL Translation Productivity</em>) on&nbsp;<a href=\"http://multifarious.filkin.com/2015/06/16/qualitivity/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Qualitivity… measuring quality and productivity</a></li><li>Presentation of the&nbsp;<a Quality Dashboard Webinar</a>&nbsp;with demonstrations of integration with its API, including Qualitivity...</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Random Screen Views</strong></p><p><a Term","description":"<p><u>The Problem</u></p><p>Adding a new term to your termbase when working in Trados Studio, particularly if you have a large termbase, can be less than performant. Even using the \"Quick Add Term\" feature will cause the Term Preview window to open up and the entire termbase to refresh. This can be frustrating when all you wish to do is quickly work through a document adding new terms as you go.</p><p><br></p><p><u>The Solution</u></p><p>The \"Rapid Add Term\" plugin provides a solution to this problem by simply adding the new terms to your termbase so they are available for term recognition immediately without having to open or refresh the term preview window.</p><p><br></p><p><br></p><p>To learn how to use this application, please visit the<strong> </strong><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/35?tab=documentation\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>Documentation</strong></a><strong> </strong>page</p><p><br></p>","documentation":"<p>The \"Rapid Add Term\" plugin add new terms to your termbase so they are available for term recognition immediately without having to open or refresh the term preview window. If there are multiple termbases on the active project terms will be added in&nbsp;<strong>default</strong>&nbsp;termbase.</p><h2>Minimum Requirements</h2><ul><li>SDL Trados Studio 2019</li></ul><h2>How to find the plugin?</h2><p>You can install Rapid Add Term either from:</p><ul><li><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/35/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">Trados AppStore</a></li><li>Within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore</li></ul><p>Provided you have successfully installed RAT, you can select it within Trados Studio.&nbsp;</p><p><br></p><p>RAT plugin can be used in 2 ways:</p><ul><li>Studio shortcut Add-ins tab</li><li>Right click action in Editor</li></ul><p>You can also use keyboard shortcuts to quickly add terms to the termbase in File tab&gt;Options&gt;Keyboard shortcuts: RapidAddTerm.</p><h2>How to use Rapid Add Term</h2><p>In Editor&nbsp;view&nbsp;<strong>select source and target text</strong>&nbsp;and click \"<strong>Rapid Add Term</strong>\". Both values are mandatory if one of them are not provided a warning message will appear.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1327x952/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3187.NewTerm.png\" alt=\" \"></p><ul><li>If the term&nbsp;<strong>does not exist</strong>&nbsp;in the Termbase&nbsp;<strong>it will be added as a new entry</strong>.&nbsp;</li><li>If the&nbsp;<strong>term exist in TB</strong>&nbsp;but the&nbsp;<strong>translation does not it will be added as a&nbsp;synonym.</strong></li><li>If the term&nbsp;<strong>exist in both source and target</strong>&nbsp;a Duplicate warning message will appear and the&nbsp;<strong>term will not be added to TB.</strong></li></ul><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1250x505/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2063.synonym.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><h2><strong>Tell me feature</strong></h2><p>Use Tell Me feature to get quick access to the Documentation, AppStore forum, Git Hub pages or app settings.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Points to note</strong></p><p>- terms will not be visible in the term preview window unless you refresh it first</p><p>- if \"Lookahead\" is enabled you will still have the problem of new terms not being available to you until you refresh the lookahead cache</p><p>- this is a \"no-questions asked\" operation apart from duplicate checking</p><p>- if you have duplicates and still want to add the term you need to add it with the Quick Add Term or Add Term features in Studio.</p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/35/versions/1765","changelog":{"Trados Source TU","description":"<p><strong>Note: This application is no longer maintained or supported.<br>Custom TM fields can be used out the box as an alternative, or <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/89\">SDLTM Import Plus</a>.</strong></p>\r\n<p>SDL Trados 2007 used to have a feature that stamped the name of the file you were translating on the Translation Unit in your Translation Memory. This feature was not implemented in any of the Studio versions to date. So this plugin for SDL Trados Studio 2015 brings this back and adds some interesting options. You can decide whether you want to stamp your Translation Units with:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>   The filename you are working on</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>   The filename plus it’s full path</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>   The Project name</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>You can add any of these, or all of them, so they are automatically recording what you worked on when you translated your projects.</p>\r\n<p>This gives you the ability to filter on these for translating work in the future and for carrying out batch editing tasks to remove unwanted translations from your translation memory, or export an extract to provide to a colleague or a client.</p>\r\n<p>The plugin works as a Translation Memory provider, so you just choose RecordSourceTU when you add a Translation Memory to your project.</p>\r\n<p>You’ll be asked to select whether you want a new field created, or if you wish to append this information to an existing one, and you’re good to go.</p>\r\n<p>As always it’s a good idea to back up your Translation Memories before you start so if you decide you don’t like to work this way it’s easy to go back.</p>\r\n<p>Once installed, you can now start using Record Source TU by selecting it as a translation memory provider when you translate a new document or in the project settings.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Warning:</strong> The application will not record any information if there is no change made to an existing TU.</p>\r\n<p>If you want to learn more about working with fields in Studio then this article might be worth a read :<a href=\"https://multifarious.filkin.com/2013/07/12/fields_attributes/&quot;\">multifarious-fields and attributes</a></p>\r\n<p>As well as <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/4720/recordsource-tu\"><strong>Record Source TU</strong></a> in the RWS Community wiki</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>Record Source enhances the functionality of Trados Studio by providing additional features related to translation units (TUs) in translation memories. It is aimed at users of Trados Studio who need to enhance their translation memory management with detailed source information. By integrating seamlessly with Trados Studio and offering customizable metadata fields, the plugin improves traceability, accountability, and collaboration within translation projects. This makes it a useful addition for translators, project managers, and organizations looking to maintain high standards of translation quality and efficiency.</p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<h5>Getting Started</h5>\r\n<p><strong>1.Minimum Requirements</strong></p>\r\n<p>Trados Studio 2015</p>\r\n<p><strong>2.Installing</strong></p>\r\n<p>You can install Record Source TU directly within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore or alternatively download and install it from the RWS AppStore.</p>\r\n<p><strong>3.Access</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Once installed, Record Source TU can be accessed in various ways:</li>\r\n<li>Trados Studio Tell Me Feature</li>\r\n<li>Default Settings: File &gt; Options &gt; All Language Pairs &gt; Translation Memory and Automation &gt; Use &gt; Record Source TU</li>\r\n<li>New Project: Create New Project -&gt; One Step &gt; General &gt; Translation Results &gt; Use &gt; Record Source TU</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Tip: Update your selected project template after completing the project preparation tasks. Alternatively, update Project Templates by going to File -&gt; Setup -&gt; Project Templates, select your project template to edit.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Existing Project Selected: Project Settings -&gt; All Language Pairs &gt; Translation Memory and Automation &gt; Use &gt; Record Source TU</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<h5><strong>Working in Trados Studio</strong></h5>\r\n<p><strong>1.Settings</strong></p>\r\n<p>Once you have located Record Source TU, the UI requires configuring to meet your needs.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/13db54a3-8d42-4596-9ffb-9c4badf07aa9/Resources/4b4e7674-b015-45f2-a70b-a38f9abbd6f7.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"> </p>\r\n<p>You can enable any of the following:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Record source filename</li>\r\n<li>Record source file complete path</li>\r\n<li>Record source project name</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>These options support how custom field data is obtained. You can either:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Create a new custom field</li>\r\n<li>Select StructureContext</li>\r\n<li>Use the source filename and path.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Once setup ensure you have it enabled for Update</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/13db54a3-8d42-4596-9ffb-9c4badf07aa9/Resources/2fd3ca82-14ee-4b25-b1ac-1651bb739aa4.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p><strong>2.Editor View</strong></p>\r\n<p>As you interact with the Translation Results obtained form your TM, you will see the field values on the right</p>\r\n<h5><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/13db54a3-8d42-4596-9ffb-9c4badf07aa9/Resources/284bdd55-d8cb-40b4-9e17-a314e0ebf4bd.png?dl=1\" alt=\"\"></h5>\r\n<h5>Frequently Asked Questions:</h5>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Why don&#39;t I see the field values within the Translation Results Window when retrieving hits from my TM?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: It could be that you need to review your Translation Results Window Display Options via: File &gt; Options &gt; Editor &gt; Translation Results Window &gt; Display Options. Ensure &quot;Show translation unit field values&quot; is enabled, as well as the number of supported listings.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Can I edit my preferences?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Yes, by going back to your Project Settings, selecting your listed Record Source TU TM, then clicking on Settings.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: I have spotted a mistake with the field values that are not correct in the Translation Results Window. How do I correct the field values?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: You can right-click on the Translation Result Entry to edit the Translation Unit, or you could reconfirm your entry by adding it as a New Translation.</p>\r\n<p>Q: Can I ensure that future projects are updated with the correct field values?</p>\r\n<p>A: Yes, this is done under: All Language Pairs &gt; Translation Memory and Automation &gt; Update.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Can I prioritise Translation Results based on the field values?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Yes, this is done under: All Language Pairs &gt; Translation Memory and Automation &gt; Filters.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Q: Where can I continue to manage my field values?</strong></p>\r\n<p>A: Directly within the Translation Memory Maintenance View.</p>\r\n<h5>Limitations:</h5>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Record Source TU only supports file-based Translation Memories (*.sdlTM).</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: available and print or share them with others. But it does have some limitations:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>it can take a while to render each report when you click on it</li>\r\n<li>there is no possibility to customise the existing reports</li>\r\n<li>there is no possibility to introduce reports in other formats, possibly an analysis from another tool for example</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p> <strong><u>The Solution</u></strong></p>\r\n<p> The &quot;Reports Viewer Plus&quot; application creates a new View in Studio that mirrors the reports in the &quot;Reports&quot; view. This new view comes with several advantages:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>click on a report and it&#39;s rendered instantly</li>\r\n<li>you can edit the name of the Studio reports so they are easier to manage date:</strong></li>\r\n<li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8540.pastedimage1599335911666v5.png\" alt=\" \"></li>\r\n<li><strong>Without the date:</strong></li>\r\n<li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3443.pastedimage1599335959646v6.png\" alt=\" \"></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h3>Custom Templates</h3>\r\n<p>The Custom Templates tab looks like this:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7870.pastedimage1599336029533v7.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>In here you have the ability to add/edit/delete as many custom templates as you like to control the way Trados Studio displays the reports you are using:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2248.pastedimage1599336200744v8.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>You can:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>add the location of the custom stylesheet you wish to use</li>\r\n<li>specify which Group the stylesheet should apply to</li>\r\n<li>Specify a specific language the stylesheet should apply to if you wish</li>\r\n<li>specify what kind of reports the stylesheets should apply to.\u00A0So just out of the box Studio reports, some custom reports you are using, or all reports</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>So this can allow you to do something like this for example (please ignore the translation... it&#39;s machine translated and without proper context!):</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7220.pastedimage1599336417330v9.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6558.pastedimage1599336482090v10.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Here I created stylesheets to apply to the out of the box Studio Analysis report that apply to the FR and RO languages.\u00A0These stylesheets are also not perfect as they were created quickly to demonstrate the point... you can find a couple here which help you if you wish to quickly see how the application works.\u00A0These stylesheets are intended to be associated with a Studio Analysis Report only:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1526.Analysis.zip\">1526.Analysis.zip</a></p>\r\n<p>To use them just download and unzip to a folder of your choice, then change the path to the image.\u00A0Very basic, don&#39;t rely on the figures either, but it does illustrate the point.\u00A0We may improve upon these in due course so we have a better example.</p>\r\n<p><strong>IMPORTANT NOTE</strong>: To be able to do this you do need to have some basic knowledge of working with\u00A0<a href=\"https://www.w3schools.com/xml/xsl_intro.asp\"><strong>XSLT</strong></a>.\u00A0But if you have this you&#39;ll see this is quite a powerful feature.\u00A0If you don&#39;t use custom stylesheets you&#39;ll still find this application is very useful because it loads the reports a lot faster than the out of the box\u00A0<strong>Report Viewer</strong>\u00A0in Studio.</p>\r\n<h2>Ribbon Features</h2>\r\n<p>The application has a number of useful features that are accessible via the ribbon.</p>\r\n<h3>Add</h3>\r\n<p>The Add button allows you to add, either by drag and drop or through selection, an XML report of some sort and an XSLT stylesheet that will render the XML in a readable format:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0312.pastedimage1599397424908v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>The\u00A0<strong>Add</strong>\u00A0feature allows you to add reports for anything you like so they are permanently linked to your active project.\u00A0It&#39;s useful in at least the following circumstances:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>you are sent the Studio analysis report for a project you&#39;ve been asked to work on.</li>\r\n<li>you are sent the analysis report for a project prepared with some other CAT tool.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>To make this easier, if you happen to have Studio XML reports you have been sent we have attached stylesheets for three\u00A0common reports in this zip (<strong>Note</strong>: for guidance only as these may not work correctly with all versions of Trados Studio):</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1665.StudioReports.zip\">1665.StudioReports.zip</a></p>\r\n<p>They might also be useful for learning.</p>\r\n<h3>Edit</h3>\r\n<p>The\u00A0<strong>Edit</strong>\u00A0feature allows you to rename the report and the description to something more useful:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2287.pastedimage1599397763092v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>So you could do something like this for example:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6180.pastedimage1599398093062v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>This is much more helpful than the same name and then the date/time when the report was run.</p>\r\n<h3>Save As</h3>\r\n<p>The\u00A0<strong>Save As</strong>\u00A0feature allows you to save your report to share with others.\u00A0Currently it only supports *.html, *.mht, *.txt:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1882.pastedimage1599398397012v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>You get XML by default (of course) and this will be found in the reports folder, so you could also use your stylesheets to create pretty much anything you like if you have the XSLT knowledge to do this.\u00A0These formats will probably extend to at least match the out of the box Report Viewer capability in due course.</p>\r\n<h3>Other ribbon icons/features</h3>\r\n<p>The other features,\u00A0<strong>Remove</strong>,\u00A0<strong>Print</strong>,\u00A0<strong>Print Preview</strong>,\u00A0<strong>Page Setup</strong>\u00A0are all self explanatory and will not be covered in this article.</p>\r\n<p>The application also supports a right-click capability:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1513.pastedimage1599398736495v5.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>This provides you with fast access to the same features that are available in the ribbon plus the ability to &quot;<strong>Open folder in explorer</strong>&quot; which can be very useful if you want to use the XML reports or go back and find any of the rendered html reports.\u00A0You&#39;ll also see that the reports are saved in the same location for the project:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5315.pastedimage1599398965036v6.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<h2>Navigation menu</h2>\r\n<p>The navigation menu has three important features that are not available in the out of the box Report Viewer.</p>\r\n<h3>Filter report name</h3>\r\n<p>This feature provides a filtering mechanism so it&#39;s easier and faster to locate the report you want:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0218.pastedimage1599400079079v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Type in the word/phrase you are searching for and the view will be filtered as you type.</p>\r\n<h3>Expand/Collapse</h3>\r\n<p>A simple plus/minus feature to collapse of expand the navigation menu:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0027.pastedimage1599400271852v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<h3>Group by</h3>\r\n<p>A quick way to group the reports either by language, or the type of report (Group) they are:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0361.pastedimage1599400413615v5.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>This can be very useful given it&#39;s possible to create your own Groups, so this makes it easier to manage your reports, particularly with large projects.</p>\r\n<h1></h1>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n By providing linguists with instant visual context, Rigi eliminates the need for manual developer queries and navigational instructions, allowing translators to work with complete autonomy. This shift has a profound impact on the efficiency and speed of the entire localization process.</p>\r\n<p>As a high-performance engine designed for high-complexity environments, Rigi transforms localization from a burdensome manual task into a streamlined, automated pillar of your CI/CD pipeline. The platform delivers a massive ROI–reducing developer support time by up to 90%–and automates UI testing across all languages during the build process. By prioritizing &quot;set-and-forget&quot; reliability and deep technical efficiency, Rigi handles the structural complexities of localization in the background, ensuring your R&amp;D resources stay focused on core innovation.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h1>Key Features</h1>\r\n<p>The Rigi add-in for Trados streamlines the localization workflow by enabling real-time, in-context translation and integrated review management. Translators can see their edits reflected instantly within the actual UI layout, ensuring that text fits perfectly within visual constraints. Furthermore, the add-in replaces cumbersome external spreadsheets by allowing translators to process reviewer comments directly within the interface; selecting a comment automatically navigates to the specific string and highlights its visual element, creating a seamless bridge between linguistic editing and architectural validation.</p>\r\n<h1>Technical Requirements</h1>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Access to the Rigi project</strong>. and stamp the TUs (Translation Units) with the filename of each imported file.</p><p><br></p><p><strong><em>The Solution</em></strong></p><p>This application, SDLTM ImportPlus supports the ability to import SDLXLIFF files and/or TMX files to multiple Translation Memories and optionally stamp each TU (Translation Unit) with the segment number and/or the file name. This provides the user working in a regulated workflow with the ability to verify that a pre-translation of an updated file is using the correct translation in the case where there are multiple translations by verifying against the segment number stored in the TM.</p><p>The application also provides the user who simply wishes to import multiple files into their Translation Memories and record the filename so they always know where the TUs came from and can easily remove, or edit them, using the batch editing features in Trados Studio.</p><p><br></p><p><br></p>","documentation":"<p>SDLTM Import Plus enhances Trados Studio by allowing you to import SDLXLIFF and TMX files into multiple translation memories (TMs) efficiently. It can also stamp each translation unit (TU) with segment numbers and/or filenames for better tracking and compliance.</p><p><strong>Minimum Requirements</strong></p><ul><li>SDL Trados Studio 2019</li></ul><p><strong>How to find the SDLTM Import Plus?</strong></p><p>You can install the SDLTM Import Plus either from:</p><p>·&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Trados AppStore SDLTmFindVars is a utility that reads translation units from a given Trados Studio translation memory (TM) file and by comparing the items found in the Source and Target segments identifies potential Variables. That is, it searches for non-translatable text. Variables are usually company names, product names, trademarks, tokens or any word or phrase that it is left untranslated within each TM. The program writes the potential Variables in a TXT file for checking and further processing before the variables are added by the user in a Studio TM. <br /><br />It can also output the frequency of appearance in the output log and if needed, it can provide details for each Variable found in a CSV file. <br /><br />The user can also choose how the program performs the search and what is outputted through a number of options. <br /><br />Trados Studio considers Variables as replaceable parts of text with no real language meaning (such as the numbers or tags). Alternatively you can update Project Templates by going to File -&gt; Setup -&gt; Project Templates, select your project template to edit and then manage your list of supported file types.</p><ul><li>Existing Project Selected: Project Settings -&gt; File Types</li></ul><p class=\"ql-indent-1\"><em>Tip: </em>You can add new files to an existing project, as long as the project is recognised as a Studio project and not a Single file project. However file type need to be manged prior to adding new files (conversion from native to *sdlxliff)</p><p><u>Note:</u> If you don’t see STL File Type as a listed file type, you may need to click on \"Additional installed File Types Exist\" to enable it as a supported file type.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/b7a6f7d0-d575-4a3e-9602-99d448d2d79f/Resources/65b60032-57d2-4690-b472-5b933565c4e8.png?dl=1\"></p><h5>Working In Trados Studio</h5><p><strong>1.Recommendation</strong></p><p>We recommend working with subtitling files through the Creating projects workflow as apose to C<span style=\"background-color: var(--pa-admin-input-color); color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground);\">reating projects using Translate Single Document workflow.&nbsp;This is so you can take full advantage of all the benefits that comes with managing Studio projects where as Single file projects are restricted.&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></p><p><strong>2.File Type Identifier</strong></p><p>When adding your STL file to a project, make sure the File Type Identifier lists ASS V 5.0.0.0. var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>The Smart Review app for Trados Studio serves as an automated linguistic review companion, rating segments as green or red, depending on their level of quality.</p>\r\n<p>Segments evaluated by Smart Review carry a score represented by a colored, vertical line:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Green segments indicate high quality.</li>\r\n<li>Red segments indicate low quality.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>You can use this rating and the explanations provided to decide whether to assess segments in more detail.</p>\r\n<p>To generate an assessment of your translation, Smart Review uses the LLMs configured in your AI Essentials subscription, while at the same time considering the terminology available.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Trados Studio 2024.</li>\r\n<li>A Trados cloud account with AI Essentials subscription.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>&nbsp;<strong>Getting the app</strong></p>\r\n<p>&nbsp;You can install the Smart Review app from:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Trados AppStore</li>\r\n<li>From within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>After installing the Smart Review app, you can select it in Trados Studio, from the Editor-&gt;View menu while editing or reviewing your translation.&nbsp;</p>\r\n<p><strong>Authentication within Trados Studio</strong>&nbsp;</p>\r\n<p>To use the Smart Review app, you need to be connected to a Trados cloud account with an AI Essentials subscription.</p>\r\n<p>The AI Essentials package uses AWS Bedrock as default LLM.&nbsp;</p>\r\n<p>Upon request, and at an additional cost, you can also add another&nbsp;LLM&nbsp;of your choice (like&nbsp;Azure OpenAI) to perform&nbsp;<strong>evaluations</strong>. <br></p>\r\n<p><strong>Configure the LLM Provider</strong><br></p>\r\n<p>The AI Essentials package, required for Smart Review, now allows users to configure LLM providers from the <strong>Connected AI</strong> section and integrate them into the translation engines of cloud projects.<br></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Multiple LLM providers can be active, but only one can be set as the default.<br></li>\r\n<li>The default LLM provider is <strong>Bedrock</strong>, but it can be changed to another provider.<br></li>\r\n<li>Active LLM providers can be added to the translation engines used for cloud projects.<br></li>\r\n<li>The default LLM provider is used by the <strong>Smart Review</strong> plugin in local Trados Studio projects.<br></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Smart Review for Trados Studio Local Projects</strong><br>\r\nTo use Smart Review in <strong>Trados Studio local projects</strong>, a default LLM is required. The AI Essentials package includes <strong>Bedrock</strong> as the default LLM provider, but it can be changed to another provider.<br></p>\r\n<p><strong>Smart Review for Trados Studio Cloud Projects</strong><br>\r\nSmart Review in <strong>Trados Studio for cloud projects</strong> will use the LLM configured inside the Translation Engine for each language pair.<br>\r\nIf no LLM provider is specified for a language pair inside the Translation Engine, Smart Review cannot evaluate segments in cloud projects.</p>\r\n<p>For more details on how to setup a new LLM provider please <a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/en-US/trados-enterprise-accelerate-791595/setting-up-azure-openai-for-llm-usage-in-trados-applications-1144735\">visit this page</a></p>\r\n<p>More details on how to setup an LLM provider inside a Translation Engine, please <a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/en-US/trados-enterprise-accelerate-791595/creating-translation-engines-710036\">visit this page</a> section 9.</p>\r\n<p>The&nbsp;<strong>AI Essentials</strong>&nbsp;package is compatible with&nbsp;<strong>Trados Enterprise</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>Trados Accelerate</strong>, and&nbsp;<strong>Trados Team</strong>.</p>\r\n<p>Contact your Trados account manager for more information.&nbsp;</p>\r\n<p><strong>Data Transmission</strong></p>\r\n<p>Smart Review operates without storing any translation content. The only data stored is the result of the evaluation for statistics purposes.<br></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/254/versions/1351","changelog":{"Trados Studio 2024":"<p><strong>1.4.2.7</strong></p><ul>\r\n<li><strong>Enhanced the configuration of the LLM provider</strong>.
