Turnaround times are getting shorter and many of those working in the industry are feeling increased price pressures in dealing with this sort of content. Translation tools today lack proper context for subtitlers, offering poor support for the variety of file formats.</p><p><br></p><p><strong><em>The Solution this app provides</em></strong></p><p>The Studio Subtitling plugin supports enhanced features for audio visual translation, editing, proofing and works synchronously with the Studio editor in support of the following filetypes:</p><p><br></p><p>- ASS (available <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/6/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">here</a> on the RWS AppStore)</p><p>- SRT (supported out of the box in Trados Studio 2021/2022/2024)</p><p>- webVTT (supported out of the box in Trados Studio 2021/2022/2024)</p><p>- STL (available <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/7\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">here</a> on the RWS AppStore)</p><p>- SBV (supported out of the box in Trados Studio 2021/2022/2024)</p><p>- TTML (supported out of the box in Trados Studio 2021/2022/2024)</p><p><br></p><p><strong>NOTE:</strong></p><ul><li><strong>The plugin does not work with single document projects</strong>, resulting in errors. To use it properly, you need to create standard projects.</li><li>It is not possible to use the numpad for non-subtitling files if this plugin is installed. This is a limitation of Studio. The workaround is to remove the keyboard shortcuts for numpad in the Studio options. They can be reset when needed by using \"Reset to Defaults\" in the Subtitling Keyboard shortcut list.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/104/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"><strong>Click here to download</strong></a> the TQA model for Trados Studio that is also supported by this plugin.</p>","documentation":"<h1>Overview</h1><p><span style=\"color: black;\">Studio Subtitling is a plugin for Trados Studio 2019-SR2 and higher that provides features for previewing subtitle captions within the video while translating segments in the Editor.&nbsp;This includes a verification provider with specific QA checks for validating subtitle content, ensuring the translations provided adhere to the standards agreed upon.</span></p><p><span style=\"color: black;\">As we move forward, we will start to extend our portfolio in supporting additional subtitle formats.&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style=\"color: black;\">The current release&nbsp;includes file type support for (SubStation Alpha) .</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">ass&nbsp;</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">(SubRip).</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">srt</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">, (WebVTT).</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">vtt</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">,(YouTube).</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">sbv</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">&nbsp;and (Spruce Subtitle file).</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">stl&nbsp;</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">formats.</span></p><p><span style=\"color: black;\">Trados Studio 2019 SR2+ and Trados Studio 2021 support the following formats ‘out of the box’-.</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">srt</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">&nbsp;.</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">vtt</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">&nbsp;.</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">sbv</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">&nbsp;and .</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">sub.&nbsp;</strong></p><p><span style=\"color: black;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1348.pastedimage1599747682085v1.png\"></span></p><p><span style=\"color: black;\">There may however be certain scenarios, where you are advised to install the separate plugin for a better experience.</span></p><p><span style=\"color: black;\">If you are working with .</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">stl</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">, and /or .</span><strong style=\"color: black;\">ass/ssa&nbsp;</strong><span style=\"color: black;\">file types, you will need to install the relevant plugin.</span></p><p><span style=\"color: black;\">In addition, we also released&nbsp;</span><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/104/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong><em>a new TQA model</em></strong></a><span style=\"color: black;\">&nbsp;generated from the&nbsp;</span><a href=\"https://jostrans.soap2.ch/issue28/art_pedersen.php\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(102, 163, 224);\"><strong><em>FAR model</em></strong></a><span style=\"color: black;\">, covering the primary requirements in a providing a functional approach to assessing quality for subtitle formats, using the integrated Translation Quality Assessment feature of Studio.</span></p><p><strong>Technical&nbsp;Requirements</strong></p><p>We recommend Windows 8.1 (preferably higher) and SDL Trados Studio 2019 SR2&nbsp;as a minimum.&nbsp;</p><h1>Setup</h1><h2>Studio Subtitling Plugin</h2><ol><li>Download the Studio Subtitling plugin to your local driveDouble click on the plugin to launch the<strong>&nbsp;Plugin Installer</strong>&nbsp;and complete the installation, selecting the supported versions of Trados Studio.</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0878.pastedimage1599742266868v1.png\"></p><p><br></p><p><br></p><p><br></p><h2>File Type Plugins</h2><p>Download the required File Type plugins to your local drive</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5050.pastedimage1599742380746v2.png\"></p><p>Double click on each of the plugins to launch the&nbsp;<strong>Plugin Installer</strong>&nbsp;and complete the installation, selecting the supported versions of SDL Studio.</p><p>Complete the installation of the File types from within the Studio Options</p><ol><li>Launch Studio.Select: File → Options → File TypesClick on the '<strong>Additional installed File Types exist</strong>' linkCheck the checkboxs associated with each of the&nbsp;File Types that you want installed from the&nbsp;<strong>Add Additional Installed File Types</strong>&nbsp;dialogClick&nbsp;<strong>OK</strong>&nbsp;to save the settings<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/893x389/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0743.Subtitling.Setup.StudioSubtitling.FileTypePlugin.png\"></li></ol><h1>Subtitling Preview &amp; Data controls</h1><p>The Subtitling preview and data controls are available for valid subtitle documents. The Preview control provides a real-time preview of the subtitles within the video, whereas the Data control displays the subtitle metadata for each of the segments, along with real time verification feedback as the linguist is providing translations in the editor.</p><h2>Configuration</h2><p>Both the preview and data controls can be displayed and positioned anywhere within the Editor view.</p><p>To display the preview or data control:</p><ol><li>Open a subtitle document in the EditorSelect the Ribbon viewSelect the option Subtitling Preview or Subtitling Data from the ‘Information’ groupReposition the control, docking or pinning it in the preferred location.</li></ol><h2>Features</h2><h3>Synchronized selection and content changes</h3><p>The selected segment from the editor is synchronized with the subtitle track that is displayed in the video. When the user moves to a new segment in the editor, the corresponding track is selected in the video and similarly when the user selects a subtitle track in the video, focus is moved to the corresponding segment in the editor.&nbsp;</p><p>Additionally, changes applied to the translation are immediately visible in the subtitle caption from the video, including any formatting that was applied (<em>e.g. bold, italic, underline…</em>).</p><p><br></p><h3>Real time verification feedback</h3><p>Displays real time verification feedback as the content is being updated, providing the linguist with a much more informed approach in making decisions as they are translating.</p><p><br></p><h3>Splitting &amp; Merging segments</h3><p>Support for splitting and merging segments within and across paragraph boundaries.&nbsp;When&nbsp;merging across paragraphs, all context and structure related to the paragraphs that have been fully merged to the parent paragraph are excluded from the native file that is regenerated with the target content.</p><p><br></p><h3>Merged and Virtually merged files</h3><p>Support for working with both merged and virtually merged files.&nbsp;Ideally each subtitle file that is added to the project should have a corresponding video reference with the same name (<em>with exception to the file extension</em>). Each time the user navigates to a new file within the document, the relative video is loaded (<em>if available</em>) and corresponding track in the video is selected.</p><p><br></p><h3>Editable time tracks</h3><p>Support for updating the display time of the subtitle captions.&nbsp;This feature permits a linguist to adapt the Start and End time-codes to align better with the translated content.&nbsp;This also includes hotkeys to automatically set the Start/End times from the current position of the video.</p><p><br></p><h3>Time-code Format</h3><p>Support for converting and displaying the&nbsp;time-code format&nbsp;between Frames and Milliseconds.</p><p><br></p><h3>Setting a minimum time span between subtitles</h3><p>Support for applying the rules of a styleguide to&nbsp;set a minimum time&nbsp;span between subtitles.</p><p><br></p><h3>Keyboard Shortcuts</h3><p>Support for assigning&nbsp;&nbsp;to help automate interaction with keyboard shortcuts the subtitling controls from the editor (<em>e.g. jump back/forward from the video in seconds or frames, link/unlink the video, play back from previous subtitle etc...</em>)</p><p>-</p><h2>&nbsp;</h2><h2>Usage</h2><h3>Pre-requisite</h3><ul><li>Install the Studio Subtitling and File Types plugins, following the steps listed in Setup.</li></ul><h3>Add a video reference</h3><ol><li>Launch the project wizard.Add a subtitle document that is supported by one of the&nbsp;<strong>File Types</strong>&nbsp;installed.Decide whether you want to add a video reference for the subtitle document during project creation or at a later stage from the&nbsp;<strong>Editor</strong>.Add a video reference during project creationThe file name of the video should match that of the subtitle document, with exception to the extension.The video will be a physical resource in the project, meaning that it will be included in the package.&nbsp;Keep this mind as video files can be quite large in size.Don't add a video reference during project creationThe video reference can be added at a later stage when the document is opened in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor</strong>.This option is more appropriate for distribution, keeping the overall size of the project small, while still allowing the linguist to download and add a reference later if required.Click on&nbsp;<strong>Finish</strong>&nbsp;to complete the project creation.Open the subtitle document for translation in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor</strong>.If the&nbsp;<strong>Subtitling Preview</strong>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<strong>Subtitling Data</strong>&nbsp;controls are not visible in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor</strong>, then follow the steps listed in the Configuration.If a video reference was not provided during the project creation, then a default image will be displayed in its place.&nbsp;To load a video reference that coincides with the subtitles in the document, browse and select the media from the settings window of the plugin.Once the video reference is loaded, the video will be displayed and subtitles will synchronize with the segments in the document.<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/988x616/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4645.Subtitling.Preview.Example.png\"><strong>Note:</strong>&nbsp;The area of the video in the Subtitling Preview control will adapt automatically to the aspect ratio of the video</li></ol><h2>Options</h2><ol><li>Go to the Subtitling Preview control.Click on&nbsp;<strong>Settings</strong>&nbsp;to customize.Video.Video pathTime-code format (<em>Milliseconds or Frames</em>)Frame RateSubtitleFont, color, wight, and size.Background color and opacity.Character edge color and style.</li></ol><p>These will be your default captions format settings until you change them again or click&nbsp;<strong>Reset&nbsp;</strong>to go back to the default captions format.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/588x586/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8182.image2019_2D00_7_2D00_31_5F00_16_2D00_56_2D00_51.png\"></p><h2>Time-code format</h2><p>The time-code format is always displayed in milliseconds from the Subtitle Preview control.&nbsp;It is read as milliseconds, unless the subtitle document has information to suggest otherwise.&nbsp;In the case that the format should be read as Frames, select the option 'Frames' from Time-code combobox in the Subtitle Preview Options dialog.</p><p>To successfully switch the Time-code format from Milliseconds to Frames:</p><ul><li>The frame rate of the video must be known.</li><li>The fractional seconds (<em>to be identified as frames</em>) cannot exceed the frame rate for any of the time code entries for each of the segments in the document.</li><li>Make reference to Time-code Conversion to understand more.</li></ul><h3>Time-code Conversion&nbsp;</h3><ul><li>How to calculate the position in milliseconds, given the frame rate and frame:</li></ul><p>Milliseconds Per Frame = 1000 / Frame Rate</p><p>Frames To Milliseconds = Frame * Milliseconds Per Frame</p><pre class=\"ql-syntax\" spellcheck=\"false\">Given:- Frame Rate = 24- Frame = 4Result:&nbsp; Milliseconds Per Frame = 41.666 (1000 / Frame Rate)&nbsp; Frame To Milliseconds = 166.666 (Frame * Milliseconds Per Frame)\r\n</pre><p><br></p><ul><li>How to calculate the position in frames, given the frame rate and milliseconds</li></ul><p>Milliseconds Per Frame = 1000 / Frame Rate</p><p>Milliseconds To Frame = Milliseconds / Milliseconds Per Frame</p><pre class=\"ql-syntax\" spellcheck=\"false\">Given:- Frame Rate = 24- Milliseconds = 166.666&nbsp;Result:&nbsp; Milliseconds Per Frame = 41.666 (1000 / Frame Rate)&nbsp; Milliseconds To Frame = 4&nbsp; (Milliseconds / Milliseconds Per Frame)\r\n</pre><h2>Valid Subtitle Document</h2><p>Any document whose paragraphs have a context of type=<strong>sdl:section</strong>, code=<strong>sec</strong>, and obtain a meta data entry with a key named&nbsp;<strong>timeStamp</strong>&nbsp;<strong>,</strong>&nbsp;whose value conforms to the following time span format</p><p><strong>Time span format</strong>: hh:mm:ss[.fffffff]</p><p>\"<em>hh</em>\"The number of hours in the time interval, ranging from 0 to 23.\"<em>mm</em>\"The number of minutes in the time interval, ranging from 0 to 59.\"<em>ss</em>\"The number of seconds in the time interval, ranging from 0 to 59.\"<em>fffffff</em>\"Fractional seconds in the time interval. This element is omitted if the time interval does not include fractional seconds. If present, fractional seconds are always expressed using seven decimal digits.</p><h1>Verification Settings</h1><p>We have integrated a specific set of verification checks for working with Subtitle documents. You can specify these verification settings for your project, along with the existing verification tools (e.g.&nbsp;<em>QA Checker 3.0</em>,&nbsp;<em>Tag Verifier</em>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<em>Terminology Verifier</em>)</p><p><strong>Procedure</strong></p><ol><li>Go to the Projects view and select the project.Go to the Home tab, and select Project Settings from the Ribbon.Select: Verification → Studio Subtitling&nbsp;→ Verification SettingsConfigure other project settings if necessary and select OK.</li></ol><h2>QA Checks</h2><p><br></p><p><strong>CPS</strong></p><p>Number of characters per secondThe number of characters per second</p><p><strong>Default settings:</strong></p><p>Report when less than:&nbsp;<strong>12</strong>&nbsp;(<em>warning</em>)</p><p>Report when greater than:&nbsp;<strong>15</strong>&nbsp;(<em>warning</em>)</p><p><strong>Calculation</strong>:</p><p>Total Seconds = (End time - Start time) → Seconds</p><p>Characters Per Second = Total Characters / Total Seconds<strong>WPM</strong></p><p>Number of words per minuteBased on the recommended rate of 160-180 words per minute, you should aim to leave a subtitle on screen for a minimum period of around 3 words per second or 0.3 seconds per word (<em>e.g. 1.2 seconds for a 4-word subtitle</em>). However, timings are ultimately an editorial decision that depends on other considerations, such as the speed of speech, text editing and shot synchronization.</p><p><strong>Default settings:</strong></p><p>Report when less than:&nbsp;<strong>160</strong>&nbsp;(<em>warning</em>)</p><p>Report when greater than:&nbsp;<strong>180</strong>&nbsp;(<em>warning</em>)</p><p><strong>Calculation</strong>:</p><p>Total Seconds = (End time - Start time) → Seconds</p><p>Words Per Second = Words / Total Seconds</p><p>Words Per Minute = Words Per Second * 60</p><p><strong>CPL</strong></p><p>Number of characters per lineThe number of characters per line</p><p><strong>Default settings:</strong></p><p>Report when greater than:&nbsp;<strong>39</strong>&nbsp;(<em>warning</em>)</p><p><strong>Method:</strong></p><p>Returns the total number of characters for each line in the subtitle caption.</p><p>Report when any of the lines contain a number of characters greater than CPL setting assigned by the user.</p><p><strong>LPS</strong></p><p>Number of lines per subtitle</p><p>A maximum subtitle length of two lines is recommended. Anything greater than that should be used if the linguist is confident that no important picture information will be obscured. When deciding between one long line or two short ones, consider line breaks, number of words, pace of speech and the image.</p><p><strong>Default settings:</strong></p><p>Report when greater than:&nbsp;<strong>2</strong>&nbsp;(<em>warning</em>)</p><h2>Character Count</h2><p>Spaces and punctuation are counted in all character counts</p><h1>TQA</h1><p>TQA can be seen as a functional approach to measuring quality in the translated content and from that assessment, evaluate and improve the process. We are introducing a new TQA model generated from&nbsp;the FAR model, covering the primary requirements in a providing a functional approach to assessing quality for subtitle formats, using the integrated Translation Quality Assessment feature of Studio.</p><h2>FAR Model</h2><p><br></p><p>Functional equivalence*FDo the subtitles convey speaker meaning?How well the message or meaning is rendered in the subtitled translation.Semantic errorsSEThe definition of a standard semantic equivalence error would be a subtitle that contains errors, but still has bearing on the actual meaning and does not seriously hamper the viewers’ progress beyond that single subtitle.&nbsp;Standard semantic errors would also be cases where utterances that are important to the plot are left unsubtitled.</p><p>A serious semantic equivalence error scores 2 penalty points and is defined as a subtitle that is so erroneous that it makes the viewers’ understanding of the subtitle nil and would hamper the viewers’ progress beyond that subtitle, either by leading to plot misunderstandings or by being so serious as to disturb the contract of illusion for more than just one subtitle.</p><p>Minor functional equivalence errors are basically lexical errors, including terminology errors which do not affect the plot of the film.Stylistic errorsSTStylistic errors are not as serious as semantic errors, as they cause nuisance, rather than misunderstandings.</p><p>Examples of stylistic errors would be erroneous terms of address, using the wrong register (too high or too low) or any other use of language that is out of tune with the style of the original (<em>e.g. using modern language in historic films</em>).</p><p>Acceptability*ADo the subtitles sound correct and natural in the target language?How well the subtitles adhere to target language norms.Grammar errorsGRThese are simply errors of target language grammar in various forms.</p><p>A serious grammar error makes the subtitle hard to read and/or comprehend. Minor errors are the pet peeves that annoy purists (e.g. misusing ‘whom’ in English). Standard errors fall in between.</p><p>Idiomaticity errorsIDErrors that fall into this category are not grammar errors, but errors which sound unnatural in the target language.</p><p>It should be pointed out that sometimes source text interference can become so serious that it becomes an equivalence issue</p><p>Spelling errorsSPSpelling errors could be judged according to gravity in the following way:</p><p><br></p><ul><li>A minor error is any spelling error.</li><li>Standard errors change the meaning of the word.</li><li>Serious errors would make a word impossible to read.</li></ul><p>Readability*RCan the subtitles be read in a fluent and non-intrusive way?How easy the subtitles are for the viewer to process.Punctuation and graphicsPG\"Eats, shoots and leaves\"Reading speed and line lengthRSConsider penalizing anything over 15 cps and up to 20 cps as a standard error.&nbsp;</p><p>Above that is serious as you wouldn't have time to do anything apart from read the subtitles, and possibly not even finish that.</p><p>Measured in cps (characters per second)Segmentation and spottingSSSpotting errors are caused by bad synchronization with speech, (subtitles appear too soon or disappear later than the permitted lag on out-times) or image (subtitles do not respect hard cuts).</p><p>Segmentation errors are when the semantic or syntactic structure of the message is not respected.</p><p>A serious spotting error would be when subtitles are out of sync by more than one utterance. A minor spotting error would be less than a second off, and a standard error in between these two extremes.</p><h1>Subtitle File Type Support</h1><p>NameExtensionDefault time-code formatSubRip *.srtsrtMillisecondsWebVTT *.vttvttMillisecondsYouTube *.sbv</p><p>sbvMillisecondsSpruce Subtitle *.stl</p><p>stlFramesAdvanced Substation Alpha *.ass</p><p>ssa/assCentiseconds (interpreted as Milliseconds)</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h1>FAQ</h1><p><strong>Q.</strong>&nbsp;Can I edit the&nbsp;<strong>Start</strong>&nbsp;and/or&nbsp;<strong>End&nbsp;</strong>time-codes of the subtitle from the Subtitling Data control?</p><p><strong>A.</strong>&nbsp;Yes, editing the time-codes of the subtitle is fully supported.&nbsp;This feature permits a linguist to adapt the Start and End times to align better with the translated content.</p><p><strong>Q.</strong>&nbsp;How does the plugin recognize which time-code format (<em>i.e. milliseconds vs frames</em>) to use?</p><p><strong>A.</strong>&nbsp;The time-code format is read as milliseconds, unless the subtitle document has information to suggest otherwise.&nbsp;In the case that the format should be read as Frames, select the option 'Frames' from Time-code combobox in the Subtitle Preview Options dialog.&nbsp;Please refer to Time-code Format for more information.</p><p><strong>Q.</strong>&nbsp;Can I merged across paragraphs when working with the supported subtitle formats?</p><p><strong>A.&nbsp;</strong>Merge across paragraphs is now fully supported with the latest release of the File Types (<em>WebVTT, SBV, STL &amp; ASS</em>) version 1.0.3+ and Subtitling plugin version 1.0.8+.&nbsp;All context and structure related to the paragraphs that have been fully merged to the parent paragraph are excluded from the native file that is regenerated with the target content.</p><p>Unmerging segments is no longer supported from Studio 2019 SR1 CU3.</p><p><strong>Q.</strong>&nbsp;Can I add a new subtitle record or remove an existing one from the list of subtitles visible from the Studio Data Control?</p><p><strong>A.</strong>&nbsp;This is currently not supported, as It would introduce a requirement to manage a structural change to the paragraphs of the document that would then need to be reflected when regenerating the native format.</p><p><strong>Q.</strong>&nbsp;I would like to add a verification check that is specific to subtitling other than those included with the Studio Subtitling plugin, how can I do that?</p><p><strong>A.</strong>&nbsp;Check if what you are looking for is not already included as a standard QA check with the other tools (e.g.&nbsp;<em>QA Checker 3.0</em>,&nbsp;<em>Tag Verifier</em>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<em>Terminology Verifier</em>). It is also possible to create/add your own, via the Regular Expressions area in the QA Checker tool. In addition, please take the opportunity to communicate any improvements of this nature to the AppStore team, as we welcome any suggestions/feedback from the community in area's where we can improve the features for future releases.</p><p><strong>Q.</strong>&nbsp;I don't have a video reference for the subtitle document that I'm working on; will the verification checks still work?</p><p><strong>A.</strong>&nbsp;Yes, the QA checks will still function correctly without a video reference, if you are working with a document that is recognized correctly by the File Types we have released to support subtitle formats</p><p><strong>Q.</strong>&nbsp;Can I update the video reference after the project has been created?</p><p><strong>A.</strong>&nbsp;The video reference can be associated with the subtitle document during project creation or linked to the active document from the editor at a later time. Any updates to the video reference path from the editor are persisted in the project file without affecting the project resources.</p><h1>Enabling Advanced Options</h1><p>The Time-code format and Frame Rate are some of the more sensitive settings when it comes to working with a subtitle document. Modifying these settings can affect precision in accurately calculating the position in the video from the subtitle Start and End time-codes, especially when switching between time-code formats (<em>milliseconds vs frames</em>). It is for this reason that they are categorized as advanced options and disabled by default.&nbsp;The user can however choose to override these settings in cases where the Frame Rate interpreted by the plugin was incorrect or possibly, the user prefers to work in different Time-code format (<em>e.g. in Frames as opposed to Milliseconds</em>).</p><h2>Procedure</h2><ol><li>Go to Subitling Preview &gt; OptionsCheck the 'Enable Advanced Options' checkbox</li></ol><p><strong>Note:</strong>&nbsp;The Time-code format and Frame Rate options are enabled when the checkbox is checked and can be updated by the user.&nbsp;The only exception to this is when a video is also assigned; then the Frame Rate remains disabled as it is inherited from the video.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/585x356/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5100.image2019_2D00_8_2D00_5_5F00_10_2D00_36_2D00_28.png\"></p><h1>FAR Methodology TQA Settings</h1><p>Trados Studio Professional provides the ability to create your own TQA profile and share it via an&nbsp;<strong><em>*.sdltqasettings</em></strong>&nbsp;file.&nbsp;The \"FAR Methodology TQA Settings\" is a TQA profile that has been created by the Trados AppStore Team for use with subtitling projects.&nbsp;It is based on&nbsp;the FAR model published by Professor Jan Pedersen of Stockholm University and is an&nbsp;attempt at creating a generalized model for assessing quality in interlingual subtitling.</p><p>The settings file can be downloaded from the&nbsp;RWS AppStore via&nbsp;<a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/plugin/104/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">this link</a>.&nbsp;The download is a zip file.&nbsp;So to install this you download the zip, unzip the contents which will give you a&nbsp;<strong><em>FAR Model 1.0.sdltqasettings</em></strong>&nbsp;file, and then you simply import it here:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1176x562/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3108.pastedimage1650898866096v2.png\"></p><p><br></p><p>It's based on these three things:</p><p><br></p><p>- Functional equivalence&nbsp;<em>(do the subtitles convey speaker meaning?)</em></p><p>- Acceptability&nbsp;<em>(do the subtitles sound correct and natural in the target language?)</em></p><p>- Readability&nbsp;<em>(can the subtitles be read in a fluent and non-intrusive way?)</em></p><p><br></p><p>For more information on this model please refer to this paper published by Prof. Jan Pederson:&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.jostrans.org/issue28/art_pedersen.php\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">The FAR model: assessing quality in interlingual subtitling</a>.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>IMPORTANT NOTE:</strong>&nbsp;TQA is a feature available in Studio for the Professional version only. Other versions of Studio can only use this model when working with a project package that has been prepared with the TQA profile configured.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1800x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7002.pastedimage1650898496176v1.png\"></p><h1>Frame Rate</h1><p>We use the Frame Rate to calculate the position of the frames in the document, whether or not a video is loaded.&nbsp;For this reason, it's important to check and associate the correct Frame Rate from the settings before you start working on the document, as the incorrect value could eventually result in a reduction of accuracy when calculating the position of the frames in the video.</p><p>The&nbsp;<strong>Frame Rate</strong>&nbsp;is automatically interpreted from the&nbsp;<strong>Start</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>End</strong>&nbsp;time-codes of the subtitles in the document when it is loaded in the editor for the first time.&nbsp;Depending on the time-code format of the document, the method in which the Frame Rate is identified can differ, as follows:</p><p><br></p><p>FramesWe first recover the highest frame identified from the Start and End times of the subtitles in the document and then select the nearest Frame Rate greater than that value from the&nbsp;<strong>Standard Frame Rates</strong>.MillisecondsWe use a regression technique in interpreting the Frame Rate when loading a document whose time-code format is in milliseconds.&nbsp;We create a regression model that contains the values for all milliseconds-per-frame * frame for all of the&nbsp;<strong>Standard Frame Rates</strong>.&nbsp;The Frame Rate is then identified by evaluating the similarity between the Start and End times of the subtitles in the document against the values in the model.</p><p>Understanding this, the Frame Rate identified through this method can be considered reliable only in cases where the milliseconds reflect the actual position of the Frames in the video with a limited tolerance.</p><h2>Modifying the Frame Rate</h2><p>It's important that the Frame Rate value chosen coincides with the Frame Rate that was used originally when creating the native file.&nbsp;There is no value in the native subtitle file that helps us identify what Frame Rate was used by the author initially when creating it, therefore we attempt to ascertain this from the time-code data of subtitle document the first time it is loaded in the Studio Editor.&nbsp;This may or may not be accurate depending on the time-code format (<em>Frames or Milliseconds</em>) and time-code data recovered.</p><p>The user can modify the Frame Rate by either loading a video or manually assigning it from the settings window.&nbsp;</p><ul><li>Load a video</li></ul><ol><li>Open the Subtitle settings windowBrowse and load a video that coincides with the subtitles in the document.&nbsp;<strong>Note:</strong>&nbsp;The Frame Rate will be inherited from the FPS of the Video and prevent the user from modifying it.Click OK</li></ol><ul><li>Manually editing</li></ul><ol><li>Open the Subtitle settings windowEnable Advanced OptionsSelect from the list of Frame Rates available in the combobox or enter a new value that coincides with the subtitles in the document.Click OK</li></ol><h3>Warnings</h3><p>The following warning messages are displayed to the user when the Frame Rate is updated, to help them become more aware of possible consequences in assigning an incorrect value.&nbsp;The types of issues that you can expect to see when modifying the Frame Rate are related to the precision in accurately calculating the position (<em>frames or milliseconds depending on the time-code format</em>) within the video; which becomes more evident when the switching between time-code formats.</p><p>Eventually, these warning messages can be hidden by checking the checkbox 'Hide this message', if they are no longer needed.</p><ul><li>The user attempts to assign a Frame Rate that is lower than the minimum value identified from a document (<em>Frames time-code format only</em>).&nbsp;This will force the plugin to recalculate the frame position for each of the subtitles and potentially reduce accuracy is locating the correct position in the video.</li><li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/523x214/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3618.image2019_2D00_7_2D00_31_5F00_16_2D00_50_2D00_45.png\"></li><li>A video is loaded with a Frame Rate that is different to the current value.</li><li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/510x232/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8130.image2019_2D00_7_2D00_31_5F00_16_2D00_42_2D00_35.png\"></li><li>A Frame Rate is assigned that is different to the current value.</li><li><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/527x233/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0882.image2019_2D00_7_2D00_31_5F00_16_2D00_44_2D00_9.png\"></li></ul><h1>Standard Frame Rates</h1><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2022c43a-e819-42af-8137-bc8c3c6c9af0/Resources/d724733e-8b5b-4492-89e7-1d6f3623d5f4.png?dl=1\"></p><h1>Keyboard Shortcuts</h1><p>The Subtitling plugin has as number of keyboard shortcut actions that you can adapt to fit your needs at any time.</p><h2>Procedure</h2><ol><li>Go to&nbsp;File&nbsp;&gt;&nbsp;Options&nbsp;and select&nbsp;Keyboard shortcuts&nbsp;&gt; Studio SubtitlingLocate the row containing the action for which you want to create or edit the shortcut.Select the cell in the&nbsp;Shortcut&nbsp;column (where the current keyboard shortcut is displayed, if one exists).Press the key, or combination of keys, that you want to use for the shortcut. The key combination appears in the&nbsp;Shortcut&nbsp;column.<strong>Note</strong>:&nbsp;Shift+F10 and Ctrl+Shift+F10 are reserved for internal use and cannot be assigned as shortcut keys.&nbsp;Similarly, single keys such as the numbers on your keypad cannot be assigned due to some limitations.Click outside the row you just edited. SDL Trados Studio checks whether the new shortcut is already in use. If the shortcut is already in use, the entire row is highlighted in red and you must remove or edit the shortcut.Select&nbsp;OK&nbsp;to save the shortcut and close the&nbsp;Options&nbsp;dialog.</li></ol><h2>Subtitling Preview view</h2><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2022c43a-e819-42af-8137-bc8c3c6c9af0/Resources/41a7cd94-2c38-4951-a270-7076b0065df7.png?dl=1\"></p><h1>Assign single key keyboard shortcuts</h1><p>Assigning single key keyboard shortcuts from within Studio is not supported. For instance, it is not possible to assign the number 1 or 2 from the numpad on your keyboard as a hotkey in the&nbsp;Keyboard Shortcut Options.</p><p>We can however work around this limitation and manually assign keyboard shortcuts by updating settings file directly, as follows:</p><h2>Procedure</h2><ol><li>Close SDL Trados Studio.Open the UserSettings.xml file in a text editor; located here: C:\\Users\\<strong><em>&lt;UserId&gt;</em></strong>\\AppData\\Roaming\\SDL\\SDL Trados Studio\\15.0.0.0\\</li></ol><p><strong>Note:</strong>&nbsp;replace&nbsp;<strong><em>&lt;UserId&gt;</em></strong>&nbsp;with the user id that signed in to the OS.</p><ol><li>Locate the ShortcutsSettingsGroup section.&nbsp;If it is not present in the file, then simply create a new settings group&lt;SettingsGroup&nbsp;Id=\"ShortcutsSettingsGroup\"&gt;&lt;/SettingsGroup&gt;Add the setting manually by associating the appropriate keyboard shortcut Id. Make reference to the&nbsp;<strong>Settings ID</strong>&nbsp;associated with each of the keyboard shortcuts from the Keyboard Shortcuts&nbsp;when identifying the appropriate action.&lt;Setting&nbsp;Id=\"SubtitlingPreviewController_SetStartTimeAction\"&gt;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&lt;Value&gt;NumPad0&lt;/Value&gt;&lt;/Setting&gt;</li></ol><p><strong>Example</strong>:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/656x289/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6327.image2019_2D00_8_2D00_5_5F00_15_2D00_41_2D00_38.png\"></p><h1>Merge across paragraphs support</h1><p>Merge across paragraphs is now fully supported with the latest release of the File Types (<em>WebVTT, SBV &amp; STL</em>) version 1.0.3+ and Subtitling plugin version 1.0.8+.&nbsp;All context and structure related to the paragraphs that have been fully merged to the parent paragraph are excluded from the native file that is regenerated with the target content.</p><p><br></p><p>The feature to merge across paragraphs in Studio is however limited, so far as there is no action to un-merge paragraphs after they have been merged, other than performing an undo operation in the editor.&nbsp;In the case where you perform an undo operation to un-merge merged paragraphs, then you will subsequently need to click on the&nbsp;<strong>Reload</strong>&nbsp;button from the Subtitling Preview control to refresh the data from both the Studio editor and Subtitling data-grids.</p><h1>Merge across paragraph settings</h1><p>There are two options that permit the user to influence the behavior of merging across paragraph boundaries that are accessible from platform and project settings.</p><h2>Project Settings</h2><p><strong>Location:</strong>&nbsp;Project Settings&gt;Project</p><ul><li>Allow source editing {checkbox}</li><li>Enable merging segments across paragraph {checkbox}</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/681x375/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2313.image2019_2D00_5_2D00_8_5F00_15_2D00_48_2D00_18.png\"></p><h2>Platform level settings</h2><p><strong>Location:</strong>&nbsp;File&gt;Options&gt;Editor&gt;Automation</p><ul><li>Hide empty segments that have been merged {checkbox}</li><li><strong>Note</strong>: If this option is not checked, the empty segments will be visible in the Studio editor, however they will remain hidden in the subtitling preview grid as long as the segment pair is identified as merged.</li><li>Set translation status for empty segments to {combobox: translation status}</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/683x473/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6170.image2019_2D00_5_2D00_8_5F00_15_2D00_48_2D00_50.png\"></p><h1>Minimum time span between subtitles</h1><p>A requirement has been identified for some agencies to have a 2 to 4 frame gap between titles. The gap triggers the viewer to look down and read the next subtitle. Without gaps between titles, it is sometimes difficult to identify when the title is changing, especially when they are formatted similarly. The BBC demands a 3-frame gap in their specification, however anything between 2 to 4 frames is acceptable.</p><h2>Procedure</h2><ol><li>Subtitling Data view &gt; Toolbar &gt; Set minimum time span between subtitlesSelect/set the appropriate time-code format and valueClick OK</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/685x446/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4401.SetMinimumTimeSpanBetweenSubtitles.png\"></p><h1>Time-code Format</h1><p>The Time-code format can be read as&nbsp;<strong>Frames</strong>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<strong>Milliseconds</strong>. What this means is, the fractional digits in the time-code (<em>e.g. the last digits after the period</em>) can represent frames of the video or milliseconds in time.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/2022c43a-e819-42af-8137-bc8c3c6c9af0/Resources/dee151be-2f32-4b37-9104-2e13076ba96d.png?dl=1\"></p><h1>Undo/Redo Support</h1><p>Currently, there is no support to automatically refresh the data in the Subtitling views when the&nbsp;<strong>Undo</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>Redo</strong>&nbsp;actions are executed from the Studio Editor.&nbsp;As a workaround, the user can click on the&nbsp;<strong>Refresh</strong>&nbsp;button from the Subtitling views to refresh the UI content in the controls.</p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/5/versions/1459","changelog":{"Trados By automatically recording activity and providing detailed reports, the plugin ensures accurate tracking of work duration, enabling better project management, billing, and time allocation.</p>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Trados Studio 2017 and higher</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p><strong>Automatic Time Tracking</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Records active time spent working within Trados Studio.</li>\r\n<li>Pauses tracking during periods of inactivity, ensuring accuracy.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Project-Specific Tracking</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Tracks time at the project level, allowing users to see how much time was spent on individual projects.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Activity Logging</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Captures details of when work started, paused, and resumed, creating a detailed log.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Reports and Summaries</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Generates reports summarizing time spent on projects, files, or tasks.</li>\r\n<li>Exports reports in formats such as Excel for billing or record-keeping purposes.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Customizable Settings</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Allows users to define tracking parameters, such as inactivity timeout thresholds and specific tasks to monitor.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Download the Studio Time Tracker app from the Trados Studio AppStore.</p>\r\n<h3>How to install the Plugin</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Download the app from the integrated studio within Studio interface</li>\r\n<li>Click Install and follow the prompts.</li>\r\n<li>Activate the app from Time Tracker side view in the navigate bar.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h3>How to Use Studio Time Tracker</h3>\r\n<p><strong>Setting Up Time Tracking</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Open Trados Studio and navigate to the Time Tracker view in the side bar.</li>\r\n<li>Select Studio Time Tracker Settings.\r\n<strong>Configure your preferences</strong></li>\r\n<li>Inactivity Timeout: Define how long the system waits before pausing tracking during inactivity.</li>\r\n<li>File-Level Tracking: Enable or disable tracking for individual files within a project.\r\n<strong>Tracking Time</strong></li>\r\n<li>Begin work on a project in Trados Studio. The plugin will automatically track the time as long as activity is detected.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Viewing and Exporting Reports</h3>\r\n<p><strong>Export Reports</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Export Studio 2024":"<p><strong>4.19.22.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>Fixed (termbase direction handling – architectural overhaul)</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Term matching now uses the termbase&#39;s declared direction, not per-entry language metadata.</strong> Legacy write-path bugs (pre-v4.19.13) left many termbases with a mix of entries whose per-entry <code>source_lang</code> / <code>target_lang</code> tags didn&#39;t agree with the termbase&#39;s declaration – and TermLens trusted those per-entry tags when deciding whether to invert for reverse-direction projects. The result was entries silently not matching even though their text was correct. Direction decisions now pull source/target language from the canonical <code>termbases</code> table. Resilient to corrupted per-entry tags, and root-cause fix for the &quot;my termbase entry exists but TermLens says no match&quot; class of bug.</li>\r\n<li><strong>The term entry editor now always shows fields in termbase-declared direction.</strong> Previously the edit dialog inverted field layout when the project direction differed from the termbase&#39;s, and did so inconsistently depending on which entry point opened it (the Termbase Editor grid right-click didn&#39;t invert, but the TermLens chip right-click did). Fields, labels, and save flow are now always in termbase order, matching the Termbase Editor grid.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed (Prompt Manager ↔ Batch Translate sync)</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>&quot;Set as active prompt for this project&quot; now takes effect immediately in the Batch Translate dropdown and works for any prompt.</strong> Right-clicking a prompt in the Prompt Manager used to have no visible effect on the Batch dropdown until the Settings dialog was closed – and even then, prompts whose <code>Category</code> was not <code>Translate</code> (or <code>Proofread</code>, in proofread mode) were silently filtered out, so the checkmark had nowhere to land. The dropdown now live-refreshes the moment the active prompt is toggled, without closing the Settings dialog, and the active prompt always appears regardless of its folder or category. Works from all entry points (AI Assistant gear, TermLens gear, About dialog). Cancelling the Settings dialog still reverts the change; clicking OK persists it.</li>\r\n<li><strong>New prompts created at the Prompt Manager tree root are now visible in the Batch Translate dropdown.</strong> Previously, creating a new prompt without first selecting a folder left its <code>Category</code> empty and the Batch dropdown&#39;s category filter silently excluded it. New prompts now default to the <code>Translate</code> category when no folder is selected.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Corrected stale pricing for Claude Opus 4.6 and Haiku 4.5.</strong> The internal pricing table had Opus 4.6 at the pre-4.6 rate of $15 / $75 per MTok – Anthropic dropped Opus pricing to $5 / $25 with the 4.6 release. Haiku 4.5 was listed at $0.80 / $4.00, corrected to the current $1.00 / $5.00. Cost estimates shown in the AI Assistant and Batch Translate were over-stating Opus usage and under-stating Haiku usage – now accurate.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Added</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>&quot;Reverse source/target&quot; right-click action in the Termbase Editor.</strong> Fixes one or many reversed-direction entries at once. Menu label dynamically shows the count when multiple rows are selected. Swaps <code>source_term</code> ↔ <code>target_term</code>, language tags, abbreviations, and flips every linked synonym&#39;s language tag, all in one transaction. Right-click on the grid now also preserves multi-row selection when the clicked row was already part of the selection (matches Windows list conventions).</li>\r\n<li><strong>Claude Opus 4.7 support.</strong> Anthropic&#39;s new flagship model (released 2026-04-16) is now selectable in AI Settings under the Claude provider and via the OpenRouter gateway (<code>anthropic/claude-opus-4.7</code>). Opus 4.7 has a 1M-token context window, 128k max output, and is Anthropic&#39;s most capable generally available model. Pricing is $5 / input MTok, $25 / output MTok – the same as Opus 4.6. Sonnet 4.6 remains the recommended default for most translation work; reach for Opus 4.7 when you need top-tier reasoning or long-context jobs.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Note on Opus 4.7 tokenizer</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Claude Opus 4.7 uses a new tokenizer that can use <strong>~1.0×–1.35× more tokens</strong> for the same text compared to earlier models. Pre-send cost estimates (the <code>chars / 4</code> heuristic) will under-estimate Opus 4.7 costs by a similar margin. Actual billing is based on Anthropic&#39;s token counts.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>4.19.13.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>Fixed (critical – termbase data integrity)</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Wrong-direction entries were silently being written to write termbases with mixed declared directions.</strong> When two or more write termbases had different declared directions (e.g. one EN→NL and one NL→EN), Alt+Down (and Ctrl+Alt+Shift+T) made a single source/target-swap decision using only the first termbase&#39;s direction and applied it to every termbase in the batch. Any termbase with a different direction ended up with text in the wrong columns, and the rot compounded on every press. Fixed in <code>TermbaseReader.InsertTermBatch</code> – each termbase now gets its own per-termbase swap decision based on its own declared direction, so the text always lands in the right source/target columns regardless of the mix. <code>QuickAddTermAction</code> no longer pre-swaps globally. Existing reversed entries need to be fixed manually using the ⇄ reverse button in the Term Entry Editor.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Duplicate check now detects reverse-direction matches.</strong> Re-adding a term that was already stored but in the opposite direction (source↔target swapped) used to create a second copy. <code>InsertTerm</code> and <code>InsertTermBatch</code> now match either <code>(source=A ∧ target=B)</code> or <code>(source=B ∧ target=A)</code> within the same termbase and reject both as duplicates.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Ctrl+Q (QuickLauncher) now reliably surfaces the Supervertaler Assistant panel.</strong> Previously, picking a QuickLauncher prompt submitted the message but the dockable Assistant panel could stay auto-hidden, unpinned, or buried behind another dock tab – the user saw nothing happen. <code>RunQuickLauncherPrompt</code> now activates the panel before submitting, matching the SuperSearch action pattern. Affects the context-menu QuickLauncher and the Ctrl+Alt+digit slot shortcuts.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Distill and Process Inbox now ignore Obsidian plugin sidecar files (<code>.edtz</code>)</strong> in the SuperMemory inbox. An <code>.edtz</code> sidecar sitting next to a Markdown note used to crash Distill with &quot;Unsupported file format: .edtz&quot;. Filtered out silently by both scanners and by bank-enumeration code paths that share the <code>MemoryBankReader.IsIgnoredSidecar</code> helper.</li>\r\n<li><strong>TSV reimport failed for exported files.</strong> The TSV export wrote language display names as column headers (e.g., &quot;Dutch (BE)&quot;, &quot;English (US)&quot;) which the importer could not reliably match back to language codes. Export now uses fixed &quot;Source&quot; / &quot;Target&quot; headers, matching the Workbench format. Language names in headers from external TSV files are still recognised as a fallback.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Added</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Auto-indexing after Process Inbox and Health Check.</strong> The <code>05_INDICES/</code> folder is now automatically populated with three master index files after every Process Inbox or Health Check run: <code>master-terminology.md</code> (flat table of all source → target term decisions with domain, client, confidence, status), <code>client-summary.md</code> (one section per client), and <code>domain-summary.md</code> (one section per domain). Built by scanning frontmatter directly – no LLM call needed, completes in under a second. Makes the bank browsable in Obsidian.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Enriched frontmatter schema for SuperMemory articles.</strong> Distill, Process Inbox, and Health Check now produce articles with richer YAML frontmatter: <code>type</code>, <code>domain</code>, <code>client</code>, <code>language_pair</code>, <code>confidence</code> (high/medium/low), <code>sources</code> (original filenames for traceability), <code>tldr</code> (one-sentence summary for fast scanning), <code>created</code>, and <code>updated</code>.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Confidence scoring in Distill and Process Inbox.</strong> Every article generated by Distill or Process Inbox now carries a <code>confidence:</code> field. Low-confidence articles are flagged for human review.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Source traceability.</strong> Every article now carries a <code>sources:</code> frontmatter field listing the original files it was derived from. Terminology articles always quote exact source and target terms verbatim.</li>\r\n<li><strong>&quot;Save to memory bank&quot; from chat.</strong> Right-click any assistant message in the AI Assistant chat and select &quot;Save to memory bank&quot; to save the Q&amp;A pair (your question and the AI&#39;s response) as an inbox note in the active memory bank. Run Process Inbox afterwards to compile it into the knowledge base – useful answers no longer vanish into chat history.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Improved</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Distill button now offers a choice.</strong> Clicking Distill shows a small dialog with two options: &quot;Distill inbox (N files)&quot; to automatically distill all non-Markdown files sitting in the active memory bank&#39;s <code>00_INBOX/</code>, or &quot;Select files…&quot; to open the file picker as before. The inbox option is disabled when the inbox has no distillable files. File names are listed in the dialog so you can see what will be processed.</li>\r\n<li><strong>TSV import confirmation dialog.</strong> Before importing, a confirmation dialog now shows the filename, row count, target termbase name, and language pair – a chance to catch mistakes before anything is written.</li>\r\n<li><strong>TSV import progress dialog.</strong> Large imports (e.g., 2,700+ terms) now show a progress bar with a running count instead of freezing the UI with a wait cursor.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Health Check gains confidence review and frontmatter backfill.</strong> Health Check now identifies articles with <code>confidence: low</code> and reports them as needing verification. It also detects articles missing enriched frontmatter fields and auto-fills them by inferring values from article content and folder location.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Health Check staleness detection improved.</strong> Articles not updated in more than four weeks with newer siblings on related topics are now flagged. When a newer article contradicts an older one, the older article is flagged as potentially superseded.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Expandable truncated chat responses.</strong> Long AI assistant responses are now truncated at 3,000 characters (up from 1,500) with a clickable &quot;Show full response&quot; link at the bottom of the bubble. Most regular chat responses now display in full without truncation.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Changed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>British English consistency</strong> – all user-facing licensing messages and UI labels now use &quot;licence&quot; instead of &quot;license&quot; (e.g., &quot;Please enter a licence key&quot;, &quot;Licence activated successfully&quot;, Settings → Licence), matching the project&#39;s British English convention.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>4.19.5.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog (since v4.18.49.0)</h2>\r\n<p>This is a significant release. The headline feature is <strong>SuperMemory multi-bank support</strong> – you can now keep several self-contained knowledge bases side by side (one per client, per domain, per language pair) and switch between them in a single click. The release also rolls up all improvements from v4.18.50 through v4.19.5.</p>\r\n<h3>Added – SuperMemory multi-bank support</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Memory Bank dropdown</strong> in the Supervertaler Assistant toolbar. Lists every bank under your user-data folder with the active one pre-selected. Switching is immediate: the next chat turn, batch translation and Process Inbox run all read from the new bank, and your chat history is preserved across the switch. The active bank is persisted in settings and survives Trados restarts.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Create new banks from the toolbar</strong> – pick <code>+ New memory bank…</code> at the bottom of the dropdown, enter a short name with a live preview of the final folder name, and the bank is created on disk with the full seven-folder skeleton and activated in one click.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Bundled template files</strong> – every new bank ships with the canonical templates in <code>06_TEMPLATES/</code>, so Process Inbox and Health Check work against a fresh bank out of the box.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Heal-on-activation prompt</strong> – if you activate an older bank missing its canonical template files, the plugin offers to restore them from the built-in defaults. Existing template files are never overwritten.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Shared with the Python Supervertaler</strong> – memory banks live in the shared Supervertaler user-data folder with byte-identical layout, so a bank created in Trados works unchanged in the standalone app and vice versa.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Legacy single-bank migration</strong> – the first time you open this release against an older single-bank installation, the plugin offers to move your existing folder into the new multi-bank layout automatically.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Added – Batch Translate</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Crash-recovery TMX backup.</strong> Batch Translate now writes every translated segment to a TMX file as it arrives from the AI. If Trados crashes mid-run, the backup contains everything received so far – import it into any TM to recover. Files are saved to <code>Supervertaler\\trados\\batch_backups\\</code> and are standard TMX 1.4.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Clipboard Mode now respects the Limit spinner.</strong> Previously &quot;Copy to Clipboard&quot; ignored the Limit value and always copied every matching segment; now it applies the same limit as the API batch path.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Added – Models</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Gemma 4 models</strong> – Google&#39;s Gemma 4 31B and Gemma 4 26B MoE added to both the Gemini provider and OpenRouter routes. 256K context window.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Changed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Term Picker shortcut – Ctrl tap (memoQ-style).</strong> Pressing and releasing Ctrl alone now opens the Term Picker dialogue, matching memoQ&#39;s behaviour. Maximum hold duration is 400 ms to prevent accidental triggers. The previous shortcut Ctrl+Alt+G is kept as a fallback.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Shorter panel names</strong> – docking tabs and ribbon buttons show &quot;TermLens&quot; and &quot;SuperSearch&quot; instead of &quot;Supervertaler TermLens&quot; and &quot;Supervertaler SuperSearch&quot;.</li>\r\n<li><strong>SuperMemory is the brand name; memory banks are the containers.</strong> The two-level terminology is now reflected consistently across the UI, docs, and help system.</li>\r\n<li><strong>SuperMemory is off by default for new installations.</strong> Most translators should start with the simpler workflow (TermLens glossaries + AI context awareness) and opt into SuperMemory once they have a populated bank. Existing users are unaffected.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Quick Add dialog redesigned (Ctrl+Alt+M).</strong> Field labels are now language-aware (&quot;Source term (Dutch):&quot; and &quot;Target term (English):&quot;). New &quot;Save as raw note&quot; checkbox lets you dump ambiguous knowledge into <code>00_INBOX/</code> for the AI to compile via Process Inbox.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Improved – SuperMemory workflow</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Distill now archives source files</strong> dropped in the inbox after a successful distill.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Process Inbox now recognises non-Markdown files</strong> in the inbox and shows a helpful message pointing you at Distill for binary files.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Health Check shows progress instantly</strong> – the scan now runs on a background thread with an immediate progress bubble.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Next-steps messages</strong> in Distill and Process Inbox summaries guide you through the recommended workflow.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Improved – SuperSearch</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Resizable preview pane</strong> with a draggable splitter bar.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Visible source/target divider</strong> for better visual separation.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Highlight rendering fix</strong> – no more &quot;documentsare&quot; word-collision artefacts.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Preview pane click reliability</strong> – preview now also updates on cell click, not just selection change.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Header label clipping fix</strong> – descenders (g, y, p) are no longer cut off.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Match truncation fix</strong> – matches no longer show &quot;Da...&quot; instead of &quot;Dawn&quot;.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed – AI Assistant chat</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Memory bank dropdown empty after licence reactivation.</strong> Deactivating and reactivating a licence mid-session left the memory bank dropdown empty and all AI Assistant event handlers unwired. The full initialisation now runs automatically when the licence state changes to active, without requiring a Trados restart.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Long AI responses are now truncated in the chat.</strong> Messages longer than 1,500 characters are truncated in the chat bubble with a note; full text is always available via right-click → Copy.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Chat scroll rewrite.</strong> Fixed the long-standing &quot;messages disappear into ghost white space&quot; bug on long chat histories. Users no longer need to click Clear before every chat message.</li>\r\n<li><strong>&quot;Thinking…&quot; bubble no longer bounces the chat.</strong> The animation timer no longer yanks the user back to the bottom every 2 seconds.</li>\r\n<li><strong>User scroll is respected during long operations.</strong> Scroll up to read history while Health Check or Distill is running; the chat stays where you put it.</li>\r\n<li><strong>User-initiated actions re-engage auto-scroll.</strong> Clicking Send, Health Check, Process Inbox, Distill, or switching memory banks now resets the &quot;user scrolled up&quot; flag so results land in view.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Health Check always shows a completion summary</strong> instead of leaving the user guessing whether the operation finished.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Process Inbox button correctly disabled when the inbox is empty.</strong></li>\r\n<li><strong>Duplicate &quot;Thinking…&quot; bubbles</strong> no longer appear.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Reports tab now shows entries newest-first.</strong></li>\r\n<li><strong>SuperMemory toolbar</strong> – Health Check button no longer stays greyed out after operations complete.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed – Terminology</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Termbase AI filtering – word-boundary matching.</strong> A term like &quot;claim&quot; no longer incorrectly matches &quot;Disclaimer&quot; or &quot;Proclaim&quot;.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Termbase AI filtering – initialisation flag.</strong> Per-project termbase exclusions are now preserved correctly across settings saves.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed – Proofreading</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Proofreading false positives for inline tags.</strong> Source and target now use the same plain-text extraction so tag markup never reaches the AI proofreader.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>4.18.49.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>Added</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>SuperSearch</strong> – new dockable ViewPart (View &gt; Supervertaler SuperSearch) for cross-file search, find &amp; replace, and segment navigation across all SDLXLIFF files in a project. Search source, target, or both with case-sensitive and regex options. Results grid with file name, segment number, source, target, and confirmation status columns. Matching text highlighted in yellow. Preview pane shows full segment text on single-click. Double-click to navigate to the segment in the editor. Find &amp; Replace in target text with single replace (undoable via Trados API) and Replace All with two-step safety confirmation for irreversible disk modifications. File selection dialog to include/exclude specific project files. Alt+S keyboard shortcut (also works from right-click context menu) with auto-fill from editor selection. Regex replace supports capture groups</li>\r\n<li><strong>Incognito Mode</strong> – new toggle in AI Settings that tells the AI to anonymise all personal and project data in its responses. Project names, file paths, TM names, and other identifying information are replaced with plausible placeholders. Useful for screen sharing, recording demos, or posting screenshots in forums</li>\r\n<li><strong>Multi-provider Studio Tools</strong> – Studio Tools now works with all major AI providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Grok, Mistral) in addition to Claude, each using its native function calling API</li>\r\n<li><strong>5 new Studio Tools</strong> – Project Statistics, File Status, Project Termbases, TM Info, and TM Search tools. Ask natural-language questions about your Trados projects, translation memories, and termbases</li>\r\n<li><strong>Studio Tools</strong> – the Supervertaler Assistant can now query your Trados Studio installation using natural language. Ask about projects, translation memories, or project templates and the AI will look up the answer directly from your local Studio data</li>\r\n<li><strong>SuperMemory knowledge base integration</strong> – SuperMemory articles (client profiles, domain knowledge, style guides, terminology decisions) are now automatically loaded into AI chat and batch translation prompts, with client and domain auto-detection and a 4,000-token budget</li>\r\n<li><strong>SuperMemory</strong> – a self-organising, AI-maintained translation knowledge base that stores client profiles, terminology decisions, domain conventions, and style preferences in a human-readable vault of interlinked Markdown files</li>\r\n<li><strong>SuperMemory Quick Add (Ctrl+Alt+M)</strong> – capture terms and corrections from the Trados editor into your SuperMemory vault. Also available via right-click in the editor grid</li>\r\n<li><strong>Per-project active prompt</strong> – right-click any translation prompt to set it as the active prompt for the current project, automatically selected in Batch Translate</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Changed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Single-tier licensing</strong> – replaced the three-tier pricing model (TermLens / Supervertaler Assistant / Bundle) with a single plan: Supervertaler for Trados at €20/month or €200/year. All features are now included in every paid licence. Existing subscribers on older plans are automatically upgraded to full access</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Improved</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>AI Assistant help</strong> – restructured into sub-pages: Context Awareness, File Attachments, Studio Tools, and Providers and Models</li>\r\n<li><strong>Studio Tools help page</strong> – expanded with all 9 tools and 30+ example questions organised by category</li>\r\n<li><strong>SuperMemory documentation</strong> – practical safety/backup advice, Obsidian Web Clipper setup instructions, AI context integration docs</li>\r\n<li><strong>Selectable proofreading report text</strong> – issue descriptions and suggestions in the Reports tab are now selectable with right-click copy support</li>\r\n<li><strong>Licence panel</strong> – simplified UI with single purchase link</li>\r\n<li><strong>Licensing documentation</strong> – rewritten for single-tier model</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>TM discovery</strong> – the &quot;List TMs&quot; and &quot;Find TM&quot; tools now scan all <code>.sdlproj</code> files for TM references, correctly resolving <code>sdltm.file:///</code> URIs and relative paths</li>\r\n<li><strong>Term direction inversion</strong> – fixed incorrect term matching direction for same-language locale pairs (e.g. en-US to en-GB)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>4.18.30.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>Added</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Clipboard Mode</strong> – new workflow for translating and proofreading via any web-based LLM (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.) without an API key; tick the &quot;Clipboard Mode&quot; checkbox in Batch Operations, click &quot;Copy to Clipboard&quot; to get a fully formatted prompt with numbered bilingual segments (including status annotations, terminology, and document context), paste into any LLM chat, then click &quot;Paste from Clipboard&quot; to import the translations back into Trados with full tag reconstruction and validation</li>\r\n<li><strong>Text transforms in QuickLauncher</strong> – new <code>type: transform</code> prompt type that performs local find-and-replace on the active target segment without calling an AI provider; rules are defined as <code>find:</code>/<code>replace:</code> pairs in the prompt content body with <code>\\uXXXX</code> Unicode escape support; built-in &quot;Strip U+2028&quot; transform removes invisible InDesign line separators; cleaned text is automatically copied to the clipboard; see <a href=\"https://supervertaler.gitbook.io/trados/features/text-transforms\">Text Transforms</a> in the help docs</li>\r\n<li><strong>Batch size spinner in AI Settings</strong> – new &quot;Batch size&quot; control lets users adjust the number of segments sent per API call during Batch Translate and Batch Proofread (range 5–100, default 20)</li>\r\n<li><strong>Customisable Ollama timeout</strong> – new &quot;Timeout (min)&quot; setting in AI Settings lets users override the automatic timeout (3–10 min based on model size) with a custom value up to 120 minutes, useful for long-running jobs on hardware without a dedicated GPU</li>\r\n<li><strong>QuickLauncher flat section display</strong> – right-click any QuickLauncher folder in Settings → Prompts and choose &quot;Show as section in menu&quot; to display its prompts as a flat list with a bold header instead of an expandable submenu</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Changed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong><code>built_in</code> YAML field renamed to <code>default</code></strong> – prompt files now use <code>default: true</code> instead of <code>built_in: true</code>; the old field name is still accepted for backward compatibility; all internal C# naming updated accordingly (<code>IsBuiltIn</code> → <code>IsDefault</code>, <code>EnsureBuiltInPrompts</code> → <code>EnsureDefaultPrompts</code>, etc.)</li>\r\n<li><strong>Term Picker shortcut changed from Ctrl+Alt+G to Ctrl+Alt+Down</strong> – updated shortcut binding, About dialog, and all documentation</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Batch Translate/Proofread no longer fails with &quot;Cannot determine source/target language&quot;</strong> – the language pair is now cached when a document is opened and when segments are navigated, so the AI Assistant can still resolve languages even when the editor&#39;s ActiveFile is temporarily unavailable</li>\r\n<li><strong>InDesign U+2028/U+2029 line separators no longer corrupt AI translations</strong> – Unicode line and paragraph separators (used by InDesign IDML as forced line breaks) are now replaced with spaces before sending to the AI provider, preventing spurious line breaks in translations</li>\r\n<li><strong>Duplicate &quot;What file is this segment from?&quot; prompt</strong> – old file variant (without trailing underscore) is now cleaned up on startup</li>\r\n<li><strong>Ollama default timeout for 10–12B models</strong> – models in the 10B–12B range (including TranslateGemma 12B) now default to 5 minutes instead of 3</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped:
