}\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>4.18.23.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>Added</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Document context in Batch Translate and Batch Proofread</strong> – when enabled, all source segments are now included in the system prompt for batch operations, allowing the AI to determine the document type (legal, medical, technical, etc.) and adapt its style accordingly</li>\r\n<li><strong>Term metadata in batch prompts</strong> – term definitions, domains, and usage notes from your termbases are now included in Batch Translate and Batch Proofread prompts, giving the AI deeper understanding of your terminology</li>\r\n<li><strong>QuickLauncher folder submenus</strong> – context menu now mirrors your prompt library&#39;s folder structure instead of a flat list, making it easier to organise and find prompts</li>\r\n<li><strong>New default prompts</strong> – three Explain prompts (brief, detailed, terminology) and two Files prompts (current filename, segment file source) ship out of the box in dedicated subfolders</li>\r\n<li><strong>Old explain prompts auto-cleaned</strong> – retired explain prompt variants are automatically deleted on startup to avoid duplicates</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Changed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>AI Settings panel restructured</strong> – AI context settings are now split into two clearly labelled sections: &quot;AI context (Batch operations, Chat and QuickLauncher)&quot; for settings that apply everywhere, and &quot;AI context (Chat and QuickLauncher)&quot; for settings that apply only to chat and QuickLauncher prompts</li>\r\n<li><strong>Domain → Category rename</strong> – the <code>Domain</code> property on prompts has been renamed to <code>Category</code> throughout the UI and codebase for clarity</li>\r\n<li><strong>Support links updated</strong> – About dialog, support docs, and help pages now point to the Supervertaler forum on TranslationTech; Groups.io and ProZ.com references removed</li>\r\n<li><strong>TermLens chips support max-width clamping</strong> – long source/target text is now truncated with an ellipsis when the chip exceeds the available width</li>\r\n<li><strong>Diagnostic loggers removed</strong> – temporary term-content loggers in TermbaseReader and TermMatcher that wrote to disk on every segment navigation have been removed</li>\r\n<li><strong>ARM64 SQLite DLL declared in manifest</strong> – <code>runtimes/win-arm64/native/e_sqlite3.dll</code> now has a corresponding `` entry in the plugin manifest</li>\r\n<li><strong>Default model names stay in sync</strong> – provider defaults now reference the <code>LlmModels</code> arrays directly instead of hardcoded strings</li>\r\n<li><strong>Multi-word term matching optimised</strong> – the list of multi-word terms is now cached and only rebuilt when the termbase index changes</li>\r\n<li><strong>Ping URL updated</strong> – usage statistics endpoint updated to the <code>supervertaler.com</code> domain</li>\r\n<li><strong>Term reader resilient to schema changes</strong> – <code>ReadTermEntry</code> uses column name lookup instead of hardcoded positional indices</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Segment writing uses pre-captured references</strong> – all segments (tagged and non-tagged) now use <code>ProcessSegmentPair</code> with the <code>ISegmentPair</code> captured at collection time, fixing potential issues where <code>SetActiveSegmentPair</code> navigation had not completed before writing the translation</li>\r\n<li><strong>Line breaks preserved in Visio and similar formats</strong> – when the source segment stores line breaks as literal <code>\\n</code> in text content (Visio <code>.vsdx</code>, Excel) rather than as separate placeholder tags (DOCX <code>w:br</code>), the newlines are now correctly preserved in the translated target instead of being silently dropped</li>\r\n<li><strong>Gemini API key no longer exposed in error messages</strong> – API key moved from URL query parameter to <code>x-goog-api-key</code> request header</li>\r\n<li><strong>Licence file corruption no longer silently resets to trial</strong> – a warning is now shown when the licence file is unreadable, prompting the user to re-enter their key</li>\r\n<li><strong>Gemini response parsing more robust</strong> – regex anchored to the <code>candidates[].content.parts[].text</code> response structure to avoid matching wrong fields</li>\r\n<li><strong>Unicode escapes in translations handled correctly</strong> – <code>\\uXXXX</code> sequences in LLM responses are now unescaped to the correct characters</li>\r\n<li><strong>Line endings preserved in translation</strong> – when the LLM emits a bare newline instead of a `` tag placeholder, the correct line ending is now restored in the target segment: soft-return tags (DOCX <code>↵</code>) are re-inserted by cloning the source tag; for file formats where the newline is already embedded in text content (Visio, Excel, plain text), the newline is preserved as-is in the target IText node</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>4.18.16.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>Added</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Cost protection</strong> – QuickLauncher prompts are now standalone (no chat history sent), preventing accidental high costs from accumulated context; chat messages are constrained by a token budget that automatically trims old messages; a cost warning dialog appears when estimated input exceeds $0.50</li>\r\n<li><strong>Enriched API error messages</strong> – common errors (403 model not enabled, 401 invalid key, 429 rate limit, 402 insufficient funds) now show user-friendly guidance instead of raw JSON</li>\r\n<li><strong>Help system improvements</strong> – fixed off-by-one in Settings tab help links; Settings help button now shows a dropdown preview consistent with other panels; added help topics for General, QuickLauncher, and Usage Statistics</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Changed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>OpenAI models streamlined</strong> – replaced GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 Mini, and o4-mini with GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.4 Mini; default model is now GPT-5.4 Mini; existing users are auto-migrated</li>\r\n<li><strong>GPT-5.x API compatibility</strong> – use <code>max_completion_tokens</code> instead of <code>max_tokens</code> for GPT-5.x models</li>\r\n<li><strong>Default prompts</strong> – &quot;Built-in&quot; renamed to &quot;Default&quot; throughout (folder, UI labels, docs); default prompts are now immutable (use Clone to modify); &quot;Show in QuickLauncher menu&quot; checkbox for hiding prompts</li>\r\n<li><strong>QuickLauncher menu</strong> – section headers (&quot;Default&quot; / &quot;Custom&quot;) replace star indicators</li>\r\n<li><strong>AutoPrompt hidden in Proofread mode</strong> – the AutoPrompt link is no longer shown when Batch Operations is in Proofread mode</li>\r\n<li><strong>AutoPrompt language softened</strong> – replaced aggressive meta-prompt wording (&quot;FORBIDDEN&quot;, &quot;NON-NEGOTIABLE&quot;) with clearer, firmer alternatives to reduce LLM safety-filter refusals</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>4.18.13.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>v4.18.13 – 25 March 2026</h3>\r\n<p><strong>Covers all changes since v4.18.8 (the previous App Store release).</strong></p>\r\n<h4>New Features</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Markdown rendering in TermLens popup</strong> – Notes and Definition fields now render Markdown formatting (tables, bold, italic, headings, bullet lists, code blocks)</li>\r\n<li><strong>Resizable TermLens popup</strong> – drag the bottom-right corner grip to resize; width is remembered for the session</li>\r\n<li><strong>Copy raw Markdown from AI Assistant</strong> – right-click → Copy preserves original Markdown (tables, formatting)</li>\r\n<li><strong>Unified prompt library schema</strong> – prompts use a consistent YAML frontmatter format shared between Workbench and Trados</li>\r\n<li><strong>App-specific prompt filtering</strong> – tag prompts for Workbench only, Trados only, or both</li>\r\n<li><strong>App dropdown in prompt editor</strong> – choose which app a prompt applies to</li>\r\n<li><strong>Variable insertion menu reorganised</strong> – Ctrl+, now groups variables into Common and Trados-specific sections</li>\r\n<li><strong>MultiTerm termbases in AI Settings</strong> – MultiTerm termbases now appear in the &quot;Termbases included in AI prompts&quot; checklist</li>\r\n<li><strong>MultiTerm diagnostic logging</strong> – loading failures logged to <code>multiterm_debug.log</code></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h4>Improvements</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Feature renamed: AutoPrompt</strong> – &quot;Analyse Project &amp; Generate Prompt&quot; is now called AutoPrompt</li>\r\n<li><strong>Wider TermLens popup</strong> – default max width increased from 500px to 700px</li>\r\n<li><strong>AI Assistant uses proper Markdown tables</strong> – pipe delimiters and separator rows</li>\r\n<li><strong>Per-project settings reliably remembered</strong> – switching between Trados projects now correctly restores termbase paths, enabled/disabled lists, and AI context settings</li>\r\n<li><strong>Per-project settings auto-saved</strong> – settings are saved on first project encounter, on project switch, and on Trados shutdown</li>\r\n<li><strong>Human-readable project settings filenames</strong> – <code>{hash} - {project name}.json</code> format</li>\r\n<li><strong>Prompt YAML keys standardised</strong> – legacy keys still accepted for backward compatibility</li>\r\n<li><strong>User data folder restructured</strong> – auto-migrated on first run</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h4>Bug Fixes</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Settings accessible when trial expires</strong> – the gear button now opens Settings even when the trial has expired, so users can enter a licence key</li>\r\n<li><strong>AI Assistant gear button visible</strong> – no longer hidden behind the licence overlay</li>\r\n<li><strong>Clearer expired trial message</strong> – both panels now say &quot;Click the ⚙ button above&quot;</li>\r\n<li><strong>In-plugin purchase links use live checkout</strong> – no longer pointing to test mode URLs</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see the <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases\">GitHub releases</a>.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n v4.18.3)</h2>\r\n<h3>v4.18.8 – 2026-03-24</h3>\r\n<h4>Fixed</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>In-plugin purchase links now use live checkout</strong> – the &quot;Buy&quot; links in Settings → License were still pointing to test mode URLs</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>v4.18.7 – 2026-03-24</h3>\r\n<h4>Changed</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Trial period reduced to 14 days</strong> – down from 90 days; the free trial now runs for 14 days from first launch</li>\r\n<li><strong>Live payment processing</strong> – switched from test mode to live Lemon Squeezy checkout</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>v4.18.6 – 2026-03-23</h3>\r\n<h4>Added</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Prompt inspector in Reports tab</strong> – every AI API call can now be logged with the full system prompt, messages, response, token counts, and estimated cost; enable via &quot;Log prompts and responses to Reports tab&quot; in AI Settings</li>\r\n<li><strong>Expandable prompt sections</strong> – click &quot;Show system prompt...&quot;, &quot;Show messages...&quot;, or &quot;Show response...&quot; to view the full text; press Escape to collapse; &quot;Copy&quot; copies a single section, &quot;Copy all&quot; copies everything</li>\r\n<li><strong>Batch translate and proofread logging</strong> – batch operations now appear in the Reports tab when prompt logging is enabled</li>\r\n<li><strong>Prompt name in Reports tab</strong> – entries show the prompt template name (e.g. &quot;QuickLauncher · Explain in Context · 14:32:05&quot;)</li>\r\n<li><strong>Clone prompt</strong> – right-click any prompt in the Prompt Manager and select &quot;Clone&quot; to create a copy with &quot;(2)&quot; appended</li>\r\n<li><strong>QuickLauncher menu heading</strong> – Ctrl+Q menu now shows &quot;Supervertaler QuickLauncher&quot; at the top; click it to open Settings → Prompts tab</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h4>Fixed</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Tracked changes no longer corrupt term additions</strong> – Add Term, Quick-Add, Non-Translatable, QuickLauncher prompts, and Expand Selection now strip deleted tracked changes, adding only the final text</li>\r\n<li><strong>QuickLauncher segment context</strong> – QuickLauncher prompts now pass clean segment text without tracked changes markup</li>\r\n<li><strong>Prompt log cards no longer squashed</strong> – the resize handler was recalculating prompt log card heights using the proofreading layout logic, hiding all expandable sections</li>\r\n<li><strong>New prompts in subfolders now saved correctly</strong> – creating a prompt while a subfolder was selected would create a wrongly-named folder instead of placing the file in the correct subfolder</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>v4.18.4 – 2026-03-23</h3>\r\n<h4>Fixed</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Tracked changes no longer confuse AI features</strong> – proofreading, batch translation, AI chat, and prompt generation now read only the final (accepted) text from segments with tracked changes, instead of including both deleted and inserted text</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h4>Changed</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Updated LLM model lineup</strong> – OpenAI: GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 Mini, GPT-5.4, o4-mini; Gemini: added 3.1 Pro (Preview); Ollama: Qwen 3 bumped to 14B</li>\r\n<li><strong>Descriptive model tooltips</strong> – each model now shows a short description to help translators choose the right one</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>4.18.3.0</strong></p><h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>Added</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Delete prompt folders</strong> – right-click any folder in the prompt library tree and select &quot;Delete Folder&quot; to remove it and all prompts inside (with confirmation dialog)</li>\r\n<li><strong>Refresh button in Prompt Manager</strong> – toolbar &quot;Refresh&quot; button reloads prompts from disk, reflecting any changes made outside Trados (e.g. in Windows Explorer or Supervertaler Workbench)</li>\r\n<li><strong>UI scale setting</strong> – new General tab in Settings with UI scale selector for high-DPI displays</li>\r\n<li><strong>Chat font size controls</strong> – A+/A− buttons in the AI Assistant to adjust chat bubble font size; persisted across sessions</li>\r\n<li><strong>Diagnostic logging for duplicate source terms</strong> – temporary logging to <code>%LocalAppData%\\Supervertaler.Trados\\termlens_diag.log</code> when multiple entries share the same source term. Helps diagnose any remaining edge cases. Will be removed in a future release.</li>\r\n<li><strong>TreeView-based Prompt Manager</strong> – the Prompts settings tab has been completely redesigned. The flat grid has been replaced with a folder-based tree view that mirrors the on-disk <code>prompt_library</code> structure. Click any prompt to see its content in the detail pane on the right. Click &quot;System Prompt&quot; to view and edit the system prompt. Folders can be created, and prompts can be dragged and dropped between folders.</li>\r\n<li><strong>QuickLauncher keyboard shortcuts</strong> – assign Ctrl+Alt+1 through Ctrl+Alt+0 to individual QuickLauncher prompts in Settings → Prompts. Each shortcut runs the assigned prompt instantly without opening the menu. Shortcuts are shown next to prompt names in the Ctrl+Q menu.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Prompt reordering</strong> – prompts within a folder can be moved up or down using the ▲/▼ buttons in the toolbar. The order is persisted via a <code>sort_order</code> field in the YAML frontmatter and applies to the Ctrl+Q menu as well.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Quick model switching</strong> – click the provider/model label at the bottom of the AI Assistant chat to change models without opening Settings. Shows &quot;Click to change model&quot; tooltip on hover.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Document attachments</strong> – attach documents directly to AI Assistant messages for context. The AI receives the full extracted text alongside your message. Supported formats: DOCX, DOC, PDF, RTF, PPTX, PPT, XLSX, XLS, CSV, TSV, TMX, SDLXLIFF, XLIFF/XLF, TBX, TXT, Markdown, HTML, JSON, and XML. Drag and drop files onto the chat input, or use the attach button to browse.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Multi-line Definition and Notes fields</strong> – the term entry editor now uses expandable multi-line text areas for Definition and Notes fields, with a pop-open button to toggle between compact (3 lines) and expanded (8 lines) views. Line breaks in definitions and notes are now preserved correctly.</li>\r\n<li><strong>TermScan filtering for prompt generation</strong> – &quot;Analyse Project &amp; Generate Prompt&quot; now scans your document&#39;s source segments and filters termbase terms down to only those that actually appear in the document. This produces dramatically smaller, more focused glossaries in the generated prompt (e.g. 123 relevant terms from 2,680 total). The status message shows the filter count: &quot;filtered X relevant from Y total&quot;.</li>\r\n<li><strong>One-click plugin update</strong> – the &quot;Update Available&quot; dialogue now has an &quot;Install Update&quot; button that downloads and installs the new version directly, without opening a browser. Just click, restart Trados, and you&#39;re running the latest version.</li>\r\n<li><strong>&quot;What&#39;s new&quot; link in update dialogue</strong> – view the release notes before updating.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Changed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>DPI-aware UI rendering</strong> – TermLens, AI Assistant chat bubbles, term blocks, and settings dialog now scale correctly on high-DPI displays using the new <code>UiScale</code> helper</li>\r\n<li><strong>TermLens colour coding docs</strong> – renamed &quot;Lavender&quot; to &quot;Purple&quot; for abbreviation match chips, as it is more recognisable as a colour name.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Improved term popup readability</strong> – definition and notes text is now darker and more readable in the TermLens hover popup.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Multi-line term fields</strong> – Definition and Notes in the term entry editor now have expand/collapse buttons (▲/▼) to toggle between compact and expanded views.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Resizable Prompts panel</strong> – the divider between the tree and detail pane in Settings → Prompts can now be dragged left or right to resize.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Prompt generator respects TM toggle</strong> – the &quot;Analyse Project &amp; Generate Prompt&quot; feature now respects the &quot;Include TM matches in AI context&quot; setting. Previously, TM reference pairs were always collected regardless of this toggle.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Auto-restart after update</strong> – clicking &quot;Install Update&quot; now offers to automatically restart Trados Studio, instead of asking the user to close and reopen it manually. Saves time on the lengthy Trados startup cycle.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Unified attach button</strong> – the image-only attach button has been replaced with a universal paperclip (\uD83D\uDCCE) icon that handles both images and documents. The file dialogue is organised into categories: Images, Documents, Spreadsheets, Translation files, and Text files. The tooltip lists all supported formats.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Improved term popup formatting</strong> – definition and notes labels are now bold in the hover popup, and multi-line content uses hanging indentation so continuation lines align with the first line of text rather than the label.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Chat avatars</strong> – each message in the AI Assistant now has a small avatar header: a gray &quot;AI&quot; circle with &quot;Supervertaler Assistant&quot; for AI responses, and a blue person silhouette with &quot;You&quot; for your messages. Makes it easy to tell who said what at a glance.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Animated thinking indicator</strong> – the AI Assistant now shows a persistent animated bubble in the chat area while waiting for a response. The bubble cycles through reassuring messages (&quot;Thinking…&quot;, &quot;Still working on it…&quot;, &quot;Generating response…&quot;, etc.) so you always know the AI is still processing. Previously, the thin &quot;Thinking…&quot; label at the bottom could disappear during long operations, making it look like the request had silently failed.</li>\r\n<li><strong>System-initiated messages styled as assistant bubbles</strong> – messages triggered by buttons (e.g. &quot;Analyse Project &amp; Generate Prompt&quot;) now display with assistant styling (gray, left-aligned) instead of user styling, since you didn&#39;t type them yourself.</li>\r\n<li><strong>TM reference pairs filtered to confirmed segments</strong> – the prompt generator now only includes human-confirmed segments (Translated, Approved, or Signed-off) as reference pairs. Unconfirmed AI-generated translations are excluded to avoid feeding unverified output back as &quot;correct&quot; references. Pairs are sampled evenly across the document for diversity.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Right-click context menu on prompt folders</strong> – right-clicking a folder in the prompt library now correctly selects the folder before showing the context menu (WinForms TreeView doesn&#39;t auto-select on right-click)</li>\r\n<li><strong>Manifest version sync</strong> – the <code>pluginpackage.manifest.xml</code> version was stuck at 4.18.0.0 while the DLL and plugin.xml were at 4.18.1.0. All three version files are now correctly synced to 4.18.2.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Editing terms in inverted-direction termbases corrupted entries</strong> – when editing a term via the multi-entry editor (right-click → Edit Term) and the termbase direction was opposite to the project direction (e.g. EN→NL termbase in a NL→EN project), the source and target terms were saved swapped. This caused the edited entry to disappear from TermLens on the next refresh and left corrupted data in the termbase. The multi-entry editor now correctly detects and handles inverted termbase directions, matching the behaviour of the single-entry editor.</li>\r\n<li><strong>HttpClient timeout causing prompt generation failures</strong> – the .NET HttpClient&#39;s default 100-second timeout was silently overriding our per-request timeout settings (up to 10 minutes). This caused all long-running API calls (prompt generation, large batch translations) to fail after exactly 100 seconds across all providers. Fixed by setting HttpClient.Timeout to infinite and managing timeouts via CancellationToken as intended.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Silent timeout errors</strong> – when an API request timed out, the thinking indicator would disappear with no error message. Now shows a diagnostic message with model name, prompt size, and max output tokens to help troubleshoot.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Expand buttons in term editor</strong> – fixed z-order issue where the Definition expand button was hidden behind the text box. Both expand buttons now render correctly.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Stale prompt dropdown</strong> – deleting prompts from the Prompt Manager no longer leaves ghost entries in the Batch Operations prompt dropdown. The dropdown now refreshes whenever the Prompt Manager closes, regardless of whether you clicked OK or Cancel (because deletions happen immediately on disk).</li>\r\n<li><strong>API timeout for large output requests</strong> – prompt generation and other requests that produce long AI responses (&gt; 8,192 tokens) now use a 10-minute timeout instead of timing out prematurely. This prevents the &quot;thinking&quot; indicator from disappearing mid-generation on complex documents.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Prompt generation truncation</strong> – the &quot;Analyse Project &amp; Generate Prompt&quot; feature no longer cuts off long prompts. Output token limit increased from 4,096 to 32,768, allowing comprehensive prompts with large glossaries and TM reference pairs.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Correct version in plugin packages</strong> – the <code>.sdlplugin</code> manifest now reads the version from the project file instead of using a hardcoded value. The DLL, manifest, and plugin.xml versions are guaranteed to match.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Stale assembly references</strong> – fixed two action entries in plugin.xml that were stuck on old version numbers (4.5.0 and 4.10.0). The version bump script now uses pattern matching to catch all references, preventing this from recurring.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a href=\"https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases\">https://github.com/Supervertaler/Supervertaler-for-Trados/releases</a></p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid Beijer"},"versions":[{"versionId":2251,"isPrivatePlugin":true,"isNavigationLink":false,"appHasStudioPluginInstaller":true,"fileHash":"0e410e6cb5f848976082d728c55b5179c507a9a3cad03d816374c5c6936f67a6","changelog":"<h2>Changelog</h2>\r\n<h3>Fixed (termbase direction handling – architectural overhaul)</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Term matching now uses the termbase&#39;s declared direction, not per-entry language metadata.</strong> Legacy write-path bugs (pre-v4.19.13) left many termbases with a mix of entries whose per-entry <code>source_lang</code> / <code>target_lang</code> tags didn&#39;t agree with the termbase&#39;s declaration – and TermLens trusted those per-entry tags when deciding whether to invert for reverse-direction projects. The result was entries silently not matching even though their text was correct. Direction decisions now pull source/target language from the canonical <code>termbases</code> table. Resilient to corrupted per-entry tags, and root-cause fix for the &quot;my termbase entry exists but TermLens says no match&quot; class of bug.</li>\r\n<li><strong>The term entry editor now always shows fields in termbase-declared direction.</strong> Previously the edit dialog inverted field layout when the project direction differed from the termbase&#39;s, and did so inconsistently depending on which entry point opened it (the Termbase Editor grid right-click didn&#39;t invert, but the TermLens chip right-click did). Fields, labels, and save flow are now always in termbase order, matching the Termbase Editor grid.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Fixed (Prompt Manager ↔ Batch Translate sync)</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>&quot;Set as active prompt for this project&quot; now takes effect immediately in the Batch Translate dropdown and works for any prompt.</strong> Right-clicking a prompt in the Prompt Manager used to have no visible effect on the Batch dropdown until the Settings dialog was closed – and even then, prompts whose <code>Category</code> was not <code>Translate</code> (or <code>Proofread</code>, in proofread mode) were silently filtered out, so the checkmark had nowhere to land. The dropdown now live-refreshes the moment the active prompt is toggled, without closing the Settings dialog, and the active prompt always appears regardless of its folder or category. Works from all entry points (AI Assistant gear, TermLens gear, About dialog). Cancelling the Settings dialog still reverts the change; clicking OK persists it.</li>\r\n<li><strong>New prompts created at the Prompt Manager tree root are now visible in the Batch Translate dropdown.</strong> Previously, creating a new prompt without first selecting a folder left its <code>Category</code> empty and the Batch dropdown&#39;s category filter silently excluded it. New prompts now default to the <code>Translate</code> category when no folder is selected.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Corrected stale pricing for Claude Opus 4.6 and Haiku 4.5.</strong> The internal pricing table had Opus 4.6 at the pre-4.6 rate of $15 / $75 per MTok – Anthropic dropped Opus pricing to $5 / $25 with the 4.6 release. Haiku 4.5 was listed at $0.80 / $4.00, corrected to the current $1.00 / $5.00. Cost estimates shown in the AI Assistant and Batch Translate were over-stating Opus usage and under-stating Haiku usage – now accurate.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Added</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>&quot;Reverse source/target&quot; right-click action in the Termbase Editor.</strong> Fixes one or many reversed-direction entries at once. Menu label dynamically shows the count when multiple rows are selected. Swaps <code>source_term</code> ↔ <code>target_term</code>, language tags, abbreviations, and flips every linked synonym&#39;s language tag, all in one transaction. Right-click on the grid now also preserves multi-row selection when the clicked row was already part of the selection (matches Windows list conventions).</li>\r\n<li><strong>Claude Opus 4.7 support.</strong> Anthropic&#39;s new flagship model (released 2026-04-16) is now selectable in AI Settings under the Claude provider and via the OpenRouter gateway (<code>anthropic/claude-opus-4.7</code>). Opus 4.7 has a 1M-token context window, 128k max output, and is Anthropic&#39;s most capable generally available model. Pricing is $5 / input MTok, $25 / output MTok – the same as Opus 4.6. Sonnet 4.6 remains the recommended default for most translation work; reach for Opus 4.7 when you need top-tier reasoning or long-context jobs.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Note on Opus 4.7 tokenizer</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Claude Opus 4.7 uses a new tokenizer that can use <strong>~1.0×–1.35× more tokens</strong> for the same text compared to earlier models. Pre-send cost estimates (the <code>chars / 4</code> heuristic) will under-estimate Opus 4.7 costs by a similar margin. Actual billing is based on Anthropic&#39;s token counts.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For the full changelog, see: <a initial;\">の汎用エンジンのほか、お客さまが独自に作成した「カスタムモデル」を使った翻訳が可能。</span><span lang=\"EN-US\" style=\"font-size: 11pt; font-family: &quot;MS Gothic&quot;; background-image: initial; background-position: initial; background-size: initial; background-repeat: initial; background-attachment: initial; background-origin: initial; background-clip: initial;\"><o:p></o:p></span></p>\r\n\r\n<p class=\"MsoNormal\" background-attachment: initial; background-origin: initial; background-clip: initial;\">少量の対訳（翻訳メモリ）からモデルを生成し、用語や表現を次回以降の翻訳に反映することで特に専門文書翻訳の効率化・コスト削減が期待できます。</span><span lang=\"EN-US\" <strong>TAUS EPIC App</strong> integrates with the TAUS EPIC API to automatically evaluate translation quality. It provides Quality Estimation (QE) scores for translated segments and, when Automatic Post-Editing (APE) is enabled, updates translations with improved output and displays the corresponding APE score.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h1>TAUS EPIC App</h1>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Table of Contents</h2>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><a href=\"#intro_section_id\">Introduction</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#features_section_id\">Key Features</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#install_section_id\">Installation</a><br>  3.1. <a href=\"#prerequisites\">Technical Requirements</a><br>  3.2. <a href=\"#new-installation\">Step-by-Step Installation</a>  </li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#updates_section_id\">Updates</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#setup_section_id\">Configuration and Setup</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#usingapp_section_id\">Using the App</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#t&amp;f_section_id\">Troubleshooting &amp; FAQs</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#entitlement_section_id\">Entitlement</a></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"intro_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>The <strong>TAUS EPIC App</strong> is an automated workflow service designed to evaluate translation quality through integration with the TAUS EPIC API. It processes all translated segments and applies <strong>Quality Estimation (QE)</strong> and <strong>Automatic Post-Editing (APE)</strong> recommendations.</p>\r\n<p>When configured, the application provides the following functionality:</p>\r\n<p><strong>Quality Estimation (QE)</strong>: Displays the QE score for each translated segment, indicating the predicted quality of the output.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Automatic Post-Editing (APE)</strong>: If enabled, the app -</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>automatically updates the translated text based on APE recommendations and </li>\r\n<li>displays the improved APE score</li>\r\n<li>displays remarks which contains short explanation for the suggested change.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>This workflow ensures that translations are systematically assessed and, when applicable, enhanced to achieve higher quality standards.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"features_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>Following are the core features of the app:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Installs on the <strong>Trados</strong> platform</li>\r\n<li>Integrates seamlessly with <strong>TAUS EPIC APIs</strong>  </li>\r\n<li>Automates quality assessment (estimation) of translated content.</li>\r\n<li>Improve the quality of segments that scored low during estimation</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"install_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Installation Guide</h2>\r\n<p><a id=\"prerequisites\"></a></p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<p>The following are the prerequisites to install and use the <strong>TAUS EPIC App</strong>:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Users should have an active <strong>Trados</strong> account.</li>\r\n<li>Users should have an active <strong>TAUS EPIC API</strong> subscription.</li>\r\n<li>Users should have the necessary permissions on <strong>Trados</strong> to install apps as well as create/configure workflow templates.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><a id=\"new-installation\"></a></p>\r\n<h3>Step-by-Step Installation</h3>\r\n<h4>Option 1: Install via RWS AppStore</h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Visit <strong><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/\">RWS AppStore</a></strong></li>\r\n<li>Search for <strong>&quot;TAUS EPIC App&quot;</strong></li>\r\n<li>Sign in to your <strong>Trados account</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>Install</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h4>Option 2: Install via Trados Account</h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Login</strong> to <strong>Trados</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click on <strong>Account Settings</strong></li>\r\n<li>Navigate to <strong>RWS AppStore</strong></li>\r\n<li>Locate <strong>TAUS EPIC App</strong> under <strong>Apps</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>Install</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Install.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\">   </p>\r\n<p>Once installed, select <strong>API Environment</strong>, enter the <strong>API Key</strong>, enable <strong>Automatic Post Editing (APE)</strong> (if needed) and enter <strong>Max-Threshold &amp; Low Threshold</strong> (if applicable) and click  <strong>Complete</strong>.   </p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Config.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\">  </p>\r\n<h5>API Environment</h5>\r\n<p>The TAUS EPIC API provides two environments: <strong>Sandbox</strong> and <strong>Production</strong>. Each environment corresponds to a different API URL. </p>\r\n<h5>API Key</h5>\r\n<p>Use the TAUS EPIC API subscription key that corresponds to the selected environment.</p>\r\n<h5>Enable Automatic Post Editing (APE)</h5>\r\n<p>This optional setting enables <strong>Automatic Post-Editing (APE)</strong>.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Enabled</strong> → APE will be applied.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Disabled</strong> → Only <strong>Quality Estimation (QE)</strong> will be used.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h5>Max-Threshold</h5>\r\n<p>When APE is enabled, segment pairs with scores <strong>below</strong> this threshold (decimal value) will be sent for APE.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Value range: 0.0 – 1.0</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h5>Low Threshold</h5>\r\n<p>When APE is enabled, segment pairs with scores <strong>above</strong> this threshold (decimal value) will be sent for APE.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Default value: 0.0</li>\r\n<li>Value range: 0.0 – 1.0</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><em><strong>Note:</strong> For detailed configuration and usage guidelines, refer to the official <a href=\"https://api.taus.net/2.0/estimate/documentation\">TAUS EPIC API documentation</a>.</em></p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"updates_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Updates</h2>\r\n<p>If an update is available, a <strong>notification</strong> will be sent via email or displayed in <strong>Trados notifications</strong>.\r\nTo check if any update to app is available, follow the steps below:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>Login to Trados  </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Click the Account Settings icon in the top-right corner  </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/UpdateSettings.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Choose “RWS AppStore”.  </p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/UpdateOption.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Under <strong>Installed Apps</strong>, it will show either <strong>Up to Date</strong> or <strong>Update Available</strong>.  </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>If an update is available, click on the app and select <strong>Update</strong>.  </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/UpdateFinal.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"setup_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Configuration and Setup</h2>\r\n<p>Following are the steps to configure the application:</p>\r\n<h3>Workflow Template Setup</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>After application is installed, go to <strong>Resources</strong> -&gt; <strong>Workflows</strong> -&gt; Click on <strong>Workflow Templates</strong>.  </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Select any existing Workflow Template and click on Copy to duplicate it.<br> &gt; Alternatively, you can click on <strong>New Workflow Template</strong> button.    </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_WT.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Enter appropriate Name, Description and select Location. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_Details.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It is recommended to place the app anywhere in the TRANSLATION phase (where text is already translated and ready to assess the quality). </p>\r\n<p> Example: After <strong>Translation Automated QA Check</strong> in TRANSLATION phase. Click on three dots on the <strong>done</strong> outcome of  <strong>Translation Automated QA Check</strong>.</p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_Dots.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Click on <strong>Change Transition</strong>.</p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_Transition.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It will open a pop-up. Under <strong>Automated Tasks</strong>, search for the app name &quot;TAUS EPIC App&quot;. Click on the App.</p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_Search_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It will place the <strong>TAUS EPIC App</strong> after <strong>Translation Automated QA Check</strong>. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_Place_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Click on plus icon, it will open the same pop-up.  Under <strong>Automated Tasks</strong>, search for the app name <strong>Translation Memory Update</strong>. Click on the App with <strong>Existing</strong> text after its name. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_NextApp.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It will complete the Workflow Template changes. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_Complete_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Click on <strong>Create &amp; Activate</strong>. </p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/Setup_Activate_1.png\" alt=\"Setup\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h3>Workflow Setup</h3>\r\n<p>Go to <strong>Resources</strong> -&gt; <strong>Workflows</strong>, and ensure that the translation Workflow uses the previously set up Workflow Template.  </p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"usingapp_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Usage Instructions</h2>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>When any <strong>Trados</strong> project reaches the <strong>TRANSLATION</strong> stage,  <strong>TAUS EPIC App</strong> will run (as per workflow order).</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>App will call the TAUS EPIC API for the configured Subscription Key and other parameters.  </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>App will update translated files and their metadata for the TAUS EPIC API response.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>After TAUS EPIC App completed execution, open the file in Trados Online Editor. Select any segment and click on Show Translation Quality Panel. It will display QE Score, APE Score and Remarks from TAUS EPIC API. </p>\r\n<p> <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/18d8b309-dcd8-4eb1-81c6-caf223b67930/Resources/SampleOutput1r1.png\" alt=\"Usage\"></p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"t&amp;f_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Troubleshooting and FAQs</h2>\r\n<h5>Q1: How can I check if the TAUS EPIC App task completed successfully?</h5>\r\n<p>In <strong>Trados Cloud</strong>, open your project and navigate to <strong>Task History</strong>. This will list all tasks.<br>Select the relevant <strong>TAUS EPIC App</strong> task to view its details.<br>In the <strong>Comment</strong> field, a value of <strong>SUCCESS</strong> indicates that all segments were evaluated successfully by the <strong>TAUS EPIC API</strong>.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h5>Q2: What happens if the evaluation of any segment fails?</h5>\r\n<p>The app automatically retries a failed segment up to <strong>3 times</strong>, with a <strong>1 second pause</strong> between attempts.  </p>\r\n<p>If a segment still fails, you can check the status in the same way as above:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Open <strong>Task History</strong> in your <strong>Trados</strong> project.  </li>\r\n<li>Select the <strong>TAUS EPIC App</strong> task.  </li>\r\n<li>In the <strong>Comment</strong> field, you will see <strong>COMPLETED WITH ERRORS</strong>, followed by a <strong>Trace ID</strong> (e.g., <code>Trace Id: 22844b79-23d9-44f2-a468</code>).</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p>Provide this Trace ID to the support team so they can investigate the error details.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h5>Q3: Which TAUS EPIC API version and endpoint does the app use?</h5>\r\n<p>The app currently uses <strong>TAUS EPIC API version 2.0</strong>.<br>For Quality Estimation (QE) and Automatic Post‑Editing (APE) evaluation, it calls the <strong>/Estimate</strong> endpoint.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p><a id=\"entitlement_section_id\"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Entitlement</h2>\r\n<p>This app does not require additional entitlement for use.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n These files are essentially SQLite so we have taken the open-source project <a href=\"https://github.com/sqlitebrowser/sqlitebrowser\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">DB Browser for SQLite</a> and published a modified version of it in the <a href=\"https://github.com/RWS/sdltm_browser\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Trados AppStore Team</a> github site. All feedback should be channelled through the <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">RWS Community forums</a>.</p><p><strong>Open-source Project</strong></p><p>DB Browser for SQLite is developed predominantly in C++, utilising the Qt framework for its graphical user interface components. As an open-source project, contributions are highly encouraged. First you have to check the Alphanumeric checkbox in &quot;Fields &amp; Settings&quot; in the Translation Memories View and then you have to Re-Index the Translation Memory.</p>\r\n<p>Trados Studio 2017 introduced upLIFT. You need to run your TMs through a two stage process one at a time to be able to prepare them for fragment matching as part of the upLIFT feature. First you build your translation model and then you align the fragments in &quot;Fragment Matching&quot; in the Translation Memories View.</p>\r\n<p>If you have many Translation Memories this exercise could take a long time! The &quot;TM Lifting&quot; application does a few important things:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>It checks the box and re-indexes the TM if you wish to Reindex the TMs</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It builds the translation model and aligns the fragments if you wish to prepare for upLIFT</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It can do this for multiple TMs at the same time</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p>So you can drag and drop your TMs into the application interface and then select &quot;Reindex&quot; or &quot;upLIFT&quot; and leave it to run. using the 2014/2015 versions, these are standalone installers (<em>Reindex Translation Memories</em>) with a shortcut placed on your Desktop -</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4520.pastedimage1600083169904v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>For version \"></p>\r\n<p><strong>NOTES:</strong></p>\r\n<p>Before starting the application, we suggest removing TM&#39;s from the &#39;Translation Memory view&#39;, as otherwise these may be &#39;locked&#39; and prevent the application from running smoothly.</p>\r\n<p>Although this application creates a new &#39;View&#39;, allowing you to work on other things in Studio, you might want to consider running this application overnight to ensure maximal system performance especially if you are working with larger TM&#39;s.</p>\r\n<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>The Application</p>\r\n<p><strong>Tm Lifting</strong>is a plugin that allows the user to UpLift multiple File based TMs and also reIndex File based/Server based TMs.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>When you first open the plugin you will see a window with two tabs: &quot;File-Based Translation Memories&quot; and &quot;Server-Based Translation Memories&quot;.</p>\r\n<p>Loading File based TMs can be achieved in multiple ways. You can click the &quot;Browse...&quot; button and select your TMs, check the &quot;Load Studio TMs&quot; checkbox to load the TMs you are already using in Trados Studio or by simply dragging and droping the TM in the box on the top-left corner of the screen.</p>\r\n<p>For reindexing or upLifting File based TMs you can check the specific checkbox for the operation and then click the &quot;Process&quot; button. During the operation, progress bar will appear on the top-right corner and also a log with the process in the lower box.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7673.wiki-tm-Lifting.JPG\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7673.wiki-tm-Lifting.JPG\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<p>When selecting the &quot;Server-Based Translation Memories&quot; tab a login form will pop-up, in which you are asked to introduce your Groupshare credentials.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7026.pastedimage1531917487799v1.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7026.pastedimage1531917487799v1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<p>After you introduce your credentials, a grid will appear with details regarding every TM from that server. You can observe that the checkboxes on the bottom-left side are disabled, because we only support the reindexing operation for Server based TMs.</p>\r\n<p>To reindex a TM, you have to select\u00A0the row for that specific TM by clicking the cell from the first column and pressing the &quot;Process&quot; button.</p>\r\n<p>The TM will be scheduled for reindexing in the server and you\u00A0will notice that the &quot;Status&quot; of that TM was changed to &quot;QUEUED&quot;.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7181.pastedimage1531926471789v1.png\"><img The TMX files are analyzed and the number of TUs and languages are displayed. If you had already converted the Workbench TMs to Studio TMs using the Studio TM upgrade wizard (and have selected option 2 on the previous page), you also have to select that Studio TM here.&nbsp;</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5633.pastedimage1548070522183v15.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/723x463/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5633.pastedimage1548070522183v15.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>On the third page, you select the TM into which the optimized translation units should be imported. This could be either a newly created TM or an existing TM. This TM should be different from the input TM you chose on the previous page.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5342.pastedimage1548070538498v16.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/880x568/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5342.pastedimage1548070538498v16.png\" alt=\" \"></a>&nbsp;</p><p>Finally, on the processing page, you will see detailed progress on the TM optimization process. When all the steps are completed, you can browse to the output TM or open the TM in Studio using the buttons that appear at the bottom of the wizard.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4338.pastedimage1548070554954v17.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/886x620/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4338.pastedimage1548070554954v17.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2>How does TM Optimizer work?</h2><h3>Increases TM leverage</h3><p>When using TRADOS Workbench with the Microsoft Word macro for translation of RTF and DOC files, translation memories are often polluted with unnecessary formatting tags. If you convert such translation memory into SDL Trados Studio you experience that translations where you expect exact matches will be reported as fuzzy matches, effectively costing you money. TM Optimizer will analyse the content of each translation unit and will optimize the tag content for optimal results in SDL Trados Studio. In addition translation unit content is harmonized so you can achieve the best results and don't spent any extra money on translations you already paid once.</p><p>The two screenshots below illustrate what this means in practice. In the first picture, the TM only returns a 99% match where you would have expected a 100% match, purely due to excessive, unnecessary tags.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8686.pastedimage1548070583105v18.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/980x199/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8686.pastedimage1548070583105v18.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>&nbsp;Using the optimized TM, you see that the TM now returns a 100% match. Note that TM Optimizer only selectively removes the unnecessary tags.&nbsp;</p><h3><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4212.pastedimage1548070602192v19.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/966x175/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4212.pastedimage1548070602192v19.png\" alt=\" \"></a></h3><h3>Removes excessive formatting, e.g. font change, kerning, tracking, spacing</h3><p>Due to limitations of the Microsoft Word macro, unnecessary formatting information is stored in the translation memory. TM Optimizer will remove such formatting giving you less work and making translation easier.</p><h3>Import of the TMX is much faster</h3><p>Due the fact that during optimization a vast amount of tags is removed, SDL Trados Studio will perform all operations which are using translation memory much faster. In cetain cases the difference is more than 500%.</p><h3>Makes migration from DOC in TagEditor to DOCX in Studio much easier</h3><p>More and more clients are migrating from the legacy DOC format towards the latest version of Microsoft Word which is using the new DOCX file format. The features mentioned above will allow you to switch from translating DOC files in Microsoft Word to translating DOCX files in SDL Trados Studio without the major issues with leverage you would otherwise see.</p><p>The pictures below show what happens when you move to the DOCX format in Studio, based on a TM that was originally created based on a DOC file using the TRADOS 2007 Word integration. You can see that after applying TM Optimizer, the leverage is almost identical even though we have change file format and TM format.</p><p>Workbench Analysis Results</p><p>Analysis results of a DOC test file against the original TRADOS Workbench TM:</p><p><br></p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4555.pastedimage1548070646395v20.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1983x988/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4555.pastedimage1548070646395v20.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/70/versions/741","changelog":{"Trados Studio 2024":"<p><strong>5.0.0.0</strong></p><ul><li>updated to support Studio 2024</li><li>updated TellMe features</li></ul><hr/>","Trados Studio 2022 (SR1)":"<p><strong>4.1.2.0</strong></p><h4>Breaking changes</h4><ul><li>Sdl.Core.Globalization (Modified)</li><li>Sdl.Core.Globalization.Async (Added)</li><li>Sdl.Desktop.Platform.Controls.Behaviors.MouseDownBehavior (Deleted)</li></ul><p><br></p><hr/>","Trados Studio 2022":"<p><strong>4.0.2.0</strong></p><ul><li>Updated plugin manifest to ensure that the plugin will not attempt to install into Trados Studio 2022 SR1. Translation Quality Assessment profile for the Professional version of Trados Studio</i><br /><br>\r\nThe TQA profile available here is based on the FAR model published by Professor Jan Pedersen of Stockholm University.<Br>  This is an attempt at creating a generalized model for assessing quality in interlingual subtitling and we’ve used it to create an optional TQA profile for Trados Studio.<br> <br> It's based on these three things:<br /><br>\r\n- Functional equivalence <i>(do the subtitles convey speaker meaning?)</i><br />\r\n- Acceptability <i>(do the subtitles sound correct and natural in the target language?)</i><br />\r\n- Readability <i>(can the subtitles be read in a fluent and non-intrusive way?)</i><br />\r\n<br />\r\nFor more information on this model please refer to this paper published by Prof. Jan Pederson: <a target=\"_blank\" href=\"https://www.jostrans.org/issue28/art_pedersen.php\">The FAR model: assessing quality in interlingual subtitling</a>.  You can also read a little bit more about this in the <a target=\"_blank\" href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/6271/far-methodology-tqa-settings\">RWS Community subtitling wiki</a>.\r\n<br /><br />\r\nIMPORTANT NOTE: TQA is a feature available in Studio for the Professional version only.  Other versions of Studio can only use this model when working with a project package that has been prepared with the TQA profile It is an Exe file that can be downloaded from:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://drive.google.com/file/d/1TnvOkr99n0urHq5UlXjr_uuIYPUsgUlV/view?usp=sharing\">https://drive.google.com/file/d/1TnvOkr99n0urHq5UlXjr_uuIYPUsgUlV/view?usp=sharing</a></p>\r\n<p>More Information and how to request your license: </p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://cool-croissant-9ca5e5.netlify.app/\">https://cool-croissant-9ca5e5.netlify.app/</a></p>\r\n<p><strong>Video Tutorials and Introduction to how to use the Plugin</strong></p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLAVw35AnnvsI_hBLuwPz0HxzoRffA_pFz\">https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLAVw35AnnvsI_hBLuwPz0HxzoRffA_pFz</a></p>\r\n<p><strong>Full Description</strong>:</p>\r\n<p><strong>Unify Your Resources. Master Your AI.</strong></p>\r\n<p>Sam’s TWAS (Translation Workflow Automation Suite) is not just another AI plugin. It&#39;s a complete, centralized powerhouse designed to solve your biggest workflow challenges. It seamlessly integrates your private TMs, TBs, and custom glossaries with the power of generative AI, ensuring the AI adopts your terminology, tone, and style.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Why TWAS is More Powerful Than Standard AI Plugins</strong>:</p>\r\n<p><strong>Deep RAG Integration</strong>: TWAS uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to feed the AI with relevant examples from your own resources. This forces the AI to produce translations that are consistent with your past work, dramatically improving quality and accuracy.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Multi-Provider AI</strong>: Don&#39;t get locked into one system. TWAS supports both Google Gemini and OpenAI (ChatGPT). You can switch providers and models (like GPT-4o-mini or Gemini 1.5 Flash) at any time.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Dual-Mode Workflow (API vs. Manual)</strong>:</p>\r\n<p><strong>API Mode</strong>: Get lightning-fast translations and enhancements directly in the editor. Ideal for processing segments quickly.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Manual Mode</strong>: Get unparalleled control. TWAS prepares the optimal prompt, but you have the conversation with the AI. This is essential for refining creative, marketing, or legal texts and even works with free AI tiers.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Unified Lookup (Alt+J)</strong>: Stop searching multiple windows. Highlight any text in the source segment and press <kbd>Alt</kbd>+<kbd>J</kbd> to instantly query all your active resources–Lexical and Semantic–in one unified, relevance-sorted view.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Core Features</strong>:</p>\r\n<p><strong>Lexical Index</strong>: A high-speed concordance engine built from your TMs, TBs, and glossaries for precise, keyword-based search.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Semantic Index</strong>: A powerful, meaning-based search engine that finds paraphrases, synonyms, and contextually relevant examples that lexical search would miss.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Term Extractor</strong>: Mine candidate terminology from your source documents, automatically enriched with context examples from your TMs.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Term Matcher</strong>: Find approved translations for term lists, using both fast lexical matching and context-aware AI.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Powerful AI Ribbon</strong>: A new &quot;TWAS AI&quot; group in the Trados Review tab gives you 11 powerful buttons for API and Manual AI tasks, with or without RAG context.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Regex Manager</strong>: A new full-fledged Regex Manager Tab, with over 200 preset Regex ready for implementation. No need to remember Regex or recreate them again. </p>\r\n<p>By eliminating information silos and making your private data the foundation for all AI interaction, TWAS Assistant transforms Trados Studio from a simple CAT tool into a true, personalized Translation Workflow Automation Suite.</p>\r\n<p><strong>Final Note</strong>: \r\nRun your CMD command prompt\r\nType the below TWAS – Translation Workflow Automation Suite</strong> transforms <strong>Trados Studio 2024 SR1</strong> into a unified and intelligent translation hub that integrates your <strong>Translation Memories (TMs)</strong>, <strong>Termbases (TBs)</strong>, and <strong>custom glossaries</strong> with the latest <strong>AI technologies</strong> from <strong>Google Gemini</strong> and <strong>OpenAI (ChatGPT)</strong>.</p>\r\n<p>It centralises lexical and semantic resources, eliminates information silos, and enables instant lookups and AI-enhanced translation workflows.\r\n Through <strong>Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)</strong>, the system learns from your own bilingual content, ensuring consistent terminology, tone, and style across all translations.</p>\r\n<p>TWAS can operate in two distinct modes:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>API Mode</strong> for speed and automation.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Manual Mode</strong> for complete conversational control, even with free AI tiers.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Together, these capabilities provide both translators and reviewers with an intelligent, adaptive assistant that extends the functionality of Trados Studio far beyond standard AI plugins.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h1>Key Features</h1>\r\n<h3>Unified Translation Hub</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Combines your TMs, TBs, and glossaries into a single, searchable workspace.</li>\r\n<li>Access all lexical and semantic data instantly using <strong>Alt + J</strong>.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Dual AI Modes</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>API Method:</strong> Automatically processes translations or reviews using configured API keys for Gemini or OpenAI.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Manual Method:</strong> Generates AI-ready prompts that you can refine interactively for maximum control.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Deep RAG Integration</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Injects relevant bilingual examples from your resources directly into AI prompts.</li>\r\n<li>Forces the AI to follow your terminology, tone, and writing style.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Seven Dedicated Interface Tabs</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Resources Tab</strong> – Build and manage lexical or semantic indexes from bilingual files.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Index Manager Tab</strong> – Activate or deactivate indexes for your current project.</li>\r\n<li><strong>AI Settings Tab</strong> – Configure API keys, models, and temperature values.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Lookup Tab</strong> – Search and analyse results from all active resources.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Term Extractor Tab</strong> – Identify new terms and enrich them with TM examples.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Term Matcher Tab</strong> – Find approved translations for lists of terms using glossaries and AI.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Regex Manager Tab</strong> - A new Full-Fledged Regex Manager Tab with over 200 preset Regex that are ready for action. No need to remember your Regex anymore or recreate them from scratch.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h3>TWAS AI Ribbon Group (Review Tab)</h3>\r\n<p>Adds 11 productivity buttons to the Trados <strong>Review</strong> ribbon for segment-level AI translation and enhancement, including:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Manual_TR / Manual_Enh</strong> – Manual prompt preparation for translation or review.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Manual_TR+RAG / Manual_Enh+RAG</strong> – Manual prompts enhanced with RAG context.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Enh_API / AI+RAG_TR_API / Enh+AI+RAG_API</strong> – Automated API workflows for speed and consistency.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Table of Contents Summary</h3>\r\n<p>TWAS provides an integrated help structure covering:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Purpose and benefits</li>\r\n<li>Core concepts (Lexical Index, Semantic Index, RAG, API vs Manual)</li>\r\n<li>Complete workflow steps (Indexing, Lookup, Term Extraction, AI Review)</li>\r\n<li>Setup and configuration guides</li>\r\n<li>Developer background and acknowledgements</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Example Workflow</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Build your indexes</strong> from TMs, TBs, or TSV files.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Activate</strong> them in the Index Manager.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Use Lookup (Alt + J)</strong> to query all resources at once.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Extract or match terminology</strong> automatically.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Leverage AI</strong> for translation or review directly in Trados Studio.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h3>User Tips</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Use <strong>low temperature (≈0.2)</strong> for accurate, consistent translations.</li>\r\n<li>Use <strong>high temperature (≈0.7)</strong> for creative or stylistic output.</li>\r\n<li>Semantic indexes require a valid <strong>Gemini</strong> or <strong>OpenAI</strong> API key.</li>\r\n<li>Manual mode is ideal for free-tier AI accounts and creative workflows.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h1>Technical Requirements</h1>\r\n<h3>Software Compatibility</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Trados Studio:</strong> Version 2024 SR1 or later</li>\r\n<li><strong>Operating System:</strong> Windows 10 / Windows recommended)</li>\r\n<li>Stable Internet connection for API mode</li>\r\n<li>1 GB available disk space for index files</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>File Format Support</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Translation Memories:</strong> <code>.sdltm</code>, <code>.tmx</code></li>\r\n<li><strong>Termbases:</strong> <code>.sdltb</code></li>\r\n<li><strong>Glossaries / Parallel Data:</strong> <code>.xlsx</code>, <code>.tsv</code></li>\r\n<li><strong>Semantic Index Input:</strong> 2-column bilingual TSV</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Permissions and Access</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Read/write permissions for creating and storing index folders.</li>\r\n<li>API access for OpenAI or Google Gemini (API key required for automated workflows).</li>\r\n<li>No elevated (administrator) rights needed once installed.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Optional Dependencies</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Free-tier or paid API subscription with either provider.</li>\r\n<li>Secure storage for API keys within Trados Studio user 2026]</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDE80 What&#39;s New: The &quot;Lightning Filter&quot; Update\r\nInstant Tag-Only Toggle: We have completely overhauled the &quot;Tag-Only Segments&quot; feature. Clicking the button on the TWAS Assistant ribbon no longer shows an instructional pop-up. Instead, it instantly filters your active Trados Editor to exclusively display segments containing tags. Click it again to instantly restore your standard view.</p>\r\n<p>Universal UI Access: You can now trigger the Tag-Only toggle directly from the Editor&#39;s Right-Click Context Menu, keeping your mouse right where the action is.</p>\r\n<p>Master Shortcut Support: The toggle filter fully supports native Trados keyboard shortcut mapping.</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDCA1 Pro-Tip: Go to File &gt; Options &gt; Keyboard Shortcuts, search for &quot;Toggle Tag-Only Segments&quot;, and assign Alt+Shift+F for a lightning-fast, mouse-free workflow!</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDEE0️ Stability &amp; Ecosystem Sync\r\nCrash-Proof Engine: Engineered a custom bypass for a known Trados Studio API bug (NullReferenceException) that can occur when clearing custom filters. The TWAS toggle now uses a fail-safe &quot;Dummy Clear&quot; state, ensuring your Editor remains 100% stable during rapid view switching.</p>\r\n<p>Ecosystem Parity: This highly requested UX upgrade is now perfectly synchronized across the entire TWAS ecosystem (Smart QA Verifier, Tagworks, and Studio Assistant). Learn it once, use it everywhere!</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>1.0.0.15</strong></p><p>\uD83D\uDE80 What&#39;s New in TWAS Assistant (Latest Release)\r\nWe are thrilled to bring you our most highly requested Quality-of-Life update yet! With a single click, you can now instantly strip every single formatting tag from your selected target segments while perfectly preserving your translated text.</p>\r\n<p>Why this matters: Machine Translation and standard &quot;dumb&quot; tag copiers often leave behind overlapping, broken, or hallucinated &quot;spaghetti tags.&quot; Running AI on already-broken tags can cause confusion. This new tool allows you to wipe the slate clean, giving the Tagworks AI a perfect, tag-free canvas to surgically inject the correct formatting.</p>\r\n<p>Built-in Safeguards: Includes a confirmation prompt before clearing tags to ensure you never accidentally delete your formatting by misclicking.</p>\r\n<p>⚙️ Minor Enhancements</p>\r\n<p>UI adjustments to accommodate the new utility button seamlessly within the Trados Studio ribbon.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<p>Architecture &amp; Security Hardening</p>\r\n<p>Enterprise Code Protection: Implemented commercial-grade code obfuscation and intellectual property protection for the production build.</p>\r\n<p>Bulletproof Network Layer: Completely refactored the internal data serialization engine. All API payloads between the Trados plugin, the local Python backend, and external LLM providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Vertex AI, OpenRouter) have been migrated to strictly typed dictionaries, ensuring 100% data integrity and preventing data scrambling during compilation.</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDEE0️ Core Stability &amp; Bug Fixes</p>\r\n<p>Lexical &amp; Semantic Indexing: Fixed an issue where the C# client could send malformed or empty schema payloads to the backend, preventing the &quot;400 Bad Request&quot; errors when generating or loading indexes.</p>\r\n<p>Automated AI Processing Loops: Resolved an issue in the Terminology Extractor and Matcher where automated API chunking would fail to read the response_text in release builds. The AI will now flawlessly loop through large terminology batches unattended.</p>\r\n<p>Live Concordance Lookup: Hardened the search payload delivery system. Background lookups and Concordance searches are now guaranteed to securely hit the backend and retrieve Exact, Fuzzy, and Semantic matches without schema corruption.</p>\r\n<p>API Tag Insertion: Ensured strict JSON contract compliance for all AI Tag Insertion calls, preventing &quot;Invalid JSON format&quot; rejections from external AI providers.</p>\r\n<p>⚡ Performance</p>\r\n<p>Eliminated anonymous object overhead in HTTP client requests, resulting in slightly faster, more predictable network serialization.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style><hr/><p><strong>1.0.0.10</strong></p><p>Version 1.0.0.10\r\n\uD83C\uDF89 Major New Feature: TWAS Tagworks\r\nI am thrilled to introduce Tagworks, a revolutionary new tool built directly into the TWAS Assistant suite to completely eliminate tag insertion and formatting headaches!</p>\r\n<p>Automatic API Tagging: Select segments with missing or broken tags, click a single button, and let your configured AI (OpenAI, Gemini, or OpenRouter) perfectly map and inject the missing tags into your target text.</p>\r\n<p>Manual AI Tagging: Prefer using free web interfaces like ChatGPT or Claude? {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>TWAS Concordance Bridge is a Trados Studio plugin that enables instant communication between the Trados Editor and the TWAS CAT Resource Suite for advanced concordance search.</p>\r\n<p>Instead of relying solely on traditional TM lookup, the plugin allows you to send selected text directly to the TWAS application using a customizable keyboard shortcut or ribbon command.</p>\r\n<p>This creates a seamless workflow where concordance search becomes immediate, powerful, and fully integrated into your translation environment.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>⚡ Instant Concordance Trigger</p>\r\n<p>Send selected segments from Trados Studio to TWAS with a single shortcut (e.g. Alt + Shift + C).</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDD17 Seamless Integration</p>\r\n<p>Works directly inside Trados Studio while leveraging the power of the external TWAS application.</p>\r\n<p>\uD83E\uDDE0 Advanced Indexed Search</p>\r\n<p>Utilizes TWAS indexed resources, enabling fast and scalable concordance search across massive datasets.</p>\r\n<p>\uD83C\uDFAF Context-Aware Workflow</p>\r\n<p>Search results are based on real indexed data, providing more relevant matches than traditional TM concordance.</p>\r\n<p>⚙️ Customizable Shortcuts</p>\r\n<p>Users can assign their own preferred keyboard shortcuts via:</p>\r\n<p>File → Options → Keyboard Shortcuts\r\n\uD83D\uDE80 Lightweight &amp; Fast</p>\r\n<p>No heavy processing inside Trados Studio. The plugin acts as a bridge while TWAS handles the processing.</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDD04 One-Step Workflow</p>\r\n<p>Select → Trigger → View results\r\nNo manual switching or repeated actions.</p>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<p>Supported Platform\r\nTrados Studio 2022 / 2024 (recommended)\r\nOperating System\r\nWindows 10 or Windows 11\r\nTWAS Application\r\nTWAS CAT Resource Suite (Standalone application) must be installed and running\r\nLicense Requirement\r\nA valid TWAS license is required for the standalone application\r\nShortcut Configuration\r\nDefault shortcut example: Alt + Shift + C\r\nUsers can customize it inside Trados Studio settings\r\nInternet Requirement\r\nNot required for concordance functionality (offline operation
