misplaced tags have been the most frustrating bottleneck for translators leveraging Machine Translation and AI, turning fast translations into a tedious chore of manual formatting and QA errors. TWAS Tagworks is a revolutionary, first-of-its-kind Trados Studio plugin designed to eliminate this problem entirely. Operating securely within your local environment, Tagworks harnesses the intelligence of top-tier AI models–such as Gemini, ChatGPT, and OpenRouter–to automatically map and seamlessly inject missing source tags into your target text with surgical precision. By bridging the gap between raw text and complex formatting, Tagworks transforms an error-prone task into a single, effortless click, allowing you to finally experience the true speed of AI translation without ever wrestling with broken tags again. Even if you do not have an API Key, you can still use the plugin to work manually, but in batches, so that you would not be restricted to purchasing an API key! </p>\r\n<p>The Proof:</p>\r\n<p>Other tools only find the problem; they don&#39;t fix it: Every major CAT tool has a QA checker that will flag missing tags and throw a red error icon at the translator. But their &quot;solution&quot; is forcing the user to manually drag and drop the tags into the right place. Tagworks actually solves the error.</p>\r\n<p>There is a popular plugin on the AppStore called &quot;Trados Copy Tags.&quot; However, it does not use AI. It simply provides a keyboard shortcut to blindly copy tags from the source to the target, leaving the translator to figure out the exact placement. Tagworks understands the context and places the tags intelligently around the correct translated words.</p>\r\n<p>General AI plugins leave a gap: Massive AI translation plugins (like DeepL or the standard OpenAI plugin) attempt to handle tags during the initial translation. But when they inevitably fail, hallucinate, or drop a tag, the translator is left stranded. There has never been a dedicated, surgical &quot;healing&quot; tool to fix tags after the text is generated.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: 1.5px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  blockquote {\r\n    border-left: 3px solid var(--lightgray);\r\n    padding-left: 10px;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    border-collapse: collapse;\r\n    width: 100%;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th,\r\n  table td {\r\n    border: 1px solid var(--table-border);\r\n    padding: 8px;\r\n    text-align: left;\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table th {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h1>Introduction</h1>\r\n<p>TWAS Tagworks – Quick Start Guide\r\nWelcome to TWAS Tagworks! This guide will get you set up and inserting tags automatically in less than 2 minutes.</p>\r\n<p>Step 1: Licensing\r\nYour plugin is secured and locked to your specific machine. Follow these steps to activate it:</p>\r\n<p>Open the Command Prompt (CMD) on your Windows computer.\r\nType the following command exactly and press Enter:\r\nwmic csproduct get UUID\r\nCopy the long string of letters and numbers that appears.\r\nEmail this UUID to <a href=\"mailto:arabiana2008@gmail.com\">arabiana2008@gmail.com</a> to receive your license.\r\nOnce you receive your license file, create a new folder on your computer at exactly C:\\TWAS.\r\nPlace your newly received license_tagworks.dat file directly inside this folder.\r\nStep 2: AI Configuration\r\nTagworks uses a &quot;Bring Your Own Key&quot; (BYOK) model. Connect it to your preferred AI provider:</p>\r\n<p>Open Trados Studio and navigate to the Home tab in the Editor view.\r\nLocate the TWAS Tagworks group on the ribbon and click Tagworks Settings.\r\nChoose your preferred AI provider (ChatGPT, Gemini, or OpenRouter) and enter your API key.\r\nSelect a model from the dropdown.\r\nPro Tip: We highly recommend selecting models marked as [⭐ Recommended - Fast &amp; Cheap] (like gpt-4o-mini or gemini-2.5-flash). They are incredibly fast, cost fractions of a cent, and are highly accurate for mechanical tag insertion.\r\nCheck the Set as default provider box and click Save.\r\nStep 3: How to Use Tagworks\r\nFixing broken or missing tags is now just a single click away.</p>\r\n<p>In the Trados Editor, select (highlight) one or more segments that are missing tags or have formatting errors.\r\nGo to the Home tab and locate the TWAS Tagworks group.\r\nFor Automatic Tagging: Click Automatic API Tagging. The plugin will securely contact your default AI provider, calculate the exact tag placements, and instantly insert them into your target text.\r\nFor Manual Tagging: Click Manual AI Tagging. This generates a strictly formatted prompt. Copy it, paste it into your favorite free AI web chat (like ChatGPT or Claude), copy the AI&#39;s response, and paste it back into the Tagworks dialog to apply the tags.\r\nStep 4: Customizing Your Ribbon (Optional)\r\nBy default, Tagworks places its buttons on the Home tab. For maximum convenience, you can create your own dedicated Ribbon tab or group just for your AI tools!</p>\r\n<p>In Trados Studio, go to the View tab and click Ribbon Customization (or simply right-click anywhere on the empty gray space of the ribbon and select Customize Ribbon...).\r\nIn the window that appears, look at the right-hand column. Click New Tab or select an existing tab and click New Group.\r\nRename your new tab/group to something like &quot;My AI Tools&quot; or &quot;Tagworks&quot;.\r\nIn the left-hand column, change the &quot;Choose commands from&quot; dropdown to All Commands.\r\nScroll down to find the Automatic API Tagging, Manual AI Tagging, and Tagworks Settings actions.\r\nSelect them and click the Add &gt;&gt; button in the middle to move them to your custom group.\r\nClick OK to save. Your Tagworks buttons are now perfectly positioned exactly where you want them!\r\nSupport &amp; Feedback\r\nIf you need any assistance, encounter a bug, or have a feature request, I am committed to providing fast and helpful support.</p>\r\n<h1>Key Features</h1>\r\n<p>One-Click Automatic Tagging: Instantly fix broken or missing XML tags directly within the Trados Studio Editor. Select your segments, click the API button, and Tagworks will securely route the text to your chosen AI to perfectly inject the missing tags.</p>\r\n<p>Context-Aware Precision: Unlike &quot;dumb&quot; tag copiers that blindly dump tags at the end of a segment, Tagworks uses AI to understand the meaning of your translation. It intelligently maps and places tags exactly where they belong around the correct localized words.</p>\r\n<p>Free &quot;Manual Mode&quot; Workflow: Don&#39;t want to use paid API keys? Tagworks can instantly generate a strictly formatted prompt containing your source tags and target text. Paste it into any free AI web interface (like ChatGPT or Claude), grab the result, and paste it back into Tagworks to instantly apply the formatting.</p>\r\n<p>Smart Model Optimization: Stop wasting expensive tokens on simple formatting tasks. Tagworks categorizes AI models in its settings, steering you toward lightning-fast, ultra-cheap models (like gemini-2.5-flash or gpt-4o-mini) that excel at mechanical tag insertion for fractions of a cent.</p>\r\n<p>100% Standalone &amp; Lightweight: Tagworks operates entirely inside Trados Studio with no heavy external Python servers or background applications required. Simply install the .sdlplugin file, enter your API key, and start tagging immediately.</p>\r\n<h1>Technical Requirements</h1>\r\n<p>Supported Platform: Trados Studio 2024 (SR1 and later).</p>\r\n<p>Operating System: Windows 10 or Windows 11 (must match Trados Studio requirements).</p>\r\n<p>Internet Connection: An active, stable internet connection is required to communicate with the AI API providers.</p>\r\n<p>AI Provider Account: A valid API key from at least one supported provider (OpenAI/ChatGPT, Google Gemini, or OpenRouter). Traditional QA checks often generate thousands of cryptic error messages that are difficult to interpret. This plugin intercepts those reports and translates them into a human-readable, actionable dashboard.</p>\r\n<p>By bridging the gap between standard verification and advanced AI, the verifier helps linguists achieve 100% accuracy in tags, terminology, and formatting while significantly reducing the time spent on manual corrections.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>The Smart QA Dashboard\r\nThe dashboard serves as your mission control for quality. Auto‑refresh on segment change.</p>\r\n<p>Offline Arabic Grammar Assistant – A complete, searchable offline database of Arabic grammar, morphology, and writing rules. Built‑in rules remain untouched; your changes are saved as overrides. Restore deleted rules at any time.</p>\r\n<p>AI Assistant – Two modes:</p>\r\n<p>Live mode: Ask Gemini, ChatGPT, OpenRouter, Azure OpenAI, or Vertex AI about any Arabic word or phrase. Returns a structured answer in Arabic.</p>\r\n<p>Manual mode: Generate the same AI prompt and copy it to clipboard – paste into any external AI agent, zero API credits consumed.</p>\r\n<p>Smart QA &amp; Backend – The included standalone backend engine (twas_api_server.exe) uses advanced NLP (Camel Tools) for deep morphological analysis. It helps you write better Arabic by showing you grammar and style rules in real time, letting you browse an offline grammar knowledge base, and giving you AI‑powered research – all without leaving your CAT tool.</p>\r\n<h2>Quick Start Guide</h2>\r\n<p>Step 1: Install the Plugin\r\nDouble‑click the .sdlplugin file. Trados Studio will install it automatically.</p>\r\n<p>Restart Trados Studio if needed.</p>\r\n<p>Step 2: Obtain and Install Your License\r\nOpen Trados Studio. The plugin will show your machine’s UUID.</p>\r\n<p>Copy that UUID and email it to <a href=\"mailto:arabiana2008@gmail.com\">arabiana2008@gmail.com</a> to receive your license file (license_zz_checker.dat).</p>\r\n<p>Create the folder C:\\TWAS on your system drive.</p>\r\n<p>Place the license file directly into C:\\TWAS.</p>\r\n<p>Step 3: Download and Set Up the Backend Engine (Mandatory)\r\nThe plugin requires a standalone backend engine to perform live rule lookups and NLP‑based analysis. You do not need to install Python – the engine is a self‑contained executable.</p>\r\n<p>Download the backend engine folder from:\r\n<a href=\"https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Fq2joCxtK5iZVLzrp3wAjA9G2y-qX4oB/view?usp=sharing\">https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Fq2joCxtK5iZVLzrp3wAjA9G2y-qX4oB/view?usp=sharing</a>\r\nYou will receive a folder named twas_api_server.dist. Do not rename it or change its internal structure. You may place this folder anywhere (Desktop, D: drive, etc.).</p>\r\n<p>Download the NLP models from:\r\n<a href=\"https://drive.google.com/file/d/1JI54x3ERrsYZZov-42JMQEUuEtOmohU9/view?usp=sharing\">https://drive.google.com/file/d/1JI54x3ERrsYZZov-42JMQEUuEtOmohU9/view?usp=sharing</a>\r\nExtract the contents directly to:\r\nC:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\NLP_Models</p>\r\n<p>Start the backend – inside the twas_api_server.dist folder, double‑click TWAS_Start_Backend.bat.\r\nThe backend runs silently in the background (no window appears – this is normal).\r\nTo verify it is running, open <a href=\"http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs\">http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs</a> in your browser. If you see the API documentation, it is working.</p>\r\n<p>If you need to stop the backend, double‑click TWAS_Stop_Backend.bat.\r\nIf the backend becomes unresponsive, use Kill_TWAS_ZZ_Backend_Port_8000.bat to force‑free port 8000.</p>\r\n<p>Step 4: Open Trados Studio and Start Using the Plugin\r\nOnce the backend is running, open Trados Studio. The plugin will connect automatically. You can now use all features.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>Say / Do Not Say Panel\r\nOpen it from the Add‑Ins ribbon → TWAS ZZ Arabic Engine → Say / Do Not Say.</p>\r\n<p>It shows the current target segment text at the top.</p>\r\n<p>The grid displays matching rules: trigger word, rule explanation, suggested correction, and status (error / style / info / obsolete).</p>\r\n<p>Auto‑lookup refreshes automatically when you move between segments. You can also click Refresh manually.</p>\r\n<p>Show obsolete rules checkbox toggles visibility of deprecated rules.</p>\r\n<p>Double‑click a row to copy the suggested correction to clipboard.</p>\r\n<p>Right‑click a row to Edit this rule (opens the full Rule Editor) or Add to custom rules (creates a user‑overridden copy).</p>\r\n<p>Arabic Grammar Assistant\r\nOpen it from the ribbon → Arabic Grammar Assistant.</p>\r\n<p>This is a completely offline grammar reference with thousands of curated Arabic rules.</p>\r\n<p>Search – normal, advanced (word‑based), or regex. Filter by topic, match mode (all words / any word / exact phrase), and scope (all fields / titles only / examples only).</p>\r\n<p>Browse by topic using the table of contents tree on the left.</p>\r\n<p>Results list shows matching rules; click any to view full details (definition, quick answer, sections, examples).</p>\r\n<p>Search hits inside the rule details are highlighted; use Previous / Next buttons to jump between matches.</p>\r\n<p>Resources &amp; Credit buttons show the list of references and the dedication to the developer’s parents.</p>\r\n<p>Rule Editor (AI Settings window)\r\nOpen it from the ribbon → AI Settings.</p>\r\n<p>The Rule Editor tab lets you manage all 2,260+ built‑in rules plus your own custom rules.</p>\r\n<p>Add Rule – create a new custom rule.</p>\r\n<p>Delete Selected – hides a built‑in rule (the original CSV is never modified) or removes a custom rule.</p>\r\n<p>Import CSV – add multiple rules from a CSV file.</p>\r\n<p>Export CSV – save the current rule list (including your modifications) to a CSV file.</p>\r\n<p>Save Changes – saves all edits, custom rules, and deleted‑rule settings, then reloads the backend.</p>\r\n<p>Restore All Deleted – brings back all previously hidden built‑in rules.</p>\r\n<p>Manage Deleted Rules – shows a list of hidden built‑in rules and allows restoring them one by one.</p>\r\n<p>Edits to built‑in rows are saved as overrides in user_custom_rules.csv. The original CSV files (Fareh_Clean_Rules.csv, twas_custom_rules.csv) remain untouched.</p>\r\n<p>AI Assistant\r\nTwo ways to get AI‑powered Arabic grammar research:</p>\r\n<p>Live AI Assistant (requires API key)\r\nOpen Ask AI Assistant from the ribbon.</p>\r\n<p>Enter a word or phrase (or let the plugin auto‑fill the current segment).</p>\r\n<p>Click Ask AI. The plugin sends a structured Arabic prompt to your chosen AI provider and displays the answer.</p>\r\n<p>Copy the answer with the Copy button.</p>\r\n<p>Supported providers: Gemini, ChatGPT, OpenRouter, Azure OpenAI, Vertex AI. Configure your API keys and default provider in AI Settings.</p>\r\n<p>Manual AI Assistant (no API key – free)\r\nOpen Ask AI Assistant (manual) from the ribbon.</p>\r\n<p>Enter your word or phrase.</p>\r\n<p>Click Generate Prompt &amp; Copy to Clipboard.</p>\r\n<p>The plugin builds the same Arabic prompt and copies it. Paste it into any external AI agent (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, etc.) to get the answer without using your API credits.</p>\r\n<p>Lookup Behaviour Settings\r\nAuto‑lookup debounce (ms) – controls how quickly the Say/Do Not Say panel refreshes after a segment change (default 300 ms).</p>\r\n<p>Show obsolete rules by default – sets the initial state of the “Show obsolete rules” checkbox.</p>\r\n<p>AI Provider Settings\r\nConfigure each provider with your own API keys or enterprise credentials. You can also set a default provider for the live AI assistant.</p>\r\n<p>Gemini – select model, store API key.</p>\r\n<p>ChatGPT / OpenAI – select model, store API key.</p>\r\n<p>OpenRouter – select model, store API key.</p>\r\n<p>Azure OpenAI (Enterprise) – endpoint URL, deployment name, API key.</p>\r\n<p>Google Vertex AI (Enterprise) – service account JSON path, project ID, location, model.</p>\r\n<p>Generation Settings – temperature (0 = strict, 1 = creative).</p>\r\n<h2>Help Guide</h2>\r\n<p>Click Help Guide in the ribbon to open this documentation file (ZZ-Smart-Checker.html).</p>\r\n<p>Folder Structure &amp; Important Paths\r\nPath\tPurpose\r\nC:\\TWAS\\license_zz_checker.dat\tLicense file (machine‑locked)\r\nC:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\\tUser dictionary folder (created automatically)\r\nC:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\user_custom_rules.csv\tYour custom and overridden rules\r\nC:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\deleted_rules.json\tList of hidden built‑in rules\r\nC:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\engine_mode.txt\tEngine mode (1 = fast regex, 2 = deep NLP)\r\nC:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\NLP_Models\\\tCamel Tools NLP models (required for deep mode)\r\nAntivirus False Positives\r\nBecause the backend engine (twas_api_server.exe) is a custom‑built, compiled Python application, some antivirus programs may flag it as “unrecognized”. This is a false positive. To resolve:</p>\r\n<p>Add an exclusion for C:\\TWAS and the folder containing twas_api_server.exe in your antivirus software.</p>\r\n<p>Then run the engine normally.</p>\r\n<h2>Troubleshooting</h2>\r\n<p>Say/Do Not Say panel shows no rules – Ensure the backend is running (check <a href=\"http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs\">http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs</a>). If not, start it with TWAS_Start_Backend.bat.</p>\r\n<p>“Backend server is not running” warning – Follow Step 3 above to start the engine.</p>\r\n<p>License error – Verify that license_zz_checker.dat exists in C:\\TWAS and that the file name is exactly correct.</p>\r\n<p>Grammar Assistant fails to load – Check that the SQLite database file is present in the plugin’s Resources\\Nahw\\… folder. Reinstall the plugin if missing.</p>\r\n<p>AI Assistant returns an API error – Confirm your API key is valid and the provider is correctly selected in Settings.</p>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<p>Platform: Trados Studio 2024 (version 18.0 – 18.9)</p>\r\n<p>OS: Windows 10 or Windows 11 (64‑bit)</p>\r\n<p>Backend: Standalone twas_api_server.exe (no Python installation required)</p>\r\n<p>License: Machine‑locked license file</p>\r\n<p>Disk space: ~2 GB for NLP models (optional, only for deep NLP mode)</p>\r\n<h2>Support &amp; Contact</h2>\r\n<p>Email: <a href=\"mailto:arabiana2008@gmail.com\">arabiana2008@gmail.com</a></p>\r\n<p>WhatsApp: +201001969120</p>\r\n<p>Enterprise licensing – contact for team and agency pricing.</p>\r\n<h2>Dedication</h2>\r\n<p>I dedicate this work to the loving memory of my beloved parents. It provides advanced NLP (Camel Tools) for deep morphological analysis. No Python installation required.</p>\r\n<p>Say / Do Not Say Panel (Real‑time Lookup) – Automatically checks every word in the current target segment against 2,260+ grammar and style rules. Displays trigger word, explanation, correction, and severity (error / style / info / obsolete). Auto‑refresh on segment change or manual refresh.</p>\r\n<p>Offline Arabic Grammar Assistant – A complete, searchable offline SQLite database of Arabic grammar, morphology, and writing rules. Browse by topic, use normal / advanced / regex search. View rule details, sections, and examples. No internet needed.</p>\r\n<p>Rule Editor – Fully editable rule set:</p>\r\n<p>Add, edit, delete rules.</p>\r\n<p>Import / export rules from/to CSV.</p>\r\n<p>Built‑in rules are never modified; your changes are saved as overrides.</p>\r\n<p>Restore all deleted rules or restore individually.</p>\r\n<p>Custom rules stored in user_custom_rules.csv.</p>\r\n<p>AI Assistant – Two Modes:</p>\r\n<p>Live Mode – Ask Gemini, ChatGPT, OpenRouter, Azure OpenAI, or Vertex AI about any Arabic word/phrase. Returns structured Arabic answer with correct usage, examples, old/modern differences, common mistakes, and references.</p>\r\n<p>Manual Mode – Generate the same AI prompt and copy to clipboard. Paste into any external AI agent – zero API credits consumed.</p>\r\n<p>Lookup Behaviour Settings – Configure auto‑lookup debounce time and whether obsolete rules are shown by default.</p>\r\n<p>Machine‑Locked Licensing – License file (license_zz_checker.dat) placed in C:\\TWAS. No online activation required.</p>\r\n<p>Help Guide – Built‑in HTML documentation accessible from the ribbon.</p>\r\n<p>⚙️ Improvements\r\nPerformance – Backend engine runs as a separate process, eliminating UI freezes during rule lookups.</p>\r\n<p>Customisation – You can now blacklist built‑in rules, add your own client‑specific rules, and export/import rule sets.</p>\r\n<p>AI Flexibility – Support for five AI providers, including enterprise options (Azure OpenAI, Vertex AI).</p>\r\n<p>Grammar Reference – Offline knowledge base replaces scattered online searches. Search with diacritic‑aware normalisation.</p>\r\n<p>User Interface – All windows (Say/Do Not Say, Grammar Assistant, Settings) have been redesigned for better readability (fixed contrast issues, permanent status legend, black text on selection).</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDD27 Breaking Changes (compared to older versions)\r\nBackend required – The plugin will not work without the backend engine running. You must download and start twas_api_server.exe before opening Trados.</p>\r\n<p>Folder structure – New required folders: C:\\TWAS\\ for the license file, and C:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\NLP_Models for the Camel Tools models.</p>\r\n<p>Rule storage – Custom rules are now stored in C:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\user_custom_rules.csv. Deleted built‑in rules are tracked in deleted_rules.json. Old rule files are no longer directly edited.</p>\r\n<p>Settings file – Moved to %AppData%\\TWAS_ZZ_Advanced_Arabic_Checker\\settings.json. Old settings are not migrated.</p>\r\n<p>AI configuration – API keys and model selections must be re‑entered in the new Settings window.</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDC1B Fixed Issues\r\nText invisibility in Rule Editor grid (selected rows now show black text on light blue highlight).</p>\r\n<p>Text invisibility in Say/Do Not Say grid (all text now forced to black).</p>\r\n<p>Missing legend explaining status colours – added permanent legend with tooltips.</p>\r\n<p>Poor contrast in various UI elements – corrected.</p>\r\n<p>\uD83D\uDCE6 New Dependencies (automatically included)\r\nStandalone backend executable (twas_api_server.exe) – provided separately.</p>\r\n<p>Camel Tools NLP models – provided separately (extract to C:\\TWAS\\UserDictionary\\NLP_Models).</p>\r\n<p>SQLite database for grammar knowledge base – included in plugin package.</p>\r\n<p>Note for existing users: This is a major upgrade. Problem</u></strong></p><p>The traditional method for wordcount is based on the source. Every now and then you get a requirement for payment based on the target word count, or even by lines as opposed to by word.</p><p><br></p><p><strong><u>The Solution</u></strong></p><p>This plugin adds a new batch task to Studio which creates a report providing the following based on your settings:</p><p>- target word count</p><p>- target character count</p><p>- analysis by lines (popular in Switzerland, Germany and many other countries)</p><p>In addition, you can apply rates to this analysis and generate the costs for delivery to your client.</p><p><br></p><p>To learn how to use this represents a batch task which counts the number of words in target and generates report based on the results.&nbsp;The application also has an option to count the number of words from the source.</p><p>How does the application works?</p><ul><li>Right click on the selected .sdlxliff file(s) which should be used for the word count</li><li>Select&nbsp;<strong>Batch Tasks</strong>&nbsp;-&gt;&nbsp;<strong>Target Word Count</strong>&nbsp;(or select the&nbsp;<strong>Target Word</strong>&nbsp;<strong>Count</strong>&nbsp;from&nbsp;<strong>Home</strong>&nbsp;tab -&gt;&nbsp;<strong>Batch Tasks</strong>&nbsp;button)</li></ul><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6014.pastedimage1549609575302v2.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1116x687/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6014.pastedimage1549609575302v2.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><ul><li>Select options from the&nbsp;<strong>Settings</strong>&nbsp;screen if needed</li><li>Press&nbsp;<strong>Finish</strong></li><li>If no error occurs, the generated report can be saved locally or viewed in the&nbsp;<strong>Reports</strong>&nbsp;View from Studio.</li></ul><p>Settings&nbsp;screen</p><p>User has ability to configured the word count based on different options:</p><ul><li>Source</li><li>Target</li><li>Calculate per line</li><li>Report locked segments separately</li><li>Country selection</li><li>Specification of characters per line</li><li>Include spaces</li><li>Rates</li></ul><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6170.pastedimage1549611519017v1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1053x800/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6170.pastedimage1549611519017v1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>Finish screen</p><p>After the Target word count batch task is processed, the generated report can be displayed in the Studio Reports view, or it can be saved locally.</p><p><a Multiterm 2009","minimumStudioVersion":"10.0.0"}]}],"categories":[{"id":2,"name":"Translation Memory"},{"id":3,"name":"Terminology"},{"id":1,"name":"File filters & converters"}]},{"id":59,"downloadCount":10832,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2016-02-25T16:06:25.3Z","updatedAt":"2025-01-12T19:30:56.956517Z","name":"TermExcelerator","description":"<p>Ever wished you could just use an Excel spreadsheet as a glossary in Studio and not have to bother with MultiTerm?</p><p><strong>The TermExcelerator</strong> is just that. It's committing the cardinal sin of terminology!</p><p>The basic idea is you select your Excel spreadsheet of terms and add it to Studio. You can then add/delete/edit terms and work directly with the spreadsheet just as you would with MultiTerm.</p>","documentation":"<h2>Introduction</h2><p>The application allows user(s) to use&nbsp;Excel spreadsheet (add/delete terms) as a glossary in Studio just as it would do within MultiTerm. The application is available for&nbsp;SDL Trados Studio 2017 and SDL Trados Studio 2019.</p><h2>How do you use it?</h2><p>Install the provider from the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/59\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">AppStore</a>. (On the AppStore, the app is found under TermExcelerator name)</p><p>Set the Excel Terminology Provider&nbsp;from Project Settings -&gt; LanguagePairs -&gt;&nbsp;All Language Pairs -&gt; Termbases</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5706.pastedimage1565682348140v11.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Once the provider is set up, the following&nbsp;options&nbsp;will be displayed:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/800x1000/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4336.pastedimage1565682795250v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><ul><li>&nbsp;<strong>Has header</strong>&nbsp;checkbox allows you to ignore any titles in the first row of the spreadsheet.&nbsp;If you don’t check this then they will treated as terms in your glossary.</li><li>&nbsp;<strong>Is Read Only</strong>&nbsp;checkbox is available just in case you do try to use this in a&nbsp;<a href=\"https://multifarious.filkin.com/2014/12/08/with-a-little-help/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong>shared environment</strong></a>, like dropbox for example.&nbsp;This way, it will minimise the danger of corrupting the file. So you write to your own Excel file and just read the shared one, safe in the knowledge you can’t write to it even if you try!</li><li>The source and target columns needs to be set to match what’s in&nbsp;the browsed&nbsp;spreadsheet, add an approved column, or notes column, set your languages and if you are using synonyms you specify whatever text separator you used.&nbsp;So to elaborate on this a little, your file could look like this:</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x800/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8228.pastedimage1565681866584v3.jpeg\" alt=\" \"></p><p>If you maintained a spreadsheet in the past with synonyms in different columns, then just concatenate them into a new column. If you are not familiar with this feature, then you can apply a formula&nbsp;like this in the Excel file:&nbsp;<strong>=B2&amp;\"|\"&amp;C2&amp;\"|\"&amp;D2</strong>.<strong>&nbsp;</strong>This would allow you to concatenate words in cells B2, C2&nbsp;and&nbsp;D2 with a pipe symbol between them, like this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x800/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8838.pastedimage1565681866584v4.jpeg\" alt=\" \"></p><p>So now you&nbsp;would have the correct string containing all synonyms in one cell, E2.&nbsp;You can then copy the cell down your spreadsheet and do them all at once.&nbsp;Make sure you then select the column, copy it and paste it in as values so you have the text as opposed to the formula, then finally delete the original three columns so you only have the one with the concatenated synoynms:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4375.pastedimage1565681866584v5.jpeg\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong><em>IMPORTANT: Make a copy of your original spreadsheet before you start to mess around with it!!</em></strong></p><p>If you don’t use synonyms then don’t worry, you only have to select your columns in Studio exactly as they are.</p><p>Once you have set your settings, just select your spreadsheet and click on the submit button:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/800x1000/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4540.pastedimage1565682854442v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The spreadsheet is now set up as a termbase and is ready to be used:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/10000x1000/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3404.pastedimage1565682876593v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Below you have the custom Termbase Viewer next to the Termbase Recognition window.&nbsp;</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x1400/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8831.pastedimage1565681866585v9.jpeg\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The recognition window you’ll be familiar with because this is just how MultiTerm works.&nbsp;You will see the terms that were found in the spreadsheet and you can also view the term details from there in the same way you would using&nbsp;MultiTerm&nbsp;to see&nbsp;the terms in the Termbase Viewer.&nbsp;In this case, there is little value in using the viewer for more detail, because it’s exactly the same as the Term Recognition window.&nbsp;However, if you want to edit anything this is where it comes into its own.&nbsp;There are a number of features around this to be aware of, and all associated with the buttons you can see in the screenshot above:</p><ul><li><strong><em>Add</em></strong>: this feature allows you to add new terms whether they are in your translation or not.&nbsp;So if you just wanted to add something you found while looking up another term on a website perhaps, then you click on&nbsp;<strong>Add</strong>&nbsp;and just type the new source term into the cell, then press the&nbsp;<strong>Tab</strong>&nbsp;key (or use the mouse) and you’ll be taken to the target where you can add that too.&nbsp;Add all the synonyms you wish, and notes or approval status, and then you must click on the&nbsp;<strong>Save Entry</strong>&nbsp;button.</li><li>So&nbsp;works the same as&nbsp;using the “Quick Add New term” and “Add New term” icons in Studio itself.&nbsp;Using these will add the terms and save them to the spreadsheet automatically.</li><li>If you keep adding new entries using the&nbsp;<strong>Add</strong>&nbsp;button in the Viewer and forget to press&nbsp;<strong>Save Entry</strong>&nbsp;after each one then you will need to use the&nbsp;<strong>Sync</strong>&nbsp;feature before you finish (see below) or you’ll lose them when you close Studio.</li><li><strong><em>Delete</em></strong>: clicking this button will delete the entry you have highlighted in the Viewer.</li><li><strong><em>Save Entry</em></strong>: this will save the entire entry you are currently editing, and only that one… you can edit an entry just by double clicking it, or selecting and pressing&nbsp;<strong>F2</strong></li><li><strong><em>Sync</em></strong>: this will synchronise all the changes you have made in Studio with the spreadsheet.&nbsp;If you have made changes into the spreadsheet while working (be careful with this, it’s not a great idea – see limitations below) then these will also be synchronised and made available in the Project.&nbsp;Restarting Studio will also synchronise changes made in the spreadsheet with the Studio&nbsp;Project.</li></ul><p>In the translation editor itself you get all the usual benefits of being able to see the highlighted terms, and place them using AutoSuggest:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1000/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8540.pastedimage1565681866585v10.jpeg\" alt=\" \"></p><p>To Add New Term/ Add Quick Term you should select a word/phrase from the source segment and the corresponding one in the target and press on the button from the Ribbon \"Add New Term\"/ \"Add Quick Term\". The same option(s) of adding new terms is available also on right click.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x1000/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3187.pastedimage1565683361253v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x1000/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7870.pastedimage1565683408242v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>After pressing on the \"Add New Term\" option, the terms will be inserted in the spreadsheet and you will see them in the Termbase Viewer:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1000/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7510.pastedimage1565683489161v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h2>Limitations and Important Precautions</h2><p><strong><em>Verification</em></strong>: at the moment the API doesn’t extend to being able to make use of the term verification features that MultiTerm offers.&nbsp;So you cannot use your Excel spreadsheet of terms for verification purposes.</p><p><strong><em>Settings</em></strong>: the settings for the spreadsheets you add can only be accessed when you add a spreadsheet.&nbsp;This is also a limitation of the current version of the API and available functionality in Studio.&nbsp;So if you wish to change something you have to remove the spreadsheet and add it again.&nbsp;During testing I occasionally corrupted the settings in Studio, probably as a result of messing around so much, but if this should happen it’s worth noting that the solution was simple in every case:</p><ol><li>Remove the spreadsheet from the list and close Studio.Delete the&nbsp;excelTerminology.json&nbsp;file from: C:\\Users\\[USERNAME]\\AppData\\Roaming\\SDL Community\\ExcelTerminology\\excelTerminology.json.Restart Studio and add your spreadsheet back.</li></ol><p><strong><em>The Excel&nbsp;file itself</em></strong>: Excel is not a database!&nbsp;There are no controls over it at all.&nbsp;So here’s a few precautions worth noting:</p><ol><li>Take a copy of your Excel Glossary and make sure you always have a backup.Don’t open the Excel glossary while working on it in Studio. Some warnings were built in to remind&nbsp;user if it's open during operations more likely to put the file at risk, but better to get into the right habit from the start.Don’t share it on a shared drive, or dropbox for example, unless you maintain it in read-only mode.</li></ol><p>Logging</p><p>In case the application crashes or issues are thrown, you can find the file which logs the problems at the following location:&nbsp;<strong>C:\\Users\\{USERNAME}\\AppData\\Roaming\\SDL\\ExcelTerminologyProvider</strong>. The&nbsp;<strong>\"ExcelTerminologyProvider.txt\"&nbsp;</strong>file should be&nbsp;attached to the message when the issue is raised on the Sdl Community forum/email.</p><p><strong>*More information about the Excel Terminology Provider can be found in the following article:&nbsp;</strong><a href=\"https://multifarious.filkin.com/2016/03/02/committing-the-cardinal-sin/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong>https://multifarious.filkin.com/2016/03/02/committing-the-cardinal-sin/</strong></a></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/59/versions/1690","changelog":{"Trados This application is no longer maintained or supported.\r\nSome of its use case can now be achieved using the build in feature of AI assistant alongside your AI provider of choice, like: <a operations.</p>\r\n<p>The purpose of TermInjector is to save the translator&#39;s time by inserting translations for known terms and by performing recurring modifications automatically.</p>\r\n<p>Whenever you find yourself doing some mechanical editing task over and over again, you could probably benefit from using TermInjector.</p>\r\n<p>With TermInjector, you can for instance insert translations from a glossary, perform customized measurement conversions, copy non-translatable material automatically from a source segment to a fuzzy match and perform systematic changes in fuzzy matches automatically.</p>\r\n<p>Support for regular expressions enables you to create powerful replacement rules, which can improve translation speed considerably.</p>\r\n<p>Includes TermInjectorPlus can be used with most translation providers (translation memories and machine translation providers) available in Trados. TermInjector acts as a wrapper around a translation provider, and it can modify both the source text sent into the translation provider and the output text that the translation provider returns. TermInjectorPlus is adapted from the <strong>Edit rules</strong> functionality in <a href=\"https://helsinki-nlp.github.io/OPUS-CAT/install\">OPUS-CAT MT Engine</a> (my main open-source project).</p>\r\n<p>In TermInjectorPlus, you can define three types of rules:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Pre-edit rules</strong>: Rules that modify source text before it is sent to a translation provider (translation memory or machine translation engine). These rules can be used to fix source errors or to modify constructions that are difficult for machine translation engines.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Post-edit rules</strong>: Rules that modify the translations from a translation provider. These rules can be used to fix recurring error in translation provider output.</li>\r\n<li><strong>No-match rules</strong>: Rules that modify the source text, which are only applied in case a translation provider does not provide a match. These rules can be used for instance to convert dates and numbers and to add translations for parts of the source text. No-match rules can also be used without specifying a translation provider.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p>Regular expressions (<a href=\"https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/standard/base-types/regular-expression-language-quick-reference\">.NET regex flavor</a>) can be used in the rules. Rules are organized into <em>rule collections</em>, which can contain any number of rules. </p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n AI Monitor</strong> lets you track and control your OpenAI API usage in Trados Studio.\r\n It works by intercepting all OpenAI API calls made from Trados Studio and calculating real-time token and cost data.\r\n This gives you full visibility into your AI translation expenses, helps verify your billing accuracy, and ensures you can manage costs confidently - without changing how you work.</p>\r\n<p>Read the full documentation here:\r\n <a DPAPI.</li>\r\n<li><strong>System Tray Mode:</strong> Runs quietly in the background.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Multi-Language Interface:</strong> Available in 12 languages.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Professional It was based on a simple premise, <em><strong>&quot;get me an analysis as quickly as possible without having to use Studio at all!&quot;</strong></em> It does this and more.</p>\r\n<p> When you install the application it does two things:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>It creates a new view in Studio for analysing files, and</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>It creates a standalone app that does the same thing without Studio running at all</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p> <strong><u>Features</u></strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>drag and drop files, or zip files, for analysis</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>use Project Templates to quickly analyse files based on specific customer or domain resources</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>optionally analyse without the need for a TM at all</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>uses the standard Studio reporting formats</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>offers a simplified CSV report format</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>offers &quot;copy to clipboard&quot; It provides users with an easy and efficient way to get detailed analysis reports for their translation work without needing to create full Studio projects. Ideal for project managers, freelancers, or anyone looking to quickly assess file content.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7701.pastedimage1612361772692v4.png\" alt=\"SDL Analyse in Studio interface\"></p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Drag and Drop Analysis</strong>: Quickly add files by dragging into the SDL Analyse interface (in Studio or standalone).</li>\r\n<li><strong>Runs Standalone or Within Studio</strong>: Offers both a standalone application and an embedded view in Trados Studio.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Report Output Options</strong>: Generates reports in HTML, XLSX, XML, CSV, and to clipboard.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Translation Memory Flexibility</strong>:<ul>\r\n<li>Advanced View: Uses TMs from templates or user-added ones.</li>\r\n<li>Simple View: Uses user-specified TMs or creates one automatically.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Minimum Studio Version</strong>: SDL Trados Studio 2017 (CU3 or higher required)</li>\r\n<li><strong>Supported OS</strong>: Windows 10 or later</li>\r\n<li><strong>Permissions</strong>: Admin rights may be needed for registry edits or updates</li>\r\n<li><strong>Dependency</strong>: Studio CU3 update (can be installed <a href=\"https://gateway.sdl.com/communityknowledge?articleName=CUs-Studio-2017\">here</a>)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Installation and Version Info</h2>\r\n<p>You can check that you have the latest version installed <a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/sdl-analyse/726/\">here</a>.</p>\r\n<p>All updates are logged in the <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/translationproductivity/w/customer-experience/5572/changelog-sdl-analyse\">ChangeLog</a>.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Usage</h2>\r\n<p>Once installed, find SDL Analyse either:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Inside Trados Studio (in a dedicated view), or</li>\r\n<li>As a desktop application.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Workflow Overview:</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Open the SDL Analyse view or app.</li>\r\n<li>Drag and drop the file(s), or browse to select.</li>\r\n<li>SDL Analyse processes the files and generates a report.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/445x303/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1300.standalone.jpg\" alt=\"Standalone version\"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/443x238/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1108.view_5F00_internal.jpg\" alt=\"Studio embedded view\"></p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Concepts and Principles</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Works with or without Translation Memories.</li>\r\n<li>Automatically uses your default language pair if no TM is selected.</li>\r\n<li>No need to create a full Trados project.</li>\r\n<li>Supports ZIP file imports.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/443x110/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3718.languages.jpg\" alt=\"Default language Version Mismatch</h3>\r\n<p>Ensure you have the correct SDL Analyse version for your version of Studio (check <a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/sdl-analyse/726/\">here</a>).</p>\r\n<h3>2. Registry Issues</h3>\r\n<p>Old registry entries from uninstalled Studio versions can cause issues.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Open <strong>regedit</strong> (Win + R → type <code>regedit</code>)</li>\r\n<li>Navigate to: <code>HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\\SOFTWARE\\WOW6432Node\\SDL\\</code></li>\r\n<li>Delete entries for Studio versions you no longer use (e.g., Studio 4 = Studio 2015).</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<blockquote>\r\n<p>⚠️ Do <strong>not</strong> delete entries for versions still in use (e.g., Studio 16 = Studio 2021).</p>\r\n</blockquote>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2148.pastedimage1612361341969v3.png\" alt=\"Registry Editor\"></p>\r\n<h3>3. Analyse</code></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Attach logs when requesting help on the <a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/translationproductivity/f/openexchange_applications\">Community Forum</a>, \"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Trados Batch\u00A0Anonymizer Settings</strong></p>\r\n<p>There are two ways of carrying out the anonymization when the Trados Batch Anonymizer batch task is The enabled values will appear in the file as the relevant author.</p>\r\n<p>In the example below, in xliff:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>&quot;Created by&quot; value for segments will be replaced with &#39;Steve&#39;</li>\r\n<li>For all comment types (File, Segment and section)\u00A0the author will be &#39;Peter&#39;</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>After selecting the values- click Finish- Close</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6862.pastedimage1599219967829v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Reopen the file\u00A0</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5857.pastedimage1599220032841v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>After running the task -</p>\r\n<p>The Translation origin/Revision and TM details have been anonymized.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4478.pastedimage1599221370767v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3465.pastedimage1599221459351v5.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7367.pastedimage1599221582935v6.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>To Anonymize ALL values - It&#39;s not essential for you to install the Connector to work with Trados Business Manager, but it might enhance your enjoyment of working with the tools.<br>  The connector will not do anything for you unless you have Trados Business Manager installed as well.  You can learn more about this exciting product for managing the business side of your translation activities <a href=\"https://www.trados.com/products/business-manager/\">here</a>.</p>\r\n<p>The connector allows you to create jobs, quotes or invoices in Trados Business Manager directly from Studio providing a neat workflow if you prefer to use Studio as the starting point for managing your work. Full instructions and user documentation is provided through the README to the <a href=\"https://github.com/RWS/language-cloud-powershell-toolkit\">GitHub Repository</a> for this Toolkit.</p>\r\n<p>The Language Cloud PowerShell Toolkit integrates with Language Cloud enabling users to script and automate actions using the publicly available APIs. The purpose of this toolkit is to automate project creation and retrieve essential resources through the PowerShell console.</p>\r\n<h5>Key Features</h5>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Modules: AuthenticationHelper, ProjectServerHelper, ResourcesHelper, UserManagerHelper, FileManagementHelper, WorkflowHelper and ReportingHelper. Each module contains functions to handle specific Language Cloud operations.</li>\r\n<li>Scripts: Sample scripts like Sample_Roundtrip.ps1 demonstrate practical usage of the toolkit for common tasks such as creating and managing projects.</li>\r\n<li>Samples: Example files included to illustrate typical automation workflows.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<p>Requirements:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>PowerShell Version 7.4 or Higher</li>\r\n<li>Access to Language Cloud with adequite user rights and permissions</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h5><strong>Getting Started</strong></h5>\r\n<p>Installation:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Download the toolkit files.</li>\r\n<li>Unblock the downloaded files as needed.</li>\r\n<li>Create necessary directories in your PowerShell user folder, such as Documents\\PowerShell\\Modules.</li>\r\n<li>Copy the relevant script and module files to the designated directories.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>After obtaining your Client ID, Client Secret, and Tenant ID update the script with the retrieved values. Example Sample_Roundtrip.ps1</p>\r\n<h5><strong>Authentication</strong></h5>\r\n<p>The Get-AccessKey function retrieves an authentication token and stores it as a PowerShell object for API use. To reduce repeated requests, it saves the token in a JSON file within the AuthenticationHelper module. Run</p>\r\n<pre><code>Get-AccessKey -id \u00A0&quot;Your_ClientId&quot; -secret &quot;Your_ClientSecret&quot; -lcTenant &quot;YourLcTenant&quot;\r\n</code></pre>\r\n<h5><strong>Running Scripts</strong></h5>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Open PowerShell 7.4 or higher.</li>\r\n<li>Set the execution policy to Unrestricted if required (Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy Unrestricted -Scope CurrentUser).</li>\r\n<li>Navigate to the folder where the script (e.g., Sample_Roundtrip.ps1) is stored.</li>\r\n<li>Run the script using .\\Sample_Roundtrip.ps1.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h5><strong>Module Usage</strong></h5>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><p>Use the Get-Module command to verify if the required modules are available in your PowerShell environment</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Import Modules</p>\r\n<p>  Import-Module -Name AuthenticationHelper\r\n  Import-Module -Name ProjectHelper\u00A0\r\n  Import-Module -Name ResourcesHelper\u00A0\r\n  Import-Module -Name UsersHelper\u00A0\r\n  Import-Module -Name TerminologyHelper\u00A0 </p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>Once imported, functions within each module can be executed for automating various operations.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h5><strong>Help &amp; Troubleshooting</strong></h5>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Use the Get-Help cmdlet to access detailed documentation for each function after the module is imported.</li>\r\n<li>Ensure all file paths are correctly configured, and the required modules are available in the Documents\\PowerShell\\Modules directory.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>For detailed guidance and information on available endpoints, please refer to the <a href=\"https://eu.cloud.trados.com/lc/api-docs/authentication\">API Documentation</a>.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: Official support for these products has ended in 2012. RWS recommends that you consider converting these legacy file types to the .SDLXLIFF format.</li><li>Add the <strong>Upgrade Migration Wizard</strong> to Trados Studio. This wizard was available out-of the box with previous Trados Studio versions, but has now moved to this dedicated app in order to declutter the Trados Studio user interface. This powerful wizard enables the following batch-oriented use cases:</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Combine multiple Trados Studio translation memories (TMs) into one. In fact you can merge any supported translation memories (based on common languages)</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Upgrade Translator's Workbench and SDLX TMs to Trados Studio translation memory format: .TMW and .MDB format to .SDLTM</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Migrate TMs from TMX to Trados Studio format in batch mode</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Migrate file-based to server-based TMs in Trados GroupShare and vice versa</li></ul><p><br></p><p>The requirement for this app came about as a result of changes introduced into Trados Studio 2021 SR2. To generate an assessment of your translation, Trados Copilot - Smart Review uses the Azure OpenAI Large Language Models (LLMs) such as gpt-35 or gpt-4 while at the same time considering, as point of reference, confirmed translations from the current document or similar Translation Memory matches.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Minimum requirements</strong></p><ul><li>Trados Studio 2022 SR1 or Trados Studio SR2</li><li>A Trados cloud account that is prepared for this capability</li><li>A Microsoft Azure subscription with an OpenAI resource</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Getting the Plugin</strong></p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>You can install the Trados Copilot - Smart Review app from:</p><ul><li>Trados AppStore</li><li>From within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore</li></ul><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;After installing the Trados Copilot - Smart Review app, you can select it in Trados Studio, from the Editor-&gt;View menu while editing or reviewing your translation.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Authentication within Trados Studio</strong></p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>In order to use the Trados Copilot - Smart Review app, you need to be logged into a Trados cloud account with a custom subscription (at the time of writing, this is based on Trados Enterprise, we will open this up for other subscriptions in future).
