Suite","description":"<p><strong><em>The Problem</em></strong></p><p>The protection of sensitive personal information in the translation process needs to address several issues.</p><p>First of all, how to protect data in translation files without exposing it to being retained in translation memories and bilingual files in a translation project.</p><p>Secondly, how to remove it from translation memories when it has already been stored.</p><p>And lastly how to retain awareness of who translated what without exposing data that is held in system fields and custom fields by the translation tools.</p><p><strong><em>The Solution</em></strong></p><p>The Trados Data Protection Suite combines two existing applications - project Anonymizer and SDLTM Anonymizer, giving you control over how sensitive data is managed.</p><p>Using plain text, or regular expressions, you can pseudonomize, or anonymize the data in your translation projects and translation memories. This helps you retain the benefits of using a translation tool by knowing who translated what work, without exposing this personal information to any unauthorised people you are sharing your projects with.</p><p>The application can be very useful in helping you meet your obligations with regard to legal requirements such as those laid down under GDPR.</p><p><br></p><p><br></p><p>To learn how to use this application, please check the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/39?tab=documentation\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Documentation </a>tab.</p>","documentation":"<p>This application delivers the installation of two apps:</p><p>- TradosTM Anonymizer</p><p>- Trados Project Anonymizer</p><h1>Trados Project Anonymizer</h1><p>Project Anonymizer contains two tasks which searches for personal information based on regular expressions and&nbsp;<strong>encrypt/decrypt or tag</strong>&nbsp;the data using&nbsp;a key&nbsp;provided by the user.&nbsp;</p><p>By default the plugin provides a list of regular expressions but also&nbsp;allows user to remove, edit, add new regular expression, import and export a list of expressions which can be used on other project.&nbsp;</p><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">Because the batch task overrides original files you should check your regular expressions&nbsp;</strong><a href=\"https://regexr.com/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong>here</strong></a><strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">&nbsp;before you process the files.&nbsp;</strong></p><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">Plugin makes a backup of the initial project before processing the file.</strong>&nbsp;If the file is not processed as you expected you can find a backup to the following path:</p><p>C:\\Users\\[username]\\AppData\\Roaming\\RWS AppStore\\TradosTMAnonymizer\\Logs</p><h3>How to use Project anonymizer plugin</h3><h3>Accept our terms and conditions</h3><p>Terms and condition window should appear when Studio starts. If you are not able to see the window please hover over Studio icon from task bar.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7801.accept.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7801.accept.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><strong style=\"color: rgb(0, 128, 0);\">Please agree with our Terms and Conditions.</strong>&nbsp;After you agree you won't see this window again.<span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">&nbsp;</span><strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">If you don't agree you won't be able to use out plugin</strong><span style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\">.</span>&nbsp;Plugin name will be available in Batch Task ribbon but you cannot use it. Batch task window will look like this. To close the window click \"Cancel\"</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4213.protect.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4213.protect.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p><strong>If by mistake you didn't agree&nbsp;but clicked \"Ok\" on terms&amp;condition page please close Studio.T&amp;C window will appear again and you will be able to accept.</strong></p><h3>How to use Protect Data batch task</h3><p>There are 3 steps:</p><p>1. Select from the grid which regular expressions should be applied.<strong>Check the box</strong>&nbsp;<strong>from \"Enable\" column</strong>&nbsp;for each expression you want to use.</p><p>2. If you want the matching data to be&nbsp;<strong>anonymized check the \"Encrypt\" box. If the box is not checked the plugin only tags the data.&nbsp;</strong></p><p>3. Add an encryption key which will used for encryption.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0523.Screenshot_5F00_1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0523.Screenshot_5F00_1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h3>How to edit existing expression</h3><p>To edit an expression just double click on the cell you want to edit.&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h3>How to delete expressions from grid</h3><p>Grid allows multiple selection.All rules can be selected with&nbsp;<strong>Ctrl+A.</strong></p><p>To select multiple rows hold&nbsp;<strong>CTRL key and select each row</strong>&nbsp;you want to delete, then pres&nbsp;<strong>Delete key</strong>.&nbsp;</p><p>An&nbsp;confirmation window appear.&nbsp;</p><h3>How to export&nbsp;rules</h3><p>Export feature is useful when you want to use same expressions for multiple projects. That expressions will be added into project settings using \"Import\" feature.</p><p><strong>To export patterns</strong>&nbsp;select the rows corresponding with the expressions you want and click<strong>&nbsp;\"Export patterns\"</strong>&nbsp;button. A window will appear where you can set the name of the file and the path to export.&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Exported file will be an excel which has on first column the rule and in the second column the description.</strong>&nbsp;</p><h3>How to add / import new expressions&nbsp;</h3><p>1.&nbsp;<strong>To add new expression</strong>&nbsp;scroll down at the end of the grid fill the empty row. You can add as many expressions you need.</p><p>2. To import expression click&nbsp;<strong>\"Import patterns\"</strong>&nbsp;button and select the excel file with expressions. The window allows to select multiple excel files within dialog. If one expression&nbsp;already exists in list it will not be added.</p><p>&nbsp;Excel file must have following structure:</p><ul><li>on first column (A) will have the rules</li><li>on second column (B) will be the description corresponding for each rule.</li></ul><p>See file for excel example.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7024.example.xlsx\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">7024.example.xlsx</a>&nbsp;</p><h3>How to use Unprotect Data batch task</h3><p>Click \"Batch Task\" button on ribbon and under&nbsp;<strong>\"Protect Data\"</strong>&nbsp;you should see&nbsp;<strong>\"Unprotect Data\"</strong>&nbsp;batch task.&nbsp;&nbsp;<strong>Add the key</strong>&nbsp;you used to encrypt the project -&gt;&nbsp;<strong>Finish</strong>.</p><p>&nbsp;<strong>If Ignore encrypted data checkbox is enabled the batch task only will transform tagged data into text again</strong>. All protected data tags will remain unchanged.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5140.Unprotect.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5140.Unprotect.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>All the personal information which in previous task were transformed into a placeholder&nbsp;or encrypted will be replaced with the original text.</p><h1>TradosTMAnonymizer</h1><h2>Overview</h2><p><strong>TradosTM Anonymizer</strong>&nbsp;is an SDL Trados Studio plugin with features to anonymize personal data within&nbsp;<em>segments</em>,&nbsp;<em>system fields</em>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<em>custom fields</em>&nbsp;in Translation Memories.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Data anonymization</strong>&nbsp;is done for the purpose of protecting private or personally identifiable data by substituting it in the Translation Units with placeholders, while still maintaining the integrity of the data itself.</p><p>The content for anonymization is identified by&nbsp;<strong>Regular Expressions</strong>&nbsp;that match paterns such as&nbsp;<em>EMails</em>,&nbsp;<em>phone numbers</em>,&nbsp;<em>IP addresses</em>,&nbsp;<em>social security numbers</em>&nbsp;etc.... and includes an integrated preview that displays a filtered list of Translation Units whose content matched the search criteria to further select and confirm the content that is anonymized prior to launching the process.</p><p>Support for working with both server and file-based Translation Memories is available.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2>Installation Instructions</h2><p>The application is installed by double clicking the sdlplugin file available through the SDL AppStore.&nbsp;</p><h2>&nbsp;</h2><h2>Terms &amp; Conditions</h2><p>When the application loads for the first time a window will appear asking you to accept the&nbsp;<strong>Terms &amp; Conditions</strong>.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1884.pastedimage1541663814753v1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/373x283/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1884.pastedimage1541663814753v1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>After you agree by selecting the checkbox option&nbsp;<strong>I agree</strong>, you'll the be able to see and work with the features from&nbsp;<strong>TradosTM Anonymizer</strong>. If you hit Close button by mistake prior to selecting the option&nbsp;<strong>I agree</strong>, then it will reappear again the next time you launch Studio.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2>Settings</h2><p>A backup of the translation memory is performed prior to applying any changes. This option is checked by default, but the user can turn this feature off if not required.</p><ul><li><strong>File-based</strong>: a full copy of the local TM (<strong>*.sdltm</strong>) is copied to the backup path.</li><li><strong>Server-based</strong>: a full TM export (<strong>*.tmx</strong>) is performed and saved in the backup path.</li></ul><p><br></p><p>A report is created to record all changes applied to the TM and saved in the path indicated by the user.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2>Navigation / Translation Memories</h2><p><strong>TradosTM&nbsp;Anonymizer</strong>&nbsp;supports working with both server and file-based Translation Memories.&nbsp;To anonymize content in a TM, it will first need to be added to the list (<em>see&nbsp;</em><strong><em>Add Translation Memory</em></strong>), and then loaded into memory, by selecting the&nbsp;<strong>Load</strong>&nbsp;<em>checkbox</em>.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7028.pastedimage1541664172690v3.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/440x400/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7028.pastedimage1541664172690v3.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2>Add Translation Memory</h2><h3>File-based (*.sdltm)</h3><ol><li>Select the&nbsp;<strong>Add file-based TM</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Translation Memories</strong>&nbsp;ribbonBrowse and select the Translation Memories from the file-system to added to the list.Select the&nbsp;<strong>Select Folder</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Translation Memories</strong>&nbsp;ribbonBrowse and select the folder on the file system that contains SDL Translation Memories<strong>Note</strong>: all TM files with the extension (<strong>*.sdltm</strong>) are added to the list.Drag and Drop the Translation Memories (<strong>*.sdltm</strong>) in the navigation area.</li></ol><h4>Example</h4><p>&nbsp;</p><h3>Server-based</h3><ol><li>Select the&nbsp;<strong>Add server</strong>&nbsp;TM button from the Translation Memories ribbonProvide the&nbsp;<strong>UrI</strong>&nbsp;and credentials to log in to the server where the Translation Memories reside<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4152.pastedimage1541664293946v4.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/440x400/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4152.pastedimage1541664293946v4.png\" alt=\" \"></a>Next, select the Translation Memories that you want to add, from the list of memories available in the&nbsp;<strong>Select Server-based Translation Memory</strong>&nbsp;window, and click OK<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8117.pastedimage1541664300230v5.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/766x386/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8117.pastedimage1541664300230v5.png\" alt=\" \"></a></li></ol><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h2>Anonymizing data</h2><h3><br></h3><h3>Content Filtering Rules</h3><p>The&nbsp;<strong>Content Filtering Rules</strong>&nbsp;tab manages the criteria we use to match content within the segments that we want to anonymize.&nbsp;A default list of rules will be added the first time you launch the plugin with some common regular expressions (e.g.&nbsp;<em>Email address, IP4 Address, MAC address, Social Security Numbe</em>rs etc..)</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4606.pastedimage1541715280637v1.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/693x321/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4606.pastedimage1541715280637v1.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h4>Add a new rule</h4><ol><li>Select the&nbsp;<strong>Click here to add a new rule</strong>&nbsp;button in the data grid, or use the hot key&nbsp;<strong>[Ctrl + N]</strong>Provide the criteria for the&nbsp;<strong>Rule</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>Description</strong>Select the&nbsp;<strong>Save</strong>&nbsp;button<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8204.pastedimage1541715631520v3.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/588x101/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8204.pastedimage1541715631520v3.png\" alt=\" \"></a></li></ol><h4>Edit rule</h4><p>The rule and description can be edited inline within the data grid.&nbsp;Simply select the cell and start typing.</p><p><br></p><h4>Delete rule</h4><p>Select the rule in the data grid and then click on the&nbsp;<strong>Delete</strong>&nbsp;button or hit the&nbsp;<strong>[Delete]</strong>&nbsp;key on your keyboard.&nbsp;To delete multiple rules, select them from the data grid individual or all of them&nbsp;<strong>[Ctrl + A]</strong>&nbsp;and then selecting the&nbsp;<strong>Delete</strong>&nbsp;button or hitting the&nbsp;<strong>[Delete]</strong>&nbsp;key.</p><p><br></p><h4>Order</h4><p>To change the order of a rule, simply select the&nbsp;<strong>up</strong>/<strong>down</strong>&nbsp;arrow keys associated with each rule in the data grid. The order in which the rules are executed is important to ensure the right content is matched in the right order, given the criteria used.&nbsp;</p><p>Let’s assume we have an email address in the segment that contains some numbers and we have two rules, one to match email format and another to match number format.&nbsp;Now, if we executed the regular expression for matching the numbers first, then the rule for matching the email would no longer be successful, as the email format would have been changed (<em>e.g. numbers would have been replaced with a tag</em>) and no longer resemble a valid e-mail convention.</p><p><br></p><h4>Export rules</h4><p>To export rules, select them from the data grid and click on the&nbsp;<strong>Export</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon. The selected rules will then be saved in an excel file with the following structure.&nbsp;You can then use this file to distribute the rules to other teams or possibly use as a backup for your own requirements, later to be imported for other projects.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7608.pastedimage1541715724687v4.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/700x500/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7608.pastedimage1541715724687v4.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h4>Import rules</h4><p>To import rules, select the&nbsp;<strong>Import</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon.&nbsp;If the&nbsp;<strong>ID</strong>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<strong>Rule</strong>&nbsp;from the imported data matches that of an existing rule in the data grid, then the existing rule will be updated, otherwise a new rule will be created and added to the list.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h3>Preview Changes</h3><p>The&nbsp;<strong>Preview changes</strong>&nbsp;window enables you to review the content that is matched by rules before applying any changes to the Translation Memory. To preview the content that is matched by the rules, select the&nbsp;<strong>Preview Changes</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon.</p><p><strong>Note:&nbsp;</strong>Ensure that you have at least one Translation Memory loaded from the navigation list and one or more rules selected from the&nbsp;<strong>Content Filter Rules</strong>&nbsp;tab.</p><p>A new window will appear with the content that matched the criteria of the rules that you selected, highlighted with an orange/brownish background.&nbsp;Select the checkbox associated with the segments whose content should be anonymized individually or select all by checking the&nbsp;<strong>Apply</strong>&nbsp;<em>checkbox</em>&nbsp;in the column header.</p><p>&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0753.pastedimage1541715894094v5.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/891x298/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0753.pastedimage1541715894094v5.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><p>As well as the rules automatically identifying the content to be anonymized, you can mark-up additional content for anonymization directly in the preview window, by selecting the appropriate content of the segment in the Source or Target and then choosing the option “<strong>Select content for anonymization”</strong>&nbsp;from the context menu, as follows:</p><p>Select the button&nbsp;<strong>Apply changes</strong>&nbsp;to start the process of anonymizing the data in the Translation Memory.</p><p><br></p><h4>Example</h4><h4><br></h4><p>&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h3>System Fields</h3><p>Once you load the Translation Memory, a unique list of the User Names that are found in the TM will be populated in the System Fields data grid.&nbsp;These fields are recovered from the&nbsp;<strong>Created By</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>Modified By</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>Last Used By</strong>&nbsp;properties that are associated with each of the Translation Units in the TM.</p><p>To update the user name value of the System Field, simply provide a new value and select the&nbsp;<strong>Change</strong>&nbsp;<em>checkbox</em>&nbsp;for that field.</p><p><br></p><p>Select the button&nbsp;<strong>Apply changes</strong>&nbsp;from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon to start the process of updating the&nbsp;<strong>System Fields</strong>&nbsp;in the Translation Memory.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h4>Export System Fields</h4><p>To export system fields, select them from the data grid and click on the&nbsp;<strong>Export</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon. The selected system fields will then be saved in an excel file with the following structure.&nbsp;You use file as a backup for your own requirements, or possibly later to be imported for other projects.</p><p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8623.pastedimage1541716300874v8.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/489x266/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8623.pastedimage1541716300874v8.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h4>Import System Fields</h4><p>To import system fields, select the&nbsp;<strong>Import</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon.&nbsp;If the&nbsp;<strong>User Name</strong>&nbsp;from the imported data matches that of an existing System Field in the data grid, then the New Value will be imported, otherwise it will be ignored.</p><p>&nbsp;</p><h3>&nbsp;</h3><h3>Custom Fields</h3><p>Once you load the Translation Memory, all of the custom fields found in that TM will be populated in the&nbsp;<strong>Custom Fields</strong>&nbsp;data grid.&nbsp;The custom fields can be of type (<em>SinglePickList</em>,&nbsp;<em>SingleString</em>,&nbsp;<em>MultiplePickList</em>,&nbsp;<em>MultipleString</em>,&nbsp;<em>Integer</em>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<em>DateTime</em>) and depending on the type, the field may contain more than one values.</p><p>To update the custom field value, select the field from the left pane, and then provide a&nbsp;<strong>New Value</strong>&nbsp;for the field value on the right, remembering to also check the&nbsp;<strong>Change</strong>&nbsp;<em>checkbox</em>&nbsp;for those field values;</p><p>&nbsp;</p><p>Select the button&nbsp;<strong>Apply changes</strong>&nbsp;from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon to start the process of updating the&nbsp;<strong>Custom Fields</strong>&nbsp;in the Translation Memory.</p><p><br></p><h4>Export Custom Fields</h4><p>To export custom fields, select them from the data grid and click on the&nbsp;<strong>Export</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon. The selected custom fields will then be saved in an excel file with the following structure.&nbsp;You use this file as a backup for your own requirements, or possibly later to be imported for other projects.</p><p>&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8306.pastedimage1541716551482v10.png\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/625x305/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8306.pastedimage1541716551482v10.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p><h4>Import Custom Fields</h4><p>To import custom fields, select the&nbsp;<strong>Import</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Actions</strong>&nbsp;ribbon.&nbsp;If the&nbsp;<strong>Name, Type and Value</strong>&nbsp;from the imported data matches that of an existing Custom Field in the data grid, then the New Value will be imported, otherwise it will be ignored.</p><h2>Report Log</h2><p>&nbsp;All changes applied to the Translation Memories are recorded in xml log files and saved in the location indicated by the user from the settings.&nbsp;These reports are added to the&nbsp;<strong>Log Report</strong>&nbsp;view when you load a TM in the plugin.</p><p>&nbsp;<a Alternatively log a case with support via the <a href=\"https://gateway.sdl.com\">gateway</a> and select Trados Studio Licensing Help.</strong></p>\r\n<p><strong>Trados Freshstart</strong> is a godsend for anyone participating in the Studio/MultiTerm Beta program, and it&#39;s also very handy for support engineers, help desk specialists and also individual users.</p>\r\n<p>The idea is the application provides an easy way to reset all the user files in a couple of clicks without having to navigate through folders, making sure you have the right permissions etc. The application also provides a useful description of what each of the users files contains. This is particularly useful as it will help you to understand what data is held where and what the impact might be if you reset these files.</p>\r\n<p>There is a restore function if you make a mistake, but generally none of the operations are fatal!</p>\r\n<p>To install, download the zip file to your desktop. Unzip it and place the MSI file onto your desktop.</p>\r\n<p>Now run the MSI installer by double clicking it.</p>\r\n<p>Once installation is complete, you will find the start icon on your desktop which you simply double-click to run.</p>\r\n<p><em><strong><u>You must have admin rights to run this application!</u></strong></em></p>\r\n<p>To learn how to use this app and for more information, application\u00A0provides an easy way to reset all the user files in a couple of clicks without having to navigate through folders, making sure you have the right permissions etc.</p>\r\n<p>The\u00A0application\u00A0also provides a useful description of what each of the users files contains.</p>\r\n<p>This is particularly useful as it will help you to understand what data is held where and what the impact might be if you reset these files.</p>\r\n<p>There is a restore function if you make a mistake, but generally none of the operations are fatal!</p>\r\n<p>How to install-</p>\r\n<p><strong>***To run this application, you will need admin rights***</strong></p>\r\n<p>The application can be downloaded from the\u00A0<a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/sdl-freshstart/876/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1701167743891.1701174261328.84&amp;__hssc=110027290.1.1701174261328&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\">AppStore</a>\u00A0or via the AppStore Studio integration (Trados Studio 2021).</p>\r\n<p>Once you have downloaded the .zip-</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0412.FS-Installer.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>save this to your Desktop, unzip and double-click the .msi installer to begin the installation.</p>\r\n<p>Once installed, you will now find the application in Control Panel- Programs and Features</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5127.Freshstart.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>When updating to a newer version, please ensure older versions are removed via the Windows Control Panel.</p>\r\n<p>When to use Freshstart</p>\r\n<p>As is the case with any software, you may be confronted with errors that cannot simply cannot be explained, which may mean the installation for instance has become corrupted.</p>\r\n<p>Removing Trados Studio and simply reinstalling, will, in most cases,\u00A0<em>not</em>\u00A0fix the issue(s) as these will remain after installation, so repairing/or resetting Trados Studio is the recommended solution.</p>\r\n<p>These errors can\u00A0appear, for example as:</p>\r\n<p>-Object reference not set...</p>\r\n<p>-An unexpected error has occurred...</p>\r\n<p>-Could not find part of path...</p>\r\n<p>-...</p>\r\n<p>Before using Trados Freshstart, it&#39;s worth perhaps considering, simply first resetting Windows Layout, as this often resolves unexplained issues without the need to reset Studio.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0820.Reset-windows.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Freshstart UI</p>\r\n<p>When you start Studio (simply click the Windows Start Icon and start typing Freshstart)</p>\r\n<p>Select Run as administrator</p>\r\n<p>\u00A0\u00A0<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6170.Run-as-Admin.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Once you have the app open- you will see which versions of Studio (MultiTerm) you have installed.</p>\r\n<p>For example, on this machine, we have Studio 2021/2019/2017</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2705.FS-Installed.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Select the version you wish to reset.</p>\r\n<p>Now when you hover over each path- you are provided a description of what data is contained in the selected path</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2148.Data.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>As default, all paths are selected. If you know exactly which folder to reset, you can deselect the ones you don&#39;t need.</p>\r\n<p>You can choose to &#39;Repair Studio&#39; or &#39;Remove&#39; the selected folders.</p>\r\n<p>Restore allows you to return to the previous state, with near zero loss of data.</p>\r\n<p>Now select Repair Studio- you will normally receive a message asking you whether you accept this application to make changes- select OK</p>\r\n<p>If you now see a message telling you to close XYZ applications- confirm and proceed.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3482.configure.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>The process will take a couple of minutes. offers an alternative way of working.</strong> </p>\r\n<p><strong>Trados InSource!</strong> is for the <em><strong>professional version of Studio only,</strong></em> because it uses the project automation api.</p>\r\n<p>The basic functionality is the automatic creation of Studio projects, based on translatable files being dropped into watch folders that are defined by you.</p>\r\n<p>You can have as many watch folders as you like and these can be local on your computer, or in Dropbox, Google Drive or any other location you wish to use.</p>\r\n<p>Each watch folder has its own project template, so you have good flexibility in being able to automate the project creation process for different clients/domains.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n    --lightgray: #e9ecef;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  code {\r\n    color: black;\r\n    background-color: var(--lightgray);\r\n    border-radius: 5px;\r\n    padding: InSource!\u00A0is for the professional version of Studio\u00A0only because it uses the Project Automation API.</p>\r\n<p>The basic functionality is the automatic creation of Studio projects based on translatable files being dropped into watch folders that are defined by you.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>Log folder location:\u00A0C:\\Users[username]\\AppData\\Roaming\\RWS AppStore\\InSourceLogs**</p>\r\n<h2>The structure of a &quot;watch&quot; folder</h2>\r\n<p>You can add as many watch folders as you want, but they must have following structure:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8228.pastedimage1617292518386v1.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Example of folder structure:</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1200x1300/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4300.pastedimage1590063371798v1.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>To add a new watch folder in the\u00A0&quot;Watch folders&quot;\u00A0area, click on the\u00A0&quot;Add folder&quot;\u00A0button. A new window will be opened from where you can select your watch folder.\u00A0</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3821.SDL-InSource-watch-folder.PNG\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Once you have added your watch folder, remember to add a project template template that you wish to be used for all projects added into that watch folder and\u00A0&quot;Save&quot;\u00A0your settings.</p>\r\n<p>If there are folders with files you your watch folders when you add them, the project requests will appear in\u00A0&quot;Project Requests&quot;\u00A0area.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3630.projectRequest.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3630.projectRequest.png\" alt=\"\"></a></p>\r\n<p>There are two ways you can check for new project requests:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Manually using\u00A0&quot;Check project requests&quot;\u00A0button\u00A0Or using the\u00A0&quot;Timer Manager&quot;\u00A0enabling this option you</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7532.check.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/500x700/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7532.check.png\" alt=\"\"></a></p>\r\n<p>When a new project request is added to &quot;Watch folder&quot; if the timer is enabled a new notification will appear in Studio with the name of the project and the request path.</p>\r\n<p>This notification will appear also when you start Studio if a new project was added to watch folder when Studio was closed.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2781.Notifications.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/500x700/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2781.Notifications.png\" alt=\"\"></a></p>\r\n<p>From the notification area you can create projects for the project requests. A confirmation message will appear after the project was created and the notification will be removed for created project.</p>\r\n<p>Newly created projects will be available in Studio Projects View.</p>\r\n<h3>How to create a project from Project Request</h3>\r\n<p>Select project name from &quot;Project Request&quot; grid and click &quot;Create Projects&quot;</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2055.create.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2055.create.png\" alt=\"\"></a></p>\r\n<p><strong>! Important:</strong></p>\r\n<p><strong>1</strong>.\u00A0If there are no project templates available for you to select once you have added your watch folders, you need to import these into\u00A0Studio first. Go to\u00A0<em><strong>File-&gt; Setup -&gt; Project Templates -&gt; Import</strong></em></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0842.pastedimage1589882168674v1.png\" alt=\"\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0116.pastedimage1589882188952v2.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p><strong>2.</strong> ****Ensure that the custom template has Language Pairs configured. It can be checked from Studio:\u00A0<em><strong>File -&gt; Setup -&gt; Project Templates -&gt; select the custom template - &gt; Edit -&gt; Language Pairs -&gt; Add</strong></em></p>\r\n<p><em><strong><img environment.</p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<p>Trados Studio 2017</p>\r\n<h2>How to find the plugin?</h2>\r\n<p>You can install the Trados Jobs either   color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/382/versions/1737","versionNumber":"1.0.0.1","minimumRequiredVersionOfStudio":"18.1.0","maximumRequiredVersionOfStudio":"18.9.0","createdAt":"2025-10-21T08:11:33.774349Z","publishedAt":"2025-10-21T08:11:33.77435Z","supportedProducts":[{"isLegacy":false,"id":48,"parentProductID":1,"productName":"Trados Studio 2024 (SR1)","minimumStudioVersion":"18.1.0"}]}],"categories":[{"id":4,"name":"Process automation & management"}]},{"id":33,"downloadCount":20964,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2016-12-13T13:18:00.203Z","updatedAt":"2025-01-13T07:20:06.909027Z","name":"Trados Number Verifier","description":"<p><strong>The problem</strong></p><p>While the standard number verification in Trados Studio may often be sufficient there are some occasions when a bit more control would be preferable, for example when translating documents that contain lots of numbers, especially when they are non-standard.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>The solution</strong></p><p>The Number Verifier plug-in allows you to fine-tune settings to provide you with the desired balance between amount of false positives and potentially missed errors.</p><p>Once installed, restart Trados Studio and you will find the settings for the Trados Number Verifier in the Options menu under 'Verification'.</p><p><br></p>","documentation":"<p>Number Verifier represents an application that allows to set up settings based on which the numbers verification process is executed.</p><p>While the standard number verification in Trados Studio may often be sufficient there are some occasions when a bit more control would be preferable, for example when translating documents that contain lots of numbers. This Number Verifier plug-in allows you to fine-tune settings to provide you with the desired balance between the number of false positives and potentially missed errors.</p><p>How to use</p><ul><li>Install application</li><li>Open project</li><li>Go to Project Settings -&gt; Verification -&gt; Number Verifier</li><li>Select all the needed settings</li><li>Press Ok.</li></ul><p>To have the app running, ensure that under the 'Verification' option the 'Number Verifier' is checked.</p><p>Using the Number Verifier plug-in is simple: the first four options always need to have at least one of them checked:</p><ul><li>&nbsp;&nbsp;Report added numbers</li><li>&nbsp;&nbsp;Report removed numbers</li><li>&nbsp;&nbsp;Report modified numbers</li><li>&nbsp;&nbsp;Report modified alphanumeric numbers</li><li>&nbsp;&nbsp;Report number format errors</li></ul><p>All other settings apply to one or more of these options. Once you are clear about what type of error you wish to check for you can set which segments should be checked. The options are basic and allow you to exclude segments that are locked or 100% matches, as well as segments that have no translation at all. An important point to note is that segments with content, but with the status&nbsp;<strong>”untranslated”</strong>, will not be checked if you use the&nbsp;<strong>”Exclude untranslated segments”</strong>&nbsp;option. It is assumed that segments with content will be draft at least.</p><p>You can select&nbsp;<strong>\"Exclude tag text\"</strong>&nbsp;if you find that you get duplicate error messages since the change of a number in a tag constitutes a tag change that is reported by the tag verifier.</p><p>There have also been cases where leading zeroes have caused problems in verifying numbers. To cater for this you will find an option at the bottom of the settings to&nbsp;<strong>”Omit leading zero”</strong>&nbsp;from the source and/or target.</p><p>The Number Verifier also creates a log file of the issues it has found. You can select the&nbsp;<strong>Extended</strong>&nbsp;option for Messages if you want the source and target text to be included in the log file. A nice enhancement in version 1.1.8.6 and above is to identify the numbers that are affected in the extended option. This is very useful if you have many numbers in the segment and only one or two are wrong.</p><p><strong>Alphanumerics</strong></p><p>In addition to plain numbers, the Number Verifier plug-in can also be used to find changes to alphanumeric names. For example, if VT500 has accidentally been translated as VR500 an error can be displayed. Here, an alphanumeric name is defined as a string of characters starting with one or more uppercase letters (A-Z) followed by any combination of digits (0-9) and uppercase letters. (Please note that if VT500 has been translated as VT300 this will also be identified as a modified number.)</p><p>You’ll also find an option to specify custom Alphanumeric separators. Studio will recognise alphanumerics but only under certain conditions:</p><ul><li>must not start or end with underscores, hyphens or full stops</li><li>must not contain both dashes and full stops</li><li>must contain at least one number and one letter</li><li>must not contain lowercase characters and dashes</li></ul><p>The Number Verifier allows you to specify whatever separators you wish and still verify correct transposition from the source to the target translation.</p><p><strong>Localizations</strong></p><p>Studio always uses the rules set by the Windows operating system to decide what a number should look like. In reality, life is more complex than that, so there are three options you can use and you must select one of them.</p><ul><li>Require localizations</li></ul><p>If a number in the source is identified as using one of the possible thousands or decimal separators specified for the source separators, the translation must contain corresponding numbers with any of the separators specified for target separators or else the number will be considered modified/unlocalized.</p><ul><li>Allow localizations</li></ul><p>If a number in the source is identified as using one of the possible thousands or decimal separators specified for the source separators, the translation may contain corresponding numbers with either the same separator as the source number or with any of the separators specified for target separators, or else the number will be considered modified/unlocalized.</p><ul><li>Prevent localizations</li></ul><p>The same thousands or decimal separators as in source must be retained in the translation or else the number will be considered modified.</p><ul><li>Thousands and decimal separators</li></ul><p>You can select the thousands and decimal separators you want to allow. For example, you may allow one or more thousands separators depending on language standards. Similarly, you may allow for both a period and a comma to be used as a decimal separator, in order to allow for cases where for some reason more than one language standard should be allowed. The selected separators will then be combined with the Localizations setting to determine what should be considered a modified/unlocalized number. You can also specify a custom separator to suit any requirement you may be dealing with.</p><ul><li>Hindi Numbers</li></ul><p>The option&nbsp;<strong>”Check for Hindi numbers”</strong>&nbsp;should be disabled unless you specifically wish to QA check the use of Hindi as opposed to Arabic numerals. The rules used are:</p><p>Arabic -&gt; Hindi</p><p>0 -&gt; ٠</p><p>1 -&gt; ١</p><p>2 -&gt; ٢</p><p>3 -&gt; ٣</p><p>4 -&gt; ٤</p><p>5 -&gt; ٥</p><p>6 -&gt; ٦</p><p>7 -&gt; ٧</p><p>8 -&gt; ٨</p><p>9 -&gt; ٩</p><p>10 -&gt; ١٠</p><p>The numbers are written left to right as they are for Arabic numerals but without any thousand separators. The decimal becomes a comma. So this:</p><p>1,234.89 or this 1.234,89</p><p>would be written like this:</p><p>١٢٣٤,٨٩</p><p><strong>Number Format Errors</strong></p><p>Choose to report number format errors when you want to recognize both generic and custom issues given specific settings provided by the user.</p><p><strong>ErrorDescription</strong>The group value is out of rangeWhen the group separator is used, it assumes that the values preceding it have 3 digits.Invalid group separatorThe thousand value is not expected, given the specific settings provided by the user.Invalid separator locationThe separator (e.g. decimal or thousand) char was not expected in the location found.&nbsp;An example of this is when two separators are provided one after the other without digits.Mixed group separatorsWhen the number is composed of a mixed set of thousand separatorsSeparator is not recognizedThe decimal or thousand separator is not recognized, given the specific settings provided by the user.</p><p><strong>How to run verification</strong></p><p>After all the settings were applied, the user can start translating the document. After confirming the segment(s), by pressing the F8 keyboard, the Number Verification process is executed. Once is finished, if any warning/errors were found, are all displayed in the Studio Messages window.</p><p>Show message details</p><p>Users can visualize the message details using 2 options, that can be selected from the Number Verifier Settings grid:</p><ul><li>Brief</li><li>Extended</li></ul><p>The&nbsp;<strong>\"Brief\"</strong>&nbsp;option shows the details&nbsp;which include the document name, error message with the text issue(s), the segment number where the issues were found and also the content of the Source and Target. (The description of the \"Brief\" option can be seen also if the user hovers the mouse&nbsp;over it.)</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/950x2406/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3276.pastedimage1563774160901v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">The&nbsp;</span><strong style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">\"Extended\"&nbsp;</strong><span style=\"color: rgb(71, 85, 97); background-color: rgb(244, 246, 251);\">option displays the exact text which was found as an issue in the translation. The found text is coloured, so it can be easily distinguished.&nbsp;(The description of the \"Extended\" option can be seen also if the user hovers the mouse&nbsp;over it.)</span></p><p><strong>Logging</strong></p><p>The application logs information about the flow which is useful to identify&nbsp;issues that might occur. If some errors are caught, the&nbsp;<strong>NumberVerifierLogs.txt</strong>&nbsp;file will be created at the following location:&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(0, 128, 0);\">C:\\Users\\{UserName}\\AppData\\Roaming\\RWS AppStore\\Number Verifier\\NumberVerifierLogs.txt</strong><span style=\"color: rgb(0, 128, 0);\">&nbsp;</span>and it will contain errors details.<strong>&nbsp;</strong>The file can be attached to the email / Sdl Community forum topic when a problem regarding the application is raised.</p><p><strong>Consistency Checker</strong></p><p>This checks to see whether numbers (or sequences, if at least one of them is not a number) from the source match those in the target.</p><p>Given the fact that the user makes some choices regarding the separators used as thousand and decimal separators for the source and the target separately, and also whether 0 can be omitted either in source or in target, to be able to decide whether the number in the source is the same as the number in the target, we have to:</p><ol><li>Establish whether we're dealing with numbers or just sequences of digits and other symbolsSee if the number in the source has the same value as the one in the target or whether they can be interpreted as such if they're ambiguous (considering settings)</li></ol><p>From the perspective of the user, the errors are of two kinds:</p><ol><li>Number-level errors (resulted from phase 1, above)Segment-pair-level errors&nbsp;(resulted from phase 2, above)</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/900x600/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1145.image2021_2D00_11_2D00_9_5F00_17_2D00_15_2D00_23.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>In the normalized form of the number:</strong></p><p>t - thousand separator</p><p>d - decimal separator</p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/33/versions/1790","changelog":{"Trados Toolkit","description":"<p>The Studio PowerShell Toolkit on GitHub, is a set of PowerShell scripts designed to automate tasks in Trados Studio. The toolkit interacts with Trados Studio's public API to automate various processes such as, initiating project creation and, updating translation memories with some capabilities when updating existing projects like package management.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Benefit:</strong> By automating these tasks, the toolkit helps engineers and project managers reduce manual work, minimise errors, and improve overall efficiency in managing translation projects. This is especially beneficial for teams handling large-scale localisation efforts.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Minimum Requirement:</strong> Must have a Trados Studio professional license</p>","documentation":"<p>Full instructions and user documentation is provided through the README to the <a href=\"https://github.com/RWS/Sdl-studio-powershell-toolkit\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">GitHub Repository</a> for this Toolkit.</p><p><strong><u>Trados PowerShell Toolkit Summary</u></strong></p><p><strong>Overview:</strong></p><p>The Trados PowerShell Toolkit enables automation of Trados Studio Professional's Project Automation API, requiring a Trados Studio Professional license.</p><p><strong>Toolkit Components:</strong></p><ul><li><em>Modules:</em> Includes various helpers like `GetGuids`, `PackageHelper`, `ProjectHelper`, `TMHelper`, etc.</li><li><em>Scripts. </em>Pre-configured scripts like `FileBasedProject_Roundtrip.ps1` and `ServerBasedProject_Roundtrip.ps1`.</li><li><em>Samples:</em> Demonstration files provided for practical use.</li></ul><p><strong>Getting Started:</strong></p><ul><li><em>Requirements:</em> Ensure you have a valid Trados Studio license and PowerShell 5 (x86) installed.</li><li><em>Installation:</em> Download the toolkit files, unblock them, create necessary directories, and place the scripts and modules in the specified folders.</li></ul><p><strong>Configuration:</strong></p><ul><li>Set the `$StudioVersion` parameter in the `ToolkitInitializer.psm1` file to match your Trados Studio version.</li><li>For server-based resources, configure GroupShare server details and credentials as required.</li></ul><p><strong>Running Scripts:</strong></p><ul><li>Open PowerShell as Administrator, set the execution policy if needed, and run the desired roundtrip script (e.g., `FileBasedProject_Roundtrip.ps1`).</li><li>Server-based scripts can be run similarly if GroupShare is used.</li></ul><p><strong>Module Usage.</strong></p><ul><li>Import the `ToolkitInitializer` module, then call `Import-ToolkitModules` to load all necessary modules.</li><li>Functions within the toolkit can then be utilised for various Trados Studio automation tasks.</li></ul><p><strong>Help &amp; Troubleshooting:</strong></p><ul><li>Use `Get-Help` cmdlet to view documentation for any specific function after the module is imported. Ensure the modules are correctly loaded and paths are configured properly.</li></ul><p>This summary covers the essentials of setting up, configuring, and using the Trados PowerShell Toolkit. Trados Cloud administrators and power users easier by providing productivity and automation features that are not available in the standard Trados Cloud product.</p>\r\n<p>The first version of Trados PowerTools is focused on monitoring and resolving failed tasks in translation projects. Future versions will add more features based on user feedback.</p>\r\n<h1>Key Features</h1>\r\n<h2>Failed Task Monitoring</h2>\r\n<p>Get full visibility into tasks failing in projects across your entire Trados Cloud account, without having to go to the project details of every project.</p>\r\n<p>Resolve errors by retrying tasks, or skip task that are optional and will continue failing.</p>\r\n<p>Search and filter across all task errors.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/52993d64-ec06-46f2-a2c2-3001457062cb/Resources/list.png\" alt=\"Failed Task Monitoring\"></p>\r\n<h2>Failed Task Analytics</h2>\r\n<p>Get insights into the health of your translation projects with statistics on failed tasks.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/52993d64-ec06-46f2-a2c2-3001457062cb/Resources/statistics.png\" alt=\"Failed Task Analytics\"></p>\r\n<h2>Automated Rules</h2>\r\n<p>Define rules to automatically retry or skip tasks when they fail, or cancel files. Define which tasks a rule applies to by specifying conditions.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/52993d64-ec06-46f2-a2c2-3001457062cb/Resources/rules.png\" alt=\"Automated Rules\"></p>\r\n<h2>Copy Project</h2>\r\n<p>Quickly create a new project by copying an existing project, using all the same settings. The new project is created immediately. Just add files, tweak name or other aspects and start the project. </p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/52993d64-ec06-46f2-a2c2-3001457062cb/Resources/copyproject.png\" alt=\"Copy Project\"></p>\r\n<h2>XSLT Transformation Task</h2>\r\n<p>Transform source or target language XML files as part of the workflow, using a XSLT stylesheet. Great if you need to do small tweaks or larger modifications as part of the workflow, either on source language files before file format conversion or on target language files after target file generation.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/874138e6-6c38-4e8a-b544-43c2c137a024/Resources/xslttransform.png\" alt=\"XSLT Transformation Task\"></p>\r\n<h2>And More</h2>\r\n<p>We will keep adding new features to Trados PowerTools based on user feedback. The vision for Trados PowerTools is to offer everything you need as a Trados power user, so let us know what you think about the current features and about new features that would really make your life easier. Any feedback is welcome at <a href=\"mailto:support@codingbreeze.com\">support@codingbreeze.com</a>.</p>\r\n<h1>Technical Requirements</h1>\r\n<p>Trados PowerTools is compatible with all products based on Trados Cloud, including Trados Go, Trados Freelance, Trados Ignite, Trados Team, Trados Accelerate and Trados Enterprise.</p>\r\n<p>Currently, only users with Administrator permissions can use Trados PowerTools. Based on user feedback, we will enable other user roles also.</p>\r\n<h2>Installation</h2>\r\n<p>Trados PowerTools is an add-on for Trados Cloud. Such add-ons need to be installed via the Trados Cloud AppStore.</p>\r\n<p>To open Trados PowerTools in the AppStore now, go <a href=\"https://languagecloud.sdl.com/lc/add-ons/68d64184806ab1e8b14d47dd\">here</a>.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/52993d64-ec06-46f2-a2c2-3001457062cb/Resources/install.png\" alt=\"Install\"></p>\r\n<p>Now, click Install. You will need to provide a CodingBreeze activation key. But there is also an API with this allowing developers to add to the &quot;Tell Me&quot; functionality with other useful things.</p>\r\n<p> The <strong>QuickInfo plugin</strong> does just that by extending the default &quot;Tell Me&quot; functionality to provide various useful bits of information such as unit conversion, maths calculation, airport codes, Unicode description and more.</p>\r\n<p> The plugin is also <a href=\"https://github.com/cromica/trados-studio-quickinfo\">opensouorced</a> providing a great tool for other developers as they can see how this functionality is used, or even contribute to this one with new bits of helpful information to extend its usefulness.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: &quot;<a href=\"https://www.trados.com/products/trados-studio/tell-me.html?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1705403525272.1705412481483.195&amp;__hssc=110027290.10.1705412481483&amp;__hsfp=467677067\">Tell Me</a>&quot; functionality,\u00A0which helps you find features in Trados Studio.\u00A0</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5040.Tell-Me.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Another benefit of this functionality, is that it includes an API, which allows developers to add to the functionality of Trados Studio &#39;Tell Me&#39;</p>\r\n<p>The\u00A0<strong>QuickInfo plugin</strong>\u00A0does just that, by extending the default &quot;Tell Me&quot; functionality to provide various useful bits of information such as unit conversion, maths calculation, airport codes, Unicode description and more.</p>\r\n<p>Simply click on Tell Me and start your search query with &#39;@info&#39; and then the additional query- for example-</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5037.info-examples.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>This is really useful when you need a quick calculation/conversion whilst translating, without the need to open your browser.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p><strong>Trados QuickInfo</strong>\u00A0supports\u00A0for example-</p>\r\n<p><em><strong>Unit conversions:</strong></em></p>\r\n<p>Weight: pounds to kg and vice versa (190 pounds, 200lb, 43 kg in lb)</p>\r\n<p>Temperature: fahrenheit/celsius (75f, 75 f, 75 fahrenheit, 23c, 23 c, 23 c to f)</p>\r\n<p>Distance: 12 miles, 100 miles in km</p>\r\n<p>Speed</p>\r\n<p>Volume</p>\r\n<p>Area: 1670 sq.ft</p>\r\n<p>Fuel efficiency (29mpg)</p>\r\n<p><em><strong>Curency conversion.</strong></em></p>\r\n<p>The following currencies are supported: EUR, GBP, USD, RUB, CZK, ILS, CAD, CHF, RON, AUD, PLN, HUF, JPY, CNY</p>\r\n<p><em><strong>Hex/decimal</strong></em>\u00A0(just type a number)</p>\r\n<p><em><strong>Colours</strong></em></p>\r\n<p>List of colors: color, colors</p>\r\n<p>Color conversions - convert an RGB triplet to Hex color or hex color to RGB (23 145 175, #eeaaf0)</p>\r\n<p><em><strong>Misc.</strong></em></p>\r\n<p>Factor an integer 2520</p>\r\n<p>Generate a random Guid guid</p>\r\n<p>My IP address ip</p>\r\n<p>Math/arithmetic expressions (24 * 365) * 4 - 1, 22/7 - pi, rnd, Random(100)</p>\r\n<p>Quick sum/average/product of a list of numbers, sort a list (separated by space or comma) 3 7 21 3 3 2 11</p>\r\n<p>Unicode: type a codepoint to see what char it is: U+2021 \\u3333</p>\r\n<p>Url decode: 2%20%2B%203 shows the original string (fun exercise: 2%2525252520%252525252B%25252525205</p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<p>The plugin is also\u00A0<a href=\"https://github.com/cromica/trados-studio-quickinfo\">opensouorced</a>\u00A0providing Studio Target Renamer","description":"<p><em><u>The Problem</u></em></p><p>You have many files in your translation project, and you are delivering the target files for review before the project is complete. So you want rename the target files to reflect their status as unfinished deliverables for review only. You have a multilingual project with many languages and you want the target files to be unique with the language code added to the filename of each one. At least two problems related to the naming of target files generated by Trados Studio.</p><p><br></p><p><em><u>The Solution</u></em></p><p>The <strong>Trados Studio Target Renamer</strong> solves these problems, and probably others, by providing a new batch task supporting the features you need:</p><ul><li>use short or long language codes as a suffix or prefix to the original filenames</li><li>use regular expressions to rename the filenames with whatever you like, but retain enough of the original filename to ensure it's recognisable</li><li>save the target files into a location of your choosing</li></ul><p>To learn how to use this application, please check the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/73?tab=documentation\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Documentation </a>tab.</p>","documentation":"<p>How does it work?</p><p>Once the plugin is installed you will find a new batch task in your list:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2311.pastedimage1627660785616v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The batch task contains various options:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2110.pastedimage1627660845542v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Location</strong></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2275.pastedimage1627660920742v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This option allows you to specify the folder you would like to use to save the target files that are created when you run this task.&nbsp;If you don't specify a custom location the files will be saved into the appropriate target language folders for your project.&nbsp;If your project is a multilingual project then the custom location will honour the target language folders to&nbsp;maintain the organisation of the files.&nbsp;For example, in my test project I have four target languages and using this custom location will create a structure like this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3716.pastedimage1627661183727v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Select Action</strong></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8244.pastedimage1627661293955v5.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>You have three options here:</p><p><em>Append As Suffix</em></p><p>This will add the language code to the end of the filename, and separated by the \"Delimiter\" you choose in these options.&nbsp;For example:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8053.pastedimage1627661469423v6.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The full language code for German(Germany) was added to the end of the filenames.</p><p>.</p><p><em>Append As Prefix</em></p><p>This will add the language code to the&nbsp;start of the filename, and separated by the \"Delimiter\" you choose in these options.&nbsp;For example:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7127.pastedimage1627661617231v7.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The full language code for German(Germany) was added to the&nbsp;start of the filenames.</p><p>.</p><p><em>Use Regular Expression</em></p><p>Selecting this option will introduce the necessary input boxes for you to search for the part of the filenames you wish to work with and then replace some of the text with your preferred naming:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8836.pastedimage1627661767415v8.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Using the same source files as an example:</p><p>IP-13-637_EN.docx</p><p>IP-14-138_EN.docx</p><p>You need to be careful not to replace the entire name with new text because if you do this you will end up with one target file for each language.&nbsp;This is because the name of each one will be exactly the same and as they are saved they will overwrite each other.&nbsp;You also don't want the file names to be the same as you won't be able to tell them apart!</p><p>So the way to use this feature is to use back references.&nbsp;For example, search for:</p><p>IP-(\\d{2}-\\d{3})_EN</p><p>And replace with this:</p><p>REVIEW_$1</p><p>This will result in the following:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8270.pastedimage1627662241471v9.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The numbering is retained as I wanted, and the names are prefixed with REVIEW_.&nbsp;This does require some basic knowledge of how to work with regular expressions, but if you need help please ask in the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/translationproductivity/f/regex_and_xpath\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong>appropriate community forum</strong></a>.&nbsp;To explain this example:</p><p>Search</p><ul><li>the search uses a simple pattern to find filenames containing 2 numbers followed by&nbsp;a hyphen followed by 3&nbsp;numbers:</li><li>\\d{2}-\\d{3}</li><li>enclosing this pattern in round brackets creates what's called a back reference</li><li>(\\d{2}-\\d{3})</li><li>this essentially means remember the result of the pattern you have found so it can be reused later</li><li>IP- and _EN and simply the actual text used in the filenames and they won't be remembered which means the replace operation will delete them</li></ul><p>Replace</p><ul><li>the replace specifies the actual text to be used in the replace operation.</li><li>so it will replace the pattern found with the text REVIEW_</li><li>followed by what it remembered and stored a a back reference.&nbsp;The recall syntax is simply $1.</li><li>if there were more patterns enclosed in brackets they are recalled sequentially starting from the left as $1, $2, $3 etc.&nbsp;In this example we only had one back reference so we only need to use $1</li></ul><p>As for the other options the files will be grouped by target language so you can easily manage sending the files in an organised manner.</p><p>.</p><p><em>Use Target Language</em></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8345.pastedimage1627663590048v12.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This option simply allows you to use short or long language codes in the filenames.&nbsp;For example:</p><p><strong>de</strong>&nbsp;OR&nbsp;<strong>de-DE</strong></p><p><strong>es</strong>&nbsp;OR&nbsp;<strong>es-ES</strong></p><p><strong>en</strong>&nbsp;OR&nbsp;<strong>en-US</strong></p><p>.</p><p><em>Append Custom String</em></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8688.pastedimage1627662974633v10.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This option allows a simple mechanism to use your own text as a prefix or suffix to the target filenames.&nbsp;Using this example I should see this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0042.pastedimage1627663493654v11.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>.</p><p><em>Delimiter</em></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6735.pastedimage1627663746150v13.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This option allows you to choose the delimiter, or string of delimiters, that you wish to use to separate the custom string from the filename.</p><p>.</p><p><span style=\"background-color: rgb(255, 255, 153);\">Known Issues</span></p><p>The initial release of this app fails to use the \"Append Custom String\" feature correctly.&nbsp;It doesn't work.</p><p>The workaround for now is to use the regular expression option where you can achieve the same thing by searching for the full name, storing in a back reference, and replacing with the appropriate text followed by the back reference recall.&nbsp;For example:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2816.pastedimage1627664056219v14.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>So search for this:</p><p>(.+)</p><p>Replace with:</p><p>\"appropriate text\"_$1</p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/73/versions/1589","changelog":{"Trados user(s) to export the SDL Trados Studio Analysis report into the CSV format using the selected Studio project(s). The application is compatible with Studio 2017 and Studio 2019.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>How to use</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Install the plugin</li>\r\n<li>Open Trados Studio</li>\r\n<li>Select project(s) which contains analysis reports. If the selected project(s) does not have the &quot;<em>Analysis Files</em>&quot; batch task executed, the project(s) will not be used for the export process and the below message will be displayed:</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/800x900/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5861.pastedimage1588752671887v1.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Right click on the selected project(s)</li>\r\n<li>Select &quot;<em><strong>Export Analysis Reports</strong></em>&quot; option (using this option, user will have automatically the selected projects checked in the &quot;<em>Export Analysis Reports</em>&quot; window)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1100x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3652.pastedimage1585057953862v1.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Note</strong>: The application can be started also from the &quot;<strong>Add-Ins</strong>&quot; tab. (using this option, user can select the needed project from the list of projects loaded from Studio)</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1100x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5353.pastedimage1585058397932v2.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Select the target language for which the analyse files will be exported into .csv format</li>\r\n<li>Select an output folder where the files will be created. (also additional options can be selected, see the below print scree).</li>\r\n<li>Press on the &quot;<strong>Export to CSV</strong>&quot; button</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1667.pastedimage1588751602259v7.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6283.pastedimage1588751626230v8.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>The report output path will be automatically saved locally when pressing on the &quot;<strong>Export to CSV</strong>&quot; button (user can select anytime a new path wich will be automatically saved locally). The path is saved locally at the following path:\u00A0<strong>C:\\Users{UserName}\\AppData\\Roaming\\RWS AppStore\\TradosExportAnalysisReports\\ExportAnalysisReportsSettings.json</strong>.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1100x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7510.pastedimage1585228342222v1.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Include single file projects</p>\r\n<p>User is allowed to add within the Export Analysis Reports app, the single file project(s) from Studio, using the new feature: &quot;<em><strong>Include single file projects</strong></em>&quot; option.</p>\r\n<p>The single file projects can be included using 2 modes:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>From Project&#39;s view -&gt; Right click on the single file project -&gt; Select the\u00A0&quot;<em>Export Analysis Reports</em>&quot; option</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4848.pastedimage1588751896093v9.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4113.pastedimage1588751998736v12.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>From &quot;<em>Add-Ins</em>&quot; tab -&gt; Click on &quot;<em>Export Analysis Reports</em>&quot; button -&gt; Check &quot;<em><strong>Include single file projects</strong></em>&quot; option</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0184.pastedimage1588752238293v13.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Load external - Clear external/Refresh projects</p>\r\n<p>User can load an external project and use it to generates reports. The projects can be removed from the list using the &quot;Clear external/Refresh projects&quot; button which will re-populate the Studio projects back to the list.</p>\r\n<p>Note:\u00A0Studio loads the external project(s) inside the projects view, which can be used afterward within the Export Analysis Reports application, only after re-opening Trados Studio.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0250.pastedimage1588751353774v3.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5875.pastedimage1588751398991v4.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p>Besides removing the external projects, the &quot;Clear external / Refresh projects&quot; button also reloads the projects on the UI and configure back\u00A0the &quot;Project status&quot; selection to &quot;All&quot;.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/900x1000/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2313.pastedimage1588751047203v2.png\" alt=\"\"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Note:</strong>\u00A0In case the batch task &quot;Analyse Files&quot; was not executed on the\u00A0project which user wants to load as external, the following message will be displayed (same message is displayed when user wants to open the project within the Projects view -&gt; Clipboard</h1>\r\n<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>Trados T-Window for Clipboard is a lightweight utility designed to streamline translation workflows by allowing you to quickly translate clipboard content using RWS Translation Memories (TMs) – no need to launch Trados Studio. It’s ideal for translators who need fast, convenient access to TMs while working in other applications.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6505.pastedimage1599481269272v2.png\" alt=\"Application main screen with T-Window interface open and a highlighted clipboard section\"></p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Clipboard Translation</strong>: Translate any copied content instantly using your connected RWS TM.</li>\r\n<li><strong>No Studio Required</strong>: Works independently of Trados Studio, so you can work in any environment.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Quick Access</strong>: Easily accessible via a desktop shortcut once installed.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Simple TM Integration</strong>: Add and enable TMs in just a few clicks.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Supported OS</strong>: Windows 10 and above</li>\r\n<li><strong>Dependencies</strong>: RWS Translation Memory (TM) access</li>\r\n<li><strong>Permissions</strong>: Requires access to clipboard content and selected TMs</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Getting Started</h2>\r\n<h3>1.
