var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: black;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(even) {\r\n    background-color: var(--table-striped);\r\n    color: white;\r\n  }\r\n  \r\n  table tr:nth-child(odd) {\r\n    background-color: white;\r\n    color: var(--table-striped);\r\n  }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>This application provides a simple way to back up your Translation Memories and Termbases on a regular basis to help you ensure that you always have a backup available to you in the event of these precious resources getting corrupted.\u00A0The application is a plugin which allows you to set up as many backup tasks as you like and run them on demand, or as scheduled backups using the Windows Task Scheduler.\u00A0It&#39;s important to note that this is no substitute for you getting into the routine of regularly exporting to a simple text based format so even if you don&#39;t notice that your resources have corrupted you won&#39;t have to rely on the backup which could contain the corrupt resource:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>SDLTM Export to TMX once a week is a good idea (best carried out through the TM View in Studio)</li>\r\n<li>SDLTB export to MultiTerm XDT and XML (easiest way to do this is with the Glossary Converter and a drag and drop)</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>NOTE:</strong> <strong>When backing up your resources, we suggest you schedule this, at a time when you are NOT working with the specific resources.</strong></p>\r\n<h2>Where can I find the Trados TMBackup plugin?</h2>\r\n<p>Once installed from the RWS AppStore, the plugin can be found in the Add-Ins menu.</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0677.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0677.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>When you click the icon for TM Backup in the Add-Ins ribbon for the first time, you&#39;ll be presented with this screen:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6837.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6837.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><em><strong>File</strong></em>\u00A0: select the available options/tasks using a File -&gt; menu command<em><strong>Create new task</strong></em>\u00A0: use this to create your backup task<em><strong>Run manual tasks</strong></em>\u00A0: this\u00A0button allows you to manually run a backup whenever you choose if you didn&#39;t create them using a scheduled backup<strong>Run disabled tasks</strong>\u00A0: if you have disabled any of the tasks in the Windows Task Scheduler you can either enable them in the Task Scheduler or click this handy\u00A0button to do them all at once<em><strong>Refresh view</strong></em>\u00A0: this button refreshes the screen.\u00A0If you deleted any tasks in the Windows Scheduler while the app is open then refreshing the screen allows the changes to be seen<em><strong>Open Windows Task Scheduler</strong></em>\u00A0: this is a quick link to open your Windows Task Scheduler if you wish to edit, disable or delete the scheduled tasks you have created<em><strong>Help</strong></em>\u00A0: clicking on here takes you to this wiki page so we can easily enhance the help with your feedback to make it easier to understand</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h2>Steps to create a backup task</h2>\r\n<h3>Create a new task</h3>\r\n<p>Click on File and select the option to\u00A0<em><strong>Create new backup</strong></em>: or just use the icon as shown:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7360.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7360.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<h3>Backup\u00A0details</h3>\r\n<p>You&#39;re going to complete the details in the next screen so they will appear something like this:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4520.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4520.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<p>You can see from this that there are 6 fields to complete which are described below.\u00A0The task in the image above would appear like this in the TM Backup list:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4338.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4338.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<p>The image above shows three tasks that have been created:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>a backup task that runs every 7-days automaticallya backup task that runs every 24-hrs automaticallya backup task that runs whenever it is manually triggerred</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p>And in your Windows Task Scheduler like this where the Task name is prefixed with\u00A0<em><strong>TMBackupTask</strong></em>\u00A0so they are easy to find (note that the windows task scheduler can sometimes take a little while to start depending on how often you use it):</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4137.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4137.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<h4>Backup name</h4>\r\n<p>This is mandatory, and it can&#39;t be edited afterwards.\u00A0It&#39;s used to identify the name in Studio and it&#39;s appended to TMBackupTask in the windows task scheduler.\u00A0So if you don&#39;t like the name you used then delete the task and recreate it</p>\r\n<h4>Backup from</h4>\r\n<p>Select the folder used to store your resources.\u00A0You can&#39;t select specific files, only the folder containing the files.</p>\r\n<h4>Backup to</h4>\r\n<p>This should be the folder you wish to backup to.\u00A0The folder you are backing up from will be recreated in\u00A0this location.</p>\r\n<h4>Description</h4>\r\n<p>This is just a description of your backup task to help you remember why you did it.\u00A0It is only saved in the Trados Studio interface and will be seen when opening up the tasks individually by double clicking them.</p>\r\n<h4>What to backup</h4>\r\n<p>The tool can back up anything you like based on the extension you use, or it can back up everything inside the selected folder by not adding an action at all:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1307.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1307.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<p>If you want to create an action to backup a specific type of file then the action name and the type of file fields are free text, so you can type whatever you like in there.\u00A0The Pattern however must follow the rules in brackets which are a period followed by the file extension.\u00A0So for translation memory files you would write this:</p>\r\n<p>.sdltm</p>\r\n<p>For termbase files this:</p>\r\n<p>.sdltb</p>\r\n<p>Click\u00A0<em><strong>Add</strong></em>\u00A0after entering each action and then\u00A0<em><strong>Ok</strong></em>\u00A0to save the entry.</p>\r\n<h4>When to backup</h4>\r\n<p>There are two options for scheduling:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3007.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3007.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<p>So you can set a scheduled time by selecting\u00A0<em><strong>Every</strong></em>\u00A0or you can choose the run the backups manually whenever you use the manual tasks option:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4341.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4341.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<p>If you want to schedule an automatic backup then use the\u00A0<em><strong>Every</strong></em>\u00A0option and set the time in hours or minutes as you see fit.\u00A0Here the task is scheduled to run every 7 days on a Monday at 6pm:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3426.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/940x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3426.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<h2>Other features</h2>\r\n<p>There are two other icons in the toolbar that are worth knowing about:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Run disabled tasks</li>\r\n<li>Refresh View</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Run disabled tasks</h3>\r\n<p>It is possible to disable tasks in the Windows Task Scheduler.\u00A0If you have done this for any reason then you can use this feature to enable them again without having to start launch the windows task scheduler.\u00A0</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0675.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0675.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<p>It&#39;s also a useful feature because you can see which tasks have been disabled in the Studio view:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0841.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0841.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<h3>Refresh view</h3>\r\n<p>This feature allows you to refresh the view and see any changes that you may have made through the windows task scheduler and avoid having to restart the application:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4743.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4743.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<h3>Open Windows Task Scheduler</h3>\r\n<p>The windows task scheduler can be launched through windows, but as this may be a feature many users are not aware of we have added an icon to the toolbar to launch the scheduler directly from Trados Studio:</p>\r\n<p><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/cfs-file/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4442.png\"><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/600x0/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4442.png\" alt=\" \"></a></p>\r\n<h1>Recommendations</h1>\r\n<p><strong>The most important thing is. that you do backup your resources.</strong>\u00A0</p>\r\n<p>Translation Memories and Termbases in Studio/MultiTerm are fairly robust but like any file on a computer they can still become corrupt under some circumstances -</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>for example sharing file-based resources simultaneously,</li>\r\n<li>security software interfering with read/write operations,</li>\r\n<li>use of external drives that lose connection etc.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>So to ensure you are able to sleep peacefully, the recommendation would be this:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>set up regular backups of your resources, at least once a week.\u00A0Anything between, you can probably recover from your active projects.</li>\r\n<li>export your backups to a flat file format like TMX on a regular basis</li>\r\n<li>check your Translation Memories once a month with an integrity check using\u00A0<a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/sdltm-repair/298/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1705403525272.1705412481483.195&amp;__hssc=110027290.9.1705412481483&amp;__hsfp=467677067\"><em><strong>TM Repair</strong></em></a>\u00A0from the RWS AppStore</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>TellMe</p>\r\n<p>Trados TMBackup Studio 2021/2019 version contains the TellMe feature, which allows you to search quickly for the plugin available actions: eg</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>navigate to store from where the Trados TMBackup can be downloaded,</li>\r\n<li>navigate to Wiki page,</li>\r\n<li>navigate to RWS Community forum.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>You can easily use TellMe feature in Trados Studio by typing Trados TMBackup word in the search box, and all available results will be displayed (see below print screen):</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4061.TellMe.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Logging</p>\r\n<p>The application logs information about the flow which is useful to identify\u00A0issues that might occur.</p>\r\n<p>The Trados<strong>TMBackupLogs.txt</strong>file can be found at the following location:\u00A0</p>\r\n<p>C:\\Users\\yourname\\AppData\\Roaming\\RWS AppStore\\Trados TM Backup</p>\r\n<h3>Technical Requirements</h3>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n management"}]},{"id":93,"downloadCount":2782,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2019-10-30T10:32:18.977Z","updatedAt":"2025-01-12T15:52:59.298698Z","name":"Trados TTS","description":"<p><strong>Trados TTS</strong> provides two new features for Trados Studio:</p><p><br></p><p>1. Text to Speech capability</p><p>2. Footpedal support</p><p><br></p><p><br></p><p><strong><em>Text to Speech</em></strong></p><p><strong>Trados TTS</strong> (Text to Speech) is a plugin which allows you to listen to the audio of a segment (source or target) or to the entire file.</p><p>We use the Microsoft Azure Text to Speech solution which is available through their Cognitive Services to generate the audio file, and through this we offer many features:</p><p>- set a default to play source OR target</p><p>- use keyboard shortcuts to toggle between source/target</p><p>- use keyboard shortcuts for common requirements when using Text to Speech</p><p>- change the reading speed</p><p>- change the reading voice (based on what is offerred by Microsoft)</p><p>- map unsupported languages to be able to play any variant of a supported language</p><p>- control what's read when working with track changes</p><p><br></p><p><strong><em>Footpedal Support</em></strong></p><p>The plugin currently only supports footpedals using the USB HID driver supplied by VEC.</p><p>An example would be the Infinity USB Foot Pedal (IN-USB-2) which is easily obtained through Amazon for example. However, if you have a footpedal that supports programmable inputs then you could map it to the keyboard shorcuts in Studio.</p><p>The main actions provided by the supported footpedals are:</p><p><br></p><p><strong><em>Right pedal</em></strong> - move to the next segment. If you hold the pedal down you will navigate through the segments automatically until you release the pedal.</p><p><strong><em>Left pedal</em></strong> - move to the previous segment. If you hold the pedal down you will navigate through the segments automatically until you release the pedal.</p><p><strong><em>Middle pedal</em></strong> - Play/ Pause the sound. To hear the audio playback press and hold the middle pedal. To pause the audio simply release the foot pedal.</p>","documentation":"<h4>This plugin runs on Studio 2019 SR2 or higher.</h4><h2>Introduction</h2><p>Trados TTS (Text to Speech) is a plugin which allows you to listen to the audio of a segment (source or target) or to the entire file.</p><p>We use the<a href=\"https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/services/cognitive-services/text-to-speech/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">&nbsp;Micosoft Azure Text&nbsp;to Speech</a>&nbsp;solution which is available through their Cognitive Services to generate the audio file.</p><h2>What is the Cognitive Text to Speech service</h2><p>This a is&nbsp;<strong>paid service</strong>&nbsp;provided by Microsoft,&nbsp;currently&nbsp;the first 5 million characters /month are free.</p><p>To use it ,you'll have to create an Azure account and generate the&nbsp;<strong>API key in order to use it in the TTS plugin.</strong></p><p>More details about the service billing can be found&nbsp;<a href=\"https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/cognitive-services/speech-services/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">here</a>.</p><h2>How to generate the API key</h2><p>After you create an Azure account, please log in into the Azure Portal and search for&nbsp;<strong>\"Cognitive services\"</strong>; then<strong>&nbsp;</strong>from&nbsp;<strong>Marketplace</strong>&nbsp;select&nbsp;<strong>\"Cognitive Services\":</strong></p><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/803x209/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3858.Marketplace.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><p>A new page will appear where you need to enter details for your Subscription, Location and Resource group (if you don't already have a resource group you'll have to create one).</p><p><strong>The location of the service is very important, please choose a location near you because this will affect the speed of the TTS plugin.</strong></p><h2>Where I can see the API key</h2><p>After the service was created successfully in the&nbsp;<strong>Azure Portal</strong>&nbsp;select the Service by clicking on its name&nbsp;-&gt;<strong>&nbsp;Quick Start</strong></p><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/944x392/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8030.quick.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><p>The API key will be used for the TTS plugin.</p><h2>How&nbsp;to open TTS Plugin</h2><p>Trados TTS can be opened in the following ways:</p><h4>&nbsp;Editor View -&gt; Add-ins tab -&gt; Trados TTS&nbsp;</h4><p>By default the TTS plugin is minimized. A bar will appear at the top of the screen. When you hover on the bar the action options will be expanded:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1124x143/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6557.bar.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>When you&nbsp;hover over the bar it will display like this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/313x45/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2402.hover2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h4>&nbsp;Using Tell Me</h4><p>In the<strong>&nbsp;Files View</strong>&nbsp;<strong>&nbsp;select the files&nbsp;</strong>you want to&nbsp;use&nbsp;Trados TTS with, activate \"Tell Me\" and type TTS into the entry field.&nbsp;Finally, select&nbsp;<strong>\"Open TTS\"</strong>&nbsp;from the search results.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/929x293/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5822.tellme.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The selected files will be opened in the Studio Editor and the&nbsp;Trados TTS plugin will be available at the top of the screen. The next step is to configure the settings by clicking on the Settings icon.</p><h2>The&nbsp;Trados TTS Settings view</h2><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/603x483/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3603.Settings3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Account options</strong></p><p>The Account options is where you will add the API key generated in the Azure portal. You also need to choose the Account region you selected when you created the account.</p><p><strong>The&nbsp;Selected Account region&nbsp;in the TradosTTS plugin must match the region from Azure Portal.&nbsp;</strong></p><p><br></p><p><strong>Audio options</strong></p><p>\"<em>Playback all segments\"</em>&nbsp;is unchecked by default. If you want to&nbsp;<strong>generate an audio file for the whole document</strong>&nbsp;(source or target segments) then check this option. Note that this could take a litle while for large files.</p><p>The audio file is saved locally in the following folder structure:&nbsp;<strong>Project Name -&gt; Language Pair -&gt; FileName.wav</strong>&nbsp;file at:&nbsp;</p><p><strong>C:\\Users\\[username]\\AppData\\Roaming\\RWS AppStore\\Trados&nbsp;TTS\\Playback</strong>&nbsp;<strong>audio</strong>&nbsp;local path</p><p>\"<em>Source/Target playback</em>\"&nbsp;<em>&nbsp;</em>allows you to select whether you wish to listen to the text for the source or target segments.</p><p>\"<em>Available devices</em>\" allows you to select a footpedal from the list of supported devices.</p><p>The first device available in the list will be selected by default. Note that the Trados&nbsp;TTS plugin&nbsp;<em>only supports footpedals using the&nbsp;USB HID driver supplied by VEC</em>.&nbsp;</p><p>An example would be the Infinity USB Foot Pedal (IN-USB-2).</p><h2>How to&nbsp;listen to the audio using the audio buttons</h2><p><strong>Playback speed can be changed any time using</strong>&nbsp;\"Speed\" button. By default is set to 1. If you hover on the button a tooltip will appear with the selected speed.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/350x76/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4135.sp.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>To change the speed click on \"Speed\" button and from dropdown select the speed:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/311x115/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4834.sp2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Select the segment you wish to listen to in the Editor and press \"Play\" .</p><p>A loading window will appear on-screen and the Play button will be disabled.</p><p>After the audio file is received from Microsoft&nbsp;the loading message will disappear and the audio will start automatically.</p><p>Typically, this is quite fast, but for long sentences you might have a lag for a few seconds.&nbsp;</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1155x566/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8737.pldis.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>After the audio starts you can P<strong>ause</strong>&nbsp;the playback at any time.&nbsp;</p><p>When the playback is paused the \"<strong>Play\"&nbsp;</strong>button will be enabled, and you&nbsp;will be able to&nbsp;resume the playback from&nbsp;the position you&nbsp;paused it.&nbsp;</p><p>Alternatively you can use&nbsp;<strong>\"Replay\"</strong>&nbsp;and this will restart the audio from the begining.</p><p><strong>If you click on \"Stop\" the audio will&nbsp;not be available anymore. If you want to hear the segment again, you'll have to click on \"Play\".</strong></p><p><strong>This means we'll make another request to the Microsoft service, and you'll be charged with the number of characters used.</strong></p><p>If you want to listen to another segment, just navigate to it in the Editor and click \"Play\".</p><h2>How interact with the plugin using&nbsp;Footpedals</h2><p><strong>Right</strong>&nbsp;pedal - move to the&nbsp;<strong>next segment</strong>. If you hold the pedal down, you will navigate through the segments automatically until you release the pedal.</p><p><strong>Left</strong>&nbsp;pedal - move&nbsp;to the&nbsp;<strong>previous segment</strong>. If you hold the pedal down, you will navigate through the segments automatically until you release the pedal.</p><p><strong>Middle</strong>&nbsp;pedal -&nbsp;<strong>Play/ Pause</strong>&nbsp;the sound. To hear the audio playback press and hold the middle pedal.&nbsp;&nbsp;To pause the&nbsp;audio&nbsp;simply release the footpedal.</p><h2>How to interact with the plugin using&nbsp;Studio shortcuts</h2><p>There are no default shortcuts, so these must be set by each user in order to become available in the plugin.</p><p><strong>When you set a key combination, please make sure it is not already used in Studio, as there may not be a warning.</strong></p><p><strong>If the shortcut fails to work, the most likely reason is duplicate shortcuts.</strong></p><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6354.TTS.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><p>An additional piece of&nbsp;functionality provided by the shortcuts is to :</p><ol><li><strong>\"Confirm and play the translation\".&nbsp;</strong>The<strong>&nbsp;</strong>selected segment will be confirmed and the text in the segment will be read back to you automatically.<strong>\"Confirm and move to the next untranslated segment\".</strong>&nbsp;The selected segment will be confirmed and the source of the next untranslated segment will be read back to you automatically</li></ol><h2>Language Mapping&nbsp;Feature</h2><p>At this point Microsoft Text to Speech Service supports 45 languages.&nbsp;TTS language mapping view allows the user to&nbsp;<strong>select another language variant for the active project in the Editor.</strong></p><p>When a file is open in the Editor, if there&nbsp;are any unsupported languages in the project, this window will appear.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/521x400/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3060.LangMapping.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The Language table will be autopopulated with the unsupported languages detected in the active project.&nbsp;</p><p>The Supported Languages dropdown list is populated with the Supported&nbsp;variants provided by Microsoft Cognitive services.</p><p><strong>Save language mapping option</strong></p><p>By default, this option is unchecked, if you&nbsp;<strong>check this option</strong>&nbsp;the next time you open the project in the Editor,</p><p>the&nbsp;<strong>Languages Mapping view will not be displayed any more</strong>&nbsp;and the languages selected previously will be used by the TTS.</p><h3>How to edit the language mappings</h3><p>If the \"save language mappings\" option is checked when you select a different variant you&nbsp;won't&nbsp;see the mappings View again,&nbsp;<strong>until you remove the mappings</strong>&nbsp;for the active project.&nbsp;</p><p>This option is availabe in the&nbsp;<strong>\"Settings\"</strong>&nbsp;view:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/414x119/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8228.langR.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>After you click on the Remove option a confirmation message will appear. After the form is closed by clicking on the \"OK\" button, the language mappings view will automatically appear.&nbsp;</p><p>If all the languages for the active project are supported by Microsoft this option&nbsp;<strong>will be disabled</strong>:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/414x119/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8838.langS.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Language Mapping option in Files View</strong></p><p>This option&nbsp;<strong>will not be visible</strong>&nbsp;when you open the \"Settings\" page from Files Controller&nbsp;<strong>unless one file from the selected project is open in the editor.</strong></p><p>Then you can edit the language mappings for all unsupported languages and the changes will also be reflected in the Editor View.</p><h3>Log file path</h3><p>The plugin has a logging system which logs the errors in a text file. These can be found at the following location:</p><h3>C:\\Users\\[username]\\AppData\\Roaming\\RWS AppStore\\Trados TTS\\Trados TTS Logs</h3><p>Should you wish to report an error in the RWS community, please save the log files and attach to the community Transcreate","description":"<p><strong>The Problem</strong></p><p>Transcreation isn't a process that obviously lends itself to a translation tool, mainly because of the creative nature of the text production in other languages to the source. Certainly terminology, concordance and other reference tools that may be available to you within a translation tool can help, but you're not going to get a lot of leverage in the way you would with a technical manual or source material like that. But in addition to the obvious linguistic challenges, the process of transcreation is something that also requires quite some administrative and disciplined management. You might have to take your source copy, provide multiple transcreations, into multiple languages, and then manage a back translation process too... finally pulling together a report that you can provide to your client with all of the transcreations, reasoning for the choice of words, back translations etc. and your recommendations for which transcreations should be used. Not insurmountable as we've doing this for decades... but still leaves room for improvement.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>The Solution</strong></p><p>The Trados Transcreate plugin for Trados Studio plays on the strengths of the project management side of a translation tool, or translation environment. It helps you to manage the overall process from receiving the original source copy and having to create the projects for each language with multiple source files for each alternative you've been asked for. It even supports a Microsoft Word workflow for when you are dealing with transcreationists who don't use a translation tool at all.</p>","documentation":"<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/127x118/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7875.sdl_5F00_transcreate_5F00_view.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This wiki will continue to evolve with full details of how to use the application.&nbsp;In the meantime you&nbsp;may find this video useful, from the Trados Spring 2021 virtual conference, as it runs through&nbsp;the overall process:</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>Definitions</strong></h1><h2><strong>What is Transcreation:</strong></h2><p><a href=\"https://www.google.com/search?q=transcreate+definition&amp;rlz=1C1CHBF_en-GBGB876GB876&amp;oq=Transcreate&amp;aqs=chrome.1.0l2j0i10i433l3j69i60l3.5703j0j7&amp;sourceid=chrome&amp;ie=UTF-8\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">WIKI</a>&nbsp;describes Transcreation as a concept used in the field of translation studies to describe the process of adapting a message from one language to another, while maintaining its intent, style, tone, and context</p><p>Often this includes Back-Translations</p><h2><strong>What are Back-Translations</strong></h2><p>&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.google.com/search?q=back+transaltion&amp;rlz=1C1CHBF_en-GBGB876GB876&amp;oq=back+transaltion+&amp;aqs=chrome..69i57j0i13l9.3214j0j7&amp;sourceid=chrome&amp;ie=UTF-8\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">WIKI:</a>&nbsp;Back translation&nbsp;is defined as: a procedure whereby a&nbsp;translator&nbsp;(or team of translators) interpret or re-translate&nbsp;a document that was previously&nbsp;translated&nbsp;into another language,&nbsp;back&nbsp;to the original language.</p><h1><strong>Overview</strong></h1><h2><strong>What does the Trados Transcreation App do?</strong>&nbsp;</h2><p>The app allows for users to convert their existing Trados Studio Projects into a Trados Transcreation Project. Transcreated Projects are unique in that they cater for numerus alternative translations as well as back translations, which are often required when adapting this type of material from one language to another.</p><h2><strong>Prerequisites</strong></h2><p>In order for the Transcreate application to work correctly you must also install the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/reports-viewer-plus/1120/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1700467239832.1700484259765.59&amp;__hssc=110027290.1.1700484259765&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\"><strong>Reports Viewer Plus</strong></a>&nbsp;application from the appstore. This requirement will be removed in the future once Trados Studio has been updated to accommodate the improved Reporting API that has been developed to support the Transcreate application.</p><h2><strong>Supported Workflows</strong>&nbsp;</h2><p>Workflows supported are both Internal and done within Trados Studio, and/or External, meaning MS Word is the users preferred environment for completing their Transcreated task.</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6433.pastedimage1616428489622v2.png\" alt=\" \"><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p><h3><strong>Workflow Phases</strong></h3><p><strong>General steps required include:</strong></p><ol><li>Bilingual Excel file(s) used to create a Trados Studio projectTrados Studio project is converted into a transcreation projectFiles are then Transcreated &amp; ReviewedBack Translations can be managed either as part of:the initial/external transcreation workflowor as a second focused back translation project</li></ol><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6232.pastedimage1616428552002v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h2><strong>Summary points:</strong>&nbsp;</h2><ul><li>A Trados Transcreate project is based on a standard Trados Studio project</li><li>Only bilingual excel files are supported as the source file</li><li>2 workflows are supported based on the users preference:</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">Internal: Trados Studio</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">External: MS Word</li><li>Back translations can be addressed as a second step in the transcreation process if preferred</li></ul><h1>Getting Started</h1><h2><strong>Questions to review before you start:</strong>&nbsp;</h2><ol><li>Do you want to keep the original Trados Studio Project displayed within the Trados Studio Projects view?How many alternative Transcreations do you require?What do you want to do with any historic translations which may be leveraged during your convert process?Will the same person perform the transcreation AND the back translation, as part of the same task?</li></ol><h2><strong>Creating your Trados Transcreation Project</strong></h2><p>Using Bilingual Excel files, follow these steps:&nbsp;</p><ol><li>Select \"Create local Project\"</li></ol><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1581.pastedimage1616428691188v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p><ol><li>Review File Type Settings (in practice it may be more convenient to do this in advance)</li></ol><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1665.pastedimage1616428718014v5.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Ensure the source file columns are configured appropriately, paying specific attention to:&nbsp;</p><ul><li>Source column</li><li>Traslation column</li><li>Context information</li></ul><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0285.pastedimage1616428756943v7.png\" alt=\" \"></p><ol><li>Complete the Project wizard to create your Trados Studio projectAlso note that by using the “Customer” field when you create your project the transcreation projects will be neatly grouped by customer.Select the project in the Projects View, right click and select \"Convert to Transcreate Project\"</li></ol><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5633.pastedimage1616428781182v8.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><ol><li>Complete the Trados Transcreate Convert Project Wizard, paying careful attention to:the number of alternative Translations requiredspecifying whether or not you wish to retain the original Trados Project listed within the Project view</li></ol><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8081.pastedimage1616428813977v9.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><ol><li>Once complete, go back to the Trados Studio Projects view and note your converted Project is now in the list. It’s clearly identifiable by:</li></ol><ul><li>a Trados Transcreate icon</li><li>the project name use a “-T” as a suffix</li><li>the “Type” column uses “Transcreate Project” as the identifier</li></ul><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3240.pastedimage1616428840038v10.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</p><ol><li>A Trados Transcreated project can be managed directly from within the Trados Transcreate View, depending on the preferred workflow (Internal / External)</li></ol><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7142.pastedimage1616428868053v11.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</p><h2><strong>Internal Workflow</strong></h2><p>It’s expected that the content in the Editor view will show duplicate segments, which are part of the alternative Transcreations specified during the initial convert process</p><p>This is further supported, as seen in the File Tree View through the alternative listings</p><p><strong>Take Note:&nbsp;</strong>Only the first occurrence of the alternative segments are translated, where possible. All other instances and the translations thereof remain empty. This is as designed in order to ensure each alternative translation is unique and appropriately differentiated as part of the Transcreate process.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7485.pastedimage1616428904115v12.png\" alt=\" \"><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p><h2><strong>External Workflow</strong></h2><p>As briefly explained above, the External Workflow would consist of both an export to MS Word as well as an import back into Trados Studio for reflect any Transcreated updates to your Project files.</p><p><strong>Exporting to MS Word</strong></p><p>Export options that are managed as part of the export to MS Word include:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7651.pastedimage1616428937253v14.png\" alt=\" \"></p><ul><li>Include Translation:</li><li>If any translations exist within the Trados Transcreate Project, do they get exported to the MS Word document?</li><li>Exclude Segments:</li><li>Management over current translations from within the Trados Transcreate Project to MS Word, can be specified via the exclusions of segments. Locked segments are not exported to MS Word by default</li><li>Copy Source To Target:</li><li>Where no translations exist within the Trados Transcreate Project, should the export copy source text to target columns?</li><li>Include Back-Translation:</li><li>Does this export to MS Word, include the task of Back-Translation</li><li>* It’s our recommendation that files first get Transcreated &amp; Reviewed. However if you want a user to perform both tasks simultaneously, then you can do so accordingly.</li></ul><p>&nbsp;<strong>To run an export follow these steps:</strong></p><ol><li>Right click on file(s) and select export to proceed to export wizard<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6281.pastedimage1616428974015v15.png\" alt=\" \"></li><li>Once exported, take note the file(s) show the blue export icon<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1440.pastedimage1616428995850v16.png\" alt=\" \"></li></ol><p>Based on export file(s) selections, users can then complete their Transcreated task directly in MS Word.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Take Note:</strong>&nbsp;This export did not include the Back Translation</p><p><strong>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5342.pastedimage1616429015677v17.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><p><strong>MS Word support includes:</strong></p><p>Tracked Changes</p><p>Tag Management</p><p><strong>Import updates from MS Word</strong></p><p>File(s) can be imported as required, in order to:</p><ul><li>Update Trados Transcrated Project *.xliff files</li><li>Revert files back to previous versions</li></ul><p>Import options that are managed as part of the export workflow from MS Word include:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6864.pastedimage1616429141278v21.png\" alt=\" \"></p><ul><li>Overwrite existing translation:</li><li>Regardless of what translations currently exists in the Trados Transcreate Project, should it be over written from the pending MS Word import?</li><li>Exclude Segments:</li><li>In order to Project any current translations within the Trados Transcreated Project and regardless of which segments were originally excluded (if any), by excluding segments based on segment status we can ignore any changes made within the pending MS Word import.</li><li>Origin system:</li><li>Otherwise known as Provider. Meaning what name should reflect against the translation segments and potentially under the TU meta data once the Translation Memory has been updated</li><li>Segment Status Assignment:</li><li>Adjusting segment status accordingly is beneficial to aid users navigation, once reviewing the updated *.sdlxliff files in Trados Studio</li></ul><p>&nbsp;<strong>To run an import follow these steps:</strong></p><ol><li>Select the file(s) and select import<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6038.pastedimage1616429100471v20.png\" alt=\" \"></li><li>Specify which file(s) need updatingAdd the updated MS Word File<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5025.pastedimage1616429170778v22.png\" alt=\" \"></li></ol><p><strong>Take Note:</strong>&nbsp;Once completed the green import icon is seen against the files</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1462.pastedimage1616429187732v23.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>&nbsp;<strong>File Management</strong></p><p>The Trados Transcreate Project is created in the same location path as your original Studio Project.</p><p>After the successful completion of the conversation between your original Studio Project and the Trados Transcreated Project, you can use the short cut to explore the folders</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4721.pastedimage1616429221710v24.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Or access the “Open Project Folder” directly from within the Project View in Trados Studio</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8078.pastedimage1616429239200v25.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>Included in the standard folder structure, you now have a folder called workflow. And in the even you created a Back Translation, you will also see an additional folder called “Back Projects”cccccctjnevgtljfddjtnvvfuntdgrcdngvtvihehicf</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3426.pastedimage1616429267482v26.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</p><p>During your numerus exports/imports you can track progressive files and versions by looking through the sub folders which follows a date/time naming convention&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6305.pastedimage1616429303890v27.png\" alt=\" \"><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p><p>It is worthy to note the following:&nbsp;</p><ul><li>It is not possible to mix files from different Trados Transcreate Projects</li></ul><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6560.pastedimage1616429339466v28.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><ul><li>If an Internal workflow was carried out, a folder will be created with a file extension that isn’t strictly recognised outside of Trados Studio.</li></ul><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1738.pastedimage1616429370898v29.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>However as the local report confirms as the Workflow, this file was managed from within Trados Studio as an Internal Workflow</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1537.pastedimage1616429387188v30.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</p><ul><li>If an External workflow was implemented nestled in the folder structure you will find a MS Word as a result of the export process. This is in addition to a local report and a detailed*.txt file with information related to that particular task (for reference)</li></ul><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0167.pastedimage1616429410177v31.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>If unsure, a user can review the Project File Activity Audit View for a full understanding of the files export/import history</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4061.pastedimage1616429441549v32.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Take Note:</strong>&nbsp;To prevent valuable work from being lost due to ongoing imports and exports while having a mixed Internal / External workflow, the following warning may show</p><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0042.pastedimage1616429460501v33.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><p>In addition, a warning icon will indicate if a file has already been exported and/or imported and therefore work may be lost if not managed appropriately</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1385.pastedimage1616429482521v34.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h1><strong>Back Translations</strong></h1><h2><strong>When to accommodate Back Translation</strong></h2><p>The command to create a Back Translation can only take place from within the Trados Transcreate View. Regardless if you included Back Translation as part of the external export to MS Word or not.&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Back Translation Workflow Options</strong></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4643.pastedimage1616429537370v35.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><br></p><p><strong>How to create a Focused Back Translation Project</strong></p><p><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p><ol><li>Select your Trados Transcreate Project file(s) and use the ribbon to complete the request for a Back Translation to be created</li></ol><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5086.pastedimage1616431943197v2.png\" alt=\" \"><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p><p>You will now see a Back Translation listed under the original Trados Transcreated Project</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1512.pastedimage1616431965066v3.png\" alt=\" \"><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p><p><strong>Take Note:</strong>&nbsp;Also within Project View in Trados Studio you will find a dedicated Back Translation Project</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0243.pastedimage1616431985316v4.png\" alt=\" \"><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p><ol><li>Complete the Back Transation either as an Internal or External workflow based on preference.</li></ol><p>&nbsp;<strong>Take Note</strong>: It’s expected that the source and target translation changes direction, to fulfil the Back Translation requirement</p><p><strong>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6403.pastedimage1616432010778v5.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</strong></p><p><strong>Included as part of Transcreate</strong></p><p>While it is recommended different users perform the actual Transcreation from the Back Translation, it maybe your requirement that during the External workflow you want to include the Back-Translation.</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1651.pastedimage1616432032109v6.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>&nbsp;<strong>Back Translation Workflow Phases</strong></p><p><strong>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0825.pastedimage1616432060221v7.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</strong></p><p><strong>General steps required include:</strong></p><ol><li>Include Back Translation as part of exported MS DocumentTranscreate target column as well as complete Back Translation ColumnImport update back into Trados Translation ProjectReview Transcreation updates in Trados StudioIncluding Review and Sign off (optional)Create Back Translation Project from within Trados Transcreate ViewAssess Back Translations originating from step 3Ongoing assessment, review and Sign off can be performed either by following the Internal (within Trados Studio) or External (export/Import MS Word) Workflow steps</li></ol><p><strong>In summary the following results are expected:</strong></p><ul><li>Initially no dedicated Back Translation Project is created</li><li>A MS Word file with an additional column for Back Translations is provided</li><li>Initial import will only show Target Segment in Trados Studio</li><li>Back Translations will be accessed only once Back Translation Project has been created</li></ul><p><strong>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8712.pastedimage1616432101863v8.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</strong></p><p><strong>&nbsp;</strong></p><h1><br></h1><p><strong>Tips:</strong></p><p><strong>TU Meta Data</strong></p><p>To maintain reference of the Transcreation origin, Trados Studio users can update Translation Memories using the *.sdlxliff meta data. During Batch Translation Memory Update it is known as “Use information from bilingual file to update TU system fields”</p><p>As defined during the import wizard the Provider of this Transcreation in this case has been noted as Lyds and will be saved in the TU accordingly</p><p>&nbsp;<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5126.pastedimage1616432195550v9.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Changing Default</strong></p><p>Trados Transcreate App default settings for convert, export and import can be changed by going to the app view’s settings</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0207.pastedimage1616432217601v10.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>&nbsp;Removing referenced files</strong></p><p>During an import, you have added a file accidently. You can remove it by right clicking and select “Clear XLIFF File”</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8284.pastedimage1616432242785v11.png\" alt=\" \">&nbsp;</p><p><strong>Missing Project</strong></p><p>If in the Trados Transcreate view you cant see your Project listed, its likely because its missing from within the Trados Studio Project View. In which case, simply select “Open Project” to relocate your missing Project from within your windows explorer.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7271.pastedimage1616432261331v12.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Advanced Display Filter</strong></p><p>In the event you have several alternative translations to manage, you can easily navigate within Trados Studio by using the Advanced Display Filter</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0246.pastedimage1616432294011v13.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p><strong>Context</strong></p><p>If working as part of the Internal Workflow, your Translation Memory and Termbase maybe great assets to integrated as supporting tools to reference as part of your Transcreate process</p><p><strong>Deleting a Back Transcreate project</strong></p><p>If you need to delete a Back Transcreate project, simply right click on the Trados Transcreate project and select “Remove Back-Translation projects”</p><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5127.pastedimage1616432308847v14.png\" alt=\" The result is a perfectly consistent, context-aware translation, free from the limitations of segment-by-segment work. </p>\r\n<h3>Core Features:</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Export source or existing your Trados Studio project to a text file. </li>\r\n<li><strong>Import translated or reviewed text</strong> from a file or clipboard directly into Trados Studio.</li>\r\n<li>Review your AI-translated Guide.</h2>\r\n<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>Translation Provider plugins in Trados Studio only send the current segment to NMT or AI systems. The GenAI model or NMT system does not know the content of the entire document or anything about the previous and next segments. In this way, each sentence is translated in complete isolation from the others. The TransAIde plugin allows you to export the entire text from Trados Studio, then translate it in the selected GenAI model or NMT system, and finally import the consistent translation back into Trados Studio. </p>\r\n<p>The TransAIde plugin is not a Translation Provider plugin. It allows you to export the entire source text from a document opened in the Trados Studio editor in text or JSON format and import the translation in text or JSON format into the document opened in the Trados Studio editor.</p>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<h3>Features Available from the Ribbon in Trados Studio Editor View</h3>\r\n<h4>Export segments</h4>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/e12be00d-1f65-41da-a4b3-dbfc170ebc43/Resources/export-segments-dialog.png\" alt=\"Export segments\"></p>\r\n<p>Export Type:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Source only (Text File)</strong>: exports the source text in text format. Each segment is on a separate line in the text file.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Target only (Text File)</strong>: exports the translation text. Each segment is on a separate line in the text file.</li>\r\n<li><strong>JSON file</strong>: exports the source text, translation, segment type and segment identification data. The JSON file also contains a space for comments. The file is intended to be updated by an AI model. <img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/e12be00d-1f65-41da-a4b3-dbfc170ebc43/Resources/json-format.png\" alt=\"JSON File Format\"></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h4>Import segments</h4>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/e12be00d-1f65-41da-a4b3-dbfc170ebc43/Resources/import-segments-dialog.png\" alt=\"Import segments\"></p>\r\n<p>Translation Import:\r\n<strong>Target Only (Text File)</strong>: imports a text file with the translation. The lines in the text file must match the segments in Trados Studio.\r\n<strong>Target Only (Clipboard)</strong>: imports the translation copied to the Windows clipboard. The lines in the text must match the segments in Trados Studio.\r\n<strong>JSON file</strong>: imports translations from the &quot;target&quot; field of a JSON file.</p>\r\n<h4>Export terms</h4>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/e12be00d-1f65-41da-a4b3-dbfc170ebc43/Resources/export-terms-dialog.png\" alt=\"Export terms\"></p>\r\n<p>Terms are exported from the local termbase in MultiTerm format. The local termbase must be the default termbase. If the default termbase is a termbase from Trados Cloud or Groupshare, a NullReferenceException error will appear, but you will be able to continue working. Support for online termbases will be added in future versions of the plugin.</p>\r\n<p>Terms are exported divided into required and forbidden terms.</p>\r\n<h3>Features Available in the TransAIde View of the Plugin</h3>\r\n<p>The plugin also offers a View Part for working with JSON files, which can be enabled in the View menu in the Trados Studio Editor view.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/e12be00d-1f65-41da-a4b3-dbfc170ebc43/Resources/transaide-view-attached.png\" alt=\"TransAIde View\"></p>\r\n<p>In this window, you can select a JSON file containing the file updated in the AI model and work with each segment. After selecting the file, you need to attach it using the Attach File button/command. The window  displays the source, translation and comment from the JSON file, as well as the difference between the translation in the current segment and the translation in the JSON file. This window can be used, for example, to translate fuzzy segments or to review translations in an AI model. To apply the translation from the TransAide window to the active segment, click the Apply button. </p>\r\n<p>The complete user manual can be found here: <a href=\"https://posteditacat.xyz/download/transaide-plugin-for-trados-studio-user-manual/\">posteditacat.xyz/download/transaide-plugin-for-trados-studio-user-manual</a></p>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<table>\r\n<thead>\r\n<tr>\r\n<th>Plugin Version</th>\r\n<th>Trados Studio Version</th>\r\n<th>Notes</th>\r\n</tr>\r\n</thead>\r\n<tbody><tr>\r\n<td>1.0.4.2021</td>\r\n<td>Trados Studio 2021 SR2</td>\r\n<td>All Trados Studio 2021 versions. Tested on Service Release 2 (SR2) version. It does not require separate MultiTerm installation to support term export.</td>\r\n</tr>\r\n<tr>\r\n<td>1.0.4.2022</td>\r\n<td>Trados Studio 2022 SR2</td>\r\n<td>All Trados Studio 2022 versions. Tested without any SR and with SR2. maintained/supported</span></p><p><br></p><p><strong>Introduction</strong></p><p>The Star Transit (TransitPackage Handler) application&nbsp;supports opening a Star Transit package (PPF file) and also the creation of a Star Transit return package (TPF file). It is available for Trados Studio 2017, 2019 and Trados Studio 2021. However, please note that only the 2019 and 2021 versions are currently supported, and only the 2021 version will be further enhanced.&nbsp;The details below are based on the 2021 version.</p><p><strong>How to use</strong></p><p>1. Installation</p><p>The plugin can be installed manually after downloading from the&nbsp;<a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/language/app/transitpackage-handler/573/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\"><strong><em>RWS AppStore</em></strong></a>, or via the integrated appstore (if using Trados Studio 2021 and above)</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1274x764/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1651823213280v2.png\"></p><p>Once installed please make sure that the transit filetype have been activated in your project templates.&nbsp;You will need to do this for all the templates you are using.&nbsp;If they are not activated you will see this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1272x662/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1651823362601v3.png\"></p><p>Once activated by checking the box and clicking on OK the filetypes will be available here:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1242x292/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/pastedimage1651823441948v4.png\"></p><p><br></p><p>2. Package</strong>\".</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6747.Install.png\"></p><ul><li>Select the .ppf package file you want to export.</li><li>The&nbsp;StarTransit window will be opened.</li><li>Select the location where the information from the .ppf package will be exported, a new folder (mandatory).</li><li>Select a template (mandatory).</li><li>The other options are&nbsp;not mandatory.</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7635.openpackage.png\"></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0257.Project.png\"></p><p>Depending on the type of package, you will see the following two options</p><p>If the Transit package&nbsp;<em>doesn't</em>&nbsp;contain TM Metadata- No options related to TM settings will appear</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5238.Transit-Information.png\"></p><p>The next step confirms the project is created without TM</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4403.Transit-Package-Deatails.png\"></p><p>Packages&nbsp;<em>with</em>&nbsp;reference files- you will see the option to 'Include Reference Files'</p><p>The options allow you to:</p><ul><li>Create Project without TM</li><li>Create and import into new TM</li><li>Import into existing TM</li><li>Set the Penalty</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0028.Transit-withTM.png\"></p><p>3. Return StarTransit package</p><p>User has the possibility to return the files which were extracted from the StarTransit package, into .tpf Star Transit return package.</p><ul><li>Select the project which was created based on the StarTrasit package.</li><li>Select \"<strong>Return StarTransit package</strong>\".</li><li>Select the files which should be returned.</li><li>Select the encoding which will be used to generate the return files name. (By default the selected encoding is<a href=\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Code_page_850\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">&nbsp;Western European</a>)</li><li>Select location.</li><li>Press on the \"<strong>Create Package</strong>\" button.</li></ul><p>Full list of the available encodings can be found on&nbsp;<a href=\"https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.text.encoding?view=net-5.0\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"color: rgb(41, 143, 194); background-color: transparent;\">Microsoft&nbsp;</a>&nbsp;documention.</p><p><strong>Note: We'll not alter the content of the file, we use the encoding just to generate the name of the files.</strong></p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1610x912/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1362.pastedimage1626938875606v1.png\"></p><ul><li>The .tpf file was created into&nbsp;<strong>Return&nbsp;package</strong>&nbsp;folder</li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2804.Return-Package.png\"></p><p><strong>Note</strong></p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;<strong>&nbsp;1.</strong>&nbsp;When all files were returned and user click on the \"StarTransit return package\", the following message will be displayed</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7331.Target.png\"></p><p>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<strong>2.</strong>&nbsp;In order to repeat the return process, user needs to revert to .sdlxliff(s). Preview</h1>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Table of Contents</h2>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><a href=\"#introduction\">Introduction</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#key-features\">Key Features</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#installation\">Installation</a><ul>\r\n<li><a href=\"#prerequisites\">Prerequisites</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#new-installation\">New Installation</a></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#updates\">Updates</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#technical-requirements\">Technical Requirements</a><ul>\r\n<li><a href=\"#file-type-configuration\">File Type Configuration</a></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#using-the-app\">Using the App</a><ul>\r\n<li><a href=\"#create-project\">Create Project</a></li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#preview\">Preview</a></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><a href=\"#references\">References</a></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Introduction</h2>\r\n<p>The <strong>Tridion Docs Preview App</strong> enables users working in the <strong>Online Editor</strong> (via Trados Team, Accelerate, or Enterprise) to visualize an <strong>in-context preview (HTML)</strong> of Tridion Docs <strong>XML (DITA) files</strong>.</p>\r\n<p>This app leverages the <strong>Tridion Docs API – ExternalPreview</strong>, which requires the <strong>External Preview module</strong> in the <strong>Tridion Docs system</strong>. See <a href=\"#prerequisites\">prerequisites</a> for details.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>In-context preview of <strong>Tridion Docs DITA files</strong></li>\r\n<li>Integration with <strong>Trados Online Editor</strong></li>\r\n<li>Compatibility with <strong>Tridion Docs API – ExternalPreview</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Installation</h2>\r\n<h3>Prerequisites</h3>\r\n<p>To install the <strong>Tridion Docs Preview App</strong>, ensure the following:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><p>The <strong>External Preview module</strong> is enabled on <strong>Tridion Docs</strong><br><a href=\"https://docs.rws.com/en-US/sdl-tridion-docs-14-sp3-806356/enabling-and-configuring-the-external-preview-module-313573\">Enable External Preview Module</a></p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>The <strong>Tridion Docs External Preview API URL</strong> must be <strong>public</strong> to allow the app to connect.</p>\r\n</li>\r\n<li><p>The <strong>API URL</strong> should not require authentication and must be enabled for the <strong>Guest</strong> user.  Also, Guest user should be part of the required User Groups to view the content. \r\n<a href=\"https://developers.rws.com/ISHDeploy/Commands/Enable-ISHExternalPreview.html\">Enable ISHExternalPreview</a></p>\r\n</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h3>New Installation</h3>\r\n<h4>Option 1: Install via RWS AppStore</h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Visit <strong><a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/\">RWS AppStore</a></strong></li>\r\n<li>Search for <strong>&quot;Tridion Docs Preview&quot;</strong></li>\r\n<li>Sign in to your <strong>Trados account</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>Install</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h4>Option 2: Install via Trados Account</h4>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Login</strong> to <strong>Trados</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click on <strong>Account Settings</strong></li>\r\n<li>Navigate to <strong>RWS AppStore</strong></li>\r\n<li>Locate <strong>Tridion Docs Preview</strong> under <strong>Apps</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>Install</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_1.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\">  </p>\r\n<p>Once installed, enter the <strong>Tridion Docs External Preview API URL</strong> (example: <code>https://mytridionsite.rws.com/ISHCM/API/ExternalPreview</code>) and click <strong>Complete</strong>.\r\n<img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_12.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\">  </p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Updates</h2>\r\n<p>If an update is available, a <strong>notification</strong> will be sent via email or displayed in <strong>Trados notifications</strong>.\r\nTo check if any update to app is available, follow the steps below:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Login to Trados</li>\r\n<li>Click on Account Settings icon on the top right corner</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_10.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<ol start=\"3\">\r\n<li>•\tChoose “RWS AppStore”.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_8.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<ol start=\"3\">\r\n<li>Under <strong>Installed Apps</strong>, it will show either <strong>Up to Date</strong> or <strong>Update Available</strong>.</li>\r\n<li>If an update is available, click on the app and select <strong>Update</strong>.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_5.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<h3>File Type Configuration</h3>\r\n<p>Once installed, the <strong>Tridion Docs Preview Extension</strong> is automatically added in the file type configuration.</p>\r\n<p>To verify:</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Navigate to <strong>Resources</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_2.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<ol start=\"2\">\r\n<li>Select <strong>File Type Configurations</strong></li>\r\n<li>Open the <strong>XML 2: OASIS DITA 1.3 Compliant</strong> file type</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_13.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<ol start=\"4\">\r\n<li>Expand <strong>Preview Settings</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_9.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<ol start=\"5\">\r\n<li>Ensure that <strong>Tridion Docs Preview Extension</strong> is enabled</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_6.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Using the App</h2>\r\n<h3>Create Project</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Login</strong> to <strong>Trados</strong> at <a href=\"https://eu.cloud.trados.com/lc\">eu.cloud.trados.com</a></li>\r\n<li>Go to <strong>Projects</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>New Project</strong></li>\r\n<li>Enter the required details:<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Project Name:</strong> [Your Project Name]</li>\r\n<li><strong>Description (Optional):</strong> [Description]</li>\r\n<li><strong>Location:</strong> Select from list</li>\r\n<li><strong>Project Template:</strong> Select from list</li>\r\n<li><strong>Source Language</strong></li>\r\n<li><strong>Target Languages</strong></li>\r\n<li><strong>Due Date</strong></li>\r\n<li><strong>File Type Configuration</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n<li>Upload <strong>DITA (XML) files</strong> (e.g., <code>SampleDITAFile.xml</code>)</li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>Create</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p>Alternatively, you can create a translation job directly in <strong>Tridion Docs Environment</strong> and access it via Trados.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h3>Preview</h3>\r\n<p>Once a project reaches the <strong>translation stage</strong>, users can enable <strong>preview</strong> in the <strong>Online Editor</strong>.</p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li><strong>Open</strong> the project in <strong>Trados Online Editor</strong></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Preview</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_3.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<ol start=\"3\">\r\n<li>By default, <strong>&quot;Preview Off&quot;</strong> is selected. Change it to your preferred preview mode.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/image_11.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<ol start=\"4\">\r\n<li>Ensure <strong>Tridion Docs Preview App</strong> is selected under <strong>Preview Type</strong></li>\r\n<li>The <strong>Preview Section</strong> will load within the same window where user can toggle between Source and Target Preview.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://rws-prod-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/042b7acd-ee37-4d0b-8e19-b68cc1a43e70/Resources/Image07.png\" alt=\"Installation Screen\"></p>\r\n<ol start=\"6\">\r\n<li>If any report with visibility into the <strong>active QA configuration</strong> used when the report was created.</p>\r\n<p>The report can include the enabled QA categories, checks, and sub-options from the verification providers used during execution, helping project managers, linguists, and compliance teams keep a clear QA audit trail.\r\n<br><br></p>\r\n<h4>Getting Started</h4>\r\n<p>To run <strong>Verify Files – Audit Report</strong> in Trados Studio:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Open the <strong>Projects</strong> view and select a project, or select specific files in the <strong>Files</strong> view.</li>\r\n<li>On the <strong>Home</strong> tab, click <strong>Batch Tasks</strong>.</li>\r\n<li>Select <strong>Verify Files – Audit Report</strong>.</li>\r\n<li>In the batch task wizard, choose the files to process and click <strong>Next</strong>.</li>\r\n<li>On the <strong>Settings</strong> page:<ul>\r\n  <li>optionally select <strong>Report ignored messages</strong></li>\r\n  <li>select <strong>Include verification details</strong> to capture the active QA configuration</li>\r\n  <li>choose which segment statuses to include in the report</li>\r\n</ul></li>\r\n<li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>The generated report is available in the <strong>Reports</strong> view as <strong>Verify Files – Audit Report</strong>.\r\nWhen <strong>Include verification details</strong> is enabled, the report records the active QA providers and verification settings used to generate the report, helping support QA traceability and audit requirements.</p>\r\n<br>\r\n\r\n<h4>Technical Requirements</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>A supported version of <strong>Trados Studio</strong></li>\r\n<li>The <strong>Verify Files – Audit Report</strong> plugin installed from the <strong>RWS AppStore</strong></li>\r\n<li>A project or project files available in <strong>Projects</strong> view</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><strong>Optional</strong>: On the batch task <strong>Settings</strong> page, enable <strong>Include verification details</strong> to record the active QA configuration in the report.</p>\r\n<br>\r\n \r\n<h4>Key Features</h4>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Runs as a <strong>batch task</strong> in Trados Studio</li>\r\n<li>Generates a dedicated <strong>Verify Files – Audit Report</strong></li>\r\n<li>Option to <strong>include ignored messages</strong></li>\r\n<li>Option to <strong>include verification details</strong> for audit purposes</li>\r\n<li>Records the <strong>active QA providers</strong> used during verification</li>\r\n<li>Summarizes enabled <strong>QA categories, checks, and sub-options</strong></li>\r\n<li>Lets you choose which segment statuses to include (Draft, Not Translated, Translated,
