It supports efficient searching across multiple dictionaries and integrates seamlessly with applications such as Microsoft Word.</p>\r\n<p>The application streamlines multilingual communication by enabling users to search, manage, and create dictionaries tailored to domain-specific language. It is particularly valuable for users in technical, medical, legal, and corporate environments where precise terminology is essential.</p>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Integrated Dictionary Search</strong>: Search across selected dictionaries with support for offline use and dictionary groups.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Hotkey Activation</strong>: Perform lookups directly from other applications using a customisable keyboard shortcut.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Machine Translation</strong>: Translate text in over 30 languages using WordFinder Translator with credit-based usage.</li>\r\n<li><strong>User Dictionary Management</strong>: Create, import, export, and edit dictionaries in Excel, TBX, or legacy WFCML formats.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Copy and Paste with Formatting</strong>: Copy or paste entries while preserving custom formatting, including pastable and non-pastable segments.</li>\r\n<li><strong>Custom Menu Links</strong>: Add personalised links to frequently used online resources.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li><strong>Supported OS</strong>: Windows 10 and above</li>\r\n<li><strong>Dependencies</strong>: Internet connection for cloud features; WordFinder Unlimited account</li>\r\n<li><strong>Permissions</strong>: File system access for local dictionary storage; optional integration with word processors</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Installation</h2>\r\n<h3>For Individual Users</h3>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Visit the <a href=\"https://www.wordfinder.com/Downloads\">Downloads page</a>.</li>\r\n<li>Download and run the installer.</li>\r\n<li>Follow the on-screen instructions.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<h3>For IT Departments</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Refer to the <a href=\"https://www.wordfinder.com/wp-content/uploads/Deployment-WordFinder-for-Windows.pdf\">deployment guide</a> for technical instructions.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>First-Time Use</h2>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>Launch the application.</li>\r\n<li>Sign in using your WordFinder Unlimited account.</li>\r\n<li>Navigate to <strong>Settings &gt; Dictionaries</strong> to select dictionaries.</li>\r\n<li>Return to the main application window to begin using WordFinder.</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Dictionary Management</h2>\r\n<h3>Selecting Dictionaries</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Use <strong>Settings &gt; Dictionaries</strong> to choose active dictionaries.</li>\r\n<li>Filter by language direction or subject matter.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Activating and Managing Dictionaries</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Click the checkmark icon to activate.</li>\r\n<li>Click the cloud icon to download dictionaries for offline use.</li>\r\n<li>Create groups of dictionaries and assign keyboard shortcuts.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>My Dictionaries</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>The currently active dictionaries form your “My Dictionaries” list.</li>\r\n<li>This selection can be updated at any time.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Searching</h2>\r\n<h3>In-App Search</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Supports the following search options:<ul>\r\n<li><code>*</code> – matches any number of characters</li>\r\n<li><code>?</code> – matches a single character</li>\r\n<li><code>^</code> – full-text search</li>\r\n<li><code>=</code> – exact match</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>From Other Applications</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Highlight a word in an external application.</li>\r\n<li>Activate WordFinder using the hotkey to search immediately.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Copy and Paste</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Double-click or press <code>Enter</code> to paste entries into the source document.</li>\r\n<li>Single-click to copy entries to the clipboard.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Quick Access</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Press <code>Esc</code> to minimise WordFinder without closing the application.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Legacy and User Dictionaries</h2>\r\n<h3>Legacy Dictionaries</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Supports formats: <code>.WFD</code>, <code>.WFI</code>, <code>.WED</code>, <code>.WEI</code></li>\r\n<li>Store files in:<ul>\r\n<li><code>%appdata%\\WordFinder\\lexica\\legacy</code></li>\r\n<li><code>C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Wfwin</code></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>User Dictionaries</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Create, import, export, and edit dictionaries.</li>\r\n<li>Supported formats: Excel, TBX (2.0/3.0), WFCML</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Import and Export Formats</h2>\r\n<h3>Excel</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Without headers: languages must be selected manually.</li>\r\n<li>With headers: ISO 639-1 codes are automatically recognised.</li>\r\n<li>Use <code>&amp;gt;</code> and <code>&amp;lt;</code> markers to designate pastable text.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>WFCML</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Traditional plain-text format with special markup.</li>\r\n<li>Supports formatting, pastable segments, and dictionary structure.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Creating or Updating User Dictionaries</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Import a new dictionary via <strong>Settings &gt; Dictionaries &gt; User Dictionaries</strong>.</li>\r\n<li>Update an existing dictionary using the <strong>Import terms</strong> icon.</li>\r\n<li>Export user dictionaries to Excel or WFCML using the <strong>Export terms</strong> icon.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>WordFinder Translator</h2>\r\n<h3>Overview</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Integrated secure machine translation service supporting over 30 languages.</li>\r\n<li>Translate text manually or via hotkey-triggered lookups from other applications.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Credits</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Translation is billed per character.</li>\r\n<li>Remaining credits are shown below the source text input field.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Preferences and Settings</h2>\r\n<h3>Hotkeys</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Default activation: <code>Ctrl + Shift (Left)</code></li>\r\n<li>Configure hotkeys via <strong>Settings &gt; Preferences</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Capitalisation</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Optionally preserve initial capital letters when pasting translated terms.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Translator Trigger</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Set the minimum number of words required to trigger automatic translation on hotkey use.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h3>Custom Menu Links</h3>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Add personal web links to the application’s main menu.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<hr>\r\n<h2>Keyboard Problem</strong></p><p>You're reviewing a document in Studio and need more than one colour to highlight the text. You also need to work quickly and don't like having to use five clicks just to access the highlight function through the Quick Insert menu.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>The Solution</strong></p><p>If that sounds familiar, then the Wordlight plugin is going to be the answer you were looking for! This plugin has the following features:</p><p>- Select from 15 colours (the same as in Microsoft Word)</p><p>- Highlight selected text with 1 click or a single keyboard shortcut</p><p>- Change the colour in 2 clicks!</p><p>- Highlight in any filetype and not just the ones supported by Quick Insert</p><p>- If the source document is a word or PDF, your target file will retain and display the highlights</p><p><br></p>","documentation":"<h1>Overview</h1><p><strong>Wordlight i</strong>s a plugin for Trados Studio 2019 and Trados Studio 2021, that provides features for highlighting selected text in the editor with a chosen colour.&nbsp;</p><p>The highlight formatting is persisted in all target files but only used in compatible formats that support this feature, such as Word and PDF when generating the target native file.&nbsp;It's important to understand this and ensure you remove the highlighting before creating target files where it's not supported to avoid unwanted complications in the native application.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/393x296/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2816.WordLight.Example.png\"></p><h1>Setup</h1><h2>Wordlight Plugin</h2><ol><li>Download the Wordlight plugin to your local driveDouble click on the plugin to launch the<strong>&nbsp;SDL Plugin Installer</strong>&nbsp;and complete the installation, selecting the supported versions of SDL Trados Studio.<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/882x408/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3704.Installation.png\"></li></ol><h1>Keyboard Shortcuts</h1><p>The Wordlight plugin has as number of keyboard shortcut actions that you can adapt to fit your needs at any time.</p><h2>Procedure</h2><ol><li>Go to&nbsp;File&nbsp;&gt;&nbsp;Options&nbsp;and select&nbsp;Keyboard shortcuts&nbsp;&gt; WordlightLocate the row containing the action for which you want to create or edit the shortcut.Select the cell in the&nbsp;Shortcut&nbsp;column (where the current keyboard shortcut is displayed, if one exists).Press the key, or combination of keys, that you want to use for the shortcut. The key combination appears in the&nbsp;Shortcut&nbsp;column.<strong>Note</strong>:&nbsp;Shift+F10 and Ctrl+Shift+F10 are reserved for internal use and cannot be assigned as shortcut keys.&nbsp;Similarly, single keys such as the numbers on your keypad cannot be assigned due to some limitations.Click outside the row you just edited. SDL Trados Studio checks whether the new shortcut is already in use. If the shortcut is already in use, the entire row is highlighted in red and you must remove or edit the shortcut.Select&nbsp;OK&nbsp;to save the shortcut and close the&nbsp;Options&nbsp;dialog.<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/774x421/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8054.KeybaordShortcuts.png\"></li></ol><h1>Usage</h1><p>Apply highlight formatting to the selected text or the entire target segment or segments</p><h2>Highlight text</h2><ol><li>Open a document in the editor.Select text in the target segments or optionally select the segment row (or multiple rows)Click on the&nbsp;<strong>Highlight Word</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Wordlight</strong>&nbsp;group in the&nbsp;<strong>Home</strong>&nbsp;ribbon.<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/202x99/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4401.WordLight.ButtonPartTop.png\"><strong>Note</strong>: If you have selected the segment row, then the highlight formatting will be applied on the entire target segment.</li></ol><h2>Change highlight color</h2><ol><li>Click on the bottom section of the&nbsp;<strong>Highlight Word</strong>&nbsp;button from the&nbsp;<strong>Wordlight</strong>&nbsp;group in the&nbsp;<strong>Home</strong>&nbsp;ribbon; near the dropdown arrow.<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/201x98/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8750.WordLight.ButtonPartBottom.png\"><strong>Note:</strong>&nbsp;The color selection window is displayed.Select the highlight color by moving mouse over the colored block and then left mouse click.<img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/155x116/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7380.WordLight.ColorSelection.png\"></li></ol><h2>Clear highlight formatting</h2><p>There are few options available to clear the highlight formatting that is added by Wordlight.&nbsp;Choose from the following actions depending on the required usage:</p><ul><li>Clear highlight formatting from filtered segments</li><li>Clear highlight formatting from selected segments</li><li>Clear highlight formatting from selected text</li></ul><h2>Invalid formatting selection</h2><p>Currently it is not possible to apply highlight formatting on text where only part of a formatting tag pair is within the selection range, which would inevitably create an invalid xml hierarchy structure.</p><p><strong>Example:</strong></p><p><strong>Target segment with selection</strong></p><p>This is a &lt;cf&gt;sim<span style=\"background-color: rgb(128, 128, 128);\">ple&lt;/cf&gt; tes</span>t</p><p><strong>Note:</strong>&nbsp;only part of the existing formatting (<em>e.g. the formatting closing tag&nbsp;</em><strong><em>&lt;/cf&gt;</em></strong>) is within the selection range.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/371x181/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4174.Invalid_5F00_formatting_5F00_selection.png\"></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/94/versions/1435","changelog":{"Trados Have you ever had to update the segmentation rules, abbreviation lists, variable lists, or ordinal follower lists in Trados Studio? Have you ever wondered how to get all of these details from a <em>Language Resource Template</em>? If you have, then you&#39;ll be familiar with the effort that is required to make changes as they need to be applied across all of your Translation Memories one at a time; and with the inability to get at these resources once they are part of a template.</p>\r\n<p><em><strong>Solution</strong></em></p>\r\n<p>The applyTM Template application removes the repetitive effort that is required to make these changes. Now you can maintain one <em>Language Resource Template</em> and apply the changes to all of your Translation Memories in one go!</p>\r\n<p>It works by selecting the template and then adding all of your TMs (browse or drag and drop) in one go; check the resources you wish to be updated based on your template; then click apply. You can maintain resources for multiple languages in the same template, and the application will show you visually which languages were changed or not.</p>\r\n<p>You can also export these resources to Excel where it might be easier to carry out maintenance and then import them back into your template afterwards. And of course, you can import the resources from your Translation Memories into a <em>Language Resource Template</em>.</p>\r\n<p>To learn how to use this application, please check the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/21?tab=documentation\">Documentation</a>tab.</p>\r\n<style>\r\n  :root {\r\n    --table-striped: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);\r\n    --table-border: #dee2e6;\r\n }\r\n</style>","documentation":"<h1>Introduction</h1>\r\n<p>The\u00A0<strong>ApplyTmTemplate</strong>\u00A0plugin&#39;s primary purpose is to\u00A0<strong>apply a template to many TMs at once,</strong>\u00A0but it can also:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>export the resources from the template to an Excel document,</li>\r\n<li>to import language resources from an Excel document to a template or</li>\r\n<li>to import language resources from a TM to a template.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>\u00A0You can find this plugin in the\u00A0<strong>Add-Ins</strong>ribbon tab.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5430.ribbon.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<h2>Technical Requirements</h2>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Trados Studio 2019 and higher</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<h2>Key Features</h2>\r\n<p>When you start the application, please first select a relevant Template.</p>\r\n<p><em>If you don&#39;t already have one, you can create a new &#39;Language Resource Template&#39; via the Translation Memories tab</em></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2234.TM-Tab.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>When you open the application, please select the relevant Template.</p>\r\n<p>(<em>If you don&#39;t select a template (and have added TM&#39;s, you will see a warning message)</em></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4478.Warning.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0815.ApplyTmTemplate2.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7888.Apply.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Select /Deselect the options you wish to apply.\u00A0Now select Add Folder or Add TM&#39;s</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0508.Apply4.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>And now select the required TM&#39;s you wish to apply the template-</p>\r\n<p><em>(nothing will happen if you don&#39;t select the required TM&#39;s- The Apply option will remain disabled)</em></p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2664.Apply5.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>If the template is new, instead of clicking on Apply you&#39;ll need to click on the template path, to expand the context options and select &quot;Import from TMs to template&quot;. This will update the template with the TMs rules.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6746.pastedimage1632726485039v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>Now select Apply. -</p>\r\n<p>Once complete, you can hover over the TM and see tool tips showing whether the Template has been applied and if not, the reasons for this.</p>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2273.Apply7.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p>If you want to export the language resources\u00A0<strong>from</strong>the\u00A0<strong>template to</strong>an\u00A0<strong>Excel\u00A0document:</strong></p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>click on the &quot;<strong>Export</strong>&quot; button</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>In case there are languages in the template which cannot be identified, a message will be displayed informing the user of all the languages that were and weren&#39;t identified (this applies to every other action in case there are languages that cannot be identified)</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>after the export is complete, a message will be displayed and the Excel file will be automatically opened after the user clicks on &quot;OK&quot;</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/640x480/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5751.Export.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>If you want to\u00A0<strong>Import</strong>\u00A0<strong>from</strong>an excel document</li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>click &quot;<strong>Import from Excel to template&quot;</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6283.pastedimage1564424542163v21.png\" alt=\" \">,</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>select an Excel\u00A0document which contains resources previously exported and wait for the completion message to be displayed.</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<ol>\r\n<li>If you want to\u00A0<strong>Import</strong>\u00A0<strong>from\u00A0TMs</strong></li>\r\n</ol>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>load TMs in the app like shown for\u00A0<a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/w/wiki/4567/applytm-template#Apply\">Apply</a>\u00A0action</li>\r\n<li>click on\u00A0<strong>&quot;Import from TMs to template&quot;</strong></li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8358.pastedimage1564424603435v22.png\" alt=\" \"></p>\r\n<p><strong>Highlights of Studio 2024</strong>: </p>\r\n<p>New Options in ApplyTMTemplate Plugin\r\nStudio 2024 introduces two powerful new options for the ApplyTMTemplate plugin, enhancing flexibility and efficiency for TM management workflows:</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>ApplyTMSettings File</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>A new option to create and load an ApplyTMSettings file, allowing users to store ApplyTMTemplate preferences. These preferences are automatically cached when Studio starts or when the ApplyTMTemplate feature is accessed, providing a seamless and time-saving experience.</p>\r\n<ul>\r\n<li>Enhanced Settings Control</li>\r\n</ul>\r\n<p>Added the ability to choose between overwriting or merging TM settings for specific languages. Trados Studio 2019","minimumStudioVersion":"15.0.0"}]}],"categories":[{"id":2,"name":"Translation Memory"},{"id":4,"name":"Process automation & management"}]},{"id":34,"downloadCount":12137,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2017-09-19T08:18:33.997Z","updatedAt":"2025-01-12T19:11:32.724699Z","name":"projectTermExtract","description":"<p>This <strong>projectTermExtract plugin</strong> adds a very neat feature to Studio allowing you to extract term candidates from your Project, or specific files within a project. The plugin works by adding a file containing the terms to Studio for translation, and then converts the file to a termbase and adds it to your project.</p><p><br></p><p>The plugin also provides for some simple refining of the extraction using a very simple and visual interface that makes the process of term extraction incredibly simple, and enjoyable to work with.</p><p><br></p><p>If you want to use these terms in your own termbase then this can be easily achieved by using the Glossary Converter, or alternatively you can provide the termbase as a MultiTerm termbase or in another format as a nice value add solution for your customer.</p><p><br></p><p><br></p><p>To learn how to use this application, please check the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/34?tab=documentation\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Documentation </a>tab.</p><p><br></p>","documentation":"<p>Introduction</p><p>The plugin allows users to extract term candidates from the Project, or specific files within a project. The plugin works by adding a file containing the terms to Studio for translation, and then converts the file to a termbase and adds it to the project.</p><p><br></p><p>The plugin also provides for some simple refining of the extraction using a very simple and visual interface that makes the process of term extraction incredibly simple, and enjoyable to work with.</p><p><br></p><p>If you want to use these terms in your own termbase then this can be easily achieved by using the Glossary Converter, or alternatively you can provide the termbase as a MultiTerm termbase or in another format as a nice value add solution for your customer.</p><p><br></p><p>The application is compatible with Studio 2017 and Studio 2019.</p><p><strong style=\"background-color: rgb(255, 255, 153);\">NOTE</strong><span style=\"background-color: rgb(255, 255, 153);\">: article needs to updated with reference to this post -&nbsp;</span><a href=\"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/translationproductivity/f/openexchange_applications/29965/projecttermextract---instructions\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: rgb(255, 255, 153); color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://community.sdl.com/product-groups/translationproductivity/f/openexchange_applications/29965/projecttermextract---instructions</a></p><p>How to use:</p><p>* Important:&nbsp;The application flow can also be found within the video:&nbsp;<a href=\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RtK0iv5RsCk&amp;feature=emb_logo\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RtK0iv5RsCk&amp;feature=emb_logo</a>&nbsp;and it's also described within the article:&nbsp;<a href=\"https://multifarious.filkin.com/2017/09/19/and-the-winner-is/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">https://multifarious.filkin.com/2017/09/19/and-the-winner-is/</a></p><p>1. Open a project / Create new one.</p><p>2. Select the file(s) on which the project term should run.</p><p>3. Right click on the selected file(s) and select the \"<strong>Extract Project Terms</strong>\" option.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3443.pastedimage1591956978138v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>4. Press on the \"<strong>Extract Terms From Selected Files</strong>\" (you can see that below, the terms are displayed within the Project Terms Cloud view.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2438.pastedimage1591957044413v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>5. After the terms were extracted, press on the \"Include&nbsp;terms file to the project\" (this allow you to generate the termbase based on the file)</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1050.pastedimage1591957161232v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>How to generate Termbase</p><p>&nbsp;After the process of including terms is finished, press F5 in Studio to refresh the project, and right click on the new added file -&gt; Select \"<strong>Generate termbase</strong>\" option.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/5852.pastedimage1591957252040v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>*You can check the new termbase from&nbsp;<strong>Project Settings-&gt; Language Pairs-&gt; All Language Pairs -&gt; Termbases</strong>&nbsp;section</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/1000x1200/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0211.pastedimage1591957305682v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>How to generate a new termbase using the same file</p><p>If you want to generate again the termbase for the same file, the following steps needs to be performed:</p><ul><li>Go to&nbsp;<strong>Project Settings -&gt; Language Pairs -&gt; All Language Pairs -&gt; Termbases</strong>&nbsp;and remove the termbase from your project.</li><li>Close Studio.</li><li>Go to your project folder in Windows Explorer and delete now to generate the new termbase.</li></ul><p><img management"}]},{"id":12,"downloadCount":9034,"paidFor":false,"createdAt":"2021-01-20T08:07:11.727Z","updatedAt":"2025-01-13T05:35:39.602338Z","name":"studioViews","description":"<p><strong><em>Problem</em></strong></p><p>Sometimes there is a need to be able to extract some segments from a file, or multiple files, and send them as a single file to others. Sometimes there is a need to open all the files in a virtual view and then save them as a single file to share with others. Sometimes you don't want to open the files at all, but you might want to split them or merge them to send to others.</p><p><br></p><p><strong><em>Resolution</em></strong></p><p>The solution to all of these requirements is <strong>studioViews</strong>. This application will not only allow you to export from the files view, filtered selections in the Editor view, or plain and simple segment selection, but it also allows you to import the translated file you exported back into your project and will update your files accordingly. The exported file is an SDLXLIFF and fully supports all the features of SDLXLIFF (segment statuses, match values, tracked changes etc.). </p><p>To learn how to use this application, please check the <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/12?tab=documentation\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Documentation </a>tab.</p>","documentation":"<h1><strong>Features</strong></h1><p>After installing the plugin you gain access to it in two different places, the&nbsp;<strong><em>Files View</em></strong>&nbsp;and the&nbsp;<strong><em>Editor View</em></strong>&nbsp;which are addressed below.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p><br></p><h2><strong><em>Files View</em></strong></h2><p>You can select one or more files and right-click (the image below is shortened for convenience) where you will find two menu items:</p><ul><li><em>Import into the selected files</em></li><li><em>Split the selected files</em></li></ul><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3678.pastedimage1611221139053v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h3><strong><em>Split the selected files</em></strong></h3><p>In practice you would most likely use the \"Split into selected files\" first and this brings up the following menu:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7571.pastedimage1611221319552v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>If you have used the \"Spit and Merge\" application from the SDL AppStore these options will be familiar for you.&nbsp;The \"Split\" operation allows you to do two important things:</p><ul><li>split the selected files by&nbsp;<em>wordcount</em>, by&nbsp;<em>equal parts</em>&nbsp;or by&nbsp;<em>segment ids</em></li><li>merge the selected files</li></ul><p>split the selected files by&nbsp;<em>wordcount</em>, by&nbsp;<em>equal parts</em>&nbsp;or by&nbsp;<em>segment ids</em></p><p>\"Split by word count\": you specify the maximum number of words you want in each file and the application will split the files to the nearest segment.</p><p>\"Split by equal parts\": you specify how many files you'd like the selected file(s) to be split into and&nbsp;the application will split the files to the nearest segment.</p><p>\"Split by ids\": you specify the segment ID numbers separated by a comma, for example with an 800 segment file you could use this list of ids:</p><p>100,250,300,500</p><p>The application will split the file(s) into five files.</p><p>1-100</p><p>101-250</p><p>251-300</p><p>301-500</p><p>501-800</p><h3><strong><em>merge the selected files</em></strong></h3><p>There is no obvious option for merging so this requires additional explanation.&nbsp;If you have multiple files in your project you could merge them all into one SDLXLIFF by entering \"1\" into the \"number of equal parts\" field:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2474.pastedimage1611224704733v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This effectively allows you to merge files after the project has been created, so it's not only something you have to consider when the project is initially created.</p><p>The naming convention for the output is managed with a simple mask feature, the default being:</p><p>StudioViewsFile.Split_[####].sdlxliff</p><p>You define the mask and the location for the \"split\" files in the Output panel here:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6366.pastedimage1611225075758v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The [####] will create a number with four digits when the files are \"split\".&nbsp;So if I split the files I selected into three files I would have three files like this:</p><p>StudioViewsFile.Split_[0001].sdlxliff</p><p>StudioViewsFile.Split_[0002].sdlxliff</p><p>StudioViewsFile.Split_[0003].sdlxliff</p><p>I could rename them as I see fit so if I wanted the name to be different and the number to only have three digits and have it at the start I could create a mask like this:</p><p>[###]_ProjectName_split.sdlxliff</p><p><br></p><h3><strong><em>Import into the selected files</em></strong></h3><p>This feature allows you to import the updated SDLXLIFF files you previously \"split\".&nbsp;The older&nbsp;<a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/sdlxliff-split-merge/20/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1700044746505.1700047950698.44&amp;__hssc=110027290.18.1700047950698&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">Split and Merge</a>&nbsp;application physically split the files and then merged them back together again.&nbsp;<a href=\"https://appstore.sdl.com/language/app/studioviews/1162/?__hstc=110027290.ce7e76fc76333341c7f24ed2786886d4.1683186741014.1700044746505.1700047950698.44&amp;__hssc=110027290.18.1700047950698&amp;__hsfp=2840097546\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" style=\"background-color: transparent; color: rgb(41, 143, 194);\">studioViews</a>&nbsp;doesn't take this approach and is more robust as a result.&nbsp;studioViews replaces the updated segments in the original project files with the ones that were updated from the split files.&nbsp;All supported features are retained:</p><ul><li>match values</li><li>statuses</li><li>locked segments</li><li>tracked changes</li><li>etc.</li></ul><p>When you select the import feature you will be presented with this window:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0535.pastedimage1611228262776v6.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>It doesn't matter whether&nbsp;you have selected the exact files you \"split\" originally to import into or not.&nbsp;You can select all the files in your project and right-click to import.&nbsp;The application is smart enough to know which segments you are updating and will only update the appropriate segments in the appropriate files.&nbsp;You can also choose to ignore the updates to segments based on statuses, type of matches or locked.&nbsp;You do this by adding the criteria into the \"Ignore Existing Segments\" part.&nbsp;The image above shows \"Locked\" which is the default setting.&nbsp;This will prevent changes made to locked segments in the \"split\" files from being imported into locked segments in the original.</p><p>Once the import is successfully completed you should see a \"Task Result\" window similar to this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0488.pastedimage1611232349588v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The report that is created is a simple log file that provides information on which files were imported and some statistics around what was updated, for example:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/4382.pastedimage1611232486165v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The original project files are also saved by appending the original files with&nbsp;<strong>.Backup_####.sdlxliff</strong>&nbsp;so you can always revert to the original if you're unhappy with the result.</p><h2><strong><em>Editor&nbsp;View</em></strong></h2><p>The&nbsp;<strong>Editor View</strong>&nbsp;provides a similar, although in many ways more functional approach to the features in the&nbsp;<strong>Files View</strong>.&nbsp;First of all you need to activate the View for&nbsp;<strong>studioViews</strong>&nbsp;by clicking on the icon in the&nbsp;<strong>View menu</strong>&nbsp;when you have opened a file in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor View</strong>:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/8540.pastedimage1611233852390v4.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This will open a new view to Trados Studio which you can position wherever you wish.&nbsp;For example:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/3443.pastedimage1611239753153v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>This view has two tabs:</p><ul><li>Export</li><li>Import</li></ul><h3><strong><em>Export</em></strong></h3><p>The&nbsp;<strong>Export</strong>&nbsp;tab allows you to export&nbsp;<em>selected</em>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<em>visible segments</em>&nbsp;to an SDLXLIFF file in a location of your choosing.</p><p><em>Selected segments</em></p><p>These would be segments that you have selected in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor</strong>&nbsp;by holding down the&nbsp;<strong>Ctrl</strong>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<strong>Shift</strong>&nbsp;key (or by using the keyboard).&nbsp;For example, here I have selected segments #3, #5, #7, #8, #9 and #12:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7870.pastedimage1611237181793v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>It doesn't matter how many files you have opened in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor</strong>, you can select whatever you wish.</p><p><em>Visible segments</em></p><p>These would be segments that are currently visible in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor</strong>&nbsp;view.&nbsp;So if you have filtered on this using the&nbsp;<strong>Advanced Display Filter</strong>&nbsp;for example then you may have something like this:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2772.pastedimage1611237450202v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>Here I filtered on 10 of the 82 segments in my two files that are open in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor</strong>.&nbsp;If I&nbsp;<strong>Export</strong>&nbsp;the visible segments I will create an SDLXLIFF with just these 10 segments in it because these are the ones that are visible.</p><p>Whether you use the&nbsp;<em>selected segments</em>&nbsp;or the&nbsp;<em>visible segments</em>&nbsp;once you run the Export you should see a message box similar to this summarising what you have done and where the file has been saved:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/7853.pastedimage1611246214031v3.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>You also have a log file which again explains which files the filtered set was taken from and the name of the file that was saved:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/2112.pastedimage1611246322713v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h3><strong><em>Import</em></strong></h3><p>The exported file can be imported at the files level as explained above, but you can also do this in the&nbsp;<strong>Editor view</strong>.</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6014.pastedimage1611249091566v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>The options are similar.&nbsp;You just set the criteria for any segments in the original source that you would like to be exempt from updating, select the sdlxliff and click on&nbsp;<strong>Import</strong>.&nbsp;This feature will provide the \"Task Result\" as before, but you will also see the updated segments change in the file you have open in the Editor:</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/1817.pastedimage1611249498519v2.png\" alt=\" \"></p><p>In this case I signed-off and locked the segments in the file I imported and these are now changed in the file you can see open under the message window.&nbsp;And of course you have a record through the log file.</p><p>The original project file&nbsp;is also saved by appending the original files with&nbsp;<strong>.Backup_####.sdlxliff</strong>&nbsp;so you can always revert to the original if you're unhappy with the result.</p><p><br></p><h2><strong>TellMe</strong></h2><p>As with most of the plugins created by the SDL Community Developers you can always find your way to help by using the&nbsp;<strong>TellMe</strong>&nbsp;feature in Trados Studio.&nbsp;It's a quick way to get some help if you need it!</p><p><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/320x240/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/0447.pastedimage1611249797344v1.png\" alt=\" \"></p><h1><strong>Important Notes</strong></h1><h2><strong><em>Content formatting, placeholders and spaces are not included in the exported document</em></strong></h2><p>Structural elements such as content formatting, placeholders and spaces that are external to segment pairs are not included in the document that is exported by studioViews. This ensures that those elements are preserved when the translations are being re-imported.&nbsp;The only exception to this rule are formatting tags located at the paragraph unit level.</p><p>Considering the example below:</p><ul><li>The following formatting will&nbsp;<strong>not</strong>&nbsp;be included in the exported content</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">The&nbsp;<strong>space</strong>&nbsp;between segment 2 and segment 3.</li><li>The formatting&nbsp;<strong>bold=true</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>color=FF000</strong>&nbsp;associated with segment 8</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\"><br></li><li>The following formatting will be included in the exported content</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">The formatting&nbsp;<strong>bold=true</strong>&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;<strong>italic=true</strong>&nbsp;associated with segment 5</li><li class=\"ql-indent-1\">The formatting&nbsp;<strong>italic=true</strong>&nbsp;associated with segments 7 &amp; 8</li></ul><p><strong>Example:</strong></p><p><strong><img src=\"https://community.rws.com/resized-image/__size/831x314/__key/communityserver-wikis-components-files/00-00-00-03-37/6330.StudioViewsExample1.1.png\" alt=\" \"></strong></p><h2><strong><em>Segment Split/Merge Support</em></strong></h2><h3><strong><em>When the exported document is changed</em></strong></h3><p>Support for identifying alignment differences to the segment pair structure within and across paragraph units.</p><p>Consider the following workflow:</p><ol><li>A Project Manager creates a new Trados Studio project with&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(153, 51, 0);\"><em>document a</em></strong>.A section of&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(153, 51, 0);\"><em>document a</em></strong>&nbsp;is exported to&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\"><em>document b</em></strong>&nbsp;using studioViews and distributed to a linguist for translation.The linguist creates a new Trados Studio project with&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\"><em>document b</em></strong>The linguist opens&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\"><em>document b</em></strong>&nbsp;in the editor and performs structural changes to the segments (e.g. split and/or merge), before providing translations.The linguist delivers document b back to the Project ManagerThe Project Manager Imports the translations from&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\"><em>document b</em></strong>&nbsp;into&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(153, 51, 0);\"><em>document a</em></strong></li></ol><p>Given this workflow, the segmentation of the paragraph units from&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(51, 153, 102);\"><em>document b</em></strong>&nbsp;are no longer aligned with those of the original&nbsp;<strong style=\"color: rgb(153, 51, 0);\"><em>document a</em></strong>, as the linguist performed additional segmentation and/or merged existing segments. The latest release provides full support in recognizing segment pair structural differences and automatically aligns the segments to be imported.</p><h4><strong><em>Best Practice</em></strong></h4><ul><li>Avoid updating the segment structure of the original document, before importing translations back from an exported document.&nbsp;&nbsp;It is expected that structural changes to the segmentation may exist in the exported document, but not in both.&nbsp;&nbsp;If structural changes to the segmentation exist in both, then those from the document being imported take precedence.</li><li>Avoid changing the source content of the original and/or exported document, before importing translations back from an exported document.&nbsp;&nbsp;We use the source content to aid in aligning the segmentation and/or merging differences.&nbsp;&nbsp;Any changes to the source content other than removing or adding spaces will affect the alignment procedure.</li></ul><p><br></p>","supportUrl":"https://community.rws.com/product-groups/trados-portfolio/rws-appstore/f/rws-appstore","downloadUrl":"http://api-appstore.rws.com/app-store-api/v1/plugins/12/versions/1410","changelog":{"Trados filetype","description":"<p>Filetype support for the webVTT filetype used in subtitling (HTML5).</p><p>Can work alongside the Studio Subtitling plugin that is available on the RWS Appstore for Can work alongside the Studio Subtitling plugin that is available on the RWS AppStore for enhanced context when translating/editing/proofing.</p>","documentation":"<h5>Overview</h5><p>WebVTT (Web Video Text Tracks) is a file format used for displaying text tracks, such as subtitles, captions, and descriptions, in HTML5 videos. While Trados Studio supports WebVTT natively, there is a WebVTT plugin available that integrates with Trados Studio. This plugin offers additional features by providing contextual placement.</p><h5>Getting Started</h5><p><strong>1.Minimum Requirements</strong></p><ul><li>Trados Studio 2019 SR2</li></ul><p><strong>2.Installation</strong></p><p>You can install WebVTT File Type either directly within Trados Studio via the Integrated AppStore or alternatively&nbsp;you can download and install from the RWS AppStore&nbsp;</p><p><strong>3.Access</strong></p><p>Once installed, WebVTT can be accessed in various ways:</p><ul><li>Trados Studio Tell Me Feature</li><li>Default Path: Go to File -&gt; Options -&gt; File Types</li><li>New Project: Create New Project -&gt; File Type Identifier Settings</li></ul><p class=\"ql-indent-1\"><em>Tip:</em> Update your selected project template with your new file type preference, after completing the project preparation tasks. Alternatively you can update Project Templates by going to File -&gt; Setup -&gt; Project Templates, select your project template to edit and then manage your list of supported file types.</p><ul><li>Existing Project Selected: Project Settings -&gt; File Types</li></ul><p class=\"ql-indent-1\"><em>Tip: </em>You can add new files to an existing project, as long as the project is recognised as a Studio project and not a Single file project. However file type need to be manged prior to adding new files (conversion from native to *sdlxliff)</p><p><u>Note:</u> If you don’t see WebVTT as a listed file type, you may need to click on \"Additional installed File Types Exist\" to enable it as a supported file type.</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/a1045b49-0c9a-4b16-9f49-f189331bfc2f/Resources/6a3efaee-bf99-4351-b0b2-9cb717ce875d.png?dl=1\"></p><h5>Working In Trados Studio</h5><p><strong>1.Recommendation</strong></p><p>We recommend working with subtitling files through the Creating projects workflow as apose to Creating projects using Translate Single Document workflow.&nbsp;This is so you can take full advantage of all the benefits that comes with managing Studio projects where as Single file projects are restricted.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p><strong>2.File Type Identifier</strong></p><p>When adding your WebVTT file to a project, make sure the File Type Identifier lists WebVTT V 5.0.0.0 to use the plugin instance, instead of Subtitles 1.0.0.0, which is native to Trados Studio. You can see the File Type Identifier either during the project creation process or in the Files View if your layout is set to \"File details layout.\"</p><p><img src=\"https://rws-ci-appstore-resources-eu-central-1.s3.amazonaws.com/a1045b49-0c9a-4b16-9f49-f189331bfc2f/Resources/cc66a654-f629-45cb-b584-ef44b82791e3.png?dl=1\" style=\"color: var(--pa-admin-input-foreground); font-family: var(--pa-admin-input-fontfamily); font-size: var(--pa-admin-input-fontsize);\"><strong>3.Reference File</strong></p><p>When working at the Studio Project level instead of \"Translate Single Document,\" you can include an MP4 file as a reference file. The advantages of this are documented in <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/8?tab=documentation\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Studio Subtitling</a></p><p><strong>4.Feature support</strong></p><p>For more additional feature support and information on how to work with subtitling files in Trados Studio, you may wish to install <a href=\"https://appstore.rws.com/Plugin/5\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Studio Subtitling</a> as a
